title: Music’s Value Gap
description: The opening session of MTUK s Innovation Investment Day brings together Scott Cohen and Cliff Fluet for a fireside chat to examine a question that sits at the heart of the modern music economy: who actually captures the value that music creates? From the rise of catalogue as an asset class to the wealth gap between platform owners and the creators who built that value, this conversation sets the scene for a day of debate about where the music technology sector goes next.

title: Comfort Is Costing Us Connection
description: In a digital world built to eliminate discomfort, what happens to our ability to connect? Hinge CEO Jackie Jantos explores how the phone reflex and a culture of ease may be quietly eroding modern relationships. While technology makes meeting people easier, real connection still requires effort, uncertainty, and emotional risk. This talk asks what we lose when everything becomes frictionless and why discomfort remains essential for meaningful connection.

title: Opening Ceremony and Inauguration hosted by Mubarak Ali Foundation, Durjoy Bangladesh Foundation and British Bangladesh Power & Inspiration Foundation.
description: Join us for Bangla House s Opening Remarks with the three organisers: Mubarak Ali Foundation, Durjoy Banglades Foundation and British Bangladesh Power Inspiration Foundation.

title: A Different Valley: Can London Win?
description: London was voted the 2nd most start-up friendly city in Europe, boasts over 50 unicorns and is setting records for investment across numerous industries. But there are also serious questions about whether His Majesty s Start-up scene can stay competitive, and stay apace with Silicon Valley, where much of the AI revolution is currently concentrated. Making a pitch for London s potential, hear from our panel:

title: Roundtable: As personal AI agents continue to grow in 2026, how can we design interfaces that foster trust without falling into the Uncanny Valley?
description: The session will be structured around three main themes for discussion: 1. Trust: Explores the factors that genuinely earn trust in AI (accuracy, transparency, brand, design), how AI should communicate uncertainty, and whether users prioritise explanations or reliable outcomes. 2. How human should AI feel?: Debates the ideal persona for AI (a person, a tool, or something new). It questions the inherent trustworthiness of abstract text-based agents versus increasingly realistic voice and video agents, and asks if the Uncanny Valley is a barrier to overcome, or a boundary to respect. 3. Design Ethics: Addresses the morality of designing AI to seem more capable than it is, the potential conflict between trust and design choices that optimize for engagement/charm, and the ethics of fixed versus dynamic AI personalities.
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: Roundtable: What will influencer marketing look like in 2026?
description: Influencer is now considered a key element of the media mix; no longer an add-on, but a central part to a campaign strategy. At the same time, there is still confusion over what it includes, whether it s driving results, and what it might look like in future. We re combatting challenges such as saturated platforms, consumer distrust, perception issues, and the infiltration of AI content on platforms leading people to switch off and try to end the doomscroll. Given all of this, and all of the new tools and practices, what do we think influencer marketing will look like in 1 year s time? From how brands work with creators, how they re scaling, how they re creating content, and how they re integrating new technology and tools. This roundtable will discuss the current influencer marketing landscape and challenges faced by brands and creators, then look at how we predict these changing over the next 12 months and how marketers can prepare.
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: The AI Product Revolution
description: While our screens have evolved at lightning speed, the products we consume and the food we eat have remained trapped in an analogue past. This is what we call the Legacy Trap. To escape it, we need a fundamental rewiring of how we innovate. Join Mat as Muchnick, Founder CEO of NotCo AI, for a first-hand look at how Giuseppe AI, the first foundational AI platform purpose-built for the consumer packaged goods industry, is revolutionising how global companies conceive, optimise, and scale products. Pioneering the intersection of AI and food innovation, Muchnick will demonstrate the power of gen AI to disrupt the end-to-end product development lifecycle and usher in a new era of product innovation.

title: The Great Fintech Convergence
description: We ve spent a decade talking about fintech disruption. Now, we re entering the era of integration. Moving past the Web3 hype, this panel explores the practical intersection of stablecoins, real-time data, and regulated finance. Hear how these technologies are actually being used to fix the financial engine for small businesses making it faster, cheaper, and more accessible. No buzzwords, just a look at the new financial stack that s finally being built for the real economy.

Anna was among the early team members building ICONIQ s European presence and has supported partnerships with companies including ElevenLabs, Rillet, Writer and Conduct AI. She was named to the Forbes 30 Under 30 Europe list in 2026. title: 'Alien' Life to Human Cures: The AI Hunt for Evolution's Hidden Solutions
description: Glen Gowers and Oliver Vince founded Basecamp Research after hauling a solar-powered lab across Western Europe s largest ice cap to conduct the world s first off-grid DNA sequencing. What they discovered was astonishing: more than half the DNA they analysed was entirely unknown to science. Today, their team is mapping Earth s genetic dark matter in 28 countries and training cutting-edge AI models on this unprecedented evolutionary dataset with the goal of designing new genetic medicines and cell therapies.

title: Deadline Reality TV Summit Keynote: SYEDA IRTIZAALI
description: Syeda Irtizaali oversees UK unscripted for Netflix and breaks down her priorities and goals as the streamer looks to build upon its slate of buzzy shows such as TK and TK. Irtizaali, who commissioned the wildly successful show The Traitors and its Celebrity version while at the BBC, is one of international TV s most respected unscripted execs, and shares her take on the fast-moving business.

title: Sound Investments 2026: Music Tech Report Launch
description: MTUK launches Sound Investments, its flagship annual report on investment into the UK music tech sector, produced with KPMG UK. Now in its second year, Sound Investments 2026 will continue to act as the definitive industry reference for investors, policymakers, and senior stakeholders seeking to understand the scale, dynamics, and challenges facing one of the UK s most innovative growth sectors. After a presentation of the data from Helena Kosinski, a panel of investors and investment experts will debate the findings.

title: AI is scaling. So is the bill - but who's paying it?
description: AI is being built at scale, and the demands on energy, land, and water are growing with it. As the economic opportunity expands, so do the questions about what it costs and who these fall on. The true price of the intelligence revolution may not yet be fully visible in our balance sheets, our regulations, or our long-term planning. This panel brings together voices from industry, policy, and civil society to ask what it would take to account for those costs properly - for the present economy, the planet, and the generations who will inherit what we build today.

title: Challenging the Attention Economy with the Light Phone
description: invention of the year 2025) joins Eric Fulwiler of Rival (the authority on challenger brands) to break down how Light has built a high-impact brand without relying on traditional advertising, large budgets, or attention-maximising tactics, and what that means for the future of marketing leadership. For CMOs and senior marketers, the value is practical and immediate: learn how to drive growth with constraint, turn brand into a performance engine, and identify what to stop doing in an industry that rewards excess. Expect clear perspectives on challenger strategy, human-centred design, and how to create relevance in a landscape where AI is making everything easier to produce, and harder to matter. Walk away with a sharper point of view on where to focus, what to cut, and how to build brands that outperform without burning attention or budget.

title: Who speaks for nature in the age of AI?
description: AI relies heavily on energy - but also on nature, especially water and land. Whilst energy deployment is already a central emphasis, the cost to nature and what will remain for future generations is yet to be explored. This session asks how nature s value should be addressed and who should be involved in the process.

title: Why We Need Immigrants
description: Immigrants have founded a third of the world s most transformative companies, but global discourse still frames migration as a crisis rather than an opportunity to drive innovation. In this powerful discussion, one of the world s leading strategy experts, Rita McGrath, is joined by Thinkers50 Radar Award Winner Neri Karra Sillaman and Forbes 30-under-30 entrepreneur Mursal Hedayat to explore how immigration unlocks new forms of creativity, resilience and organisational reinvention. From frontier technologies to future-of-work models, the speakers reveal how migrant entrepreneurs overperform not despite adversity but because of it, reframing migration as the most underused competitive advantage of our times.

title: Rewiring Connection: Why Festivals Matter More Than Ever
Truth Under Fire: How Journalism Can Win the Information War
How Can Europe Compete in the Age of AI and Deep Tech?
description: Join legendary entrepreneur and ARM co-founder Hermann Hauser for a conversation on innovation, investment, talent, and the strategies Europe must embrace to lead in the technologies shaping the future.

title: TradFi Meets Web3
description: As institutional giants embrace blockchain and decentralized protocols adapt to global regulation, the friction between legacy systems and Web3 is giving way to a new, unified financial architecture. Stani Kulechov, Keith Grose, and Emma Mayer explore the massive shift from crypto speculation to real-world integration, examining how the world s largest pools of capital are finally moving on-chain to redefine liquidity, ownership, and the very nature of global markets.

title: Why the Future Belongs to the Unthinkable
description: Comfort doesn t change the world. Comfort means complacency, comfort means to be to standstill. The ideas that reshape industries rarely feel safe they re the ones that seem too steep to climb, too loud to voice, and too sharp to embrace. Join Mastercard s Raja Rajamannar as he explores why the ideas most worth backing often feel unreasonable at first. Through real-world examples, he challenges leaders and creatives to stop self‑editing, grab hold of what feels different, and be bold enough to give the unthinkable room to breathe.

title: Deadline Reality TV Summit Panel: AI GOES OFF SCRIPT
description: Producers and creatives are using artificial intelligence to power character generation, create whole new formats, and generate movie-level sequences in their shows. Executives at the cutting edge of this shift discuss how AI is changing reality, factual and entertainment programming and what it means for the future of production.

title: From Zero to IP: A Film Founder’s Guide to Building Across Borders
description: A fireside conversation exploring what it takes to build a creative career and company from the outside - without inherited networks, across countries, and within an industry that can be difficult to access. Romanian-born, London-based producer Mara Cracaleanu reflects on her journey moving between Romania and the UK, starting from zero connections, and learning in real time how development, financing, international collaboration, and creative entrepreneurship actually work in practice. Structured as a layered conversation, the session moves from personal experience into wider industry insight: access, proof, borders, ownership, and scale. Through examples from her work across documentary and fiction, Mara will explore how emerging producers can build credibility, create opportunities, and think beyond traditional production models toward IP, cross-platform storytelling, and internationally connected creative businesses. At its core, this is a conversation about building without a roadmap, and creating your own blueprint.

title: Music and More
description: A chance for delegates to meet and enjoy a performance, alongside light refreshments.

title: Roundtable: Human Curiosity in the Age of Machine Intelligence
description: When we have answers at our fingertips, it s tempting to delegate our thinking along with our tasks. But entire fields in science and technology exist because someone followed a line of curiosity that wasn t obviously useful at the time. In this roundtable led by neuroscientist Anne-Laure Le Cunff, participants will explore what we risk losing when we outsource not just our work but our wondering, and what it takes to protect curiosity as a form of human agency in the age of machine intelligence. Drawing on research and lived experience across disciplines, the group will trade perspectives to map where curiosity still matters most, and what each of us can do to keep it alive in our own work. Anchoring the discussion, a deceptively simple question: in an age of infinite answers, what s still worth wondering about? Thought Starter Questions: Where in your work or life have you noticed yourself reaching for AI instead of sitting with a question? What s a question you think AI can t answer for you? What would you want your future self or future generations to stay curious about, regardless of what AI can do?
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: Roundtable: Why Experience Is So Hard to Measure (And What To Do About It)
description: Everyone agrees that customer experience matters, but few marketers can confidently prove how it creates value or where it moves the needle. Most organisations still lean heavily on NPS and other legacy metrics that were never designed to capture the emotional reality of modern experiences. Yet bonuses, budgets, and boardroom decisions still depend on them. In this roundtable, we ll unpack why experience measurement is broken and explore more effective, emotionally aware ways to understand what truly drives impact. Drawing on Spikes Excitement Points methodology and a database of over 750,000 datapoints, we ll look at the small number of emotionally resonant interactions that create disproportionate value for brands, and why they are so often missed by standard KPIs. This will be a practical, conversational session. We ll share real-world challenges, compare approaches, and discuss concrete ways to embed emotion into experience strategies and measurement frameworks that marketers can actually use.
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: Time's Up For Touts?
description: The UK has been at the forefront of a global push back against industrial-scale online ticket touting. With the British Government now promising new legislation to put fans first and effectively ban the resale of tickets for profit, we hear from four individuals driving these changes and discuss the implications for live events and live entertainment.

title: It’s Clinical Not Cosmetic
description: GLP-1 medications are transforming how we think about chronic disease, yet up to 60% of patients quit within the first year. In this fireside chat with Sky News David Rhodes, eMed CEO Linda Yaccarino makes the case that GLP-1s are not a weight loss story, they re a preventative care story. From groundbreaking new data on GLP-1 outcomes in women to the role employers must play in closing the access and adherence gap, this session brings an urgent, unfiltered perspective on what s broken in healthcare and what it looks like when it actually works.

title: Relay Mindset: What London Can Learn From The Estonian Scene
description: Estonia has birthed more unicorns per capita than any other European nation, driven by a unique Relay Mindset a culture where founders immediately reinvest capital, talent, and lessons back into the ecosystem. Join Sten Tamkivi (Plural), Martin Sokk (Lightyear), and Tiffany Tuisk (GenZ Founders) to explore how London can adopt this pay-it-forward playbook. Discover how radical transparency and ecosystem recycling can transform a tech hub from a collection of silos into a high-velocity engine for global growth.

title: Mike Skinner and Rory Sutherland in Conversation
Flo Cartwright (Film Marketing + Fundraising)
description: Flo (she/her) is a film marketing and events specialist with a decade of experience across screen industries, higher education and non-profits. She is currently Head of Campaigns at Reclaim The Frame, finding audiences for female and marginalised gender led film. She has managed marketing and fundraising at SOAS University of London, One World Media, and the British Independent Film Awards (BIFA), developing audiences for British independent cinema. Speak with her about marketing, comms and PR strategies for film and how to identify, target and find an audience for your project. His work sits at the intersection of music and visual storytelling, collaborating with sync teams to shape music-led narratives. His short film Kill Two Birds screened at 32 international festivals and earned a Golden Trailer Award nomination for Best Independent Trailer. He is in post-production on his next film, Tomorrow (2027). Ask him about trailer craft, music-driven storytelling, and short-form marketing films. title: Nicolee Tsin (Filmmaking + Cultural Preservation)
description: Nicolee s work as a writer and director is influenced by her upbringing in Hong Kong, grounded in textured visual storytelling with themes of food, memories and modern relationships. As she shifts toward fiction, her goal is to create mixed-genre stories that are strong yet minimal, universal yet intimate. She intends to preserve her culture through film, drawing equal inspiration from indie and Asian cinema. Having honed her voice through music videos for Olivia Dean, Lola Young and Nia Archives, she has since directed commercials for Nike, Adidas, Coca-Cola and Intel. description: Rose is Head of Artists Moving Image at FLAMIN, Film London s Artists Moving Image Network. FLAMIN supports art world practitioners working with moving image, commissioning over 200 productions by artist film-makers and offering advice sessions, residencies and workshops. Rose holds an MA in Arts Management from City University and a Fine Art degree from the University of Tasmania. She previously worked at CCA Glasgow and Arts Council England. Drawing on 30 years of experience, Rose can offer advice on engaging with the art world and gallery sector, including funding opportunities and career strategy. title: Sarah Linton (Film & TV Development)
description: Sarah is Head of Development at Instinct Productions, overseeing high-end scripted film, television and documentary. With over two decades in development, script editing and production, she has held senior roles at BBC Studios, Kudos, Carnival Films and NBCUniversal, and consulted for ABC Network as UK Comedy and Drama Scout. Her Script Editor credits include the first series of Killing Eve, Downton Abbey, The Halcyon and Lucky Man. Sarah has mentored for Netflix Grow Creative Fiction Labs, Channel 4 Screenwriting, and Serial Eyes at DFFB. Ask her about scripted comedy and drama development, storytelling, navigating the UK, US and European TV landscape, script editing, identifying talent, and building a career across the industry. title: Screen Meet-Up with Pact
description: This Screen industry meet-up is hosted by Pact, the UK screen sector trade body for independent production and distribution companies. Join Pact at this year s SXSW London, to bring together professionals from across the UK and international screen sectors for an afternoon of networking, conversation and collaboration. Since 1991, Pact s work has helped transform the sector from a cottage industry into a British success story which employs thousands of people and is worth billions of pounds to the UK economy. Its members range from small companies just starting out through to well-established international super indies . Pact supports its members to grow both domestically and internationally by offering them the tools and knowledge they need and ensuring the best possible economic, regulatory and legislative environment for them to do business in.

title: Susan Simnett (Film Production + Development)
description: Susan Simnett is an award-winning independent producer and founder of Over The Fence Films, a boutique production company specialising in features under 5m. With experience across creative development, funding and distribution, mentor sessions can look at project development, building a pitch, financial structures and plotting a route to market. title: Tania Hung (Brand-Talent Partnerships + Cultural Impact)
description: Tania Hung is Director of Partnerships Ventures at Attachment, an independent entertainment marketing agency that helps brands harness the power of celebrities, creators and entertainment to build cultural impact. She leads brand-talent partnerships for global names like Snap, Uber and the BFC, while also guiding early-stage startups on securing and structuring celebrity collaborations. With over a decade architecting partnerships across entertainment, consumer and tech, Tania specialises in matching brands with the right talent and crafting deals that deliver both creative credibility and commercial value.

title: Beautiful British Games with the Beautiful Games Club podcast
description: ustwo games - the studio behind Monument Valley, Alba and Assemble With Care - are bringing their Beautiful Games Club to a live stage at UK House, and they re asking the question that sits at the heart of everything they make: what actually makes a game beautiful? Part book club, part live podcast, the Beautiful Games Club celebrates video games that deserve a closer look - games that resonate visually, creatively and emotionally long after you ve put the controller down. In this special live conversation, the team at ustwo games will unpack some standout British-made games, sharing their personal responses to them, the ideas they bring into the world, and the wider cultural impact they leave behind. Come join and have a cosy chat about games over a cuppa.

title: City Diplomacy in an Age of Risk
description: As cities become the front line for tackling the world s biggest challenges, from climate change and migration to technology and economic growth, mayors are emerging as powerful global actors. This panel brings together leading mayors from across the world to explore how cities are forging international partnerships, driving innovation, and influencing policy beyond national borders.

title: Laughter as Medicine: Can comedy help men’s health?
description: Men are significantly less likely to access traditional talking therapies, yet experience disproportionately poor mental health outcomes. The UK is emerging as a global frontrunner in addressing this gap by pairing the new Men s Health Strategy with the world-first integration of comedy into public creative healthcare, with the NHS, called Comedy-on-Prescription. Dr Simon Opher brings his Simon Says, Let s Talk podcast to SXSW London 2026. The panel will explore how comedy, creative health, and technology can complement traditional healthcare spanning prevention, social prescribing, and scalable delivery showing how culturally accessible interventions can improve wellbeing and be delivered at scale globally. It will also examine the wider positive effects of integrating comedy into national healthcare systems, including new paid, sustainable opportunities for creators amid AI-driven disruption to the comedy labour market.

title: Live Q&A with David Grinspoon & Patrice Vermette
description: Join astrobiologist David Grinspoon and Academy Award-winning production designer Patrice Vermette for a live Q A exploring the intersection of science, storytelling and world-building. From imagining alien worlds to visualising the future of humanity, this conversation will delve into how science and cinema shape the way we see our place in the universe.

Rose holds an MA in Arts Management from City University and a Fine Art degree from the University of Tasmania. She previously worked at CCA Glasgow and Arts Council England. Drawing on 30 years of experience, Rose can offer advice on engaging with the art world and gallery sector, including funding opportunities and career strategy. title: Screen Roundtable: How to Make a Film Sustainably
description: As the industry shifts into more and more co-productions, often involving large logistical operations with high carbon footprint, and new technology such as artificial intelligence threatens to set environmentally-focused initiatives back a decade, sustainability in film is more important than ever. This roundtable will explore the many different ways to make your film production more sustainable.

title: Susan Simnett (Film Production + Development)
description: Susan Simnett is an award-winning independent producer and founder of Over The Fence Films, a boutique production company specialising in features under 5m. With experience across creative development, funding and distribution, mentor sessions can look at project development, building a pitch, financial structures and plotting a route to market. title: Tania Hung (Brand-Talent Partnerships + Cultural Impact)
description: Tania Hung is Director of Partnerships Ventures at Attachment, an independent entertainment marketing agency that helps brands harness the power of celebrities, creators and entertainment to build cultural impact. She leads brand-talent partnerships for global names like Snap, Uber and the BFC, while also guiding early-stage startups on securing and structuring celebrity collaborations. With over a decade architecting partnerships across entertainment, consumer and tech, Tania specialises in matching brands with the right talent and crafting deals that deliver both creative credibility and commercial value.

title: AI & Industry: New Systems of Logic
description: AI is transforming industrial operations, creating new systems of logic that can drive higher efficiency and agility. Drawing on real-time data from across the asset lifecycle, industrial plants are beginning to run daily operations, test scenarios and act on problems autonomously. When any worker can ask questions of their natural language data and receive trustworthy answers, teams can act upon insights almost instantly. Such innovation poses questions: How can automated processes and conversational interfaces augment, rather than replace, human decision-making? How do we ensure underlying data foundations remain highly accurate and appropriately governed? How can models such as open source propel greater accessibility and accountability? In this discussion, thought leaders spanning the tech, industry and energy sectors will explore how interdisciplinary collaboration can effectively put practical, trustworthy information in more hands while keeping humans in the loop to ensure safety, security and agile operations.

title: The Robotaxi Reality: Can Waymo Master London’s Complexity?
description: London s streets are the ultimate stress test. Between Shoreditch s tight turns, throngs of people in Soho on a Saturday night, and the delicate dance with thousands of cyclists, if autonomous vehicles can master this environment, they can succeed anywhere. Join Waymo Chief Product Officer Saswat Panigrahi and Sky News Rowland Manthorpe for a candid breakdown of how Waymo is teaching its Driver for the UK s unique urban environment. They ll explore lessons learned from real-world operations across the U.S., the distinct technical challenges of navigating London s roadways, and why shifting the burden of driving to AI (or robots) might actually help us all be more human. Join us to learn what it takes to bring the world s leading autonomous ride-hailing service to its first European home but certainly not its last!

title: Why Creativity Needs a Seat at the Adult's Table
description: As creatives, we are often told to sit at the kids table and leave the business to the adults. It s time for creatives to move upstream to better position themselves for a future that is rapidly changing and making many parts of our jobs irrelevant. David Lee, Chief Brand and Creative Officer at Squarespace has spent the last decade growing the company from a small, humble start-up into a public facing company with thousands of employees, while simultaneously transforming its creative team into one of the most awarded in-house agencies and brands in the world. In this discussion, David will walk the audience through some of the tips and lessons he has learned from spending half his career on the agency side and the other half on the brand side.

title: Austrian Music Market Presentation & Company Pitches
description: Meet the Austrian music delegation and gain insights into Austria s music market. The session begins with a short introduction to the Austrian music business landscape, followed by company presentations from Annemarie Reisinger-Treiber , Ink Music , Northchain Records, RAIN and Tape Capitol Music . The session is presented by Austrian Music Export.

title: Fanbase Forward: How Technology is Transforming Music's Most Valuable Asset
description: Music fandom has always been the engine of the industry - but the tools available to harness it have changed beyond recognition. From AI-powered audience segmentation and real-time consumption data to short-form video, this panel examines what the new fandom economy looks like, and who is building the infrastructure to power it. A new generation of companies is building products at the intersection of fan data, monetisation, and engagement - tools that help artists understand their audiences, deepen relationships, and create sustainable revenue beyond streaming. The conversation will also explore how consumption patterns are shifting. Younger audiences are engaging with music from well before their time, drawn in through cultural moments, e.g. a TV soundtrack, a viral clip, a game placement, and converted into active fans of legacy artists and catalogues. Claire Mas, Strategic Advisor, CriticalMas (Moderator) Will Beardmore, Director of Fan Data Strategy, Universal Music Group (Panelist) Dot Levine, SVP Global Communications, Vevo London (Panelist) James Morrison, Co-founder CEO, Planet Fans (Panelist) Lauren Kreisler, Director of Brand Digital, Official Charts (Panelist)

Speak with her about marketing, comms and PR strategies for film and how to identify, target and find an audience for your project. title: Music Groupchat:  Inside A&R Today
description: Inside A R Today: behind the closed doors of the industry s most decisive room. Three tables, twenty-minute rounds, no filter, no handouts. From the first demo listen to the signing sprint, label poker to artist meltdowns, get the unpolished truth from active A Rs, former gatekeepers, and the people who actually press green. Bring your questions, leave your ego. This is where tracks get chosen and careers change hands.

title: Nicolee Tsin (Filmmaking + Cultural Preservation)
description: Nicolee s work as a writer and director is influenced by her upbringing in Hong Kong, grounded in textured visual storytelling with themes of food, memories and modern relationships. As she shifts toward fiction, her goal is to create mixed-genre stories that are strong yet minimal, universal yet intimate. She intends to preserve her culture through film, drawing equal inspiration from indie and Asian cinema. Having honed her voice through music videos for Olivia Dean, Lola Young and Nia Archives, she has since directed commercials for Nike, Adidas, Coca-Cola and Intel. title: The Architecture of Invisible Finance
description: The best financial services are the ones you never see. We are entering the era of invisible finance, where banking is no longer a destination but a background layer woven into the fabric of our daily digital lives. From instant credit at checkout to real-time treasury management, the traditional bank branch is being replaced by high-performance APIs and seamless code. Hear our panel pull back the curtain on the infrastructure powering this transformation, discussing how the convergence of Open Banking, BaaS, and deep-tech integration is finally making finance frictionless, ubiquitous, and entirely transparent.

title: AI's Next Frontier: Life Itself
description: AI s next frontier isn t digital - it s biological. From scientific discovery to global shift changes, this panel examines how AI is unlocking the ability to understand and build with biology in entirely new ways. Join the pioneers shaping one of the most consequential innovation waves of our time.

title: The Future of Ocean Exploration Presented by New Scientist
description: The deep ocean is more accessible than ever before. This opens up the exciting prospect of new scientific discoveries from amazing marine creatures to the potential origins of life. At the same time, there are increasing political and economic pressures to extract resources like fish or critical minerals from the ocean floor. This panel will discuss what the future holds for the depths of our oceans, including the implementation of the High Seas Treaty, the march toward deep‑sea mining and the cutting of national research budgets. What will these changes mean for the future of ocean science, exploration and the sustainable management of the planet s last true wilderness?

title: Karolina Lebek
description: Karolina Lebek is an artist and musician based between London and Wroclaw, working across a range of media including video, photography, installation and live performance. Her practice, exploring interests in consciousness, ecology and mental health, gives new form to the exploration of the sacred, the ritual and the body, in context of geological time. Concerned with generational trauma, histories of migration and displacement, and the intersections between human artefacts, psychology, and ancestral knowledge, her work embraces a poetry of decay and transformation that rearranges history to shed new light on the present.

title: Play Bigger: Creativity, Culture and the Power of Scale
description: Creativity and play are evolving - it s shaping how culture is created, shared, and experienced. From a single brick to a global entertainment leader, the LEGO Group has spent more than 90 years evolving how creativity shows up in the world - and how audiences participate in it. In this session, Julia Goldin (Chief Product and Marketing Officer) and Federico Begher (Senior Vice President, Global Marketing) explore how the LEGO Group operates not just as a brand, but as a system for creativity and a platform for participation - powered by the LEGO System in Play, where six bricks unlock over 900 million possibilities. They will share how a relentless focus on consumer obsession, creativity at scale and innovation is helping the LEGO Group push the boundaries of play; from cultural collaborations to new, interactive experiences. Join them to discover how play is shaping culture and what it takes to build creativity at global scale.

title: Surviving Extreme Weather: A Story of Resilience
description: When climate shocks cease being isolated events and start changing what is insurable, investable and liveable, we know we need to adapt. Whether through extreme heat, fire, floods, storms or droughts, a warmer world brings consequences that are already re-shaping lives. This session explores whether we can go beyond survival mode to build true resilience in a world where people can still thrive.

title: Zumba Taster
hall: Dray Walk Gallery - Zumba House, Class

title: Introduction by Alberto Perlman
description: Session introduction to Stay Loud. Stay Well by Zumba CEO Alberto Perlman

title: The Future of UK R&B: Why the Official UK R&B Chart Matters
description: For years UK R B has continued to grow culturally, commercially and globally, yet the genre has never had an official chart dedicated to documenting and spotlighting the movement. This conversation will explore why the Official UK R B Chart is a major moment for the scene and why infrastructure, visibility and cultural recognition matter for the future of UK R B. Hosted by DJ Ace ( Everything RnB), the panel will feature representatives from the Official Charts Company, DSP platforms and UK R B artists for the first public industry-facing conversation and unveiling of the Official UK R B Chart. Together the panel will discuss why the chart matters, the wider vision behind the project, plans for launch, weekly content ideas and how the chart can help shape key cultural moments throughout the year. The session will also explore the growth of UK R B, the challenges artists have faced within the industry and why now feels like the right time for an official platform dedicated to the genre.

title: AI & Education: Time to Get to Work
description: By the time you finish reading this description, thousands of students will have used AI to write an essay, solve a proof, or summarise a chapter. Rather than ponder whether students should use AI, however, the more pertinent question in many countries is now how. In this session, Joleen Liang (Squirrel AI) and two additional experts will bring together distinct perspectives exploring how AI tools are propelling personalised learning at scale, reflect on lessons from the first nation to make AI literacy a mandatory part of the national curriculum, and consider governance approaches to ensure AI learning maintains high standards for youth safety and privacy.

title: Stay Loud. Stay Well
description: Join iconic artist and advocate Mel B in conversation with Zumba CMO Carolina Moraes to explore how mental and physical health drive collective empowerment. Mel B, who recently completed a trauma-informed care course at Leeds Beckett University, will share her personal journey of resilience and her coping strategies for reclaiming mental health and happiness in her own life. Alongside her, Carolina Moraes will explore how the global fitness movement goes far beyond physical exercise, directly impacting mental wellbeing. Moderated by Writer Louise Gannon, this discussion delves into how we can feel stronger, more connected and more balanced in our everyday lives.

title: Tigers Beyond Borders: Startups Shaping Tomorrow
description: Bangladesh and its diaspora are producing a new generation of founders building ambitious companies with global potential. From Shikho, Bangladesh s leading education technology platform serving more than 3 million learners, to Strideup, the British Bangladeshi founded UK fintech making home ownership more accessible, to Digital Bridge Labs building intelligence and digital infrastructure systems for institutions, this conversation explores what it takes to build globally relevant companies from overlooked ecosystems. Please note, spaces are limited as it s a small venue - entry will close once capacity is full.

title: Flo Cartwright (Film Marketing + Fundraising)
description: Flo (she/her) is a film marketing and events specialist with a decade of experience across screen industries, higher education and non-profits. She is currently Head of Campaigns at Reclaim The Frame, finding audiences for female and marginalised gender led film. She has managed marketing and fundraising at SOAS University of London, One World Media, and the British Independent Film Awards (BIFA), developing audiences for British independent cinema. Speak with her about marketing, comms and PR strategies for film and how to identify, target and find an audience for your project. Having honed her voice through music videos for Olivia Dean, Lola Young and Nia Archives, she has since directed commercials for Nike, Adidas, Coca-Cola and Intel. title: Can Creative Industries Spark a Story of Resilience?
description: Climate shocks are clearly no longer isolated events. Fires, floods, intense weather, droughts and more are changing what is insurable, investable and liveable everywhere. We know we need to adapt. But when the facts are scary, the information is polarising and often politicised, who will shape the space for social acceptance of new priorities? This panel explores what we can learn from creatives, the entertainment sector and cultural sensemakers that we can apply to shaping the story of a future economy where people can still thrive?

title: Feedback - The Music Tech Pitch Competition
description: Join us for the most exciting pitch competition in town! Hosted by Gareth Deakin, Feedback will see music tech founders pitch their tech to leading music industry people in a fun high-energy competition. Feedback s panel of experts each have 100,000 of virtual investment budgets to allocate across the contestants - no actual money changes hands. It all depends on how well they pitch. Who will win the most investment? Join us to find out... Gareth Deakin, MTUK Board Member (Host) Hari Ashurst-Venn, Senior Director of Digital and New Tech, Sony Music (Judge) Andrea Rosen, Portfolio Manager, Best Nights VC (Judge) Amman Ahmed, Founder, MusicforPets (Judge) Anthony Achille, Emerging Tech Innovation Programs Lead, Abbey Road (Judge) Katie Debrah, Tech Founder and Creative Director, Halo and Echo (Competitor) Gayathri Sudhakaran, CEO Founder, Melsonic (Competitor) Arthen Sirola-Hashemi, Co-founder, Hundun Labs (Competitor) Aaron Oxenham, Managing Director, POCA audio (Competitor)

title: SIGNALS: Designing TV for a Responsive Audience
description: Television has always tried to talk with us - from the phone-in shows of the seventies, to CEEFAX surfing over the picture, to the red button waiting patiently in the corner. Each was a signal of something bigger: an audience that wants to participate. In this session, Manon Dave, Head of Future World Design at BBC R D, shares the story behind SIGNALS - a project reimagining interactive television for an audience expecting to participate. Expect a candid look at the prototypes, provocations, and design principles shaping what comes next for broadcast - and a glimpse of the responsive, conversational TV of tomorrow.

title: Scaling US Companies in Europe
description: Crossing the Atlantic isn t just adding more miles to the engine, it s swapping the engine out mid-flight. This SXSW panel brings together leadership from some of the newest American category leaders establishing their first physical presence across the Atlantic, sharing the raw Day 1 hurdles of navigating a fragmented landscape. From localized product-market fit to the cultural nuances of European talent, the discussion moves past the PR gloss to dissect how to translate a domestic success story into a global standard without losing the momentum that fueled their original rise.

title: David Droga in conversation with Matthew Freud
Healing Arts: Art Cure?
description: This session brings together rigorous scientific evidence with artistic intuition to explore both the opportunity and the tension in proposing the arts as essential infrastructure for healthier societies. Expect an exchange of ideas, as artistic and scientific perspectives diverge and converge around one central question: can the arts be understood and valued as a health behaviour?

title: Roundtable: If You Don’t Own the Audience, You Don’t Own the Future
description: For years, documentary makers and production companies relied on broadcasters, streamers and platforms to reach audiences. But that model is changing fast. In a fragmented media landscape, owning a direct relationship with your audience is becoming as important as making the work itself. Led by Zandland founder Ben Zand, this roundtable will explore what it means to build an independent documentary company in the creator economy one that can make premium work for major platforms while also growing its own audience through YouTube, social, newsletters, podcasts and live events. The discussion will ask whether true independence now means owning your distribution, how serious long-form storytelling can survive algorithm-led platforms, and what the next generation of media companies need to do to stay culturally relevant.
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: Roundtable: The Importance of Partnerships and Community Building and Being Creative Within That When Scaling
description: Roundtables are topic-led, small-group discussions hosted by our Leads, offering an intimate, peer-to-peer space where participants of all levels including beginners can engage, learn, and exchange ideas more directly.
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: Attention is the Product
description: Working description - A high-energy conversation about how attention is created, captured, and monetised today, from iconic advertising campaigns to TikTok, YouTube, and personal brands.

title: "What’s Investable in the Future of Creative Industries? CoSTAR Foresight Lab × UK House @ SXSW London 2026"
description: A curated UK House investor discussion at SXSW London 2026, combining cinematic foresight with real world insight from CoSTAR Foresight Lab (futures opportunities), OlsbergSPI (incentives and policy), CoSTAR National Lab (startup pipeline), and Station 12 (investor perspective) focused on what s investable now, what s emerging next, and what unlocks opportunity in the creative industries.

title: Aileen De La Cruz
description: Aileen de la Cruz is a Canadian Filipina-born, London-based artist, songwriter and producer. Her songwriting and vocals have featured on Keeping Up with the Kardashians, The Voice UK, global campaigns for Reebok, Nikon and T-Mobile, and films including Bad Moms and Fantastic Mr. Fox. She has performed alongside Blur, Gorillaz and Annie Lennox at venues including the Royal Albert Hall, Glastonbury and Pacha Ibiza. She is Co-President of ESEA Music and mentors emerging artists through the SESAME Mentorship Programme, recognised with the Music Week Women in Music DE I Award. https://www.instagram.com/aileen_de_la_cruz/ Information for mentees: - Please book a session using the RSVP function via the app. - Please arrive 5 minutes early to check in for your mentor session. Late arrivals (anyone not present at the start of their booked session) may lose their slot to a walk-up attendee. - If a session is fully booked, you re welcome to try as a walk-up in case a booked attendee doesn t attend. - We would recommend preparing a brief introduction about yourself, along with any specific questions or areas you d like guidance on from your mentor.

title: Alpha - A---Z (Anne Duffau)
description: Alpha - A---Z (Anne Duffau) Anne Duffau is a cultural producer, researcher, and founder of A- - -Z , an exploratory/nomadic curatorial platform. She produces soundscapes including otherworldly voices with electronic affective music under Alpha. Her platform: A- - -Z aims to open up to audiences by sharing discursive practices in order to challenge preconceived ideas about race, gender identities and so-called history in terms of power relationships. She has worked with PAF Olomouc since 2017, programming screenings and exhibitions. She co-funded and programmed the collectives: Transmissions.tv and D cal . She is a Tutor at the Royal College of Art s Contemporary Art Practice Programme and worked as a freelance producer/researcher/curator for Somerset House Studios, Wysing Art Centre, Southwark Park Galleries and more. She produces monthly shows on Resonance FM and stegi.radio and live music under A---Z/Alpha through several projects and collaborations (MAENADS, Polisonics, The New Adolescents). Her latest project was hosted at FACT, Corsica Studios and Camden Art Center (Ap-Nov. 2025) and is an ongoing series titled: Always Coming Home ..

title: Ayaz Aftab Hussain (Marketing + Artist Strategy)
description: Ayaz Aftab Hussain is a music marketing strategist and founder of HitmakerOS, an AI Song Release Coach helping independent artists plan, create and launch social media content. He has consulted for SB.TV , GRM Daily, Amazon Music +44, TuneCore UK, Relentless Records and others, generating over 100 million streams across campaigns for Anastacia, Marvin Humes, LaRoux, Yemi Alade and hundreds more. He hosts the podcast All About Helping Musicians and is author of the Amazon-published book Zero To 10,000 Plays In 60 Days . title: Enla Arboleda
description: Enla Arboleda is a Colombian artist based in London. His music blends alternative electronic sounds with acoustic elements and personal songwriting. A guitarist for over 27 years, he moved to the UK to study Audio Production and now produces his own music. His work brings together synthesisers, guitars, clarinet, field recordings, and sound design with reflective lyrics to explore identity, displacement, and belonging. His second EP, Other Ways to Find Yourself, reflects on the process of starting again. He is currently working towards the release of his album, continuing to develop a musical language that connects his Latin American roots with a contemporary sound.

title: How Big Tech Broke the Internet
description: The internet promised openness, creativity, and connection but today a handful of tech giants control much of the digital world. In this talk, Cory Doctorow explores how platform monopolies reshaped the internet, why online services keep getting worse, and what it will take to rebuild a fairer digital ecosystem.

title: Junaid Khan (AI + Music Technology)
description: Junaid Khan is founder of Black Lion, a music-tech company building products at the intersection of music, AI and software. He works with artists, labels and creative businesses to develop scalable infrastructure for growth, monetisation and long-term career strategy. With experience across music distribution, digital strategy and AI-driven product development, he bridges creativity and technology. Speak with him about leveraging AI in music, building sustainable artist careers, structuring music ventures, scaling creative businesses, and turning ideas into functional tech products. description: Kateryna Dmytrenko is CEO of ENKO, Ukraine s largest independent music label as featured by Forbes Ukraine. Over five years, she has built the label s distribution, publishing, commercial and marketing operations, growing royalties by 811% between 2022 and 2025. Since launching ENKO s distribution arm with Universal Music Group in 2022, the label s catalog has surpassed 19 billion streams across 149+ artists including alyona alyona, FIЇNKA, KALUSH and Schugar. She is also showrunner of Urban Music Conf, Ukraine s leading music industry conference. title: Not just Hollywood: where franchises start now.
description: Bluey started in Brisbane. Paddington started in a London picture book. Between them they ve conquered theme parks, broken streaming and box office records, moved grown adults to tears, and built fan communities around the world. So, what does that tell us about where franchises actually come from now? This conversation explores why emotional specificity travels further than generic, what Bluey and Paddington can teach the next generation of franchise builders, and where the properties audiences will fall in love with next are already taking shape.

description: Peter Todd has over 15 years of experience working with musicians and brands. He also runs marketing agency Organised Noise, helping brands execute culturally relevant projects for clients including Old Spice, JBL, ASOS, Beats By Dre, Ford, Foot Asylum and Indeed. Speak with him about brand partnerships, securing synchs, artist development, funding opportunities, content creation and releasing music. title: Rake's Bar Happy Hour by Andaz Hotel
description: Rake s Bar is part of the Andaz London Liverpool Street - a five-star lifestyle hotel where the energy of East London meets the heritage of the City. Set beside Liverpool Street Station and moments from Shoreditch, the hotel brings together bold design, local culture, spacious rooms and suites, distinctive restaurants and bars, and characterful event spaces in one of London s most connected neighborhoods. From creative stays to social dining and standout gatherings, Andaz London invites you to experience the city with curiosity, individuality and a strong sense of place Andaz London Liverpool Street is showing up at SXSW London as a cultural home base for visitors, creators, founders and curious minds exploring the festival. Located at the gateway between the City and East London, the hotel places guests close to Shoreditch and the surrounding creative districts, with restaurants, bars and event spaces designed for connection before, during and after the day s programming. As SXSW London brings together music, screen, tech, culture and ideas from 1 6 June 2026, Andaz London offers a stylish, locally rooted place to stay, meet, dine and keep the conversation going.

title: Sarah Jones
description: Sarah Jones is former General Manager of Songkick, where she helped drive the company to profitability for the first time while leading fan-first product innovation across ticketing, artist marketing and live discovery. With over 20 years across music, media and technology including senior commercial roles at the BBC, she has led global partnerships with ticketing, streaming and digital platforms. Speak with her about music tech innovation, commercial growth in live entertainment, artist-fan engagement, leadership and building sustainable careers in music. As Director of Africa at Sony Music UK, she leads the company s vision for the continent, amplifying African artists globally. She began her career at Warner Music UK, rising from receptionist to leading publicity campaigns for Ed Sheeran, Jay-Z, Missy Elliott, Sean Paul and Rudimental. In 2014, she moved to Johannesburg to lead Talent and Music at Viacom, later joining Columbia Records as Head of Publicity working with Calvin Harris, Wizkid, Davido, Koffee and Rosal a. She serves on the PRS Foundation Power Up steering committee and is a founding member of the Black Music Coalition. title: When Digital and Physical Worlds Converge: Translating Movement into Fashion Environments
description: How can we use emerging technologies to reshape the way we experience fashion? Presented by London s Fashion District, this panel brings together international experts in fashion, media art and technology from Fashion Innovation Agency and Jason Bruges Studio. They will explore how data can be used to create inhabitable media environments that blur the boundaries between physical and virtual worlds. The panel will discuss how capturing movement across 3D and 4D can be used to create unique spatial realms that move beyond static imagery to encourage a more dynamic, participatory connection with fashion, and also introduce their exclusive collaboration Digital Umbra, held at Spitalfields Market.

title: B2B Marketing Meet Up
description: Senior marketers and agency leaders coming together at SXSW London to unpack what s actually shifting. As AI and LLMs reshape discovery, and video and CTV redefine how brands show up, this is a space for honest perspectives, shared challenges, and conversations grounded in real experience.

title: The New Architectural Brain
description: While beautiful architecture has historically been limited to the elite, Maket s generative AI platform might have cracked the code on a future of affordable, high quality homes for all. Join Patrick Murphy, Co-Founder CEO of Maket, to witness how AI has radically transformed the speed, cost and scale of the architectural process to democratise affordable housing design at scale.

title: Gaza's Twins, Come Back to Me
description: Director: Mohammed Sawwaf A mother fights to be reunited with her infant twins as the war in Gaza escalates As the war in Gaza escalates, Rania goes into high-risk labour with triplets. One girl dies at birth. Siblings Jowan and Hamoud survive, but acute health complications lead to a need for in-hospital care. When their hospital is evacuated, the infant twins are moved to southern Gaza, while Rania is trapped by travel bans in place in the north. Mohammed Sawwaf s deeply moving doc chronicles a mother s plight to be reunited with her children amongst the destruction of war, while also exposing the cruelty of withholding basic healthcare for families at times of unspeakable violence. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: The Next Great Chip Revolution
description: Where will the next great chip revolution come from? How will we navigate the complex intersection of global supply chains, geopolitical chip wars, and the desperate need for energy-efficient processing? Join pioneering leaders to consider how we will achieve the next transformational innovation in chip technology.

title: The Preventative Health Shift: Building for Longevity
description: As people live longer, healthcare systems built for reactive treatment are struggling to keep pace. Chronic disease develops quietly over decades, often long before symptoms appear, yet care still begins too late. In this fireside chat, Hjalmar Nilsonne, co‑founder and CEO of Neko Health, explores a different kind of healthcare experience built around prevention, early insight, accessibility and longevity. The conversation will examine how data‑driven diagnostics, AI, and consumer‑centred design can help people live longer lives with better outcomes and higher quality of life.

title: Building Artistic Universes Without Borders
description: Anthony Russo, Joe Russo and Donald Mustard in conversation Storytelling no longer sits on a single screen or inside one format. The projects that endure are built to move between film, television, gaming, and interactive, meeting audiences wherever they are and continuing to evolve once they get there. The real challenge is not just expanding across platforms but building something that sustains attention and meaning at a global scale. Anthony and Joe Russo, directors of Avengers: Infinity War and Avengers: Endgame, join Donald Mustard, former Chief Creative Officer of Epic Games and the creative force behind Fortnite, which has reached hundreds of millions of players and reshaped how interactive worlds operate at scale. Now a partner at AGBO founded by the Russos Mustard brings a perspective shaped by both cinematic storytelling and live, evolving digital worlds. Film and gaming treat participation, canon, and continuity very differently, and those differences do not resolve themselves when you try to bridge the two. That tension is what gives this conversation weight. Their collaboration traces back to 2018, when the Russos and Mustard worked together to bring Thanos into Fortnite during the production of Infinity War, an early signal of how storytelling could move between film and interactive in real time. That intersection is now reflected in AGBO, the artist-first studio they ve built together, grounded in the belief that creators should take risks and develop stories not bound to a single medium. Across film, television, and interactive, AGBO s projects reflect that philosophy in practice from narrative features to series to experiences that don t fit neatly into either category. The discussion will focus on how these worlds are actually built across film and gaming, what it takes to keep audiences invested over time, and how storytelling continues to shift as audiences move from watching to participating.

title: Intelligence Rising
description: Director: Elena Andreicheva Cast: Marc Warner, General Patrick Sanders, Patricia Geli, Skype Jaan Tallinn, Lucy Lim, General Stanley McChrystal, Pippa Malmgren, Yuval Noah Harari Leading global authorities join AI expert Marc Warner for a war-game exploring how artificial intelligence might reshape power. Much has been said about artificial intelligence s effect on fields such as the creative arts, but we are only now beginning to experience the geopolitical consequences. Intelligence Rising chronicles a bold experiment: some of the world s most prominent thinkers in politics, military strategy, philosophy, and technology gather to play an intricate wargame designed by Marc Warner, a founder and leader who s been at the frontier of AI progress for 15 years. What ensues is a stark warning, but also a much-needed reminder of the power of human autonomy and empathy. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Adam Kaasa
description: Adam Jovanović Kaasa / Bliss Carmxn is a Canadian-born London-based artist, composer, performer, curator, and interdisciplinary scholar working across song, sound, performance, archives, outer space, and public culture. Their practice connects original music, artistic research, and large-scale curatorial projects, bringing together queer poetics, speculative thinking, and a deep commitment to voice as a medium of memory, relation, and transformation. Working musically as Bliss Carmxn, Adam makes genre-blurring alt-pop shaped by electronic shimmer, piano-led composition, folk intimacy, and an experimental performance sensibility. Their debut EP, Hold My Hand And I ll Hold Yours (2024), established Bliss Carmxn as a distinctive new voice, following earlier singles supported by BBC London and Berlin s Wicked Hag label. Their second EP, Open Love , is released in June 2026 and features the singles Caffeine Orange and Never Wanted. Adam s musical practice extends across theatre and dance. Their work was performed in Stefan Jovanović Kaasa s Constellations at Sadler s Wells in 2019, and they later collaborated with Jovanović Kaasa on the dance-theatre work When the Clarion Came to Call , creating an original sound score and a nine-song original album for the production, which premiered at the Cockpit Theatre in London in October 2025. At the centre of Adam s current work is Andromeda , an expansive artistic-research project unfolding as both a monograph and an eight-part song cycle. Bringing together astronomy, myth, archival research, speculative fiction, and collective singing, Andromeda explores how voice can create forms of belonging across time, distance, and loss. Performances connected to this work have taken place at Matt s Gallery in London, Swedenborg Hall, and the Performing Arts Forum in France. Alongside their artistic practice, Adam is co-lead of the MFA Arts Humanities at the Royal College of Art, co-founder of the RCA s outer space research network _SPACE, a founding member of SOUND@RCA, and co-founder of Theatrum Mundi, the international research centre on arts and urban culture. Their work has been developed and presented through collaborations and institutions including the Venice Architecture Biennale, Haus der Kulturen der Welt, Whitechapel Gallery, Tate Britain, the Southbank Centre, Harvard GSD, MIT, Columbia, NYU, and the Museu do Amanh . In 2021, they curated RELAY at the Venice Architecture Biennale, a 24-hour durational performance spanning global time zones with more than 60 participants. In 2026, Adam is curating The Art of Song at the Royal College of Art and developing Future of Voice with IKLECTIK, the London Festival of Architecture, and the Biennale Dello Stretto, a major new project exploring voice, listening, technology, and democracy in an age of AI.

title: NHS Clinical Entrepreneurship Programme - Rising Stars in Health Tech
description: Three founders. Three ways to reinvent the NHS from the inside. Alex Trewby s Apian spun out of the NHS Clinical Entrepreneur Programme and is now running the world s most prominent drone delivery service in central London for the NHS, cutting journeys from half an hour to minutes. Natasha Davendralingam founded Anubix to modernise death investigation, replacing invasive autopsies with post-mortem CT imaging to give families faster, more dignified answers. And Anthony Hannides built Heal Music, a music wellness app that uses biometric data and AI to show users how their bodies respond to music in real time, now backed by the NHS Clinical Entrepreneur Programme. Chaired by Tony Young, Innovation Lead for the NHS, this session gets honest about what it actually takes to build and scale health tech within one of the world s most complex healthcare systems and how accelerator and incubator programmes can be the difference between a great idea and a product that reaches patients.

title: Olayá
description: Olay is a female-led flute and percussion band inspired by northeastern Brazilian bandas de pifanos (bamboo flutes), with the elemental force of flutes, rich vocal harmonies, driving percussion and bass. Olay s music is heavily rhythmic and contagiously fun, a fresh take on a traditional Brazilian sound never before heard in the UK. United by their passion for Brazilian music and exploring sounds, Olay formed in 2022. After their sold-out debut gig in May 2023, they were quickly approached by bookers across the country and have since headlined at festivals including Jaminaround, Basket of Light, Ealing Jazz Festival Leeds Festival as well as selling out multiple London gigs. The band have just recorded their debut EP featuring original tunes which weave seamlessly between musical influences such as forr , maracat , soul, jazz and rock. Olay s musicians have long been at the forefront of London s Brazilian music scene. They have played in festivals and venues internationally with a plethora of artists including Gal Costa, Gilberto Gil, Alvorada and Antonio Forcione and have appeared on BBC Radio 3, Jazz FM, Worldwide FM and the West End stage. Olay are: Rosanna Ter-Berg (flutes, vocals) Rachel Hayter (flutes, vocals) In s Loubet (vocals, percussion) Julio de Castro (electric bass, vocals) Jansen Santana (percussion, vocals)

title: Roundtable: Are We Afraid of the Wrong Things?
description: Roundtables are topic-led, small-group discussions hosted by our Leads, offering an intimate, peer-to-peer space where participants of all levels including beginners can engage, learn, and exchange ideas more directly. We ve got risk backwards. Most people think jumping out of a plane is dangerous. Those same people will check their phone doing 80mph on the motorway and not think twice. We catastrophise the dramatic and normalise the dangerous. We avoid the leap and sleepwalk into the real threat: never moving at all. I ve summited Everest. I was the first woman to skydive it. But the experience that fundamentally rewired how I think about risk wasn t on a mountain. It was patrolling on the front line in Africa alongside female anti-poaching rangers. These women don t choose their risk. That distinction - privileged risk (adventure) versus survival risk (frontline) - is where the real conversation starts. This roundtable isn t a talk. It s a provocation for founders and leaders navigating uncertainty at scale. At a moment when AI, systemic disruption, and geopolitical instability are forcing everyone to make high-stakes calls with incomplete information, the question isn t how to avoid risk. It s how to get fluent in it. We ll dig into: Why we fear the wrong things (and what that s costing us) How to recognise opportunity disguised as threat The difference between luck and preparation (and why only one of them scales) What it actually means to outplay fear, not suppress it The question I want people to leave the session with: How much more am I capable of than I think? What opportunities am I missing out on because they are disguised as risk? (Cos that s where the gold lies).
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: Roundtable: Innovation and creativity at the space frontier
description: Let s discuss the technological frontier the bleeding edge of innovation in space and related fields. title: Roundtable: SexTech, AI & Ethics: What is regulated, what is right and where is the line?
description: In 2026, SexTech sits at the crossroads of inti m acy, agency, and artificial intelligence and the rules governing this evolving landscape are still catching up. We will explore how new develop m ents create tension between policy, autono m y and freedo m of expression, innovation, ethics and the potential for both positive and negative societal i m pacts. This roundtable seeks to bring together founders, policy experts, investors, and operators to explore one of the m ost co m plex frontiers in tech and society today. Thought Starter Questions: What recent and upco m ing innovations should require regulation? How should policy and product decisions consider the role of agency, innovation and ethics? What ethical fra m eworks should co m panies use to guide product road m aps? How should co m panies think about m axi m ising benefit and m ini m ising har m for both custo m ers and society, and how should each be defined?

title: Whistle
description: Director: Christopher Nelius N ot everyone in the world can whistle. Not many people in the world can whistle, even fewer can whistle Mozart s Queen of the Night aria. The top whistlers in the world travel from all over the world to compete in the prestigious Masters of Musical Whistling competition in L.A. At the heart of this riot of a documentary is organiser extraordinaire and competition founder Carole Anne Kaufman. A passionate whistler, she runs a tight but gentle ship and offers a heartfelt behind the scenes look into neverending drama of event production. Part musical, part comedy, and with the stakes of a sports movie, Whistle sets the scene for a showdown: will nerves take over? Or is it as simple as put your lips together and blow ? (ISRA AL KASSI)

title: Mic to Millions: In Conversation with Harry Stebbings
description: Harry Stebbings is a venture capitalist, entrepreneur, and the founder of the influential 20VC media and venture fund. He is best known for creating the popular 20 Minute VC podcast, which features interviews with top investors and founders, and for his investment work with the 20VC fund, which focuses on early-stage technology companies. He has also co-founded Stride.vc and has a background in social media management and angel investing. Join Harry Stebbings in conversation with Tara Keeney ( Look Who I Found) to hear Harry s journey motivations.

title: Open Models, Open Future: Who Should Control the World's Most Powerful Technology?
description: The most transformative technology of our generation is being built behind closed doors by a handful of companies. Meanwhile, China is flooding the world with open models that are rapidly becoming the global default. Reflection AI s Joe Spisak and WordPress s Mary Hubbard sit down to discuss how platforms can develop openly, accountably, and without concentrated control. This conversation will explore why the choice between closed Western labs and Chinese open models is a false one, and what it will take to build superintelligence that belongs to everyone.

title: The Inclusive Health Revolution?
description: For marginalised communities, the traditional medical system has often been a source of friction rather than healing. However, new platforms are providing life-saving resources through personalised, community-driven care. Join Liana Guzm n (Folx Health), Mohan Sundararaj (Grindr for Equality) and Anna Vybornova (Polari Labs) for an exploration of how new platforms are reducing access barriers and propelling the next generation of inclusive health.

title: The Career Ladder LIVE
description: Join acclaimed creator Max Klymenko for a live edition of the Career Ladder at SXSW London!

title: We are all builders in the resilience economy
description: As AI reshapes how we work and climate reshapes where we live and what we consume, the defining economic challenge of our time depends on how we adapt to powerful forces of nature and technology. The uncomfortable question is whether we can build true resilience by doubling down on technology and climate solutions, or whether it s better to start by building the social cohesion that makes collective action possible? Add your voice to this fishbowl to explore the critical steps needed to thrive into the future. Curated by Kite Insights

title: Yxng Dave
description: Yxng Dave is a rising UK-Colombian artist paving his path with a mix of Spanish English in his records. At 23 years old, he has amassed over 3 million streams on Canada Fur . Alongside a co-sign from J Balvin on the Lechero Apple Music interview. Yxng Dave has also linked up with one of the UK s goats, Chip on Blacked Out . Yxng Dave has toured with Dappy KSI, performing at the iconic 12,500-capacity Wembley Arena twice, performing the ring walk for KSI s boxing match in front of 20,000 people at the O2 Arena. And, performing at Manchester AO Arena in front of 22,000 people. To top it off, he recently performed with Colombian legend Nanpa B sico in London. He has been featured on the popular video game NBA 2K22 with the song Coupe , and has recently dropped his debut EP FAMOSO where he mixes his Latino culture with the UK style. Yxng Dave is one to watch for the upcoming years.

title: AI Is Already Shaping Childhood. Who Is Shaping AI? Balancing Innovation, Evidence, and Safety in Youth Mental Health
description: A conversation on how governments and societies can prepare young people for the psychological, social, and civic challenges of an AI-driven world. As AI increasingly shapes education, relationships, and decision-making, youth are experiencing its impacts earlier and more intensely than any generation before. This session explores the role of public leadership in anticipating harm before it becomes systemic establishing guardrails, fostering digital resilience, and ensuring that innovation advances hand-in-hand with youth mental health and wellbeing.

title: BrokenPen
description: BrokenPen is a UK-based rapper, spoken word artist and performer originally from Angola and raised in South East London, known for blending raw lyricism, storytelling, humour, spirituality and high-energy live performance. Major Career Achievements Semi-Finalist on The Voice UK (2021) MOBO Awards Unsung Class of 2025 Headlined Glastonbury Festival (2025) Performed internationally across France, Germany and Sweden, building a growing European fanbase. Featured in an official Premier League sync placement Viral breakout single The Ends gained millions of streams and on social media. Over 300 live shows performed in the last four years (including headline shows and festivals). Festival appearances and international showcases including Brazil s Feira Preta. BrokenPen is recognised for: Bridging spoken word poetry and UK rap Deep storytelling rooted in real life, faith, community and personal growth High-energy, crowd-engaging live performances Mixing humour, vulnerability and social commentary A self-described wordsmith focused on authentic expression and connection

title: PYTKO
description: London-based, Polish-born PYTKO is a producer, singer, and songwriter working at the crossroads of experimental soundscapes and intimate songwriting. Fascinated by treating music as material , she layers voice, field recordings, and unconventional textures into intricate, ethereal compositions. From a makeshift studio, often discovered in a cupboard somewhere in South London , PYTKO crafts immersive sonic worlds that blur the line between song, sculpture, and storytelling.

title: Responsible AI Community Mixer with All Tech Is Human
description: Join All Tech Is Human for a Responsible AI Community Mixer at the historic Old Spitalfields Market - come mingle over drinks and music with others who deeply care about tackling thorny tech society issues. This mixer is part of All Tech Is Human s (ATIH) mission to build the social infrastructure necessary for the Responsible Tech movement to thrive globally. By intentionally connecting diverse stakeholders from policy experts and engineers to artists and community organizers, ATIH transforms abstract principles into a tangible, collaborative ecosystem. Through essential physical and digital touchpoints, All Tech Is Human bridges the gap between innovation and responsibility, empowering a global network to co-create a more tech-integrated world that prioritizes human values.

title: yunji
description: DJ/producer yunji, based in Seoul, South Korea, creates electronic music that fluidly moves across multiple genres, focusing on smooth emotional textures and refined chords. Her debut single, Underwater Fairytale, presented her artistry not only through music but also through music video planning and performance. She expanded her musical spectrum through production work with a range of K-POP artists including EXO-SC, Giriboy, and Pentagon. Building on her strengths as both a producer and DJ, as well as her deep affinity for K-POP, she joined the K-POP ScreaM North America Tour in 2024, connecting directly with audiences across the U.S. and Canada through sets that blended K-POP and dance music. In 2025, she continued her DJ performance by going on a European tour, performing at major stages such as Glastonbury Festival and SXSW London, where she attracted more European audiences. Alongside her expanding DJ career, she has continued to explore her identity as a producer. On the collaborative album Re-Enter The K-BASS by ScreaM Records and Seoul Community Radio, she translated the musical inspiration gained during her European tour into the track ECHO. yunji also regularly releases mix sets on YouTube featuring UKG, dub, jungle, and other European-rooted dance styles. Through these, she has built a global listener base, reaching over 30,000 subscribers and more than 780,000 total views. Moving forward, she aims to continue crafting works that seamlessly weave dance music, K-POP, and visual elements, focusing on developing her artistic identity.

title: Healthcare Rewired
description: Join Sneh Khemka CBE (The National Insurance Company) in conversation with Stuart Peak (Helios X) and Robert Lorenz (Cleveland Clinic London) to ponder how digital health, AI, and preventative care strategies are reshaping the future of medicine. From the role of insurers in driving innovation, to the rapid rise of digital-first healthcare platforms, to the operational realities inside hospitals, this conversation will offer a comprehensive view of where healthcare is heading next and the strategies it will take to get there.

title: Closing Remarks
description: Outro from TWIF

title: Nonô
description: Non is a Brazilian born, London-Miami based artist that mixes Latin and Brazilian influences like baile funk, samba and hip-hop with electronic and pop music .
