Selected as one of NME s 100 and with over 500 million streams on Spotify and 120k followers on Instagram , she has reached fans and made collaborations with artists like Sofi Tukker, Charli XCX, Armin Van Buuren, Alok, Zerb and others around the globe. Non s song All Day with Irish DJ, Welshy ended up reaching number 6th in the Irish Shazam chart and earned both of them a Platinum Plaque in Ireland. Her latest EP +55 , released in December 2024 , received raving reviews including one from Dork magazine that rates the EP with a 4 star grade and says Non s +55 EP lands with the grace of someone who s mastered the art of cultural collision. . +55 also landed her great sync placements. Her track AL became the soundtrack of the Samsung x Verizon AD in the USA, she also got on the soundtrack of the game F1 24 by EA plus two performance slots during the Formula 1 British Grand Prix in Silverstone . The second song on the EP, VEM is on the soundtrack of the highly anticipated football game by EA: FC 25 (previously known as Fifa) . In 2024, she was selected to perform at SXSW Austin plus became the first ever Brazilian invited to attend the official SXSW songwriting camp. Non also brought her sunny flare to the stages of Future Echoes Festival in Sweden and to close off the year on a high note, she landed a headline slot on the Climate Live stage at Reading Festival. In 2025, she released her solo single OLD SKOOL which landed her a space in the 2025 F1 EA soundtrack and in June she released the highly anticipated single PICK UP THE PHONE with Sofi Tukker , bringing her Brazilian flare to the collab which peaked at #6 on the Beatport Tech House charts and #16 overall. By July she was performing on the Main Stage of the Formula 1 British Grand Prix supporting to dance music legend, Fatboy Slim . In 2026, Non performed for a second time at SXSW Austin on the prestigious Tropiclub night alongside Brazilian stars like Latin Grammy award winning singer Jota.P and in June she will be performing at SXSW London. Non s vibrant music and style resonates with listeners around the world. As NME puts it, she s a rising talent ready to become your new favourite artist . So, get your caipirinhas ready because the Brazilian heat is here to stay!

title: Youth Mental Health After Conflict
description: Young people living amid conflict, displacement, and prolonged instability face profound psychological challenges, often in settings with limited mental health infrastructure. This session explores how societies can move beyond short-term response toward sustainable, youth-centered systems grounded in prevention, early identification, community engagement, and cultural context positioning youth mental health as a cornerstone of long-term recovery and resilience.

title: Screen Happy Hour The Innovate UK Immersive Tech Network
description: Join SXSW London and our Screen partners at daily Happy Hour drinks. Meet filmmakers and industry professionals from all over the world. This is your unmissable stop before your evening screening. The Innovate UK Immersive Tech Network is the UK government s national cross-sector network for immersive technology innovators. The network s mission is to grow and scale UK-based immersive tech companies by fostering collaborations across industry, academia, and government, supporting access to public and private funding for businesses, and driving cross-sector innovation.

title: Adriana Blu
description: Adriana Blu is the creator of Urban Mystical Pop a raw, genre-bending fusion where the soul of Flamenco and Bolero meets the underground pulse of Electronica and Reggaeton. More than a performance, Adriana s live show is a high-vibration experience. She treats the stage as a space for collective elevation, using deep beats and ethereal vocals to bridge the gap between street energy and spiritual awakening. Based in London but rooted in the passion of her Spanish heritage, Adriana is currently sparking a rapidly growing movement on TikTok , where her blend of conscious frequency and urban sound has begun to resonate with a global audience hungry for authenticity. Her latest release FUEGO is a call to the fire of neurodiversity and female mysticism a celebration of intensity and radical authenticity for those who feel too much in a world that often asks for less. While her voice has already caught the attention of platforms like Universal and Netflix, she remains a fiercely independent artist focused on building a community that values inner power as much as a global beat. I want my music to be a portal, says Adriana. A place where the rhythm leads you back to your true self.

title: Funky Freaky Freaks
description: Director: Han Chang-lok Cast: Baek Ji-hye, Shin Jung-hang, Joo Min-hyeong A trio of teens gets into all sorts of mischief in this cool, fast-paced slice of South Korean youth Han Chang-lok s stylish feature debut sees a tight friendship between three teens begin to unravel following the arrival of a handsome new student at their high school. Edited with the frenzied touch of early 2000s television and featuring a soundtrack that matches that early MTV aesthetics, Funky Freaky Freaks paints a vivid portrayal of South Korean youth while drawing timely reflections on the anxieties of being young and online, the gnarly knots of Gen Z communication, and the ever-dangerous beauty standards of a generation raised on the perfectly polished imagery of pop idols. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Imlay
description: DJ. Producer, Songwriter, ARTIST. In a country known for its booming K-pop industry, IMLAY is at the forefront of the rapidly developing dance music scene with his distinctive fusion of eastern scales, future bass, and modern pop elements. After being nominated for Best Dance Electronic Song at the 14th Korean Music Awards with the debut EP Zanzan , IMLAY emerged as a rising star representing the alternative wave of the Korean electronic scene. He has since proven their prowess as both a producer and DJ, performing at premier global festivals including Heineken 5TARDIUM, World DJ Festival, Ultra Japan, and SM Spectrum Music Festival. His tracks were featured in prominent international media outlets like NEST HQ, Dancing Astronaut, and Earmilk, as he expanded his reach onto the global stage. To this day, he continues his active career, participating in events such as Water Bomb Festival, SM Town Live, and the K-POP ScreaM North America Tour. IMLAY has been broadening his production spectrum through collaborations with top-tier K-POP artists, including NCT, EXO, SHINee, and aespa. He continues to work on projects that fluidly bridge the gap between K-POP and electronic music. Notably, the music video for Too Good , which vividly showcases his unique musical color, has surpassed 2 million views, achieving both commercial success and artistic recognition. In 2025, IMLAYlaunchedtheproject Citron Studio under his alternate persona, JAEBIN. Following its successful debut, his journey of CitronStudio is shaping with organic sounds of house music. Following a successful debut launch party with Maruwa, IMLAY / Jaebin is carving out his own unique journey.

title: Phoenix (Festival & Platinum Pass Only)
description: Please note, this is only open to Festival Platinum pass holders, not music wristbands. Phoenix is an emerging force in the UK music scene, building a strong online following with her eclectic sets that fuse Afrobeats, House, and RnB and more. Since moving to London, she has made her mark with highenergy performances!

title: Marc By Sofia
description: Director: Sofia Coppola Cast: Marc Jacobs Acclaimed director Sofia Coppola s first documentary captures her long-time friend and collaborator, designer Marc Jacobs In her first foray into documentary-making, Oscar-winning auteur Sofia Coppola turns to her long-time friend and iconic fashion designer Marc Jacobs. Their collaborations have spanned decades, with Coppola starring in his campaigns, collaborating with him during his time at Louis Vuitton, and directing his perfume ads after he launched his own label. Taking a fly-on-the-wall approach, Coppola gives us an intimate glimpse into the everyday process and creative drive of a singular voice in the fashion world. The doc takes us back to the heyday of the nineties, when his star was on the ascendant as fashion s most trend-averse designer, working his way from Perry Ellis to Louis Vuitton, up until the time he eventually launched his own label. Watching Marc by Sofia is an invitation to join the most glamorous of hangs. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: The Playoffs (Episodes 1 and 2)
description: Director: Bruno Safadi Cast: Cau Reymond, Let cia Colin, Ricardo Teodoro, Mariana Sena, Juan Paiva A former football star turned agent runs from the militia, his family, and himself on the way to regaining glory. A bankrupt former football player, Maur cio s (Cau Reymond) best days are now firmly behind him. In an attempt to dip his toes back into the game, he becomes an agent to promising (and gullible, of course) young players. But much of the job is less about the sport and the roar of filled stadiums and more about babysitting grown men while trying to bankroll an obscenely lavish lifestyle. This riotous series, created by and starring one of Brazil s biggest screen names, explores the little-seen underbelly of Brazilian football, pushing ethics to their very limits as Mauricio dodges an increasingly violent militia, tries to corral hormone-fuelled footballers, and escape the overbearing shadow of his father. Screening a few weeks before the FIFA World Cup, this is the perfect primer. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Jax Jones - Tuesday (Festival & Platinum Pass Only)
description: Please note, this is only open to festival platinum pass holders, not music wristbands. He s an all-round, multi-faceted musician - Hypebeast The UK dance superproducer on a mission to break down barriers - The Rolling Stone Jax Jones (real name Timucin Timz Lam) is a globally renowned DJ, songwriter and producer who has spent over a decade at the forefront of the British music scene. As one of the UK s most in-demand musical exports, he has racked up more than 65 platinum singles worldwide, over 9 billion global streams and 60 million record sales. The Grammy-, BRIT- and Ivor Novello-nominated artist has cemented his reputation as one of his generation s most talented hitmakers and one of dance music s most successful producers with more than 180 songwriting credits to his name. Jax was born and raised in South London. From an early age it was clear music would be his path. At just eight years old he began studying classical guitar through a council-funded programme. An experience that still shapes how he approaches pop and dance music today. That formal training was balanced by the sounds filling his home: Afrobeat, Dancehall, Soul, Motown, Hip-Hop and R B. His Nigerian stepfather s eclectic musical taste had a lasting influence, shaping Jax sense of groove, rhythm and melody. As a teenager he began spending time in a Brixton studio, moving from guitar accompaniment to recording mixtapes and writing his own songs. By 16 he was performing at open mic nights and producing beats. Despite being a straight-A student, music ultimately took priority and he left university to pursue it full-time. Jax has spoken openly about the challenges he faced growing up. Periods of homelessness, difficult relationships, mental health struggles and painful family circumstances meant music became more than just a creative outlet. It was a lifeline. By the age of 21 he was living and working alone in a garage studio. Music was all I had , he says. Inspired by legendary producers such as Pharrell Williams, he set out to follow a similar path, seeing success in music as a way to build a better future. Early collaborations across the UK rap scene such as N-Dubz and Tinchy Stryder helped him developing his reputation as both a songwriter and producer. Songwriting soon proved to be his breakthrough. After early work including co-writing a track with Bruno Mars, Jax deepened his involvement in dance music culture and began collaborating with Duke Dumont. Immersing himself in house music s roots and community, he found a space that resonated deeply with him. Their collaboration on the chart-topping single I Got U marked a turning point in his career, reached Number #1 on the UK Single Charts and bringing him global recognition. From there momentum built quickly. Collaborations with artists including MNEK, Raye, Demi Lovato, Charli XCX and Mabel and helped establish Jax as a trusted creative partner across pop and dance music. His breakthrough solo hit You Don t Know Me (feat. Raye) became one of his defining records, reached the UK Top 3 and charted across Europe. His debut album Snacks later cemented his place in the global dance landscape. Tracks such as the platinum-certified collaboration Where Did You Go with MNEK combine Eurodance nostalgia with contemporary pop sensibilities while Whistle (feat. Calum Scott) was written along the bus route Jax took as a child in South London. Beyond his own music, Jax is also dedicated to building opportunities for emerging artists. His record label WUGD champions the next generation of UK talent while his work as a producer and collaborator continues to span genres and continents. Alongside a packed international touring schedule, he remains a prolific studio presence - whether crafting original releases or producing club-ready remixes. At the heart of Jax Jones story is a deep belief in music s ability to connect people. Drawing on influences that span cultures, genres and lived experiences. From London to the global dancefloor. He continues to push pop and dance music forward while staying rooted in the sounds that first inspired him.

title: Lokkhi Terra
description: From the Mela s of Bangladesh to the streets of Havana, via some districts in Lagos and on to a bustling club in London. This is what is giving London it s new accent. Described by Songlines as probably the world s best Afrobeat-Cuban-Bangladeshi group London based Lokkhi Terra released their debut album No Visa Required in 2010 and have been tearing up the music rule book ever since, playing their unique blend of traditions and wowing audiences across the genres. Their second album Che Guava s Rickshaw Diaries (2012) received much critical acclaim - the multicultural settings effortlessly encapsulate 21st-century London. Traditional songs from Bangladesh collide with Latin rhythms, township jazz, funk and ambient shades, all seasoned with a lilting pop sensibility...Gold medals all round. Sunday Times Magazine Their latest release CUBANGLA is their sixth album, with journalist John Armstrong declaring it to be their crowning glory to date.........as well as being one of the outstanding albums of the year so far for me . It follows on from a series of Lokkhi Terra meets collaboration albums, including CubAfrobeat with Afrobeat Ambassador Dele Sosimi (2018) described as a total stonking blinder (Chris May - All About Jazz), as well with Bangladeshi roots legends Shikor Bangladesh All Stars (releasing 2 Eps - Bangla Rasta in 2016, and Introducing Baby Akhtar April 2017). Their live shows continue to thrill. The pairing of the Shikor Bangladesh All Stars with London-based salsa group LoKkhi TeRa should not have worked, but really did. Kishon Khan s keyboard and artistic direction allowed the Bangladeshis to meld with his Cuban rhythm section to really fine effect, blending Latin, Afrobeat, jazz, dub and all manner of other elements to create something truly exceptional. Once again, you are unlikely to hear anything like this outside of WOMAD and I m not the only one that was really glad the organisers featured it. - United Reggae - Womad 2015 Awesome Cross-cultural collaboration - London Jazz - Womad 2015 Widely acknowledged as an international force to be reckoned with...... beautiful, powerful, spectacular stuff - Songlines Magazine The band is made up of musicians with serious pedigree - led by pianist Kishon Khan (Hugh Masekela, Gilles Peterson s Havana Cultura, Tony Allen). Featuring Bellowhead s Justin Thurgur on Trombone, and Graeme Flowers (Kyle Eastwood) on trumpet. Cuban maestro percussionist Oreste Noda (Ssmbroso All Stars) on congas, and fellow Cuban, bassist Frank Portuondo (New Regency Orchestra) alongside UK s Phil Dawson (Tony Allen) on guitar, and Turkish drummer Tansay Omar (Bjork). To add to this impressive list, Bengali singer Sohini Alam (Akram Khan ) sings alongside Cuban vocalist/percussionist Javier Camilo (Sambroso All Stars). Background This project was conceived in Cuba where, whilst studying there, Kishon Khan noticed a synergy between the rumba on the streets of Havana and baul music in the mela s of Bangladesh. This strange connection would make more sense back in London, where different communities hang out together learning and sharing their different musical heritages. The result is Lokkhi Terra. The band line up boasts a team full of band-leaders all session players renowned for their authentic playing of different world traditions, but who tend to choose a more contemporary route when focusing on their own projects. Between them they have played with the likes of Hugh Masakela, Tony Allen, Changuito, Ibrahim Ferrer, Jazz Jamaica, Bjork, Kylie Minogue, Galiano, James Taylor Quartet, Bellowhead, Giles Peterson s Havana Cultura, Kyle Eastwood, Cornell Campbell, Johhny Clarke, Orchestra Imperial, Dele Sosimi to name a very few. Their music has been used in 2 international films The Last Thakur (Channel 4) and Everywhere and Nowhere (Arena), and radio play has been extensive (from Radio 1 Gilles Peterson to World Multiples in New York). Stunning Headliners... A majestic multi-cultural blend of sounds... effortlessly builds bridges between rolling Indian raga rhythms, Afro-Cuban grooves, Acid Jazz/funk and free flowing improvisation - Timeout London Kishon Khan leant back from his keyboards with the glee of a man driving a super-car, and played as if distilling the entire 1970s work of Herbie Hancock into a high-octane drive in the country, as congas bounced and brass slid around him... FT.com

title: Maddie's Secret
description: Director: John Early Cast: John Early, Kate Berlant, Kristen Johnston, Vanessa Bayer, and Chris Bauer. Comedian John Early s first film is a tragicomedy about a foodfluencer struggling with bulimia. Known for his signature offbeat delivery, comedian and actor John Early s directorial debut delivers as much snark as it does heart. He stars as the food influencer Maddie, struggling to balance her aspirations for a career surrounded by food with a years-long eating disorder she s kept secret from all those closes to her. Perhaps hardest of all, Maddie s Secret handles the horror of bulimia with startling clarity, bringing to light the shame, control and self-destruction that underpin binge-eating and purging. It s a powerful piece of work, risky but deeply rewarding. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Arkins
description: Arkins is a Seoul-based producer and DJ who has built a strong reputation for his powerful, high-energy sound, and striking versatility. With his signature fast-paced and distinctive style, he delivers both original tracks and remixes that dominate the Beatport charts, earning support from global headliners such as Hardwell, Blasterjaxx, Steve Aoki, Dimitri Vegas Like Mike, KSHMR, R3HAB, and Afrojack. Through collaborations with leading European and South American labels including Dharma Worldwide, CLUBWRK, Barong Family, Revealed Recordings, SKINK, and HEXAGON, Arkins continues to expand hisglobal reach. His tracks have amassed over 1.4 billion views across social media, proving his worldwide influence. Beyond his releases, he presents genre-crossing sensibility and memorable production through numerous mixsets on SoundCloud, YouTube, and radio platforms, maintaining a strong presence both online and offline. As the leader of the crew nowhere nowhere, Arkins pioneered the K-Bounce style genre that began Seoul and rapidly spread across Asia, evolving into a cultural movement beyond the club scene. As a true pioneer of K-Bounce, he continues to push its boundaries, aiming to establish it as a global phenomenon while exploring new artistic directions that transcend the genre.

title: Blakchyl
description: Rapper Blakchyl (formerly known as T-Fly) is a talented, community-minded wordsmith, having come up in the local hip hop community collaborating with music collectives, nonprofits and filmmakers. The queer artist has three European tours under her belt plus opening slots for Talib Kweli, Snow Tha Product, Jean Grae, Bizzy Bone and Scarface and her new album Better Than I Imagined (produced by The Mask), was accompanied by a short film and dropped in July. Always indefatigable, she recently landed a deal with Brighton, UK-based Tru Thought Records as well as an Austin Chronicle cover, was nominated for Best Hip Hop Artist, released an EP (Call Me Sometimes) and played a Waterloo Records in-store. It all comes down to her powerful and effervescent songs (CultureMap) and her love for the craft and it s community.

title: Flow by Adrian Utley & Hans-Martin Buff
description: Flow is a groundbreaking 360 spatial music composition. It is a 30-minute journey in sound: an orchestra of guitars, voices, four double basses, electronic oscillators, and field recordings from Iceland. Created by Adrian Utley, the guitarist and sonic architect of Portishead, and produced by Grammy Award-winning engineer Hans-Martin Buff, the piece pushes the boundaries of immersive composition. Flow was recorded at the world-renowned Real World Studios in Box, Wiltshire. I think creating this project Flow has changed how I feel about making music. Adrian Utley Flow was funded by UKRI s Strength in Places project MyWorld in the West of England with support from Digital Catapult and research led by Dr Ruth Farrar from Bath Spa University in collaboration with Dr Felix Carter from the University of Bristol and Theo Kozlowski from the University of Bath. Flow Social links : Real World Studios: https://www.instagram.com/realworldstudios/ Hans-Martin Buff: https://www.instagram.com/buffwerk/ Adrian Utley: https://www.instagram.com/adrian.utley/

title: Harry Edohoukwa
description: Harry Edohoukwa [eh-doh-kwa] occupies a rare cultural space. Dallas-born and LA-based, his music doesn t offer salvation, softness, or clean conclusions. Instead, it documents the aftermath, the emotional fallout of ego, addiction, faith, love, and the pressure to be him. Across songs like ZOMBiES, Drugs Don t Work, Found Me, Never Enough, and Whale Watching, Harry writes from the perspective of a man both worshipped and haunted, celebrated and fractured, spiritual and self-destructive. His work positions him as a Black Alt-Pop anti-hero, a global voice for the modern haunted Black male psyche. He doesn t mythologize his mistakes, but he doesn t sanitize them either. The writing is direct, confessional, and emotionally exposed, blending spiritual imagery with lived experience to show how masculinity, validation, addiction, and inherited expectations collide. Harry s work has carried him from SXSW s Must-See Artist lists to stages like Lollapalooza and School Night LA, with Found Me earning indie playlist support and Good Wine reaching #15 on iTunes R B/Soul and appearing on Bel-Air. Rather than presenting himself as a savior, poet, or philosopher, Harry allows the mess to be the point. His songs feel less like performances and more like emotional scripture, capturing the tension between impulse and morality, ego and shame, tenderness and chaos. The result is a body of work that feels mythic without losing its human core, rooted in the lineage of artists who turned personal struggle, spiritual conviction, and emotional rebellion into communal reflection, in the tradition of boundary-pushing voices who made their inner lives public. Harry s music isn t written from hindsight or healing. It s written mid-crash.

title: Young Rebel Set
description: Formed in Stockton on Tees in 2007, Young Rebel Set forged influence from modern singer-songwriter artists such as Springsteen, Dylan, Ashcroft and Cash to deliver hard-hitting indie sounds. The band rapidly gained a cult following, with audiences around the UK and Europe drawn in by their raw, edgy sound and captivating lyrical portrayals of heartbreak and hedonism. The early days of YRS are best described as beautifully messy . The band toured the UK and Europe, leaving a trail of drunken escapades and raucous, chaotic gigs in their wake. After signing to Ignition Records in 2011, they released their debut album, Curse Our Love, an exhilarating and introspective ride through modern indie-folk, replete with harmonicas and crooning about lost loves. After their debut was warmly received by fans and industry figures alike, they followed up with 2013 s Crocodile. While there was progression in the music, with the album s sound more mellow and distilled than that of their debut, the band had lost none of their trademark intensity. Despite continuing to tour and having a spate of new songs in the works for a potential 3rd album, the band went on a hiatus from 2015. Lead singer Matty Chipchase continued to push on, and convinced his old bandmates to get back together for a set of reunion gigs in 2019. But it was here that tragedy struck, as Matty passed away before the band were to take to the stage again. It was a devastating turn of events, and the reunion was put on hold. In spite of the tragic loss of their talisman, YRS continued to live on in the hearts and minds of their fans, and especially in those of Matty s former bandmates. In September 2024, the original members reconvened for a memorial night marking Matty s 40th birthday at the venue where the band was first founded, reeling off all of the old favourites in front of a packed-out crowd. It was an emotional evening, and one that lit a spark in three of the founding members - Andy Parmley, Chris Parmley and Luke Evans - to reignite the YRS flame. The band released a 5-track EP SUN in August 2025 which held the band s classic timeless influences now merged with a new, more forward-thinking sound, with airplay BBC Introducing, and Steve Lamacq s Radio 6 Music s show, A 12-date tour of Germany followed in September 2025 with a string of sold-out dates across the country, and their comeback year was topped off with two sold-out hometown shows to over 1000-strong crowds in November 2025. As of Spring 2026, the band are writing and demoing for the long -awaited release of Album 3, and fans can expect to hear never-before-released material in what is a continuation, rather than a retelling, of the YRS story, and one that seeks to add another, more positive chapter to the band s history.

title: Sofia Monroy
description: Sofia Monroy is a Mexican-Swedish artist born April 20, 1999, in Mexico City. For her first two years, she lived in a small rooftop room with her father, originally from Tapachula, Chiapas, and her mother, originally from Lule , Sweden. When she was two, Sofia and her parents decided to move to her mother s native country. At age 15, Sofia decided to participate in Swedish IDOL. Sofia signed with Universal Music Mexico shortly after a visit to the country in 2022, which led her to make the decision to move there, starting from scratch. This sparked a profound exploration of both her music and culture. Two years later, Sofia released her debut album, Morena; an album as personal, vulnerable, transparent, and eclectic as she is: a love letter to her roots and the two worlds that live within her. Sofia has recently opened for major Mexican artists such as Jay de la Cueva in 2024, and in 2025 she performed three sold-out shows for Zayn (formerly of One Direction) at the Palacio de los Deportes in Mexico City, playing for over 50,000 fans. At 26, Sofia is poised to become the next big international artist, having been named an Artist To Watch 2026 by two international platforms: Amazon Music and Premios Lo Nuestro (Univision). Now she s embarking on a new musical era, releasing her new single Pegadito, paving the way for what promises to be a life-changing 2026.

title: Comedy: Let’s Not Die!
description: A resounding hit in 2025, Let s Not Die returns for its second edition at Nature Climate House London 2026. Created and produced by Yellow Dot Studios, featuring some of the UK s funniest comedians. Not to be missed!

title: Deadline Reality TV Summit: TASKMASTER: A TV MASTERCLASS WITH ALEX HORNE
description: With Taskmaster celebrating 21 series in the UK and numerous international adaptations finding global success, creator Alex Horne is joined by director Andy Devonshire, series producer, Andy Cartwright, and executive producer Jon Thoday as they take us behind the scenes of one of TV s most enduring entertainment formats.

title: Earthset presents: "Plastics Without Borders: Inside Us"
description: Panel discussion: Why plastics matter, what they re doing inside our bodies, and how we fight back. Plastics are no longer just an environmental issue, they are a human health one. Micro- and nano-particles are now found in our lungs, blood and placentas, entering our bodies through the products we use, the food we eat and the air we breathe. This session explores how we fight back, from rethinking everyday consumer materials to pushing for the policy and system changes needed to reduce exposure at scale. Speakers: Sian Sutherland, Founder CEO, A Plastic Planet / Plastic Health Council Laura Harnett, Founder, Seep Moderator: Fiona Howarth, Founder Octopus EV and Earth Set

title: Les Itinerantes
description: Les Itin rantes (French for The Wanderers ) are one of France s most acclaimed a cappella trios, known for transforming vocal performance into a powerful, ritual-like live experience. Founded in Paris in 2017, Manon, Pauline, and lodie create an ethereal, haunting sound that blends ancient and traditional songs with original compositions. Singing in over fifty languages - from ancient dialects to newly imagined tongues - they move through space as they perform, surrounding audiences in a rich, atmospheric soundworld that reimagines the traditional concert format. Without instruments, they treat the voice as a living, embodied medium. It carries memory, connection, and human experience across a repertoire spanning nine centuries. Praised by the French press for the purity and emotional depth of their work, they are known for exceptional vocal precision and a striking sense of presence, creating intimate performances that invite audiences into a shared act of listening.

title: Xefer
description: Xefer was born and raised in Dhaka, Bangladesh, and her journey in music started on YouTube, where she would upload covers of herself performing songs in English by the likes of Bruno Mars, Jessie J and Paramore. She released her first single, Judge, in 2016, followed by her debut album, Uncaged, in 2017. Since then her style has changed and evolved, writing hit singles in Bengali such as Nei Proyojon, Jhumka and Harbo Na.

title: 2Spade
description: 2Spade is one of the most promising new names in the dance music scene, blending the heavy impact of trap and bass with hip-hop s raw energy and dancefloor-driven bass grooves. Rooted in hip-hop attitude and refined bass production, he defines his sound as HDM (Hiphop Dance Music). HDM represents 2Spade s musical identity, shaped by a lifelong exposure to both hip-hop and electronic music. It reflects his reinterpretations of hip-hop sensibility through the framework of dance music. His first HDM project, HDM K-Hiphop Flipz, reimagines classic and contemporary Korean hip-hop tracks as clubready dance music, receiving strong and fresh responses from the scene. On stage, 2Spade combines the explosive energy of a punk rock frontman, the rhythm of a rapper, and the precision of a DJ, delivering bold and high-impact performances. His originality has earned support from artists such as Yellow Claw, Flosstradamus, Ekali, 4B, JSTJR, DJ Diesel, and Yetep, positioning him as a rising force in global bass music. He has collaborated with leading labels including Barong Family, Sable Valley, and Dim Mak, and performed at major festivals such as EDC Korea, EDC Thailand, Ultra Korea, S2O Korea, Mystic Valley (Thailand), Waterbomb Manila (Philippines), and GAWLI Festival (Myanmar). His remixes for top K-pop groups (including aespa, Stray Kids, NCT 127, and ITZY) highlight the crossover between mainstream pop and underground dance music, while original tracks such as Violet (Pauline Herr Remix), ROCKXTAR, and Killers in the Jungle illustrates his versatility and artistic range. Expanding his global network through constant collaboration, 2Spade has emerged as a defining figure in Asian bass music scene. He has been bridging Seoul s underground scene with international festivals, events, and collaborative projects with local / global artists and music platform (David Yungin Kim, Ookay, Doberman, Insomniac Radio, EDC Thailand, EDC Korea, and etc.)

title: Briscoe
description: If Briscoe s debut album was a coming-of-age soundtrack set against the backdrop of the Texas Hill Country, then its follow-up, Heat of July, is the sound of a fully-formed duo chasing down a broader horizon. Inspired by marriage, landscapes, and the cross-country tours that took the band across America, these new songs were written on the road, as eclectic and evocative as the terrain that unfolded just outside the van s windshield. Once again, Briscoe teamed up with Grammy-nominated producer Brad Cook to bring the songs to life, collaboratively creating a record that expands Briscoe s folk-rock sound in all directions. There are bigger arrangements, sharper vocal harmonies, and enough top-tier guest musicians including members of Houndmouth, Hiss Golden Messenger, Bon Iver, Mipso, and Watchhouse to form their own supergroup. The result is a cinematic, widescreen version of American roots music, created by two lifelong friends who ve learned to distill the thrill of the open road and the challenges of growing up into an ever-evolving soundtrack.

title: GRAMN.
description: GRAMN. doesn t do polished in the traditional sense. Their music is an unfiltered exploration of modern life balancing social commentary with the messy, beautiful nuances of the human experience. From the creative melting pot of East London, GRAMN. is a genre-defying collective that sits at the intersection of Soul, Hip-Hop, and gritty R B. Led by the powerhouse vocals and razor-sharp pen of Aux , the group has carved out a unique space in the UK scene by pairing heavy, experimental production by James Lowe, with an unapologetically dry, London wit. The Voice: Aux s soulful, versatile delivery bridges the gap between classic jazz sensibilities and modern rap flow. The Energy: Expect heavy basslines, live instrumentation, and a take us as we are attitude that resonates with the disillusioned and the dreamers alike. With a string of critically acclaimed EPs and a reputation for high-octane live shows, GRAMN. continues to push the boundaries of the independent music landscape. They are the voice for those who find themselves somewhere in the middle: too soulful for the underground, too raw for the mainstream, and far too authentic to ignore.

title: Venus Grrrls
description: GRRRL AS F**K Venus Grrrls crystallise their emerging goth-grunge sonority with a strong early riot grrrl-influenced undercurrent. Evoking an alternate timeline where Bikini Kill predated both Romanticism and Siouxsie and The Banshees, it s VENUS GRRRLS narrative talent that stands out once again; lead singer GK s vocal inflections are a commanding presence against cascading riffs and synth motifs. She is joined by Eliza Lee (Lead Guitar), Hannah Barraclough (Bass), and Alannagh Doherty (Drums), where the Leeds/Newcastle rock coven have been heard on the likes of BBC Radio 1, BBC 6 Music, Radio X, and Sirius XM, and acclaimed from the likes of Kerrang!, DORK, Hot Press, Distorted Sound and DIY. Excellent Kerrang! This is what i m talking about, by being bold in your music Jack Saunders (BBC Radio 1) A band who really sound like they want to make things happen; to affect some sort of change Steve Lamacq (BBC 6 Music)

title: afromerm
description: afromerm is the Oram and YMA Music Producer Award-winning project of Cecilia Morgan, a South London-based composer and sound artist blending electronic/organic textures, voice, live instrumentation, and her self-built motion-reactive instrument, Juniper. Featured on BBC Radio 1 as a sonic innovator , with performance credits spanning from London Jazz Festival to Paradiso Amsterdam, afromerm immerses the listener in the elemental mythology of her soundworld.

title: Emika
description: This performance is part of the Crack X MyWorld Spatial Audio Evening. Emika, a pioneer in spatial music, will perform a solo piano piece. Emika is sound artist-in-residence for the Immersive Audio Network, supported by MyWorld. I hope she becomes very famous. - Thom Yorke She is sonically out of this world. - The Weeknd Diamond hard production and a Symphonic level of detail in sound that leads to new discoveries on every listen. - Robert Ames (London Contemporary Orchestra) Emika has her own sound and is following her own path. Since the turn of the decade she has been producing electronic and neo classical music, combining her skills as a sound designer, classically trained composer, and prolific singer-singer writer, she has released 8 critically acclaimed albums to date and works independently running her own label, Emika Records. Born in Milton Keynes, England, with a British father and Czech mother, she started making music using a 4-track cassette recorder as a young teen, combining classical piano, electronics through sampling techniques, getting her first job at her friend s local recording studio. She later went on to study Creative Music Technology at Bath Spa University to learn the art of record production, while living in Bristol and discovering the city s early bass music scene which would later evolve to become the now world-famous Dubstep movement. As a postgraduate student she moved overseas to Berlin to work as a Sound Designer at one of the leading global music tech companies Native Instruments, developing products for producers and DJs alike. She collaborated with internationally known club Berghain, to record a library of field-recordings inside the club which were then used for their 5 year anniversary release F nf (2010). She signed her first two albums Emika (2011) and Dva (2013) to UK indie label Ninjatune which both received critical acclaim in the music media and personal support from BBC Radio 1 s Mary Anne Hobbs and Thom Yorke who played her music said on his BBC 6 Music programme, in 2012 saying, I hope she becomes very famous . Her bass-heavy, edgy songs with her mix of piano and sultry breathy voice, had now become her trademark. In 2015 Emika established her own imprint Emika Records and continues to develop her work in electronic and classical music, embracing the creative freedom which came with such a venture and using this to push new classical music forwards and experiment with the blueprint of being a Berlin DJ. Some of her solo piano works from her album Klavirni (2015) have been streamed more than 17 million times on Spotify. She developed a strong connection to her fans via her social media platforms and live performances and through their support her first full symphony Melanfonie (2017) was crowdfunded via Kickstarter where she received 25,000 from fans in a 3 month period. She was able to hire the City Of Prague s 50-piece Philharmonic Orchestra and explore her Eastern Europe roots as inspiration for the piece. As a performer Emika takes to the stage with her piano, synths, voice and tech set up similar to her music studio. Since 2019 she has been focusing on developing her show into an immersive live experience with 360 degree dome visuals. Her show If We Disappear has sold out multiple nights in some of the biggest planetariums in Berlin, Moscow, Hamburg, Prague, Krakow, M nster. The show received great reviews from DJ Mag, Uncut, and BE-AT.TV. Previously she has toured coast-to-coast across the USA, China, Russia, Mexico, and Europe multiple times, performing at major music festivals such as Glastonbury (2016), Atlas Weekend (2019), and she was invited twice to perform at David Lynch s club Silencio in Paris. Emika has been remixed by Marcel Dettmann, Brand Brauer Frick, Pinch, Rebekah, and has made remixes herself for St Vincent, Nina Kraviz, Len Faki, Amon Tobin and Fink. She has collaborated with Hank Shocklee from Public Enemy who executive produced her album Dva , and The Exaltics who co-produced her album Falling In Love With Sadness . Her music and voice from her song Professional Loving were sampled by The Weeknd on his song Professional , which was performed live on Jimmy Kimmel (2013). Her music has been featured in films such as Spielmacher (2018), Bad Banks (ZDF, 2018), and the film trailer for Mission Impossible 4 as well as USA hit TV series The Blacklist (NBC, 2013) and How To Get Away With Murder (ABC, 2016) including a feature on the popular game Wipeout from Playstation. She has received chart success with her icy electronic cover of Chris Isaak s Wicked Game which reached No.2 in the USA iTunes charts and No.1 in Canada (2013). After the end of the pandemic in early 2022, she left Berlin after being a core member of it s electronic music scene for 15 years and relocated with her young family near Schwarzwald (The Black Forest) in South Germany. She has built her own music studio and Emika Records label office surrounded by trees, and is currently working on a new symphony, creating a film for planetariums world-wide in Dolby Atmos, and music for the film Another End by Piero Messina, starring Gael Garcia Bernal (Berlinale Film Festival main competition 2024). Emika s effortless performances... sound just as good, if not even better, on stage than they do on record. - DJ Mag She s already worlds apart, worth praising to the skies. - Classic Pop Mag A glamorous Zola Jesus. - Uncut Mag

title: Hanakiv
description: Hanakiv is a London-based, Estonian-born pianist and composer who creates meditative, piano-based music, with classical, and electronic elements. She made her debut on Gondwana Records in 2023 with the album Goodbyes , which draws on influences as diverse as Tim Hecker, Bj rk s Vespertine, Kara-Lis Coverdale, Arvo P rt, Erkki-Sven T r, and Aphex Twin, as well as her own cultural heritage. Music plays an important role in Estonian culture, particularly choral music and its traditions, but Hanakiv also draws inspiration from her love of nature - the beautiful Estonian seaside and forests, and from her time in Iceland. On her second album, Interlude , Hanakiv showcases an expanded sound, introducing vocals, string arrangements, and beats alongside prepared piano. The compositions trace a journey of overcoming the past, unfolding into a seductively unconventional style imbued with hope and a therapeutic quality. First inspired by crystallised moments where time almost stands still it provides, all the same, a sense of hope that standing still is part of living. This album is a product of creative and personal revelation, reached only through an encounter with one s true self.

title: Nish
description: Born in East London to Bangladeshi parents, Nish is a boundary-pushing artist blending East London culture with South Asian roots. Inspired by contemporary global music, he creates internationally resonant sounds that fuse modern influences with Bengali classical elements. From London s underground to international audiences, Nish continues to innovate while shaping trends and pushing boundaries. With nearly a decade in the industry, his emotional storytelling and distinctive sound have earned him recognition as a pioneering voice in modern Bangla-inspired global music.

title: Orestes Gomez
description: Orestes G mez is much more than a percussionist; he is an architect of rhythm and a visionary of hybrid production. Originally from Venezuela, he has established himself as a leading Latin American voice by fusing the heritage of Afro-Caribbean drums with the avant-garde of electronic music, jazz, and hip-hop. With prodigious technique and a unique sensitivity for sound design, Orestes transforms every beat into a narrative. His ability to integrate hardware and electronic elements into his acoustic setup places him at the frontier of contemporary musical innovation, collaborating with some of the most influential names in today s urban and alternative scenes. Links: https://youtu.be/PlfeGR3EWJ0?si=RW4jG8hn3pjajOex https://youtu.be/0Xhz66VmjPk?si=TeU7GwrE4mpy-Cmd https://youtu.be/m4MYQMkQzJI?si=kWbhm5KZDdxR0EHH https://youtu.be/Szy5TtG_ZmY?si=QC7y-QQTT44HDOX7 https://youtu.be/Pp1mbLaa3Dw?si=_0uO8fZv3hE0ak4n https://youtu.be/TzCbnLL605I?si=25UFo8QHq_wX_sNU

title: Shorts: Tiny, Lonely Fish Floating in Dark Water
description: TINY, LONELY FISH FLOATING IN DARK WATER Urban fl nerie featuring unfolding city lives, collective struggles, and fleeting intimacies amid the hustle and bustle. TINY, LONELY FISH FLOATING IN DARK WATER brings together a mosaic of urban gazes across continents, weaving everyday situations and collective anecdotes into cinematic vignettes. From neon-lit nights in 1980s Tokyo to the fractured infrastructures of New York, from the rhythms of a Mexico City neighbourhood to the quiet vastness of Ulaanbaatar s empty high-rises, these films observe how people navigate urban space and each other. Together they orbit the many hearts of disparate cities, probing identities, intimacy, and resilience in search of forms of belonging that surface, fracture, and reconfigure within the shifting currents of urban life. (97) Total Running time: 92 mins Content Warnings : Flashing images, cartoon violence, strong language, gun violence, alcohol use, discussions of domestic violence, death

title: All Night Wrong
description: Director: Jason James Cast: Zach Cherry, Maria Bakalova, Emily Hampshire, Tyler Labine Severance s Zach Cherry and Borat s Maria Bakalova go down a spiral of murder, lies, and (almost) sex in this riotous comedy. On one fateful night, Gary (Zach Cherry) and Eli (Maria Bakalova) decide to take their brief dating app exchange into real life. When they meet at a bar, things move fast, and soon the duo is at a roadside motel ready for your regular one-night stand. But nothing about their night is regular as they find themselves driving the car of a possible killer, lugging a dead body, and running from the police while carrying a mysterious suitcase filled to the brim with dirty cash in this fast-paced, anxiety-inducing Canadian comedy. A regular one-night-stand, indeed. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Charlie Hooper-Williams
description: This performance is part of the Crack X MyWorld Spatial Audio Evening. Concert pianist and Shazam developer Charlie Hooper-Williams presents his vividly beautiful piano music. Using his custom-built software system, developed through MyWorld, soul-soothing spatial music will drive handcrafted lighting responses for each piece. Charlie Hooper-Williams bridges the worlds of technology and music like few others can. Dubbed film scoring inverted, this performance turns music into light, building a visual world note by note in one of London s most atmospheric spaces.

title: Avengers - Infinity War
description: Directors: Anthony Russo, Joe Russo Cast: Robert Downey Jr., Chris Hemsworth, Mark Ruffalo, Chris Evans, Scarlett Johansson, Don Cheadle, Benedict Cumberbatch, Tom Holland, Chadwick Boseman, Zo Saldana With the powerful Thanos on the verge of raining destruction upon the universe, the Avengers and their Super Hero allies risk everything in the ultimate showdown of all time.

title: Carolina Cury & Kostako
description: Carolina Cury Kostako ARE performing. Award-winning pianist, singer, and composer Carolina Cury and sound machinator Kostako (aka Konstantinos Damianakis), both Studio Richter Mahr resident artists, join Classical Unlocked for a deconstructed set of Carolina s upcoming album GLISSBLISS produced by Kostako, braiding avant-pop, contemporary classical, and abstract electronics, into an immersive journey of chimeric vocal enchantment.

title: Cure for Paranoia
description: CURE FOR PARANOIA is the powerhouse Dallas alternative hip-hop collective founded by songwriter and front-man CAMERON MCCLOUD. Cameron captivates audiences with his thoughtful lyrics and an electrifying stage presence that keeps them craving his next move. For SXSW London, the project will be represented by Cameron McCloud alongside his DJ. Fresh from being named the 2026 NPR Tiny Desk Competition winner, Cameron continues to establish himself as one of the most exciting rising voices in alternative hip-hop.

title: La Carn
description: Director: Joan Porcel Cast: Lluis Garau A queer performance artist gets dangerously close to a stranger in an online chat room. Spanish dancer Lluis Garau adapts his own one-man-show of the same name into an electric and erotic nightmare of digital missed connections. Playing a version of himself, Garau is a young dancer with a penchant for trolling Chatroulette until someone interesting, or hot, or both, pops up. The film s tone switches to something darker when he connects with a masked man who may be behind some targeted murders of gay men in the area. Dancing between the intimacy of dark rooms and the perceived intimacy of theaters, Garau s central performance is so raw and all-absorbing, it becomes the film s heartbeat as the tension escalates to a fiery finale. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Michael Aldag
description: Michael Aldag is a 24-year-old singer/songwriter from Liverpool whose emotionally charged indie-pop blends infectious melodies, brutal honesty, and sharp, self-deprecating humor. A self-taught producer, he began writing and performing at a young age, later building a loyal fanbase through TikTok with his raw lyrics and authentic online presence. He has sold out four UK headline tours, supported artists like Bastille and Dean Lewis, and performed at major festivals including Reading Leeds and BBC Radio 1 s Big Weekend. With over 1.3 million followers and 20 million streams, Michael continues to rise.

title: Nagavalli
description: Declared an Austin, Texas music-scene multi-hyphenate by the Austin American-Statesman (2025), Nagavalli is an Indian-American singer, composer, and live performer whose groove-driven sound blends Indian musical tradition with contemporary production. Drawing from the melodic depth and improvisational spirit of her heritage, she creates expansive sonic landscapes shaped by layered percussion and powerful vocals. Primarily writing in English, her music remains immediately accessible while maintaining rhythmic and emotional complexity, resulting in a sound that is both globally rooted and distinctly modern. Her work moves fluidly across traditions, genres, and audiences, reflecting an early and independent exploration of cross-cultural global sound now gaining broader international attention.

title: rom&BAAWLA
description: R B is a Seoul-based bass duo formed by producers and DJs rom and BAAWLA. Combining rom s heavy low-end sound and experimental rhythm with BAAWLA s hybrid approach rooted in UK Garage, UK Bass, and Baile Funk, the pair has quickly become one of the most talked-about acts in Seoul s club scene. The project first gained attention through the ANTICLIMAX EP, released via Quality Goods Records, and has since built serious momentum through their ongoing R B Edit series. The edits have become near-unavoidable on Seoul dancefloors, with virtually every working DJ in the city running them in rotation. That reach has extended well beyond Korea: UK artists including Saint Ludo have picked up and played the edits, earning the duo international support and recognition within the global bass community. Beyond the booth, rom and BAAWLA have grown a strong presence on social media through reels and short-form content, connecting with a wider audience and establishing themselves as a voice for a new generation of selectors in the Korean scene. As members of SPIN Da Up, R B continues to expand across Asia, bringing their club-driven energy to international stages while representing the next wave of Asian bass music.

title: Entropies
description: Entropies are a neo-classical musical project that documents human stories through live sound and moving image. Featuring vocals, keys, violin, cello, and percussion, they describe their music as an evocative blend of alternative, neo-classical, ambient pop jazz. Adapting songs they have composed for British-Turkish-Iranian lead vocalist and director Sheida Kiran s films, Entropies combine these scores with new writing to create a musical style that unites raw storytelling with powerful orchestral arrangements. Their poised and melancholic chamber pop style draws from artists such as Hania Rani, Max Richter, and Agnes Obel, and they blend classical influences with electronic sounds, as well as live field recordings. Their most recent project, Songs For Our Land , draws inspiration from Sheida s latest BBC Our World documentary Turkey s Earthquake: Those Who Stayed , and was performed in sold-out shows in London and Cambridge. Performances with partners include FUJIFILM, Channel 4, and Sofar Sounds, and they have been featured on BBC Music Introducing and Radio X s X-Posure show with John Kennedy. Supported by Help Musicians and the Bert Jansch Foundation, their debut EP The Harvest , released in 2025, tells the stories of female tea pickers on the Black Sea coast of Turkey, based on the BBC documentary Harvesting Turkey s Tea . They have recently completed scoring Sheida s film coming out in late 2026 which was commissioned by the BFI.

title: Sega Bodega - Tuesday
description: This performance is part of the Crack X MyWorld Spatial Audio Evening. If you are a Platinum Pass Holder eligible for advance booking, then you can reserve your spot to see Sega Bodega by RSVPing to the Crack showcase on the official SXSW London app. Please note that reservation spaces are limited and that entry is only guaranteed if you arrive before the showcase starts at 7:00pm. Admission for late arrivals will be subject to venue capacity. Sega Bodega presents I Brought You To The Church To Watch Me Play My Ambient Album By The Altar. Salvador Navarrete has always created space for things to exist in curious friction. The release of his 2020 debut album Salvador and its successor, Romeo , introduced a reinvention of dance music driven by a kind of dream-logic which addled the mind but made sense to the body. Even when he stretches synths to their gleaming extremes, striving toward a vision of a future we haven t caught up with, his voice is still tender and undefined like the outline of a memory. Navarrete s ambitions aren t easily contained. His irreplicable sound has been woven into the production of pop disruptors including Eartheater, Caroline Polachek and Shygirl, on her Mercury Prize-nominated record Nymph . Alongside co-founding the underground music collective NUXXE and contributing production to Bj rk and Rosal a s collaborative single Oral , he has also formed the project Kiss Facility with Emirati-Egyptian artist Mayah Alkhateri yet another avenue of his expression. In his career, he has known lifetimes; the challenge he rises to for his third album is to contain a lifetime to a single night and day.

title: Farooque Bhai Project
description: Farooque Bhai Project is the musical brainchild of NYC-based Bangladeshi Canadian artist Issa Farooque, widely recognized as a pioneer of modern Bangla Alt-Pop. Blending witty, nostalgic, and often socially conscious lyrics with catchy, funk-pop tunes, he has gained popularity in the Bengali indie music scene with hits like Golapi , 1990 (Naach Bangali Naach) , Duita Dekhi and Shokaleo . The vibes are real. Dushtami Sharakhon.

title: Miss Blanche
description: Miss Blanche, born Blanca de Arteaga Waldburger, is a 25-year-old singer songwriter who has redefined R B and neo soul. With her unique blend of jazz, funk hip hop mixing English Spanish in her lyrics. Her style, both classic contemporary, conveys deep emotional intensity, tackling themes such as female empowerment and love. Influenced by legends like Lauryn Hill, Aretha Franklin, and Stevie Wonder, as well as contemporary artists like Raye Doechii, she has created an authentic and vibrant sound that sets her apart in the music scene. A graduate in Performance Songwriting from the prestigious LIPA University, Miss Blanche has conquered international stages in cities such as London, Liverpool Madrid. Her victory in the 2025 Madcool Talent contest has solidified her place as one of the emerging stars of R B. With eleven singles released, Undress Me has surpassed 380,000 streams, reflecting the strong impact her music has. This fall she had performed at the prestigious Fira B festival, bringing her talent to global audiences. With her first EP dropping out first week of June, Miss Blanche is, without a doubt, one of the most exciting and promising voices in today s music scene.

title: Earl Sweatshirt - Tuesday
description: If you are a Platinum Pass Holder eligible for advance booking, then you can reserve your spot to see Earl Sweatshirt by RSVPing to the Notion showcase on the official SXSW London app. Please note that reservation spaces are limited and that entry is only guaranteed if you arrive before the showcase starts at 7:00pm. Admission for late arrivals will be subject to venue capacity. For over a decade, the rapper Earl Sweatshirt has galvanized fans with his virtuosic storytelling, generous vulnerability, and lyrical gift. A wunderkind that emerged at just 16 from the raw and incendiary Odd Future collective, Earl quickly set himself apart with his dense introspection, off- kilter cadences, and a brooding intellect that belied his youth. Across a number of albums and widespread critical acclaim, Earl has cemented himself as a definitive poet chronicling growth, self-discovery, and the resonant moods of a generation. His live shows embody the intimacy of a cipher with the volume of a stadium, creating a space where fans don t just listen, they testify. In 2024, Earl celebrated 10 years since the release of his debut album Doris, a cerebral and emotionally layered record that marked the arrival of a singular voice in hip-hop. With the seminal I Don t Like Shit, I Don t Go Outside (2015), he turned inward to reclaim himself. Earl s 2018 album Some Rap Songs saw him explode traditional form and embrace sonic imperfection, layering thick samples, short verses, and abstract poetics into a textured collage of grief and transformation. Sick! (2022) captured the tumult of the pandemic era with quiet urgency and collaborative contributions from artists and producers like Zelooperz, Nak-el Smith, Armand Hammer, Black Noi$e, and The Alchemist. The 2023 collaborative project with The Alchemist, Voir Dire is a cryptic, soulful self-excavation marked by murky loops, fragmented memories, and Earl s signature elliptical lyricism. His new album, Live Laugh Love, is as thoughtful and surrealist, and embarks on a contemplation of the chaos of existence. A work that began before it began, LLL was initially named in satire and social commentary. What started as a tongue-in-cheek critique of the irony in the phrase developed into a genuine examination of the nostalgia of joy and the simplicity of genuine connection. LLL charts Earl s path to growth, one that demands a constant wrestling with the past and aspires to return to such moments. I named it before I wrote it, he says. And then everything started clicking. With LLL, Earl deepens a long-standing fascination with language, structure, and synchronicity. Constrictions breed creativity, he says. There are rules to reality. It reminds me of If how spirits operate within that system. I need rules. I need assignments. For Earl, those rules often begin with a word, a phrase, or a symbol. They give shape to his intuition. Take Tourmaline , the title of a track where Earl sing-raps about spiritual protection, love, and his responsibility as a father with hazy, cinematic flair over a looped melancholic melody. It was a word that simply came to him before he knew its spiritual significance. I had to see why. When I looked it up, it made perfect sense. Tourmaline is a receptive stone. It is celebrated by its ability to inspire enlightenment and balance. Across the record on Gamma (need the Love) , lyrics reference the late Dave Trugoy (Plug 2) of De La Soul, whose passing Earl says frames the album in its timing and his intentions as an artist to be self aware and comfortable in his black masculinity, but also to be creative, funny, and to strive for greatness. A prophetic line about Roy Ayers follows with uncanny foresight. Tarot imagery like the Eight of Cups entered his writing before he consciously understood its spiritual meaning, only to find later that the card s symbolism carried his intended message: the importance of walking away from what s no longer serving you, moving on, and being grounded in the mundane. That s what growth is, he reflects. You can get woo-woo and heady about things, but you still have to change diapers. This is the tension Earl thrives in: the poetic and the practical, the sacred and the routine, the heavy and the hilarious. LLL reflects on the alchemy of those layers as an album steeped in allegory but rooted in an artist s everyday life (the track INFATUATION , a love letter to food, chronicles the very real challenge of obtaining sustenance while on tour). This careful balancing act manifests as deceptively effortless, off-the-dome delivery. And yet, his true process is one of endurance and consistency: If I m not putting in military hours, sleeping at the studio, waking up at 6:45 and rapping, then making beats all day, and then rapping again at night, and then doing that for four days, then I feel like I m really behind, he admits. Earl s intensity isn t about ego. It s about legacy and contribution. Music is the thing I m the most leave it cleaner than you found it about, he explains. He s critical of the complacency that can come with success: the yes-men, the perks, the comfort. If you re not hard on yourself as an established artist, it atrophies you. If you re not resilient about the play of this shit the core essence your art becomes what it is: widely accepted but not challenging. Standout track CRISCO embodies the balance he s constantly navigating. It starts out with the energy of a Saturday night before landing in a moment of sobering Sunday morning vulnerability. It s the most human verse I ve written, he says, reflecting on lines like, I got the failure beaten out of me. It wasn t even an option. This honesty cuts through the album s dream logic, giving listeners not just a window into Earl s mind but also into the context that surrounds his life lessons. The end result of Earl s latest work is a prophetic, spiritually guided, and celebratory exploration of narrative as a means of surviving life s confusions, constraints, and change. As the music came, so did signs that Earl was channeling something beyond himself. LLL reflects both a profound inner shift and is also a generous snapshot and meditative processing of where Earl is now: a father, an artist in his 30s, and a person who has spent half of his life making art in public. LLL boldly confirms Earl s evolution without fanfare it is steady, palpable growth that marks the joy in embracing the journey, however imperfect.

title: Joe Harvey-Whyte
description: Pedal steel player and multi-instrumentalist Joe Harvey-Whyte has released 3 collaborative albums in the last 12 months to rave reviews. Pedal steel bliss - BBC Radio 3 Night Tracks This guy is outer space - Tony Visconti Sublime - **** Mojo magazine Joe s solo work combines the sound of the pedal steel and regular guitar with electronics and field recordings to make moving instrumental pieces that create a space for deep listening reflection. Aside from his own releases Joe is also a highly in-demand session player working with the likes of Aldous Harding, James Vincent Mcmorrow, Nil fer Yanya, Liam Gallagher and John Squire, Billy Bragg and many more. Joe also hosts a monthly radio show on Soho Radio showcasing new releases across the ambient, field recording and drone genres.

title: Nadine Shah
description: Shah is a Mercury Prize- nominated songwriter and musician who released her fifth critically acclaimed album Filthy Underneath in Spring 2024. The album received huge acclaim (including a cover feature with The Guardian) and debuted at #25 in the Official UK Albums Chart, her highest ever entry. Alongside A-list support at 6 music across all the singles, this album campaign also provided for Nadine s first tv show appearances with full band - with Jools Holland - and on RTE S Other Voices - along with live performances on 6 Music s Steve Lamacq s show and BBC Radio 4 s Loose Ends show with Clive Anderson . Much loved and respected by other artists ranging from Y oung Fathers, Depeche Mode and Patti Smith to New Order, Elbow and Brian Eno with whom she has regularly collaborated and shared a bill. Nadine played her own short Winter Tour across Europe in late November 24 culminating in a triumphant sold out Kentish Town Forum show in December - her biggest UK show to date. Her LIVE IN LONDON album capturing the excitement of that evening has just been released www.liveherenow.com/ nadineshah At the end of 2024, she appeared as a guest vocalist on the Pogues 40th Anniversary iteration of their Red Roses for Me album around the UK culminating at 3 Arena Dublin and continues currently as their special guest on their Sodomy The Lash tour across the UK. She s just played a busy summer of gigs and festivals in her own right - to include Glastonbury s Other Stage - alongside an autumn of supporting Self Esteem on various dates across the UK. Her albums touching on such subjects as mental health and addiction, Shah has lent her voice to many other causes that she is passionate about. She has also established herself as a presenter across a number of events such as The Q Awards and the AIM Awards ; on programmes for 6 Music, BBC Sounds and Radio 4 ; and via her own Instagram Stories series PAYBACK , which saw her interview music journalists during lockdown. Currently working on a new album set for release 2026.

title: Quentin
description: Quentin (any pronouns) is a visionary artist, singer and performer based in Austin, TX, known for work that fuses raw emotional intensity with striking visual storytelling and fearless self-expression. Their artistry blends commanding, soulful vocals with cinematic aesthetics, weaving themes of identity, empowerment, cultural pride, and transformation into immersive performances where vulnerability and power coexist. Quentin has brought this presence to major stages, including festivals like Austin City Limits (ACL), as well as acclaimed venues in Los Angeles and New York, steadily building a reputation for spill-your-guts live shows that are both technically powerful and deeply human. Set for release on July 24, Tomgirl marks a defining new chapter in their evolving body of work a bold, genre-blurring project that pushes past artistic and cultural boundaries while exploring gender, duality, self-invention, and fluid identity. Across all mediums, Quentin s work asserts a world where performance becomes liberation, honesty becomes strength, and nothing about identity is fixed or off-limits.

title: Brown Testament
description: Seeham Rahman, also known as The Brown Testament, is a Bangladeshi DJ, curator, and event organizer known for his emotionally driven progressive and deep house sound. Beginning his journey with SFM in Dhaka, he later founded Elysium Affairs in the United States, bringing international electronic artists to Washington, D.C., while touring across the U.S., Mexico, and Canada. After returning to Bangladesh, he launched Limitless, Soundesh, Nobo Festival, and The Matcha Society, helping shape a new wave of underground electronic music culture through immersive events and curated experiences.

title: Liu Lee
description: Liu Lee, a multifaceted artist hailing from Seoul, South Korea, embodies the roles of a vocalist, electronic musician, and DJ. Drawing inspiration from the pulsating beats of 90s Techno, Electro-Clash, and Breakbeat, she fearlessly expresses her artistic narrative. Her inaugural release, the Jasmine EP, left an indelible mark with its raw and provocative style. Exploring the thematic realms of sensuality and knowledge, Liu has fervently delved into defining her musical trajectory. Liu Lee persistently forges new avenues, inviting femme Asian artists to explore and redefine the music landscape.

title: Blossom Caldarone
description: Blossom Caldarone is an emerging musician known for her emotive sound and distinctive artistic voice. Blending elements of contemporary pop with atmospheric and alternative influences, she crafts music that feels both intimate and expansive. Her work highlights expressive vocals, thoughtful songwriting, and a growing presence within the independent music scene.

title: Kimmy gone
description: Kimmy gone is a rising artist under the South Korean music label AT AREA , recognized for her genre-bending musicality. Seamlessly navigating through the realms of Hyperpop, Jerk, and Hip-Hop , she reinterprets modern sounds with a distinct, edgy flair that resonates with a global audience. Her recent works, including the standout track Changes , showcase a unique blend of experimental soundscapes and a sophisticated visual identity. Driven by AT AREA s visionary production, Kimmy gone continues to carve out her own narrative, emerging as one of the most compelling and forward-thinking voices in the contemporary music scene.

title: Bojvck
description: BOJVCK is a Seoul-based underground electronic artist, DJ, and creative associated with the label/collective AREA X. Born in 2002, he blends raw club energy with experimental production and live hardware performance, using samplers and drum machines. His work crosses music, fashion, and visual culture, with collaborations and appearances in Seoul s underground scene and modelling/editorial work. He recently released his debut single, Break/HEAD.

title: EK
description: EK is a South Korean rapper and experimental artist known for pushing the boundaries of Korean hip-hop through hyperpop, digicore and electronic-influenced production. Originally emerging through Korea s underground rap scene, he has evolved into a more genre-bending sound, mixing distorted vocals, high-energy production and internet-era aesthetics. Recent projects such as YAHO and tracks like MollyWorld and TRIANGLE showcase his shift toward experimental club-rap and alternative electronic sounds while maintaining roots in Korean rap culture.

title: DJ Co.kr B2B H4rdy
description: DJ Co.kr B2B H4rdy are a Seoul-based underground DJ/producer duo blending UK garage, grime, jungle, UK funky, breaks and bass-heavy club music with the energy of Korea s alternative nightlife scene. Known for high-intensity back-to-back sets and releases under the Samsungboyz banner, the pair have become key figures in Seoul s bass underground, connecting Korean club culture with London-inspired rave sounds through parties, international bookings and collaborative productions.

title: Zumba Morning Workout
hall: Dray Walk Gallery - Zumba House, Class

title: Women in Tech Breakfast with Female Founders Rise
description: This breakfast meet-up is hosted by Female Founders Rise, bringing together female founders, entrepreneurs and those passionate about building and supporting women-led businesses. Female Founders Rise is a UK-based community dedicated to empowering women in business through networking, education and events, creating spaces for ambitious founders to connect, share knowledge and grow. Please note: all meet-ups are first come, first served once capacity is reached, entry will close. Arrive early, as spaces are limited.

title: Zumba + Lift Morning Workout
hall: Dray Walk Gallery - Zumba House, Class

title: NL House: Moral Ambition - Redefine Success
description: The world doesn t lack awareness. It lacks people willing to act on what they already know. In this session, designer Harald Dunnink shares the turning points that led him and historian Rutger Bregman to co-found The School for Moral Ambition, a global movement now spanning 25,000 members across 150+ countries and fellowship programs at institutions including Harvard. Moral ambition is not a theory, but something you can build by shaping how ideas take form, how people find each other, and how movements grow. A call to redefine success from personal gain to collective progress, and to redesign what we already know is broken. This session is part of NL House where Dutch ambition meets a global audience on June 3 at SXSW London. Non-badge holders can apply for entry by signing up via the Luma website. For the full overview of sessions and experiences, visit NL House on Luma .

title: Waymo Studio + Cafe
description: Open Monday-Thursday 09.00am-5.30pm. Experience the future of mobility at Waymo Studio + Cafe in Protein Studios. Grab a cuppa and see firsthand how our fully autonomous, sustainable and accessible driver will integrate into London transportation network.

title: Zumba Taster
hall: Dray Walk Gallery - Zumba House, Class

title: NL House
description: New Dutch Wave the Embassy of the Kingdom of the Netherlands. present the NL House! The full force of Dutch creativity and tech, right in the heart of SXSW London On June 3, NL House opens for a day that runs from a morning wake-up call about where ambitious people should actually point their talent, to late-night conversations about what Amsterdam s after-dark culture is building next. Expect cities to make an honest case for why they re worth your attention. Brands and studios showing work that actually moves people. Artists and technologists whose projects refuse to stay in one discipline. And the people behind all of it, live on stage and in the room. Ten promising Dutch startups, presented by the Dutch Embassy and ROM Utrecht, are here too early conversations welcome. During the day, step into Mequation an immersive installation by Thijs Biersteker and astrophysicist Yamila Miguel that brings research on the universe to life as a direct, physical experience. It exists only here, only today. Catch a session. Stay for the borrel. Rave with us. Or do all of it. This is where Dutch ambition meets a global crowd. Come catch the wave. NL House where Dutch ambition meets a global audience on June 3 at SXSW London. Non-badge holders can apply for entry by signing up via the Luma website. For the full overview of sessions and experiences, visit NL House on Luma .

title: The Making of David Bowie: You’re Not Alone
description: A behind the scenes conversation with co-directors Mark Grimmer and Tom Wexler of Journey - exploring the creative process behind David Bowie: You re Not Alone, the new immersive experience at Lightroom, in collaboration with the David Bowie Estate. This fireside chat will unpack how the team transformed thousands of archive materials, from live performance footage and photography to handwritten notes and audio recordings, into a large scale spatial experience that places audiences inside Bowie s creative universe. The session will explore storytelling within immersive environments, the role of sound and projection in shaping emotional connection, and the challenge of balancing spectacle with intimacy when working with one of music s most influential figures. From archive to atmosphere, the discussion will reveal how the show was crafted to celebrate Bowie as a restless creative force whose work continues to resonate across generations.

title: Art as Action: Can Creativity Change Society?
description: From climate activism to social justice, art is increasingly being used not just to reflect the world but to reshape it. In this timely conversation, artist and activist Molly Gochman joins curator and cultural leader Patrick Moore, moderated by Marine Tanguy, to explore how creative practice can drive real-world impact.

title: Can Europe Compete?
description: As global technology power consolidates in the US and China, Europe faces a defining question: how can it regain control of its digital future? The need for European tech sovereignty is clear but can Europe wrestle the back control?

title: Myth vs. Reality: Inside Africa’s Tech, Culture & Governance Transformation
description: Africa is often framed through outdated narratives high risk, weak governance, limited innovation but these perceptions do not reflect reality. This panel brings together experts across tech, finance, regulation, and culture to unpack what the world misunderstands about the continent. Through data, lived experience, and on-the-ground insight, speakers will challenge these misconceptions and spotlight the forces shaping Africa s potential: a young population, creativity-fuelled economies, and innovation emerging from unexpected places. By moving beyond clich s, the session aims to inspire a more accurate, opportunity-driven understanding of Africa, and why shifting the narrative matters now.

title: The Miscalibration of Shame
description: Has society become hopelessly shameless? Or utterly shamed? There s too much shame where there shouldn t be around things that signal growth or discernment (like sobriety, being a beginner, or having a normal body). Meanwhile, there s not enough shame where there should be around the things that quietly erode trust and social fabric (AI abuse, flaunting luxury next to suffering, casual dishonesty in dating profiles). Perhaps the world could do with a little more shame. But what does that really look like in practice? How do we retool our personal and professional relationships with shame? Who should have the power to decide what is and isn t worthy of shame? How do we balance permission and prohibition? And when it comes to brands, do they have an ethical obligation to help us recalibrate shame? Can they? Where do they fit in, especially in today s tumultuous business and cultural climates?

title: The Workforce Reimagined: Scaling AI for Organisational Transformation
description: What if your workforce could build over 60,000 AI agents in just 12 months? Where would you use them? Which functions would transform the most? How would you manage any regulatory and compliance hurdles? This session reveals the secrets behind one of the largest AI agent deployments in the world - 40,000 employees across industries, geographies, and languages using AI agents at scale. Euro Beinat, Global Head of AI Data Science at Prosus, and another industry leader will discuss strategies for adopting AI agents, how you create return on investment, and the everyday tasks that lend themselves best for transformation.
