Don t miss this opportunity to learn how AI is moving beyond traditional corporate structures to enable fully autonomous, AI-driven organisations and why rebuilding core functions from the ground up is the next frontier.

title: Abbey Road: Studio Four
description: Abbey Road: Studio Four, in association with Meta, is happening at 93 Feet East, Brick Lane, on 3 4 June. Studio Four is not a programme, a conference, or a showcase. It s a live creative environment - a place where the conversations, collisions and breakthroughs reshaping music, film, technology and art are happening in front of you. We re living through a moment of genuine rupture and renaissance in how music is made, heard, scored, performed, owned and felt. Studio Four puts you inside that moment. Not as an observer, but as someone who was there, and who helped shape it. As you move through the space, tracks are broken down and rebuilt live with audience input. Performances are captured and re-experienced in immersive spatial sound. Conversations that won t exist anywhere else bring together the people who defined the past with those actively reshaping the future. New creative tools are tested in public for the first time, by the people whose creative practices will determine how they re used. There is no boundary between process and performance, artist and audience. Everything is immediate, participatory and alive. You have to be there. Not to watch what s next. To step inside it while it s still being figured out.

title: NL House: Who’s Backing Europe’s Next Creative Scale-Ups?
description: Europe has the ideas. The challenge is helping the best ones scale. As hybrid realities blur the boundaries between physical and digital experience, new opportunities are opening up across the cultural and creative industries, especially in media, immersive experience and creative technology. The challenge is turning that momentum into ventures that can grow, compete and create value. This session brings together European founders, funders and ecosystem builders to explore what it takes to build innovation and technology capabilities and connect them to funding, partnerships and market traction. Expect sharp takes, real examples, and a candid look at how creative ideas grounded in European values move from signal to scale. The programme is curated by EIT Culture Creativity, with support from the City of Amsterdam. EIT Culture Creativity is a Knowledge and Innovation Community (KIC) of the European Institute of Innovation and Technology (EIT), launched to foster innovation across Europe s creative sectors. It connects creators, businesses, and researchers, supporting SMEs and professionals with funding, mentoring, and acceleration programs focused on sustainable economic growth, digital transition, and green energy This session is part of NL House where Dutch ambition meets a global audience on June 3 at SXSW London. For the full overview of sessions and experiences, visit NL House on Luma . (Non-badge holders can apply for entry by signing up via the Luma website)

Followed by more in-personal demo s after the pitches. Join us for short presentations and a live Q A with the following four Dutch startups. title: Roundtable: Gaming Isn’t a Channel. It’s the Future of Media
description: Gaming is a $200B+ industry, yet many brands still treat it as a test or a one-off line item. If the data already proves the ROI, why is the barrier to entry still perceived as so high? It is time to move from experimental budgets to long-term strategies that reach the global 225 million-strong gaming community authentically. The Discussion: Drawing on 14 years at Spotify, Playwire, and EFG, Dominic Grant leads an audience-led, collaborative deep dive into shifting from transactional ads to cultural utility. This session moves beyond the why of gaming to provide a practical blueprint for how to work within the games and esports space from a brand perspective. Learning from the success of partners like DHL, we will explore how brands can move from being an interruption to a functional part of the fan experience. We ll discuss the shift from simply buying space to building real value, showing that when gaming feels like a new frontier for a brand, an authentic connection is the only way to deliver sustained results. The goal is to provide a roadmap for accessing 225 million global gaming fans by becoming a member of the community rather than just a sponsor. Key questions for the table: What still stops brands from making gaming an always-on strategy? What makes a brand feel valuable to a gaming community instead of intrusive? How can brands stay culturally relevant across increasingly fragmented audience touchpoints? What does gaming understand about modern audiences that other industries still miss?
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: Roundtable: London 2050: The Battle for Urban Dignity
description: Cities have long been sold as engines of opportunity. By 2050, will London maintain that offer? Will this city still make a dignified everyday life possible? Living standards are becoming untenable. With London trending to become hotter and wetter year on year, prices inflating far beyond normal, and technological surveillance slipping out of our control, the insurability of our city is at risk, and basic essentials feel less secure. What must London do to remain liveable, fair - and human? Will we build cooler homes, greener streets, stronger communities and accessible public spaces for people to gather, rest and belong? Or are we drifting towards a city that is more advanced on paper than in reality? Bringing together voices from climate, design, technology, culture and civic life, this roundtable asks: What kind of London we are shaping now? Which future are we drifting towards, and what are we willing to pay to reach a better one?
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: Roundtable: The Role of Universities in creating the Entrepreneurs of the Future
description: Universities are uniquely placed to shape the next generation of entrepreneurs, but doing so requires more than courses, competitions, or incubators in isolation. This roundtable brings together perspectives from higher education and venture-building to explore how universities can intentionally equip students with entrepreneurial mindsets, practical skills, and real‑world exposure that extend well beyond graduation. Co‑hosted by Ruth Weir , Head of Entrepreneurship, and James Hayward, COO of Cambridge Future Tech, the session will examine how universities can better support early‑stage student startups, bridge the gap between academic learning and scalable venture creation, and build ecosystems that enable students to thrive as founders, future employees, and lifelong innovators. Thought Starter Questions: What entrepreneurial skills, mindsets, and experiences should every student graduate with, regardless of whether they launch a startup, and how can universities embed these meaningfully into the student journey? What is being done well, what needs more work? How can universities better support early‑stage student startups to move from idea to traction, while working effectively with external partners such as accelerators, investors, and industry ecosystems? What does success look like for student entrepreneurship post‑university, and how should universities measure and design support for long‑term founder and career outcomes, not just startups formed on campus?
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: Young Entrepreneurs Forum Breakfast Meet-up
description: This meet-up is hosted by Young Entrepreneurs Forum, bringing together founders, entrepreneurs and those passionate about innovation, business growth and building impactful ventures. Please note: all meet-ups are first come, first served once capacity is reached, entry will close. Arrive early, as spaces are limited. Entrepreneurs Network is a UK-based business community and media platform connecting ambitious entrepreneurs through events, content and networking opportunities designed to inspire collaboration, share insights and support growth.

title: Crossed Wires: The Live Podcasting Boom
description: Podcasting has escaped the studio. What began as an intimate, on-demand medium is now filling theatres, selling out arenas and becoming one of the most powerful forms of live entertainment in media. In this session, Alice Levine and Dino Sofos discuss the rise of live podcasting and the extraordinary growth of Crossed Wires - the podcast festival they founded - which is now the biggest in the world. They re joined by Danny Robins, creator of Uncanny, has taken his podcast to the stage and toured it around the UK to sellout crowds. We ll also hear from Jonny Kanagasooriam from BBC Sounds, who have partnered with Crossed Wires to programme two stages featuring their best shows. The conversation will unpack why podcast audiences are increasingly seeking real-world connection, how live shows have evolved from simple recordings into major cultural events, and what makes podcast fandom feel different from traditional broadcast audiences. From building formats that work both in headphones and on stage, to the economics of live events, audience community, and the growing role podcasts now play in culture and entertainment, this session will examine why live podcasting has become one of the industry s biggest growth areas.

title: Ranking Reveal: Top 100 in Consumer Tech in Europe by Sifted
description: Europe is producing world-class consumer technology but has lacked a single cultural moment where breakout startups are discovered at scale. Until now. In partnership with Sifted, SXSW London is proud to host the live reveal of the Top 100 breakout consumer tech startups in Europe: a data-driven, editorially curated ranking that identifies the companies showing real mainstream adoption, cultural momentum and global potential. Taking place live on stage all 100 ranked startups will be invited to SXSW London for a high-impact reveal in front of an audience of early adopters, investors, media and brand partners. This is Europe s answer to a definitive who s next list and the beginning of what SXSW London intends to make the annual go-to-market moment for the next generation of category-defining consumer companies.

title: Brand 2030
description: Meeting today s consumer expectations is already a challenge so how do you design for a future that won t sit still? With trends evolving, habits shifting, and tastes constantly changing, the real question is: how do you stay ahead of it all? This talk explores how to remain an agent of change, creating products, experiences, and strategies that don t just keep up but click with tomorrow s customers.

title: Have We Lost Control of Our Brains?
description: Join Dr. Gloria Mark, Chancellor s Professor Emerita at the University of California, Irvine, and author of Attention Span, for a thought-provoking conversation about how technology is reshaping our minds, emotions, and relationships. Drawing on her latest research, Dr. Mark will explore how society is responding to rising loneliness, shrinking attention spans, and weakening critical reasoning. But this path is not inevitable. She will also share practical strategies for using technology more intentionally so it strengthens our focus, deepens our connections, and supports the future of human capabilities.

title: Hugo Sunnucks
description: Hugo is an exited founder with hands-on experience across tech, venture capital and private equity. He studied Business Finance at Durham University before founding and exiting a low carbon e-bike fleet start-up acquired in 2017. He then became an Investment Manager at Velocity Capital and a Private Equity Consultant and Analyst at Active Partners. He has led or supported investments in over 100 companies, overseen multiple exits, and served on the boards of several rapidly growing companies. Outside work, he enjoys kite surfing and sailing. title: MANORS: Gaming the Algorithm to Bring an Old-School Sport to a New Generation
description: Golf has long been dominated by a few key brands with little incentive to shake things up. MANORS believed the game had more to offer. To grow its appeal and bring the new generation of golfers on board, the brand s strategic output set out to beat the algorithm and showcase golf apparel in a new light. In this session, MANORS Co-Founder Luke Davies joins the makers of their ground-breaking No Thanks, I ll Walk campaign at Stink to unpack their approach. From concept to impact, learn about intentional community growth and changing people s perception with disruptive marketing.

title: Ozlem	Tuskan
description: Ozlem Tuskan (Oz) is a leadership coach, futurist, brand strategist and award-winning entrepreneur working at the intersection of leadership and business growth. With over 25 years across media, advertising and foresight, she works with scale-up founders on strategy, decision-making and resilient growth. Formerly Head of Strategy at The Future Laboratory, she built and exited consultancy BrandSeers, working with brands including Google, L Or al, Diageo and Lacoste. She is a Founding Advisory Member of Female Founders Rise and Entrepreneur in Residence at Warwick University. In this session, Oz will integrate strategic clarity and future thinking with inner breakthrough to power intuitive business. She has led innovation and growth across start-ups to global brands including Telef nica, Samsung, Danone, Unilever, Meta and adidas, connecting brand, product, marketing and emerging technology. title: Screen Roundtable: How to Make a Film About AI
description: Much has been said about the growing use of artificial intelligence within filmmaking, but what about the filmmakers making films about AI? This roundtable will discuss different ways of approaching the hot topic of today, how to facilitate vital conversations while avoiding AI fatigue and what directors have learned from immersing themselves in a world that threatens their creative practice.

title: Severin Zugmayer
description: Mentor Session: Raising VC Money for your Startup Severin is the Solo-GP behind New Renaissance Ventures , the first VC fund dedicated to tech startups in the Cultural and Creative Industries. The fund started 2 years ago has already invested in 18 creative tech companies alongside leading VCs such as a16z, Northzone, LocalGlobe and saw up-rounds by Atomico and Balderton. Severin has over a decade of experience spanning top-tier VC, global FMCG, and Big Tech. He began his career at Red Bull, shaping culture and brand experiences, and later advised high-growth consumer companies at Google. In his most recent role at Speedinvest, he invested in and worked with leading consumer tech companies such as GoStudent and Inkitt. title: Tom Lorimer
description: Tom has previously built two VC-backed businesses across consumer and tech. More recently, he was Managing Director of Pyrsos AI Research, an ML consultancy building enterprise-grade AI systems. title: Beyond the Bot: Building Collective Intelligence that Scales
description: The chatbot era was just the prologue. Now, enterprises are building networks of thousands of autonomous agents that must think, act, and represent a brand with integrity. In this fireside chat, Paula Goldman, Salesforce s Chief Ethical Humane Use Officer, explores how to design that future responsibly where ethics isn t an afterthought, but the foundation of agility. She ll share how leaders can bridge the gap between policy, code, and culture to create an ecosystem of humans and AI working in concert. Learn how to build a workforce that is fast, adaptive, and - above all - trustworthy.

title: Power, Rewritten
description: The old power system was built on control: of information, institutions and access. That model is breaking down fast. What s replacing it looks very different - and much of it is being shaped by women. Not because women are finally being let in , but because the traits power once dismissed as soft - emotional intelligence, transparency, relatability, community and pattern recognition - are now proving to be strategic advantages. From high-stakes journalism to talent, media and entrepreneurship, this panel explores how truth, trust and influence are being rewired in real time - and why the future of power may belong to those building differently.

title: The Operator's Case for Sovereign AI
description: This session explores a new model for scaling frontier AI: government not as funder of last resort, but as a strategic partner in the company-building stack. Drawing on the UK s 500m Sovereign AI Fund, which combines direct investment with R D grants, compute access, procurement routes and cross-Whitehall navigation, as well as Prima Mente s experience building AI for the molecular biology of the brain, this panel will examine what sovereign capability can practically offer ambitious founders. The conversation will offer unique insights into how the UK can turn national capability into startup advantage and help build companies with global consequence.

She has led innovation and growth across start-ups to global brands including Telef nica, Samsung, Danone, Unilever, Meta and adidas, connecting brand, product, marketing and emerging technology. As founder of PIVYT, she partners with leadership teams to turn ambitious ideas into real products, platforms and growth from strategy through to execution. Expect clear thinking, no jargon, and ideas you can act on immediately. title: Beyond Podcasting: Building Shows People Belong To
description: The definition of a podcast is changing. The most successful shows today aren t just audio products - they re 360 brands with communities and ongoing conversations that live across multiple platforms. Dino Sofos, the CEO of Persephonica, is joined by Miquita Oliver, the host of their hit show, Miss Me? , along with Jamie Laing and Craig Strachan from Jampot. They ll explore how podcasting is being rebuilt around personality, fandom and shareability. From formats engineered for TikTok and YouTube, to the rise of video-first podcasting, to building listener communities that feel closer and more engaged than traditional media ever allowed, the panel will unpack what actually makes shows cut through in 2026. They ll also discuss how talent is evolving in the creator era, why audience intimacy has become such a valuable currency, and where the biggest opportunities for podcast growth now exist. A practical discussion about the future of podcasting - and the new playbook for building shows people don t just listen to, but belong to.

title: Diamonds Are Not Forever: How AI Killed the Double Diamond
description: For 20 years, the Double Diamond defined design thinking: A tidy diverge‑converge loop taught in every design school. But beneath the symmetry lies an inconvenient truth: It s slow, siloed.. And built for a pre‑AI world. Leading with real-world examples with some of the world s most famous brands and backed by fresh Harvard research on AI‑augmented teams, this session shows why the Double Diamond is no longer fit for purpose - and exactly what to build instead.

title: Life After the Website: 25 Data-Backed Shifts Defining 2026
description: The website is no longer the destination, yet marketing teams are still obsessed with a journey consumers no longer take. As buyers bypass Google for AI-driven answers, the resulting traffic decline is a structural shift that more content and better SEO cannot fix. Drawing on proprietary data from NP Digital and Ubersuggest across millions of sites and campaigns, Neil Patel maps 25 shifts defining brand visibility in 2026 and the strategic pivots required to lead the transition. He reveals how LLMs rank brands and why EEAT signals have replaced domain authority as the currency of visibility. From structured data to owned audiences and community, you ll leave with a concrete framework for reallocating budgets and measuring brand performance in a world where clicks are no longer the signal that matters.

title: Yoto: From Kickstarter to Global
description: Few founders in the UK have built a hardware business from a soldering iron and a Kickstarter to 100 million+ in revenue and done it sustainably. In this fireside chat, Yoto CEO Ben Drury joins Simon Scott of ATC Music Group to trace the journey from a moment of realisation about hands-on, child-led learning to a globally shipped product in over 200 countries: what it actually takes to build a hardware business, how to crack international markets without losing the plot, and why patient capital beats fast money when your product ships in a box.

title: Zumba Taster
hall: Dray Walk Gallery - Zumba House, Class

title: AI & National Security
description: There is nothing artificial about intelligence. Yet AI is transforming the way we produce the vital intelligence that helps to keep us safe online and on our streets. Like others, the UK s national security community are harnessing the power of AI. Unlike others they don t get to talk about it very often. Join us for a rare and unique conversation with three of the most senior and inspirational leaders who are putting AI onto the front line of our national security community.

title: Roundtable: From reactive care to predictive cities: building London’s health system for the future
description: How do we move from a system that treats illness to one that predicts, prevents, and is centred on individual needs? As we reimagine how healthcare could look like in the future, from virtual hospitals to health-focused smart cities we need to get practical about what it will take to make this real. In this roundtable, participants will explore how AI, data, urban design and new models of care might come together to create a more proactive, personalised health system embedded within the fabric of the city. We ll look at the real-world challenges behind this shift from data trust and investment, to workforce and delivery and share perspectives from across health and technology. Through discussion, we ll challenge assumptions, surface the key tensions, and identify practical ideas to help cities like London shift towards a predictive, prevention-first model of health.
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: Roundtable: The Consent Economy: AI Cloning, Artist Identity, and the Deals That Need to Catch Up
description: As generative AI makes it trivially easy to clone an artist s voice or likeness, the entertainment and technology industries are facing a fundamental question: who controls the use of a person s identity in an AI-generated world, and what does meaningful consent actually look like? This roundtable would explore the emerging commercial and consent frameworks around AI voice and likeness rights, including where existing public policies fall short, what deals are being negotiated right now, and how platforms, labels, studios, and developers can build consent-first approaches. The roundtable is designed to be practitioner-focused, drawing on participants from across the entertainment and technology spaces.
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: Scaling the “Taboo": How to Build and Fund Startups in Overlooked Markets
description: Less than three percent of VC funding goes to female-founding teams and even less to those addressing stigmatized health categories. Little data exists around funding for non-binary or trans founders at all. Yet, this so-called niche represents a multi-billion dollar opportunity. How do you turn systemic underfunding into a competitive advantage? How do you circumvent online censorship to successfully market products and services critical for half of our global population? Join Hannah Samano (Unfabled), Cindy Gallop (MakeLoveNotPorn), and Kenny Ethan Jones (LostFame) for a transparent conversation around the power of community-led growth and creative strategies to successfully break into under-funded markets.

She is a member of UK AI and feeds into policy on AI skills, UK Brand Ambassador for SheAI, and a regular conference speaker on AI, skills and the future of work. With vast mentoring experience, she loves helping new business owners find their footing. title: Lee	Lindley
description: Lee Lindley is an investor at Mercia Ventures, focusing on late Seed and Series A investments across Life Sciences, HealthTech and DeepTech including AI-enabled software and computer vision. Since joining Mercia in 2021, Lee has returned to science-led innovation. Previously, Lee worked in Corporate Finance at PwC in healthcare and spent two years on M A across energy and digital infrastructure. Their career began on the NHS Graduate Management Training Scheme, where they qualified as a Chartered Management Accountant. Lee studied Natural Sciences at Cambridge specialising in Cognitive Neuroscience and holds a Master s in Research Methods from Manchester. He led successful tenfold growth post-startup acquisition by a public company. title: Lukas Rippitsch
description: VC investor in Deep Tech.

title: Ozlem	Tuskan
description: Ozlem Tuskan (Oz) is a leadership coach, futurist, brand strategist and award-winning entrepreneur working at the intersection of leadership and business growth. With over 25 years across media, advertising and foresight, she works with scale-up founders on strategy, decision-making and resilient growth. Formerly Head of Strategy at The Future Laboratory, she built and exited consultancy BrandSeers, working with brands including Google, L Or al, Diageo and Lacoste. She is a Founding Advisory Member of Female Founders Rise and Entrepreneur in Residence at Warwick University. In this session, Oz will integrate strategic clarity and future thinking with inner breakthrough to power intuitive business. description: Pat is a performance coach who helps clients feel more confident and communicate more clearly, strongly and memorably. She works with science start-ups at ETH Zurich and has delivered workshops at London Business School s Women in Leadership programme and University of the Arts London. She also helps private clients from Private Equity to the UN make a stronger impact. Work with Pat on pitches or other communication to get feedback and actionable tips on being inspiring, exciting and retellable. She helps founders build robust financial foundations through hands-on leadership and rigorous analysis of unit economics, cash flow, scaling potential and operational efficiency. Over the past two years she has also angel invested, applying financial acumen to support early-stage companies. What sets her approach apart is combining deep financial analysis with practical operational guidance, helping founders turn strategic vision into financial reality. She has led innovation and growth across start-ups to global brands including Telef nica, Samsung, Danone, Unilever, Meta and adidas, connecting brand, product, marketing and emerging technology. As founder of PIVYT, she partners with leadership teams to turn ambitious ideas into real products, platforms and growth from strategy through to execution. Expect clear thinking, no jargon, and ideas you can act on immediately. The fund started 2 years ago has already invested in 18 creative tech companies alongside leading VCs such as a16z, Northzone, LocalGlobe and saw up-rounds by Atomico and Balderton. Severin has over a decade of experience spanning top-tier VC, global FMCG, and Big Tech. He began his career at Red Bull, shaping culture and brand experiences, and later advised high-growth consumer companies at Google. In his most recent role at Speedinvest, he invested in and worked with leading consumer tech companies such as GoStudent and Inkitt. title: Tom Lorimer
description: Tom has previously built two VC-backed businesses across consumer and tech. More recently, he was Managing Director of Pyrsos AI Research, an ML consultancy building enterprise-grade AI systems. Having received a BSc from Bristol University, he went on to do a number of AI-related Executive Management programmes with INSEAD and Oxford University. His entrepreneurial background has given him broad cross-sector commercial experience. title: Creating New Realities: From Deep Space to the Big Screen
description: The iconic, futuristic worlds that appear before us in cinematic projects like Dune are not merely works of speculative fiction. Such productions draw upon ecology, aerospace engineering and physics to ground their design. At the same time, the bold imaginations of filmmakers and game designers help to inspire the next generation of real-life exploration. Join Tom Cwik, Technology Space Innovator, Patrice Vermette - Production Designer, Dune Arrival, and David Sheldon-Hicks - Founder, Territory Studio, to explore the interconnected relationship between real-world space engineering and the aesthetics of sci-fi storytelling.

title: Human-AI Strategy: From Adoption to Organisational Advantage
description: The session will be about shifting the AI conversation from tool adoption to organisational design. It will explore why AI acts as an amplifier - multiplying both strengths and weaknesses - and why speed alone does not equal advantage. A key focus will be the rise of M-shaped talent: professionals with depth in more than one domain and the integrative capability to connect across disciplines. In a world where AI handles routine execution, advantage increasingly sits at the intersections - where judgment, synthesis, and connection happen. The session will examine what conditions allow this kind of talent to thrive, and how organisations can redesign work so that AI-created capacity fuels integration and innovation, not just more output.

title: Women's Sport. Now What?
description: Sport stars Jill Scott MBE and Jess Breach have lived the women s sport revolution firsthand; from playing in front of dozens to raising trophies in front of sell-out stadiums. Now they re joining host Georgia and fan culture expert Amy for an unfiltered conversation about what needs to happen next. What barriers remain? What does true parity look like? Where are brands getting it wrong, and how do they make a genuine contribution versus performative support? This isn t another celebration of how far we ve come. It s an honest reckoning with how far we still need to go and how we step up.

title: Can Africa Reset the Rules of Luxury Fashion?
description: For over a century, luxury fashion has been defined by a handful of global capitals, shaping who holds creative authority. Today, that power is shifting as designers across Africa and its diaspora redefine luxury through heritage, innovation, and cultural authenticity. Despite systemic barriers, these creators are not emerging they are leading a transformation, challenging a historically unequal industry. This panel explores how digital access, changing consumer values, and platforms like Brand63Africa are enabling more equitable global participation and reshaping the future of luxury.

title: Art, Tech & Nature: The Glitched Sublime
description: Artist Maja Petrić examines the uneasy alliance between technology and the natural world. She frames the glitched sublime as a paradox in which machines archive ecosystems they also accelerate toward loss. Are we using technology to preserve nature, or building systems to feel what we ve already forgotten?

title: How to Build an International Music Tech Business
description: Building a music tech business that works beyond its home market requires more than a great product. This panel examines what international expansion actually looks like in practice - from when to make the move, how to navigate different regulatory and licensing environments, and where to find the capital and partnerships that make it viable.

title: What Happens When Advertising Doesn’t Let Us Look Away?
description: Are you ready to feel uncomfortable? Maybe even a little queasy? In this SXSW session, Helen Rhodes, Chief Creative Officer, Grey London and Elisa Allen, Vice President, PETA UK unpack how they deliberately hijacked the warm, comforting tropes of our favourite holiday to reveal an uncomfortable truth hiding in plain sight. Rather than shouting, Happy Christmassacre whispers first, inviting audiences in with nostalgia, humour and familiarity, before forcing a double take. Snowflakes become saw blades. Santa s red becomes something far more sinister. A novelty knit reveals a slaughterhouse stitched into its seams. Suddenly, the most well-established of traditions feels harder to swallow. Grey London and PETA will share how creative discomfort can be used to drive behaviour change, why humour can be a Trojan Horse for serious messaging and how celebrity, craft and cultural timing (from cinema releases to National Christmas Jumper Day ) can amplify purpose-driven work without softening its bite.

title: Patrick Darling & The Ceili House Band w/ ElevenLabs
description: For over a decade, The Ceili House Band have been a beloved fixture of Bristol s Irish folk scene equal parts musicians and mischief-makers, known as much for their energy and spirit as their musicianship. Originally performing simply as The Ceili, the band evolved over hundreds of gigs, with lineup changes gradually shaping a tighter, more focused sound rooted in Irish tradition but fuelled by punk attitude and live-wire charisma. A major turning point came in 2014 when Patrick Darling joined the group after responding to a Facebook post announcing the departure of founding member Chris CJ James. A Bristol music student with a love of Irish folk and heavy metal, Patrick quickly became the heart of the band singing, playing banjo, guitar and melodica across nearly 400 performances. In 2021, with only one original member remaining, the group officially became The Ceili House Band. But behind the scenes, Patrick s health had begun to decline. What first appeared to be clumsiness gradually developed into severe mobility issues, eventually leaving him unable to stand, then unable to play banjo, and finally unable to perform at all. In 2024, Patrick stepped away from the band after being diagnosed with MND. Then, in early 2026, a new chapter began. Through a groundbreaking project involving speech therapist Richard Cave and UK tech company ElevenLabs, Patrick was given a state-of-the-art AI voice model, allowing him to perform again using technology designed to restore voices lost to conditions such as MND. The band reunited for an emotional performance of Patrick s song Ghost of a Man I Never Met at London s O2 Magazine Theatre a world-first live performance using the technology. The moment drew international attention, with coverage from the BBC, ITV, The Telegraph, The Times and media outlets across Europe, the US, India and Japan. Now, following that extraordinary performance, The Ceili House Band arrive at SXSW London for another landmark moment performing at Abbey Road Studios as part of one of the world s leading festivals for music and innovation.

title: The Podcasting Blueprint: 10 Years of Laughter and Loyalty
description: How do you sustain a cult audience for over a decade while evolving into a commercial powerhouse? Lizzy Pollott (Acast) is joined by award-winning podcasters Elis James and John Robins to deconstruct the blueprint of their creative journey and approach to community-building. They ll reveal the strategy behind forming their own independent company, Significant Productions, and partnering with Audio Always to pioneer the BBC s new Audio Release Policy - becoming the first major title to commercialise via Acast while remaining a staple on BBC platforms. For creators, learn how to navigate platform pivots and scale a loyal fanbase into a multi-channel 1.5m+ audience. For advertisers, discover the untapped potential of partnering with a legacy podcast community built on ten years of deep-rooted listener trust. For everyone else, come and have a laugh.

title: Jodie Bryant
description: Jodie Bryant is a presenter and radio host. She is the host of the Future Pop show on Radio 1 and has hosted the AIM Independent Music Awards, as well as presented for the BBC Children in Need livestream. She is an ambassador for Youth Music, recognising her work championing new artists and grassroots music. Jodie also hosts her own live music night, Discover Live, supporting emerging artists, some of whom have gone on to break through following performances on the platform. She also presents her digital series Fangirl Files, where she interviews artists, celebrities and personalities as a super-fan. Guests have included pop icon Cher Lloyd, December10 and Mimi Webb.

title: Max Cooper - Spatial Sound Installation presented by L-Acoustics
description: Max Cooper is an electronic composer, multidisciplinary artist, music label founder and former scientist who has carved out a unique space in music and visual art. He holds a PhD in computational biology while also being the first contemporary electronic musician to perform at the ancient Acropolis theatre in Athens and one of the first wave of artists to produce in Dolby Atmos. Cooper has spent over 15 years exploring the intersections between the arts and sciences through music, collaborations, and his label Mesh, alongside installations, performances, immersive experiences, online media, music videos and live events. Anchored throughout is his emotive approach, connecting how ideas and forms feel in a way that is accessible to all. I ve always had a strong emotive bond with aesthetics, and found at an early age that the purified forms of electronic music carried a lot of weight for me, as did the reductive natural aesthetics of the sciences. That felt connection to both opened up a world I ve been lost in ever since. Pivoting from computational biology to electronic music might not seem like the most natural career transition, but for Cooper the commonalities are clear: In both fields, you re in a sense free. Free from the limits of living systems, free from the limits of corporeal sound. The only constraints are in your mind. Max Cooper has collaborated with Zaha Hadid Architects, Dolby, L-Acoustics, 4DSOUND, Barbican Centre, W1 Curates, The Babraham Institute, and has exhibited work at institutions including the Centre Pompidou Metz, ZKM Karlsruhe, Museum Wave Seoul and the Science Museum London.

title: NL House: If You Build It, Will They Come? Designing Places Where Tech Talent Thrives
description: Every city wants to be a tech hub. Most aren t. What separates the places that actually attract and keep exceptional talent from the ones that build the campus and wonder where everyone went? This session brings together people who ve watched it work and those who ve watched it fail from London s Knowledge Quarter, now home to OpenAI, Google DeepMind and Anthropic, to the Amsterdam metropolitan region s next wave of expansion, and to the Dealroom data on where ecosystems are actually growing. This session is part of NL House where Dutch ambition meets a global audience on June 3 at SXSW London. For the full overview of sessions and experiences, visit NL House on Luma . (Non-badge holders can apply for entry by signing up via the Luma website)

title: Racing to win, changing the game - with Sir Ben Ainslie, Hannah Mills OBE and Kai Hockley
description: What does it take to change the future of sailing while competing at the very highest level of global sport? Sir Ben Ainslie, Hannah Mills OBE and Kai Hockley share insights from the cutting edge of the America s Cup and SailGP, where world-class athletes, engineers and innovators are pushing the boundaries of performance, technology and teamwork. From creating the fastest and most advanced boats in the world to driving greater diversity, equality and accessibility within the sport, this conversation explores how sailing is evolving to build its global audience and inspire the next generation. Join Ben, Hannah and Kai for a discussion on how elite sport is helping shape innovation, opportunity and the future of sailing.

title: Tina Edwards
description: Edwards is out there extensively researching scenes, genres and artists, cutting through the convenient listening of your latest Made For You playlist and helping us remember what true discovery should be Dazed and Confused Edwards is deeply plugged into what s next for global record culture - Cue BURN, vinyl alliance Essential jazz into dance selections every time - Gilles Peterson An internationally-acclaimed DJ, Tina Edwards encourages dancers to ignore the booth and stay present on the dance floor. Tina began DJing shortly before the pandemic; since, her demand behind the decks has taken her and her red box of 45s around the world, performing in Australia, Japan, Singapore, LA, Chicago and across Europe. She s played Glastonbury, Montreux Jazz, North Sea and headline sets for the likes of BAFTAs Official After Party (2025, 2026), film premieres, Netflix and Ray Ban. Tina s sets are wildly eclectic and full of wild cards but her grounding comes from the language of jazz; the Londoner is recognised as one of the few DJs of her generation to uphold jazz dance floor culture. She does this with her Internationally-renowned night Love Is Everywhere, with some of the most established jazz dancers from across Europe and Asia making a point of performing. Wherever she plays, her sets avoid predictable arcs; Latin jazz, cumbia, off-beat disco, Afro-diasporic grooves, psychedelia and obscure 45s from her travels all contribute to her playful, intoxicating sets. Elsewhere, Tina s reputation for sharing and breaking new artists is upheld on her Internationally revered radio show Bandcamp Selects, Bandcamp fan club Jazz-ish Jazz Club, and through live events too, namely with LGBTQIA+ community-oriented platform Queer Jazz and resonate; the latter of which aims to democratise information in the music industry through events, discussions and editorial. Tina Edwards is one of Worldwide FM s longest serving residents (2017-2022), and she hosted and produced Next Big Thing for British Airways for seven years, reaching millions of listeners. In 2022 she presented the TV documentary JAZZ UK: Spitting Fire (BBC One, BBC Four). She s also regularly heard on BBC Radio 3 (Jazz Fix, Round Midnight), and has written for Downbeat, The Guardian, Monocle, WeJazz and many more well-respected platforms. She was Editor of limited edition Listen Zine for KEF Speakers.

title: AI Left the Chat
description: As AI moves beyond chat windows and into the devices we already use, the question becomes less about what AI can do and more about how it should show up in our lives. On phones, wearables, cars and home devices, AI can respond in the moment: translating a conversation, detecting signs of fatigue, improving accessibility or helping a car react before danger hits. Will Abbey explores the shift from AI as something we prompt to AI as something that supports us, and how AI closer to people can become faster, more personal, more private and more human.

title: How AI is Rewriting the Scaling Playbook
description: Even as recently as five years ago, the level of operational tooling available to founders was a world away from the capabilities present today, and companies are scaling using a completely different playbook. Founders are now skipping the regional phase entirely, compressing years of work into months, and launching products globally from day one. In this fireside chat, Douglas Stark (Voi) and Tobias Bengtsdahl (former founder-turned-investor at Antler in the Nordics) will lay out what this new era of scaling looks like, reflections on how AI has made the tech world default-global, the force multipliers today s founders are wielding, and advice to those thinking of starting up in 2026.

title: Technology as an Instrument: Inside the AI Workflows of the World's Biggest Producers, Mixers, and DJs.
description: Every major shift in music has been defined by a new instrument, from the synthesizer to the sampler, and now AI. The technology has rapidly become part of modern music production, but much of the public conversation still focuses on fear and speculation rather than how artists and producers are actually using it every day. This session moves beyond sensational headlines to explore how creators are integrating AI into real workflows, where the technology genuinely adds value, and why many of the world s top artists and producers increasingly view AI as a creative instrument rather than a replacement for human artistry. Hosted by Ryan Merchant of Moises , the music platform used by more than 75 million musicians, the conversation features two of the most respected voices in modern music. Manny Marroquin is an 18-time Grammy-winning mixing engineer whose work appears on 250 million albums, including projects by Taylor Swift, Post Malone, Rihanna, Ed Sheeran, and The Weeknd. Laidback Luke is one of dance music s most influential pioneers, with a career spanning more than two decades as a DJ, producer, educator, and early adopter of production and performance technology, with hundreds of remix credits for artists including Daft Punk, Madonna, Jay-Z, Maroon 5, and Pharrell. Attendees will gain firsthand insight into how AI is being used at the highest levels of music today, hear candid perspectives on creativity and authenticity, and leave with a clearer view of where the technology is genuinely earning its place in the studio.

title: One Brand, 200 Destinations: A Founder’s Guide to Global Communication
description: Building a product is one thing, but getting the world to notice is another. Vykintas Maknickas, CEO of Saily, pulls back the curtain on growing a travel eSIM brand to a global scale. From finding the right message for different markets to making creative bets that paid off, Vykintas shares the strategies, pivots, and lessons that fueled Saily s growth.

title: Where Ideas Really Come From
description: The world s most original creators know something others don t: true creativity comes from stillness. The iconic portrait of Queen Elizabeth II - one of the most reproduced images in modern times - didn t come from chasing output, but from perception quiet attention. This session goes beneath creativity as craft to ask where originality really begins. Drawing on David Lynch s belief that great ideas emerge by diving beneath conscious thought - this panel explores how altered states of attention instinct-led storytelling produce work with cultural impact. Not wellness. Not productivity. But the perceptual depth that makes work that hits different is impossible to ignore.

title: The Isomorphic Shift: Accelerating Drug Discovery at Digital Speed
description: This discussion will explore how long-term investment can lay the groundwork for transformative scientific breakthroughs and provide a forward-thinking outlook on how Isomorphic Labs may one day solve all disease with the help of AI.

title: Roundtable: PR in the Age of AI: How to Become the Brand Humans Trust and AI Recommends
description: Today, buyers don t just search they ask AI who to trust, who to work with and which brands stand out. These systems don t rely on adverts or self-promotion; they learn from credible third-party signals such as media coverage, expert commentary and consistent authority across the web. In this roundtable, Alison Shadrack, Founder of Adia PR, will explore how strategic PR builds the kind of credibility that journalists trust, customers act on and AI models draw upon when generating recommendations. As we move from a world of search results to one of recommended answers, businesses need to think beyond visibility and focus on authority. Drawing on real-world examples, this session will unpack how to build a trusted reputation that resonates with both human audiences and AI systems and how entrepreneurs can take control of whether they are recognised as a trusted authority or overlooked entirely in the age of AI. Questions to think about: If a potential client asked an AI tool about your business or expertise today, what would it say and would you be happy with that answer? What credible, third-party signals currently support your reputation and are they strong enough to influence both human trust and AI interpretation? Are you building a consistent, compounding digital footprint or are your visibility efforts still fragmented?
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: Roundtable: The Infrastructure of Opportunity: How Digital Finance Powers Economic Growth
description: Digital financial platforms are becoming core infrastructure for economic participation. By enabling people to save, transact, and access credit, they unlock opportunities for individuals and small businesses that were historically excluded from the formal economy.
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: How To Build a World Around a Podcast
description: The best podcasts today aren t just shows - they re brands, communities and businesses. Joe Marler, research assistant Jake Bhardwaj and the team behind Joe Marler Will See You Now reveals how they crafted a world around an idea, a format that goes beyond the typical celebrity interview, that is built for every screen and monetised for every opportunity.

title: Materials Shape Civilisation
description: Materials have always defined human progress. Now, a new era is emerging. Join SurpRice for a provocative conversation on how consumer demand, cultural shifts, and government regulation are accelerating the race to develop next-generation materials beyond fossil-fuel synthetics like PVC and PTU. For years, industries have talked about replacing fossil-fuel-based materials like PVC and PU and yet most alternatives have failed when faced with the realities of cost, consistency, performance, and scale. Until now. This session explores why the next generation of materials is finally ready for industrial adoption, and how breakthrough innovation is transforming agricultural waste currently burned into the atmosphere across millions of hectares of South Asian farmland into premium, scalable materials for fashion, interiors, automotive, and beyond. Introducing SurpRice s patented rice cultivation-waste technology, developed using advanced Italian manufacturing systems, this is not a conversation about future possibilities. It is a conversation about materials that are ready now and the industries bold enough to move first. Drawing on SurpRice s journey from laboratory proof-of-concept to patented industrial process, this session will challenge the conventional narrative that sustainability requires compromise.

title: The Company You Pitched Isn't the Company that Thrives
description: Cloud kitchens was one of the most hyped F B categories of 2020-2022. Most peers died. Kitopi raised a Series D and kept growing by evolving business model in public into omnichannel (cloud kitchens, dine-in, hybrid). Honest retrospective, hard calls, and mistakes, with direct lessons for anyone building a business today.

title: Can Brands Think for Themselves?
description: The era of brand control is dead. As AI becomes the primary interface through which we search, decide, and act, brands are being disintermediated. No longer encountered through a curated feed or a fixed campaign, your brand is now being summarized, filtered, and interpreted by intelligent agents before a consumer ever sees it. In this session, R/GA explores the rise of Intelligent Brand Systems, an operating model that unifies brand intelligence, adaptive experiences, and generative storytelling. When brand strategy becomes living logic rather than static guidelines, it can power experiences that respond in real time and stories that generate at scale. Brands stop acting as messages and start functioning as systems that create relevance, value, and growth. The choice is simple: build your brand as a system that can think for itself, or become the brand that AI filters out.

title: Angela Moneke (Film Production + Curation)
description: Angela is a BIFA-nominated and BAFTA-longlisted producer from South London. She started as a factual TV researcher before moving to Working Title Films, where she is now Vice President of Production covering film and TV credits including POLITE SOCIETY and THE SUBSTANCE. As a producer, her short films ESSEX GIRLS (BFI) and MUNA (BBC Film) have played at Sundance, Berlinale and London Film Festival. She is developing her debut feature for BBC Film and Working Title. Angela is also co-founder of T A P E, a curatorial film collective focused on identity, heritage and culture, which has collaborated with the BFI, Picturehouse and Sheffield DocFest. She was featured in Forbes 30 Under 30 2022 and T A P E were named Film London Lodestars 2022. Information for mentees: - In order to book a session with one of our mentors, please download the SXSW London app. - Please arrive 5 minutes early to check in for your mentor session. Late arrivals (anyone not present at the start of their booked session) may lose their slot to a walk-up attendee. - If a session is fully booked, you re welcome to try as a walk-up in case a booked attendee doesn t attend. - We would recommend preparing a brief introduction about yourself, along with any specific questions or areas you d like guidance on from your mentor.

title: Bettina Kadoorie (Filmmaking + Philanthropy)
description: Bettina Kadoorie is a filmmaker and philanthropist based in London. She earned her MFA in Screenwriting, Directing and Producing from Columbia University in 2017 and has worked on films from Cambodia to Colombia. In 2019, she produced The Short History of the Long Road. Her short Santiaguera travelled to festivals in 2020. Recent films include Cielo, which has played at over 30 festivals and releases in US cinemas Summer 2026, and executive producing Claire Denis The Fence (2025) and upcoming documentaries Orca (2026) and Earth Elders (2026). Information for mentees: - In order to book a session with one of our mentors, please download the SXSW London app. - Please arrive 5 minutes early to check in for your mentor session. Late arrivals (anyone not present at the start of their booked session) may lose their slot to a walk-up attendee. - If a session is fully booked, you re welcome to try as a walk-up in case a booked attendee doesn t attend. - We would recommend preparing a brief introduction about yourself, along with any specific questions or areas you d like guidance on from your mentor.

title: Carmen Thompson (Film Distribution + Festival Programming)
description: Carmen is a film programmer, distributor and cultural producer based in Edinburgh. She is Head of Distribution Special Projects at We Are Parable and an International Features Programmer at Hot Docs. With over a decade of experience across exhibition, festivals, sales and distribution, her work centres on Black, African and diaspora storytelling with a focus on documentary. She has worked with Sheffield DocFest, Red Sea International Film Festival and Durban FilmMart. She is deputy chair of BIFA and a BAFTA and European Film Academy voting member. Speak with her about festival strategy, audience building for marginalised communities, alternative distribution models, and documentary projects from idea to release. title: The Road to Universal World Simulators
description: The hardest problems facing humanity are rooted in physical reality. To move beyond existing human knowledge, we need to learn directly from raw observations of the world around us. Anastasis Germanidis will detail Runway s vision for the Universal World Simulator a foundational technology that transcends creative entertainment to address our most pressing challenges spanning the realms of robotics and medicine to climate science. This discussion will uncover how world models representing the accessible intersection of art and science may offer the key to unlocking our next frontier of discovery.

title: Zumba Taster
hall: Dray Walk Gallery - Zumba House, Class

title: Revising in Perpetuity
description: What happens when a brand wants to turn a broadcast ad with a star athlete into an endless stream of AI-generated sequels? In this mock negotiation, four legal experts step into character: the athlete, the athlete s lawyer, the brand s CMO, and the brand s General Counsel. They ll clash over rights and compensation in a deal where synthetic likeness meets sports celebrity. The deal will unfold in real time, and the audience will learn about the intersection of AI innovation and NIL (name, image, and likeness) rights and how the future of marketing is being reshaped by companies and talent.

title: What Happens When Young People Stop Asking for Permission?
description: At a time when society is being reshaped by new technologies, shifting cultural norms, and growing distrust in traditional institutions, young people are increasingly stepping forward as changemakers, creating solutions, building communities, and driving social impact in real time. As the official charity partner of SXSW London, The Diana Award brings together inspiring young leaders alongside voices from media and culture for a bold conversation about the future of leadership, influence, and social change. Together, the panel will explore how a new generation is rewiring power by using creativity, activism, entrepreneurship, and community action to tackle the issues shaping their futures, from mental health and inequality to online harms and social division. This session celebrates the power of young people not only as future leaders, but as changemakers are already transforming society today, and asks what institutions, businesses, and audiences can learn from the way they lead.

title: Angela Moneke (Film Production + Curation)
description: Angela is a BIFA-nominated and BAFTA-longlisted producer from South London. She started as a factual TV researcher before moving to Working Title Films, where she is now Vice President of Production covering film and TV credits including POLITE SOCIETY and THE SUBSTANCE. As a producer, her short films ESSEX GIRLS (BFI) and MUNA (BBC Film) have played at Sundance, Berlinale and London Film Festival. She is developing her debut feature for BBC Film and Working Title. Angela is also co-founder of T A P E, a curatorial film collective focused on identity, heritage and culture, which has collaborated with the BFI, Picturehouse and Sheffield DocFest. She was featured in Forbes 30 Under 30 2022 and T A P E were named Film London Lodestars 2022. title: Building Brands that People Actually Talk About
description: Discover how the most memorable brands turn customers into passionate fans. Learn the secrets of creating buzz, shareable campaigns, and cultural relevance. Jamie Laing shares real-world strategies for building brands people can t stop talking about.

title: Music Groupchat: Leadership in Music
description: Rotating tables, rotating perspectives. One hour of honest executive exchange. From building resilient teams to championing emerging talent, fostering creative risk taking and shaping the future of the industry. Join label heads, festival directors, and senior executives for uplifting conversations on decision making, mentorship, and the joy of growing the next generation of music leaders. Real stories. Real support. A shared commitment to leading with purpose and possibility.

title: Noa Nwande (International Sales + Festival Management)
description: Noa Nwande is an International Sales Festival Manager at Watermelon Pictures, where she handles a slate of acclaimed documentary and feature films, including Academy Awards shortlisted titles, with a focus on projects by underrepresented filmmakers. title: Screen Roundtable: How to Release a Music Video
description: Beyond online and social media platforms - where do music videos go? With some film festivals and aficionados, there is still a desire and concentrated effort to experience music videos on big screens and beyond. Join this roundtable led by artists and producers on the best practices of working with labels and musicians on keeping the art of directing music videos alive. After the pitch, the edit, and the mix - how do you release a music video to the masses in today s landscape?

title: Breaking News: Is Your Anchor Now a Creator?
description: Media isn t dying, it s fragmenting, and the most interesting voices are the ones who ve figured out how to operate across all of it. Creators are breaking stories, setting cultural agendas, and earning trust in ways that traditional formats are still catching up to. The lines between journalist, creator, and cultural voice have never been more blurred, or more important to understand.

title: Reclaiming AI for the Public Good
description: Today s AI systems are not only shaped by algorithms, code, data, and capital. They are shaped by the imagined futures of the people building them. This session explores how dominant AI sociotechnical narratives and imaginaries, particularly those rooted in elite fears of societal collapse, national security, and technological pessimism, quietly influence what AI is built, who it serves, and which harms are prioritized or ignored. Drawing on real-world examples from AI development, governance debates, and workforce pipelines, this talk explores how sociotechnical imaginaries shape assumptions about the future that become embedded in technology itself. The session reframes AI not as a system to be managed by an elite minority, but as a social project that must be shaped by many, lest it ultimately serve only a homogenous few.

title: Techno-Feudalism is Here. Who Are the Lords?
description: Power is shifting from governments to platforms, from voters to algorithms. As AI, cloud infrastructure, and surveillance capitalism reshape the global order, a small group of tech leaders and companies now wield influence once reserved for nation states. This session explores who really holds power in the digital age and what it means for democracy, accountability, and the future of society.

title: Your Free Pass to the Future of TV: Rakuten TV, AMC, & the FAST Channel Revolution
description: Ever wondered how to get more of the content you love, for free? Join Rakuten TV CEO, C dric Dufour, and Lucy Halliday, VP International Business Development for AMC Global Media in this short, focused on-stage conversation to discover how FAST (Free-Ad-supported Streaming TV) channels are offering an exciting new world of entertainment choices without a subscription. This session will look at FAST in practice: why broadcasters are moving into FAST now, what actually performs with audiences, and how data led distribution partnerships are changing the rules. Drawing on Rakuten TV s FAST platform experience across Europe and AMCNI UK s FAST launches across TRUE CRIME and AMC branded channels, the discussion explores how premium, IPled factual content reaches broader, younger audiences, and why FAST is proving additive rather than cannibalistic for traditional broadcasters. With exclusive insights from new research along with selected audience data from Rakuten TV, the session offers a clear, consumer friendly snapshot of how free streaming is expanding reach, driving discovery and opening up new opportunities for content owners.

title: Making History in Real-Time: Orchestrating the Agentic Future
description: The industry has reached a shift change where technology is finally capable of replacing manual legacy craft at enterprise scale. But while the industry remains stuck in theoretical hype, the world s most ambitious brands are already moving beyond experimentation to technical orchestration. This session brings together the architects of the AI economy to discuss the collapse of the traditional marketing supply chain to explore how the best of one model is resolving enterprise complexity. From the 65% efficiency gains seen in content production to the 70% faster turnarounds in AI-native systems, we go inside the data and the code to reveal the new economic standard of brand building. In this market, you are either orchestrating the future in real-time, or you are becoming history.

title: From Listeners to Fans: Community-Driven Podcasting
description: Join the minds behind The Fellas Studios, as they dive into how they have leveraged communities across multiple titles, building one of the most successful products in the UK scene. Now under the Global umbrella, we ll hear how Cal and Elliot are planning on taking their basement set, to the world.

title: Angela Moneke (Film Production + Curation)
description: Angela is a BIFA-nominated and BAFTA-longlisted producer from South London. She started as a factual TV researcher before moving to Working Title Films, where she is now Vice President of Production covering film and TV credits including POLITE SOCIETY and THE SUBSTANCE. As a producer, her short films ESSEX GIRLS (BFI) and MUNA (BBC Film) have played at Sundance, Berlinale and London Film Festival. She is developing her debut feature for BBC Film and Working Title. Angela is also co-founder of T A P E, a curatorial film collective focused on identity, heritage and culture, which has collaborated with the BFI, Picturehouse and Sheffield DocFest. She was featured in Forbes 30 Under 30 2022 and T A P E were named Film London Lodestars 2022. title: Deconstructing the Music Exhibition – Sound, Vision and Creativity at the V&A.
description: tbc

title: Dugong Jr
description: Melbourne / Naarm artist Dugong Jr blends house, hip hop and cinematic textures into a genre-bending electronic sound shaped by his Chinese-Australian heritage. Known for crafting lush, soulful productions that sit between club music and songwriting, Dugong Jr has quickly established himself as one of Australia s most exciting emerging electronic artists. In 2024 he released his debut album Good Time Hotel, which received widespread global support from the likes of Triple J, Majestic Casual, BBC 1Xtra, ABC Australia + more, which currently boasts over 3 Million combined streams. In 2025, Dugong Jr signed with Island Records, releasing his label debut Patience (feat. Jordan Dennis Connie Diiamond) which received triple j rotation while the music video was selected as RAGE s ABC s Featured Video of the week .

title: Gannesh Rajah (Documentary + Producing)
description: Gannesh Rajah is a BAFTA-longlisted and multiple BIFA-winning producer. His first feature documentary, IF THE STREETS WERE ON FIRE, premiered at BFI London Film Festival, was acquired by BBC Storyville, and won two BIFAs including Best Feature Documentary. He produced short documentary PUFFLING, which premiered at SXSW, qualified for the Academy Awards, was acquired by The New Yorker, and selected for 60+ festivals. He is a DOC SOCIETY FELLOW, BFI INSIGHT PRODUCER, Sheffield Doc Fest Future Producer, BAFTA CONNECT member, Film London Lodestar, and part of Film London s Breaking the Glass Ceiling cohort mentored by Barbara Broccoli. title: NL House: The Netherlands Film Commission and the British Film Commission present: Crossing Borders
description: Creative and innovative audiovisual collaboration between the UK and the Netherlands How has co-producing between the United Kingdom and the Netherlands evolved in the wake of major political, economic, and industry shifts since 2020? And what new opportunities are emerging? In this dynamic panel, producers and filmmakers from both countries reflect on the cultural and strategic landscape shaping collaboration between our neighbouring industries. The Netherlands Film Fund is the national cultural fund for the professional, independent film sector in the Netherlands and the Caribbean parts of the Kingdom. It finances films that contribute to a varied and distinctive offering of Dutch cinema for diverse audiences. The British Film Commission is the national agency with a remit to maximise and support the production of international feature films and television in the UK. This session is part of NL House where Dutch ambition meets a global audience on June 3 at SXSW London. For the full overview of sessions and experiences, visit NL House on Luma . (Non-badge holders can apply for entry by signing up via the Luma website)

title: NL Lounge Pitch Session 2
description: Presented by ROM Utrecht Region and the Embassy of the Kingdom of the Netherlands in London, this pitch session will showcase four innovative Dutch startups at the forefront of emerging technology and entrepreneurship. Discover cutting-edge ideas, connect with founders, and explore opportunities for collaboration between the Dutch and international innovation ecosystems. Followed by more in-personal demo s after the pitches. Join us for short presentations and a live Q A with the following four Dutch startups. Hosted by: Berend Casper , ROM Utrecht Region Danna Mulya , CEO Podego A startup using AI to create personalised audio guides for museums and cultural venues, available in 30+ languages and multiple storytelling styles. Floris Smit, Founder IX Producer, UpscaleXR A production house specialising in large-scale XR for live events and cultural institutions the only European organisation consistently running 100 to 300 simultaneous headsets. Roger Oliveira , Co-Founder Ver.ID Reinventing online identity: from simple age checks to official business verification, access any credentials you need straight from your customer s identity app. Ren van Engelenburg , Artistic General Director Dropstuff Media A pioneer in immersive media design, creating interactive public installations and experiences that blend new technology with storytelling for festivals, museums, and cultural organisations. This session is part of NL House where Dutch ambition meets a global audience on June 3 at SXSW London. For the full overview of sessions and experiences, visit NL House on Luma . Non-badge holders can apply for entry by signing up via the Luma website.

title: Naomi Simon
description: From the North to the heart of London, Naomi is carving out a unique space in the music scene with her soulful fusion of Neo-soul, R B, and Jazz drawing inspiration from artists like Shae Universe, Erykah Badu, Yebba, and RAYE. Naomi s captivating voice has filled stadiums, offering a soulful twist to the national anthem for crowds of over 85,000 and shared the stage with luminaries such as RAYE, Beverly Knight, and Skye Newman as part of the Flames Collective Choir. Witness Naomi s blend of rhythm and soul as she seeks to connect with listeners through her heartfelt lyricism

title: Noa Nwande (International Sales + Festival Management)
description: Noa Nwande is an International Sales Festival Manager at Watermelon Pictures, where she handles a slate of acclaimed documentary and feature films, including Academy Awards shortlisted titles, with a focus on projects by underrepresented filmmakers. She works across international distribution and festival positioning, collaborating with distributors, broadcasters, and platforms worldwide. They previously worked at STUDIOCANAL. title: Screen Meet-Up with BAFTA Games
description: BAFTA is an independent arts charity. Supporting careers, driving progressive industry culture, celebrating excellence and inspiring the future of film, games and TV. BAFTA will be at SXSW London as an Official Meet-Up Partner. Members can join us for an afternoon of networking and socialising with industry professionals, we especially welcome anyone working in Games.

title: Startup Showcase
description: The Startup Showcase gives music tech founders from around the world seven minutes to demonstrate what they re building - followed by live Q A from an expert panel. Fast-paced and product-focused, it offers a live cross-section of what the global music tech ecosystem is building right now - from early-stage tools to companies already operating at scale. Speakers: Rachel Lyske, Advisory Board, MTUK (Host) Michael Rohde B wadt, CEO, SoundInvest (Expert Panelist) Maciej Dulski, MD, Music Tech Lab (Expert Panelist) Christina Hummer, Investor, Best Nights VC (Expert Panelist) Xavier Tumminello, Innovation Project Manager, Centre National de la Musique (Expert Panelist) Remi Agostini, CEO, Mewo (Startup Founder) Hans Ascrawat, Founder / Sales Partnerships, AndR (Startup Founder) Emanuele Sanfelici, CEO Founder, HAT Music (Startup Founder) Kerry Adams, Director/Founder, Cactus Innovation (Startup Founder)

title: The £22 Trillion Blind Spot: Why CMOs Can’t Afford to Ignore the Longevity Generation
description: The longevity economy is one of the biggest commercial opportunities being overlooked by modern marketing. This SXSW London panel brings together leaders across culture, social media, beauty and strategy to unpack why the 55+ audience has become one of the most influential, affluent and culturally engaged generations, YET it still remains significantly underrepresented in advertising, innovation and brand strategy. Grounded in the work of Edelman s Longevity Lab and insights from the Power of 55 list, the session explores how brands can move beyond outdated anti-ageing narratives and instead build aspirational, inclusive campaigns that reflect the realities of modern ageing. Designed for a CMO and senior marketing audience, the panel combines cultural insight, commercial evidence and real-world marketing lessons to help leaders rethink growth, relevance and audience strategy for the next decade.

title: Zumba Taster
hall: Dray Walk Gallery - Zumba House, Class

title: We Didn’t Come Alone
description: What does it mean to build a creative practice rooted in where you re from while shaping where you are? In this conversation, Foday Dumbuya (LABRUM London) and Yinka Ilori explore how their West African heritage and immigrant upbringings have become the foundation of their design languages. From storytelling through textiles and furniture to public installations and global collaborations, both designers reflect on how culture travels through memory, material, and community. Over the past five years, they ve each built great support networks across London and beyond not just as individuals, but as part of a wider diaspora ecosystem.This talk unpacks identity, collaboration, and the responsibility of representation asking how designers/Artist can create work that is both deeply personal and universally resonant. Because none of us arrived here alone.
