title: Why Live Music Still Wins
description: Live music is thriving, but it s also facing pressures. Alex Hill, President CEO of AEG International, explores the challenges shaping the industry today - and why, despite them, live music remains strong, resilient, and future-proof.

title: GTM as a Creative Act
description: The Wall Street Journal says corporate America is in an AI dread era office culture stripped of fun, perks cut, workloads ballooning, and the joy and magic required to build iconic companies, gone. The culprit, ironically, is the same technology that was supposed to make work better. In this session, Varun Anand will delve into how Clay is building the antidote.

title: Reimagining Scientific Discovery in the Age of AI
description: How do we unlock the full potential of AI as an instrument for scientific discovery? Pushmeet Kohli, VP - AI for Science, Google DeepMind, and Kathleen Fisher, CEO, ARIA, will offer distinct perspectives on the unique opportunity of AI for science and what is required to facilitate breakthrough discoveries at scale. Their conversation will consider the opportunities and challenges ahead, the long-term planning that fuels breakthrough research, and the UK s unique positioning to build upon this unique technological moment.

title: GIFTHORSE
description: GIFTHORSE gallop in from North London town, with their self-proclaimed frazzled English Pop , fizzing with anthemic guitars, colourful synths and Naomi Mann s perkily bubblesome vocals. Fronted by the songwriting partnership of Mann and guitarist Charlie Butler, the group s debut EP, Queens of Highgate , has received stunning reviews from the likes of Far Out, Clash and Under The Radar and enjoyed airplay on Radio X, BBC 6 Music and BBC London. With their observational lyric-writing, sharp aesthetic and joyous performance style, the group has gained a cult live following and this summer they host their own frazzled English Summer residency at the Elephant s Head in Camden. In July, GIFTHORSE will perform on Tim Burgess stage at Kendal Calling before touring with iconic 80s band Altered Images in the autumn. They continue to add to their growing songbook with the release of new single Friday s Girl on July 10th, with more music to follow.

title: We The People Means ALL of Us
description: What does it mean to be Global at a moment when democracy, identity, and belonging are being renegotiated in real time? As the United States approaches its 250th anniversary, debates over immigration, reproductive freedom, LGBTQ+ rights, free expression, and democracy itself are reshaping the meaning of We the People. Join Padma Lakshmi and the American Civil Liberties Union s Jessica Herman Weitz for a conversation about how culture and authentic storytelling can expand empathy, challenge exclusion, and help build a more inclusive vision of America s future.

title: Can a Planet be Intelligent?
description: What happens when a planet develops a species capable of altering its own climate and biosphere? David Grinspoon explores the idea of planetary intelligence and asks whether humanity might become the first civilization to consciously manage a planet.

title: How to Launch a Pod in 30 Days
description: From idea to airtime in just 30 days what s vital? Content? Branding? Set? Format? Distribution? Guests? Join our experts as we breakdown just how you go from idea to multi channel pod and what areas you can skip and what is vital from the jump.

title: KV!TKA
description: KV!TKA is a four-piece alternative band based in London. Drawing inspiration from Sonic Youth, Slint, and Cocteau Twins, KV!TKA mixes ethereal textures with abrasive noise. Their sound merges soft, haunting vocals with distorted guitars and atmospheric melodica to create a unique contrast. Formed in 2025, the band has quickly built momentum, performing across multiple London venues (Elephants Head, Old Dispensary) and touring to Manchester (Caf Blah). They are currently recording their debut EP and plan to release soon.

title: Frontlines & Headlines: How to Protect Our Press
description: The world we live in is one of hostility, division, violence and uncertainty. We re aware of this thanks to the bravery and steadfastness of journalists and their work across the world. So why aren t governments doing more to protect them? Over 500 journalists have been killed on-duty since 2020. 1000s more continue to be censored or impeded when they re just trying to do their jobs. This has to stop. But how?

title: Albion VC's Rising Stars in Deep Tech
description: Three founders. Three very different bets on the future of technology. James Regan is CEO of Oriole Networks, a UCL spinout using photonics to accelerate AI and machine learning in a low-carbon world, tackling the data centre bottlenecks that are holding the AI revolution back. Mingtian Zhang founded Vectify AI to build the next generation of RAG systems, with its open-source PageIndex framework rethinking how AI retrieves information entirely. Tom Heenan co-founded Gaussion, applying an external magnetic field to existing battery cells to unlock rapid charging and slash degradation, backed by AlbionVC through the UCL Technology Fund. Hosted by Sebastian Hunte, AlbionVC s Deep Tech Director, this session gets into what it actually takes to build and scale deep tech today: the shared grind of moving science from lab to market, the moments where VC support made the difference, and why the UK deep tech ecosystem might be more formidable than the world realises.

title: Roundtable: DO LOOK UP - when Creators and Out of Home unite
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: Roundtable: Who Will Own Trust in the Creator Economy? Why Expertise Beats Reach in the Algorithmic Age
description: Roundtables are topic-led, small-group discussions hosted by our Leads, offering an intimate, peer-to-peer space where participants of all levels including beginners can engage, learn, and exchange ideas more directly.
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: Roundtable: Writing in the Age of AI: Doing It Well, Not Just Doing It
description: There s a growing gap between using AI to produce words and using it to produce good writing. This roundtable cuts through the hype and the anxiety: when to use AI in your writing process, and how to do it in a way that sharpens your voice rather than flattening it. Expect candid conversation about real workflows and what it actually means to stay in creative control.
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: Support on the Sidelines: How Football Can Save Lives
description: How did broadcaster Roman Kemp join forces with the Premier League to support suicide prevention? Motivated by personal experience, two influential documentaries, and the reality that men account for three quarters of all reported suicides, Roman partnered with Samaritans and the Premier League to launch Together Against Suicide. The initiative creates safe, stigma free spaces for fans to access mental health support on match days. This panel explores its impact across football communities, what the sport has learned, and the work still needed to ensure players, staff, and supporters receive meaningful support on and off the pitch.

title: Gaia
description: GA A makes neo-soul straight from the source. She is a French singer-songwriter based in Glasgow, blending nu-jazz, R B, and neo-soul. She began her artistic journey in Glasgow s vibrant grassroots jazz scene listening, learning, and developing her craft eventually gaining recognition through local jam sessions and collaborations. Deeply influenced by Black music, GA A draws on her mixed heritage to create a genre-fluid sound rooted in soul, hip-hop, and R B. Her songwriting explores identity, belonging, and self-expression, shaped by both personal experience and community. In 2023 and 2024, GA A was nominated for back-to-back Scottish Jazz Awards, highlighting her growing impact within the Glasgow Jazz scene. Since debuting her project, she has supported a diverse range of artists including Bemz, Theon Cross, Brian Jackson, and corto.alto, and performed at Scottish festivals such as Celtic Connections, Eden, Kelburn, Houseguest, and both Glasgow and Edinburgh Jazz Festivals. Earlier this year, GA A released her first mini-project, live in my living room a self-produced live session series filmed in her flat in the southside of Glasgow. The project featured many of her close collaborators and offered a raw, intimate insight into her sound and process. It also secured her first national radio plays on BBC Introducing Scotland and BBC Radio 6, marking a new phase in her visibility as an artist. GA A continues to build her presence in the soul and rnb scene, always remaining grounded in collaboration and creative exchange. She is now focused on expanding her artistic vision and reaching new audiences across Scotland and beyond. This next stage represents a significant step in her evolution as an artist, rooted in community but aiming outward toward broader recognition.

title: NL House: World's Firsts — Building innovation that actually matters
description: From brainwave communication to rewriting the landscape of innovation partnerships. A conversation with Florian Root and Joeri Bakker of Dentsu Lab on where to look for opportunities to maximise the potential of your innovation. These two are no strangers to rethinking how industries operate and building valuable innovations for humanity. Always looking for opportunities to create projects that didn t exist before someone decided they should. Florian Root and Joeri Bakker of Dentsu Lab don t talk about innovation in the abstract. They talk about what it actually takes: the right partnerships, the right timing, and the discipline to stay focused on human need when the technology wants to be the story. This session is part of NL House where Dutch ambition meets a global audience on June 3 at SXSW London. For the full overview of sessions and experiences, visit NL House on Luma . title: Zumba Taster
hall: Dray Walk Gallery - Zumba House, Class

title: My Agent, My Rules
description: This session addresses the next big trend in AI innovation: AI agents. Agents remain a nascent technology, and the opportunities and risks they present are not well understood. Cross-community engagement is needed to develop greater understanding about what a responsible approach to building and deploying AI agents looks like, and how best to ensure user safety, privacy and agency in this new context. The discussion will then consider how to embed user-centricity in the building and deploying of agentic products. How should companies govern what data agents should have access to? How do we manage the trade-off between privacy and effectiveness? How can we balance agentic autonomy with rigorous oversight and end-user control?

title: Wilbe's Rising Stars in Deep Tech
description: Join Wilbe COO Devika alongside three of deep tech s most compelling rising stars: Anshika Srivastava of Apoha, Assia Kasdi of Milvus Advanced, and Jordan Abdi of U-Ploid. For an honest, unfiltered conversation about what it really takes to build and scale a deep tech company in the UK. In just 20 minutes on the Protein Studios stage, this session goes beyond the pitch deck to explore the founder journey from the inside: the breakthroughs, the hard decisions, and the critical role that the right investor and incubator relationships play in turning a bold scientific idea into a scalable business. Whether you re a venture or angel investor looking for your next conviction bet in deep tech, or a founder navigating the early stages of building something extraordinary, this session offers rare, candid insight from three founders who are doing it right now, in real time.

title: Lola Rose
description: Lola Rose is an indie folk singer-songwriter, creating songs rooted from personal experience and shared struggles. She believes that honesty in music can create connection and understanding between her and her listeners. She s focused on finding beauty in her vulnerability, and sharing her stories with others.

title: Creative AI, Inclusive Growth: How the UK Avoids a Dual-track Future that Benefits the Few
description: The UK stands at a pivotal moment in the global AI race. While the potential for economic growth is immense, there s a critical need to ensure these benefits are broadly distributed. For AI adoption to truly be successful for the UK, it must be right for its 5.7 million small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and its vibrant creative sector. Without their full participation, the promise of AI risks benefiting a select few. This panel will reflect on how the UK can strategically harness AI to drive health, happiness and progress for the nation. We will focus on the unique opportunities and challenges for creative industries and, crucially, for start-ups and SMEs. We will also delve into transforming workforce skills and fostering social mobility, to prevent a dual-track economy where only the largest firms and best-educated workers thrive. We ll examine the shared accountability required from government, industry, and education to build an inclusive AI future.

title: The Digital Playground
description: Children are growing up in a world shaped by social media, gaming platforms, and always-on connectivity. In this conversation, experts explore the challenges of online safety for young people in the UK , from regulation and platform responsibility to education and parental support, and what must happen next to create safer digital spaces for the next generation.

title: Screen Roundtable: How to Make a Million Look Like 5 Million
description: Colourists are a powerful weapon when it comes to making your film not only look great, but feel like it was made for tenfold the original budget. At a time when audiences are watching more and more films on small devices, colour feels even more important to transmit a director s vision faithfully across all formats. Join specialists in this panel to talk all things colour in film from both a colourist and a director perspective.

title: Tess Kendall (Independent Label + Global Marketing)
description: Tess Kendall has over 20 years of experience in the independent music sector, starting at Glastonbury s Dance Tent and Ministry of Sound s tour department. She set up and ran Defected Records events business and booking agency, then moved to Berlin to work at !K7 Records. She is now Managing Director of Marketing Operations at Ninja Tune Records, where she has been for 12 years, overseeing global campaigns for artists including Bicep, Young Fathers, ODESZA, Bonobo and The Cinematic Orchestra. title: The Long-Form Shift for Short-Form Creators
description: As creators outgrow short-form platforms like TikTok, many are turning to podcasts to build deeper connections with audiences. Industry leaders discuss what drives the shift from viral clips to long-form content and what it means for the creator economy.

title: Toks Dada
description: Toks Dada is Head of Classical Music at Southbank Centre, leading the UK s largest year-round classical music programme. He founded Sinfonia Newydd in Wales in 2010 and previously worked at Town Hall Symphony Hall in Birmingham. He was Guest Curator of Wonderfeel 2022, has served as a Board Director at Welsh National Opera and Sinfonia Cymru, and was a Fellow of the International Society for the Performing Arts (2020-2025). He holds a Masters in Arts Management and a Bachelor of Music from the Royal Welsh College of Music Drama, where he is an Honorary Associate. title: Yasmin Leung (Global Marketing + Artist Development)
description: Yasmin Leung is Director of International Marketing at All Flowers Group, with over a decade of experience across music campaigns in the UK, Europe, Asia-Pacific, the Middle East and Africa. Based in London, she leads international strategy for Ghostly International and drink sum wtr, working with artists including Annahstasia, Dua Saleh, Yaya Bey and Ginger Root. She previously held roles at Secretly Group and Domino Recordings, and led Digital Marketing at All Tomorrow s Parties. She is an active member of ESEA Music. title: InnovateUK's Rising Stars in Deep Tech
description: Three founders. Three breakthroughs. One question: what does it actually take to build a deep tech company in the UK? From adaptive AI spinning out of a nuclear fusion lab, to a wearable quantum brain scanner, to microfluidic technology transforming IVF success rates Matt Carr (Luffy AI), David Woolger (Cerca Magnetics) and Virginia Pensabene (IVFmicro) are turning cutting-edge research into real-world impact. All three have worked with Innovate UK to get there. Chaired by Sarah El-Hanfy, Director of AI and Digital at Innovate UK, this session cuts to the honest realities of deep tech entrepreneurship in the UK the funding gaps, the lab-to-market leap, and the moments where the right support made the difference.

title: Attractions That Think: How AI is transforming static venues into living, responsive experiences
description: Attraction venues have long been designed as static, one-way experiences but what happens when they can think, respond, and evolve in real time? This session explores how artificial intelligence is transforming museums, exhibitions, and immersive spaces into dynamic environments that can adapt to every visitor. Merlin Entertainment, who are at the forefront of bringing innovative attractions to audiences partnered with new, up and coming AI company TellMe through the support provided by Digital Catapult on the BridgeAI programme to build a visitor attraction experience and test the use case around TellMe s technology.

title: Gaza's Twins, Come Back to Me
description: Director: Mohammed Sawwaf A mother fights to be reunited with her infant twins as the war in Gaza escalates As the war in Gaza escalates, Rania goes into high-risk labour with triplets. One girl dies at birth. Siblings Jowan and Hamoud survive, but acute health complications lead to a need for in-hospital care. When their hospital is evacuated, the infant twins are moved to southern Gaza, while Rania is trapped by travel bans in place in the north. Mohammed Sawwaf s deeply moving doc chronicles a mother s plight to be reunited with her children amongst the destruction of war, while also exposing the cruelty of withholding basic healthcare for families at times of unspeakable violence. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Disruptive, New Media in the Age of Misinformation
description: Power over truth and narrative has dispersed across new platforms driven by social media, algorithms, AI, and a rapidly shifting information economy. At a moment when the world urgently needs rigorous, independent journalism, many establishment and corporate media outlets are failing to meet the public interest. That s why Jeremy Scahill, Ryan Grim, and Nausicaa Renner founded Drop Site News: a reader-funded investigative newsroom dedicated to exposing the crimes of the powerful, particularly in overt and covert conflicts where the U.S. government plays a central role. In less than two years, Drop Site has grown to more than 700,000+ subscribers, building a fiercely loyal community of readers who don t just consume journalism they fund it directly. This panel examines how Drop Site is challenging the broken economics of legacy media, why audiences are increasingly abandoning traditional outlets, and what the rise of reader-funded investigative journalism means for the future of journalism in the pursuit of truth.

title: Anthony Lee (Project Management + Artist Development)
description: Anthony Lee is a Marketing and Project Manager with over 10 years of experience across the music industry. He has worked at AWAL, BMG, Sony Music, and currently with music therapy not-for-profit Nordoff and Robbins. Speak with him about building out a world, playing to your strengths, and creating long-term connections with fans. He currently serves as Chair of the Association of Independent Promoters (AIP), supporting the next generation of promoters and strengthening the grassroots music ecosystem. He has also consulted for the Music Venue Trust (MVT), contributing to advocacy for grassroots venues. He is committed to creating sustainable opportunities for emerging artists, promoters and venues. title: Mimi Itseli (Music Journalism + Brand Strategy)
description: Mimi The Music Blogger is a London-based music journalist, host and analyst covering UK and global music culture. She has written for Complex UK, Clash, Dazed, Red Bull Music and Mixtape Madness, and contributed to BBC Radio 1Xtra. She has appeared on panels for Apple Music, Amazon Music and JBL. She won Journalist of the Year at the Young Music Boss Awards in 2022 and 2023 and was named to the Dazed 100 in 2023. Since 2025, she has worked as a Creative Strategist at Ogilvy, bridging media and brand strategy with cultural precision. His principal management client is genre-blending artist Berry Galazka. Trained as a Social Worker and having worked in the charity sector, he found a creative outlet writing for Clash and The Line of Best Fit. He is a member of the Music Managers Forum and MOBO Awards voting member, and has worked with grassroots organisations including iLuvLive and Limitless Live. Find him @PJSomervelle. Now with ten years at Kobalt Publishing, she has worked with a diverse international roster across the US, South Korea, Nigeria and the UK, supporting clients with AV production, copyright acquisition and genre-specific expertise including Afrobeats and Funk. She is now Managing Director of Marketing Operations at Ninja Tune Records, where she has been for 12 years, overseeing global campaigns for artists including Bicep, Young Fathers, ODESZA, Bonobo and The Cinematic Orchestra. title: Toks Dada
description: Toks Dada is Head of Classical Music at Southbank Centre, leading the UK s largest year-round classical music programme. He founded Sinfonia Newydd in Wales in 2010 and previously worked at Town Hall Symphony Hall in Birmingham. He was Guest Curator of Wonderfeel 2022, has served as a Board Director at Welsh National Opera and Sinfonia Cymru, and was a Fellow of the International Society for the Performing Arts (2020-2025). He holds a Masters in Arts Management and a Bachelor of Music from the Royal Welsh College of Music Drama, where he is an Honorary Associate. description: Yasmin Leung is Director of International Marketing at All Flowers Group, with over a decade of experience across music campaigns in the UK, Europe, Asia-Pacific, the Middle East and Africa. Based in London, she leads international strategy for Ghostly International and drink sum wtr, working with artists including Annahstasia, Dua Saleh, Yaya Bey and Ginger Root. She previously held roles at Secretly Group and Domino Recordings, and led Digital Marketing at All Tomorrow s Parties. She is an active member of ESEA Music. title: Shorts: Brief Encounters
description: Fateful meetings and clandestine confrontations. Short films depicting the ways in which we cross paths. From roads, rodeos, saunas, trains or pools, our lives are populated by small, brief encounters in unlikely places. From the thralls of a dark Western obsession, to the self-examinings of a cyber cowboy. Waiting for a hunk to scoop us up from the bottom of a public pool, or realising the worst about your partner after being trapped in a private sauna. Our encounters with God, the Other, the unexplained, whether in the dense Cuban rainforest or a crowded French train. BRIEF ENCOUNTERS brings together the best in contemporary short films, asking a simple question: who might we meet next? Total Running Time : 88 min Content Warnings: Violence, implied animal cruelty, strong language, sex and nudity, distressing scenes, blood, alcohol use

title: Sylvie Bridge
description: Sylvie Bridge is an alternative artist carving out a space in experimental indie. Her upcoming EP, inspired by the cult film I Believe in Unicorns , was self-produced and showcases her commitment to elevating female voices in the studio. Her sound falls between the delicate, orchestral folk of Mutual Benefit and the guitar-driven energy of Wolf Alice and Japanese Breakfast. Sylvie delivers a sharp, cinematic exploration that feels both intimate and raw.

title: In Conversation with Todd Boehly
description: Todd L. Boehly, Co-founder, Chairman and CEO of Eldridge Industries, joins us for a conversation on investing, media, and building diversified global businesses. From private capital to sports and entertainment, Boehly shares insights on leadership, long-term strategy, and navigating opportunities in an increasingly complex economic landscape.

title: From Pitch to Platform: The Evolving Role of Talent in Sports Sponsorship
description: Join us as we explore how brands and agencies are forging authentic collaborations with athletes to create impactful sponsorship campaigns. The session will emphasise the importance of aligning partnerships with both the athlete s personal brand and the company s values. Our speakers will examine the role of athletes as storytellers, advocates and cultural influencers beyond their sport. We will talk about managing reputational risks and offer practical insights into maintaining trust and navigating challenges in this swiftly evolving space.

title: Venture Spotlight - Semi Final 1
description: The best university technology in Europe doesn t always make it out of the lab. Venture Spotlight is here to change that. The first ten companies. Three minutes each. One shot at the final. Spinning out of universities across Europe, these founders are working at the frontier of deep tech, life sciences, biotech, climate and creative technology, pitching live to a panel of judges for a place in Thursday s Grand Final and a shot at a 100,000 prize. University research has given the world penicillin, the World Wide Web, and mRNA vaccines. The next generation of world-changing technology is sitting in labs right now: underfunded and under-commercialised. If Europe is going to compete on the global technology stage, it starts here. Watch closely. The companies pitching today could be the ones that matter most tomorrow.

title: Anthony Lee (Project Management + Artist Development)
description: Anthony Lee is a Marketing and Project Manager with over 10 years of experience across the music industry. He has worked at AWAL, BMG, Sony Music, and currently with music therapy not-for-profit Nordoff and Robbins. Speak with him about building out a world, playing to your strengths, and creating long-term connections with fans. description: Ant is founder and director of Notion Live Events, an independent music promotion and consultancy company based in North East England. Since 2021, he has delivered 100+ shows annually across genres and venue sizes, from grassroots club shows to multi-venue festivals. He created A Stone s Throw Festival, a coastal multi-venue event for emerging artists in North Tyneside. Alongside promotion, he works with venues on programming, operations and long-term strategy. Speak with him about developing as an independent promoter, structuring deals and cutting through in a competitive industry. title: Beyond AI: the Secret of Staying Relevant
description: By 2030, 70% of skills may be obsolete, but a perspective based only on technology is a mistake. Human imagination is boundless, so why constrain it? Education expert Mary Curnock Cook, and prize-winning architect Simon Allford explore why lifelong learning is not just the price of survival - it s a superpower. Learn how to do more than, and understand how to beat the future.

description: Callum Johnson is an artist manager and strategic consultant across music and tech. His management roster includes Ashaine White (Marshall Records), Antigoni (Eurovision 2026) and looms. (lo behold), overseeing artist development, business management, commercial strategy and international growth. He also works at the intersection of commercial partnerships, developing a membership club for companies across entertainment and travel. He supports emerging and established talent while building bridges between music and high-growth sectors. description: Chlo Abrahams-Duperry is Senior Manager of Artist Promoter Services at AXS, overseeing ticketing strategy across the UK and Europe with venues, promoters and agents. Previously, she was Senior Artist Relations Manager at Ticketmaster Music. Across these roles, she has managed ticketing strategy for EMEA tours for artists including C line Dion, Bad Bunny, Kendrick Lamar, SZA, Billie Eilish, Karol G, Coldplay and Imagine Dragons. Earlier, she worked as a Booking Agent at Higher Ground in Berlin. Alongside her full-time role, she founded and curates LARMES, a quarterly showcase for emerging talent, and holds a residency on Voices Radio after five years at Refuge Worldwide. description: Keke Hammond is CEO of Road14 Studios, a creative, media and talent agency amplifying representation of the African child, and Chief Operating Officer of Flytime Promotions, the force behind Flytime Fest and Rhythm Unplugged, West Africa s longest-running music festival. With over 22 years of experience on stage and behind the scenes, she has driven Flytime s growth through operational efficiency and strategic vision. Under her leadership, the festival has expanded to a five-day experience, introduced disability access and on-stage seating, and launched The Future , a platform for rising stars aged 12-17. title: Klaverson
description: Klaverson creates cinematic downtempo music that blends soul, ambient electronica and textured live instrumentation into deeply immersive soundscapes. With over 50 million streams, the producer has earned support from BBC Radio 1, BBC Radio 6 Music, Spotify and BBC Introducing, while also performing DJ sets alongside artists including Thundercat and Gorillaz. Earlier this year, Klaverson debuted his live show at Spectaculare in Prague, marking a new chapter in the evolution of his atmospheric and emotionally driven live experience.

title: Krish Kudhail (Artist Marketing + Creative Strategy)
description: Krish Kudhail is an award-nominated creative marketing strategist with an MA in Music Business Management and over 9 years of experience across major and independent labels. She currently runs 11K Creative, working directly with labels and artists. She has worked at MTV, GRM Daily, Warner Music, Universal Music, Sony Music and Apple. She has led campaigns for Digga D, Rema, Ayra Starr, Potter Payper and K-Trap, achieving number one records, chart-topping singles, GRAMMY nominations and certifications. Earlier, she assisted campaigns for Coldplay, Gorillaz, Paul Weller and Lily Allen. title: Liv East
description: Liv East is East London songwriter bringing us a honeyed fusion of Neo-soul, House and R B. Off the back of a hugely successful breakout year in 2023 with her first solo EP LVE1 , Liv has spent the last 2 years creating her debut album which is set to drop in the autumn. She has been listed as one to watch by both Soho Rising s Brick Lane Jazz Festival s women in Jazz.

title: Music Groupchat: Inside The Minds of Live Programmers
description: From local clubs to major venues, hear the logic behind the lineup. How one yes changes a room s identity. How one no saves a month s budget. Join the people who actually build the bills, balancing buzz with balance sheets, artist egos with audience energy, and supporting local roots while chasing national moments. No booking myths. No armchair managing. Just the honest, thoughtful craft of filling rooms with purpose, night after night.

title: NL House: Make Them Feel Something: The Future of Experiential in a Scroll-Fatigued World
description: Audiences have never been harder to reach or more desperate for something real. As attention fragments and digital noise grows, the smartest brands are pouring budget back into live experience. But what separates an experience people post about from one they actually remember? This session unpacks the state of experiential marketing: what s working now, what s tired, and where it goes next. From global brand activations to grassroots cultural moments, we ll explore why physical presence is having its moment again, and what it takes to make audiences genuinely feel something. This session is part of NL House where Dutch ambition meets a global audience on June 3 at SXSW London. For the full overview of sessions and experiences, visit NL House on Luma . (Non-badge holders can apply for entry by signing up via the Luma website)

title: Psweatpants
description: Psweatpants is a 27 year-old artist from London, now based in Scotland, known for his sharp metaphors, conceptual delivery and crazy live performances. Drawing influence from Slowthai, Skepta, AJ Tracey, Vince Staples, JPEGMAFIA and Paris Texas, he blends UK grit with US edge. Nominated for Best Hip Hop in 2022 and 2024 at the Scottish Alternative Music Awards winning the latter as well as Best Live Act 2024, he s also been shortlisted for the Sound of Young Scotland and SMIA s Best Hip Hop Live Act. His music has been championed by BBC Radio 1, 1Xtra and BBC Introducing, where he featured in the 2023 Rap Cypher and earned Track of the Week slots. On stage, he s supported Bemz at a sold-out King Tut s, as well as Pinty, The Mouse Outfit, Nova, The Snuts, Declan Welsh, Master Peace, Vlure and Hak Baker, while headlining SWG3 in 2024 Aswell as being featured in the last iteration of the Fifa Football game (FC26). Poised as the next breakout voice from Scotland s scene, Psweatpants is an artist to watch.

title: Roundtable: A Female Founder's Guide to Getting Angel Investment
description: Less than 2% of UK venture capital goes to female founders, and The Rise Report of Female Entrepreneurship found that 45% say funding is their biggest challenge. Join Female Founders Rise Show cohosts Emmie Faust, exited founder and angel investor in 30+ female-led businesses, and Mae Yip, a founder who has raised over 1 million including angel investment, for a candid conversation on what s actually working when approaching angels, how to find them, how to work out what s right for you, and the lessons we ve learnt along the way. Whether you re curious about angel investment, preparing for your first raise, or stuck mid-round, come ready to share and leave with actionable insights. Come prepared: What feels like the biggest unknown or blocker in your fundraising journey right now, and what would help you move past it? Do you know what makes a business investable versus a good business that isn t right for investment, and which one is yours? How are you currently finding and reaching angel investors, and is it working?
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: Roundtable: How to Write a Book and Beat Procrastination
description: This round-table mentoring session focuses on the joys of writing a book and the barriers to getting it done: procrastination, self-doubt, overwhelm, lack of structure, and the challenge of turning ideas into a finished manuscript that someone will read. International author and book coach Susie Pearl shares a practical, structured approach to moving from concept to completion. Drawing on years of experience in helping aspiring writers, entrepreneurs, creatives, and leaders develop their ideas into publishable books, this session delivers clear frameworks, creative techniques, and momentum-building strategies. Susie has books published with major publishing houses on creativity collaboration wtih David Lynch, on happiness and a memoir and has helped others to get mainstream published and self-published. Topics include: Finding the core idea that makes a book compelling Structuring a book quickly and clearly Beating procrastination and creative resistance Writing with confidence and flow Turning expertise, experience, or story into a strong concept Traditional publishing vs. self-publishing vs. hybrid models Positioning a book to build brand, influence, and opportunity Participants gain practical insights, personalised guidance, and a clear path forward. The session is interactive, focused, and designed for immediate progress. Ideal for founders, creatives, thought leaders, professionals, and anyone ready to turn ideas into a finished book. This is a practical focussed session. Here you will get some powerful prompts and input to get your book done in a way that it will be ready for publishing. Real ideas. Real momentum. Real outcomes. Join for a fun session. All levels welcome from those starting out with an idea to those who have walked the road of writing a book and want some expert guidance and inspiration along the way.
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: Roundtable: Pandora's box: Going viral
description: In Contagion we predicted the pandemic 10 years before it happened. Even earlier, in 1999, a high level defense think tank convened plant, animal and computer virus hunters to discuss threats and solutions common to their systems. How can our experience with infectious diseases inform the way we think about the viral spread of misinformation? What s next and how do we contain it?
venue: Conference Roundtables at Truman Brewery's Half Press Room

title: The Internet’s Dumbest Shows Power the Smartest Business
description: Comedian and podcaster Fin Taylor, the man behind the internet s dumbest shows, sits down with Patreon COO Paige Fitzgerald to unpack how he built one of the fastest-growing, fan-funded podcast businesses out there. With 31K+ paying members, more than 4x growth in a year, and a new tour on the horizon, Fin has turned chaos, comedy, and internet culture into a thriving business on his own terms. Expect a candid, slightly unhinged look at the business of being online: from turning listeners into paying fans to building something that can actually hold up in today s volatile video and podcast economy.

title: The Invite
description: Director: Olivia Wilde Cast: Olivia Wilde, Seth Rogen, Penelope Cruz, Edward Norton Olivia Wilde directs and stars in a poignant, hilarious, and emotionally honest portrayal of human relationships led by a stellar cast Annoying neighbours are a dime a dozen. Annoying neighbours who pop round to share a flan and invite you into their weekly orgy schedule not so much. The Invite is a portrait of two married couples as they meet and clash over a casual get-together. J For a film set entirely in one apartment, Wilde s direction is vivid and energetic, each scene building on and subverting the next, unpicking the layers of the relationships between the key characters. With all four cast members Rogen, Cruz, Norton, and Wilde herself on top form, The Invite is a type of filmmaking that is sorely lacking on our screens: a film about adult feelings for adults. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: How Reinforcement Learning is Winning the Game of Intelligence
description: Reinforcement learning (RL) a Machine Learning (ML) process that dynamically adapts through trial and error is quietly powering the next chapter of AI. Exemplified by Sony AI s recently launch of Ace the AI-powered robot capable of beating professional table-tennis players such mind-bending breakthroughs are redefining the future of game play. Join President of Sony AI, Michael Spranger, who is a creator, technologist and artist himself, to discover why RL is essential for building truly autonomous, intelligent systems, and explore how today s breakthroughs are laying the groundwork for the next wave of transformative AI.

title: What the Fuck Do We Do Now?
description: At a time when many people feel alienated from traditional politics, the discussion asks what more participatory, citizen-led models might look like, and how they could help us get the world we want.

title: Flossie Thorne
description: FLOSSIE THORNE is an emerging solo artist from South West London, performing with her live band and bringing a distinctive, eccentric energy to the stage. Her sound blends shoegaze and pop rock with elements of soul and electronic music, drawing influence from artists such as Alice in Chains, Sofia Isella, and Siouxsie and the Banshees. A British singer-songwriter, Flossie creates music that is loud, vulnerable, and unapologetically honest. Her lyrics confront themes of patriarchy, political tension, and personal experience, rejecting the idea of sugar-coated storytelling for complete transparency. For Flossie, music is a way of revealing the self beyond performance, challenging the idea that public image is more than a fa ade. Her upcoming album, No Silver Lining , is a collection of what she considers her most powerful work to date. True to its title, the project leans into unfiltered lyricism, embracing discomfort rather than softening it. Rooted in a strong DIY ethos, her artistry extends beyond sound into performance, where she takes on an alternative, puppet-like persona to explore themes of control, identity, and feminism - balancing irony with confrontation. After performing in previous bands that never fully reflected her music, Flossie Thorne now comes to the stage as a solo artist for the first time. Her live shows prioritise raw energy and emotional intensity, bringing an unrestrained and immersive experience. SXSW will show a sneak peak of her album playing her favourite songs in her set... Definitely not one to miss.

title: Why AI-Native Companies Are Playing a Different Game
description: The next wave of industry leaders won t be built on legacy architecture, they ll be built from scratch. WRITER COO Brian O Reilly sits down to explore what it truly means to be AI-native, and how companies built around AI from the ground up are redefining product, operations, and competitive advantage.

title: Yasmin Leung (Global Marketing + Artist Development)
description: Yasmin Leung is Director of International Marketing at All Flowers Group, with over a decade of experience across music campaigns in the UK, Europe, Asia-Pacific, the Middle East and Africa. title: DJ AG - Wednesday
description: DJ AG is the Tottenham-raised People s DJ turning London s streets into global stages. A viral sensation with nearly 100 million monthly views, he is celebrated for his inclusive open-format sets and high-profile guests including Central Cee, Skepta, Jorja Smith, Craig David, Ed Sheeran, Ciara, and JME. Named Time Out Londoner of the Year, AG s movement is built on community and raw talent. Whether outside King s Cross, Care homes or in local schools, he doesn t just play music he creates moments, amplifies voices, and brings people together through the power of rhythm.

title: Lamaya
description: LAMAYA s music is borderless by design. A Scottish-Nigerian artist who builds an alternative Afrobeats sound through a distinctly UK-underground lens, her music arrives in layers. Rooted in Scotland, shaped by Nigerian lineage and the warmth of Spanish culture.

title: Venture Spotlight - Semi Final 2
description: Ten more founders. Ten more shots at the final. The second cohort of European university spinouts takes the stage: more breakthrough science from the frontiers of deep tech, life sciences, biotech, climate and creative technology, compressed into three minutes each. But this session has higher stakes. Alongside the pitches, the scores from both semi-finals will be revealed and the Grand Final lineup announced. The moment we find out who gets a shot at Thursday s 100,000 prize. Europe s most exciting emerging technology deserves more than a laboratory. Today, we find out who makes the cut.

title: AI for Autonomy & Creative Breakthroughs
description: The potential of AI is world-changing, but so are the risks. As we face disruption at scale, the very fabric of society is being redefined. How can we preserve human autonomy, rather than handing decision-making over to machines? How can AI help reduce friction and expand creativity? Join us to explore strategies to advance a future of discovery driven by successful human-machine collaboration.

title: Affinity for Africa: Banking the next Billion
description: Affinity is a digital bank built in Ghana for Africa. After receiving the first license grant in 15 years, in just 18 months it has grown to acquire over 135,000 customers organically 65% of whom were previously unbanked. This session explores what happens when African fintech scales with discipline and speed, why the next generation of global banks are being built on the continent, and what full-stack banking means for capital, opportunity, and inclusion across the region.

title: Nostalgia Reloaded: How Iconic Brands Are Reborn Through Fans and Creators
description: Nostalgia no longer lives only on traditional screens; it thrives on platforms. From fan edits and TikTok recaps to podcasts and digital commentary, audiences are actively reshaping and extending the cultural life of iconic shows, music and moments. This panel explores how comfort content is being reinterpreted for a new generation, and how that renewed relevance is translating into real commercial value. As fans and creators take a more active role in storytelling, legacy brands are finding new ways to grow, evolve and connect.

title: Radical Goodness: The Case for Brands that Mean It
description: Plenty of brands claim to care. Few make it matter. Natura, one of the world s most commercially successful B Corps, has built a global beauty business on values it actually lives by. Here Design has spent twenty years helping brands translate what they stand for into something people can see, feel, and want. In this session, Natura CMO Tatiana Ponce and Here Design s Creative Partner Kate Marlow get into the real work: how companies can bridge the gap between brand belief and brand behaviour, and why making good choices desirable is the most powerful thing a company can do.

title: nad
description: Nad is an alternative artist based in London. With influences such as Frank Ocean, Dijon, James Blake and Joni Mitchell, he similarly often goes into themes such as loneliness and love, but always from an honest, youthful, and personal perspective.

title: Music Meet-up: Association of Independent Festivals (AIF)
description: This meet-up is hosted by the Association of Independent Festivals (AIF). Please note, all meet-ups are first come first served - entry closes once capacity is full. Arrive early as spaces are limited. AIF is the UK s leading trade body representing independent music festivals, championing the interests of grassroots and large-scale events outside major corporate ownership. Supporting over 150 member festivals, AIF advocates for fair policy, sustainability, diversity and the long-term health of the live music sector. Through industry insight, networking and campaigning, it helps festival founders and promoters navigate evolving challenges while protecting the cultural importance of independent festivals within the UK s music ecosystem.

title: NL House: Beyond profit - creating impact through immersive experiences
description: What happens when we measure success in lives changed rather than revenue? And how do you transform complex scientific research into something a person can actually feel? In this closing session by CIIIC , artist and Woven founder Thijs Biersteker shares how real-time data can become immersive storytelling that makes complex issues understandable, urgent and actionable for broad audiences. Dagmar van Ravenswaay Claasen and Robin Coops join for a conversation shaped by insights from the CIIIC Expert Roundtable Sessions on IX held that afternoon. Together, they question how immersive technologies are redefining value, authorship and human agency itself. This session is part of NL House where Dutch ambition meets a global audience on June 3 at SXSW London. For the full overview of sessions and experiences, visit NL House on Luma . (Non-badge holders can apply for entry by signing up via the Luma website)

title: Quantum Computing’s "ChatGPT Moment"
description: Quantum technology harnesses the power of nature to unlock a new paradigm for computation. The societal impact of quantum computing in areas such as AI, genomics, and cryptography is already well anticipated. Less clear is what it means at the level of everyday experience. Moth s Founder Harry Kumar explains why they believe the ChatGPT moment for quantum computing is approaching, why it is likely to emerge through creative applications, and what early signals suggest about how close the industry is to a broad public engagement.

title: Meet Charlie: The AI That Works for You, Not the Platform
description: Every AI platform you use today is quietly building a file on you. They call it memory and say it gives you a better experience. But we ve been down this road before. Your preferences, your finances, your relationships, your intentions accumulated inside walled gardens you don t control, used in ways you can t see. World Wide Web inventor Sir Tim Berners-Lee saw this coming. At SXSW London, he and Inrupt CEO John Bruce won t just talk about the problem. They ll show you the solution they ve built Charlie. Charlie is a personal AI assistant built on the Solid protocol, the open web standard Sir Tim created to empower individuals with control over their data. Charlie acts as your advocate and gatekeeper in front of the AI tools you already use: surfacing exactly what data is being sent before it leaves your device, protecting your personal information, and keeping your memory portable across every AI platform you choose. Come see what the world looks like when AI truly works for you.

title: Extended Liquid Minds
description: What if AI isn t just a tool you use - but a genuine extension of how you think? What if using it well doesn t outsource your intelligence, but expands it? Hundreds of millions of people are already in cognitive partnership with AI systems that shape their beliefs, scaffold their reasoning, and structure their emotional lives. Dr Daniel Hulme introduces the Extended Liquid Mind - and makes the case for why its composition may be the most important design decision of the next decade.

title: Mavica
description: MAVICA is the project of London-based artist Marta Casanova. Her sound blends folk with indie, pop and electronic sounds. Originally from Cartagena, Spain, she moved to London when she started studying Performance and Production at the London College of Creative Media. There, she wrote debut EP Gone , including breakout song Fire , which received critical acclaim from The Line of Best Fit and The 405. Her 2023 debut album sometimes a person never comes back (but that s okay) received support from Rolling Stone, Billboard Latin, Clash, C-Heads, Early Rising, Paste, BBC Radio 1, KCRW and got her on the cover of Spotify s Radar Indie Tidal s Global Rising playlists. MAVICA has played festivals like Primavera Sound, Mad Cool, The Great Escape, Wilderness, SXSW (Austin London) and has opened for Alice Phoebe Lou, Roo Panes, Blanco White and Low Island.

title: Screen Happy Hour with Women In Film & TV
description: Join SXSW London and our Screen partners at daily Happy Hour drinks. Meet filmmakers and industry professionals from all over the world. This is your unmissable stop before your evening screening. Women in Film TV (UK) is the leading membership organisation for women working in creative media in the UK and proud members of Women in Film Television International they are part of a global network of over 12,000 women. Members of the not-for-profit organisation come from a broad range of professions spanning the entire film and television industry.

title: Screen Meet-Up with Film Locos  British Latinos Production Community
description: Join us for rooftop drinks at this meet-up hosted by Film Locos, bringing together filmmakers, creatives and those passionate about independent film and visual storytelling. Film Locos is a UK-based creative community connecting emerging and established talent across film through events, networking and collaborative opportunities, creating an inclusive space for connection, conversation and industry growth. Please note: all meet-ups are first come, first served - once capacity is reached, entry will close. Arrive early, as spaces are limited.

title: Cocktail reception hosted by Cygnet Gin
Embers
description: Director: Aoqian Sun Cast: Song Jia, Wen Qi, Zu Feng, Zhang Zhanyi, Ma Ling A crematorium worker in China mixes up two sets of ashes with deadly consequences Huang Yan (Song Jia) manages the furnace at a crematorium. On one fateful afternoon, she mixes two sets of ashes at work, with the families of the dead receiving wrongful remains. The woman believes the bad luck coming from the mix-up is causing the deadly illness ravaging her body and, on a desperate attempt to save her life, sets out to undo her mistake with the unexpected help of a young girl caught in the embroil. Aoqian Sun expertly traverses the lines between social drama and thriller to prod at questions of death and family within contemporary Chinese society. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: From Club Culture to Art
description: This Artforum Atelier, presented in partnership with SXSW London, brings together artists Mark Leckey and Christelle Oyiri/CRYSTALLMESS for a conversation to explore the relationship between club culture and contemporary art. Moderated by Pablo Larios, Editor, Artforum. Wednesday, June 3, 2026 6:00 - 8:00 PM Studio 6, Protein Studios 31 New Inn Yard, London EC2A 3EY 6:00 - 7:00 PM Cocktail reception hosted by Cygnet Gin 7:00 - 8:00 PM Conversation

title: Hijra
description: Director: Shahad Ameen Cast: Khairiah Nathmy, Lamar Faden, Raghad Bokhari, Nawaf Al-Dhufairy A young girl and her grandmother have their pilgrimage to Mecca interrupted by a runaway in this multigenerational Saudi road movie A pilgrimage to Mecca is derailed when a devout grandmother (Khairiah Nathmy) loses one of her granddaughters and decides to embark on a road trip with remaining grandchild Janna (Lamar Faden) to find her before anyone else does. With the help of opportunistic driver Ahmed (Nawaf Al-Dhufairy), they make their way cross-country, letting the contradictions and unspoken tensions between them unfold. Director Shahad Ameen uses the landscape to draw parallels between the physical and emotional journeys of her characters (and, in a broader sense, the multiplicity of women in Saudi Arabia) so powerfully, you ll feel the wind on your skin long after leaving the cinema. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Qawemana: Arab + SWANA Mixer
description: SXSW by day, SWANA by night. From the region to the diaspora, join SWANA* professionals and creators as Qawmena (قومنا) brings our community together over SWANA beats and Arab-inspired food, in a room that feels like home. The past few years have pushed our diaspora to rethink the world around us, and where we must look: inward, and to each other. This is networking that goes deeper than small talk connections that move things forward. Qawmena uplifts Arab and SWANA professionals across industries. *SWANA: a decolonial term for MENA, including Arabs, Persians, Kurds, Turks, Assyrians +. Allies are welcome! #Networking #Meetup #SWANA #ForFounders #ForTechies #HappyHours MeetUps #Community

title: Rappers Corner Q&A with Lil Cherry, Goldbudda
description: Q A with Lil Cherry Goldbudda

title: Tina Edwards
description: Edwards is out there extensively researching scenes, genres and artists, cutting through the convenient listening of your latest Made For You playlist and helping us remember what true discovery should be Dazed and Confused Edwards is deeply plugged into what s next for global record culture - Cue BURN, vinyl alliance Essential jazz into dance selections every time - Gilles Peterson An internationally-acclaimed DJ, Tina Edwards encourages dancers to ignore the booth and stay present on the dance floor. Tina began DJing shortly before the pandemic; since, her demand behind the decks has taken her and her red box of 45s around the world, performing in Australia, Japan, Singapore, LA, Chicago and across Europe. She s played Glastonbury, Montreux Jazz, North Sea and headline sets for the likes of BAFTAs Official After Party (2025, 2026), film premieres, Netflix and Ray Ban. Tina s sets are wildly eclectic and full of wild cards but her grounding comes from the language of jazz; the Londoner is recognised as one of the few DJs of her generation to uphold jazz dance floor culture. She does this with her Internationally-renowned night Love Is Everywhere, with some of the most established jazz dancers from across Europe and Asia making a point of performing. Wherever she plays, her sets avoid predictable arcs; Latin jazz, cumbia, off-beat disco, Afro-diasporic grooves, psychedelia and obscure 45s from her travels all contribute to her playful, intoxicating sets. Elsewhere, Tina s reputation for sharing and breaking new artists is upheld on her Internationally revered radio show Bandcamp Selects, Bandcamp fan club Jazz-ish Jazz Club, and through live events too, namely with LGBTQIA+ community-oriented platform Queer Jazz and resonate; the latter of which aims to democratise information in the music industry through events, discussions and editorial. Tina Edwards is one of Worldwide FM s longest serving residents (2017-2022), and she hosted and produced Next Big Thing for British Airways for seven years, reaching millions of listeners. In 2022 she presented the TV documentary JAZZ UK: Spitting Fire (BBC One, BBC Four). She s also regularly heard on BBC Radio 3 (Jazz Fix, Round Midnight), and has written for Downbeat, The Guardian, Monocle, WeJazz and many more well-respected platforms. She was Editor of limited edition Listen Zine for KEF Speakers.

title: All Night Wrong
description: Director: Jason James Cast: Zach Cherry, Maria Bakalova, Emily Hampshire, Tyler Labine Severance s Zach Cherry and Borat s Maria Bakalova go down a spiral of murder, lies, and (almost) sex in this riotous comedy. On one fateful night, Gary (Zach Cherry) and Eli (Maria Bakalova) decide to take their brief dating app exchange into real life. When they meet at a bar, things move fast, and soon the duo is at a roadside motel ready for your regular one-night stand. But nothing about their night is regular as they find themselves driving the car of a possible killer, lugging a dead body, and running from the police while carrying a mysterious suitcase filled to the brim with dirty cash in this fast-paced, anxiety-inducing Canadian comedy. A regular one-night-stand, indeed. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: James: Getting Away With It
description: Director: Chris Atkins Cast: Jim Glennie, Tim Booth, David Baynton-Power, Saul Davies, Mark Hunter The first-ever authorised tell-all documentary about iconic British band James If you ever caught yourself screaming, Oh, sit down! Sit down! Sit down next to me! amongst friends and strangers at a rowdy pub as a barman calls final rounds, you know you have had a proper good British night out. This feeling is courtesy of iconic Manchester band James, whose hits have accompanied generations of Brits for almost half a century. In their first-ever authorised documentary, the band chronicles their rise and fall, and rise again, as well as their unique phases, including a brief stint in a cult in the 80s, taking direct advice from psychics, and participating in medical experiments to pay the bills. A treat for fans old and new. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Only Rebels Win
description: Director: Danielle Arbid Cast: Hiam Abbass, Mahamat Amine Benrachid, Charbel Kamel, Shaden Fakih. A May-December romance unravels amidst a collision of desire and displacement in Beirut When 60-year-old Suzanne (Hiam Abass) intervenes in a racist attack against Osmane (Amine Benrachid), she feels an instant connection to the mysterious man half her age. The violence of their first meeting comes to permeate the relationship that follows, with the love between the two marked by harsh judgment of their age gap, as well as the interfaith and interracial nature of their pairing. Unable to film in Lebanon amongst the escalating war, Danielle Arbid beautifully recreates the landscape of her home city of Beirut: one constantly under attack, but still able to foster and harbour a tender love story. (ISRA AL KASSI)

title: Gilles Peterson - Wednesday (Festival & Platinum Pass Only)
description: Please note, this is only open to festival platinum pass holders, not music wristbands. Since the 1980s, Gilles Peterson has been a pivotal figure in the club scene, renowned for his genre-defying approach to music with jazz at its core. As one of the UK s most iconic DJs, he has spent over 40 years shaping music trends as a radio presenter, club DJ, producer, and festival curator. His work has influenced DJs and artists around the world and earned him the enduring admiration of listeners. Currently, he hosts a flagship weekend programme on BBC Radio 6 Music and, in 2016, launched his online radio platform, Worldwide FM . He is founder of the Worldwide Festival in the South of France and of We Out Here festival in the UK. As a label owner, Peterson remains dedicated to discovering and promoting new talent through Brownswood Recordings, bringing fresh voices to the global stage. Additionally, Peterson published his first book, *Lockdown FM - Broadcasting In A Pandemic*, further cementing his status as a multifaceted cultural tastemaker.

title: Test
description: Director: Sam McConnell Cast: Brock Yurich, Matthew Morrison, Tammy Blanchard, Mike Edward, Paloma Garcia-Lee An amateur bodybuilder wrestles with his queerness while pursuing his dreams of becoming a champion Eddie (Brock Yurich) dreams of becoming a bodybuilding champion and escaping rural Ohio. His gruelling training routine makes it easier to hide unspoken desires from a doting mother and girlfriend, but the pressures of an upcoming championship and the arrival of a charming coach bring his pretense to a startling halt. In a world where conversations around societal expectations on the body both when it comes to performance and aesthetics are everywhere, Sam McConnell offers a sharp insight into the dichotomy between a polished exterior and crumbling interior, all while sensitively observing the joys and challenges of realising one s queerness in an environment hostile to difference. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Chavez Cartel
description: A four-piece alt-rock band from Gold Coast Australia . With attacking guitar, thumping drums, fat bass and powerhouse vocals, they have been on an upward climb since early 2023.

title: David Kofi
description: David Kofi is a London-based pianist and composer whose sound blends jazz with gospel, soul, and R B influences. His music is rooted in groove, emotion, and storytelling, creating a sound that connects with both jazz audiences and wider listeners. He has performed sold-out shows at the Elgar Room at the Royal Albert Hall and has been featured on BBC Radio, Jazz FM, Soho Radio, and Times Radio. His live performances combine improvisation, energy, and musicality, reflecting a contemporary approach to jazz with a distinct and recognisable voice. His live album The David Kofi Experience: Live at Late Night Jazz Royal Albert Hall captures the energy of these performances, highlighting his ability to translate his sound from stage to record.

title: Daytime TV
description: Daytime TV look to push the boundaries of what a guitar band can be in 2026 with an ambitious and sonically diverse sound. After spending the last year writing and recording, the band have emerged with bold new music that pushes them into deeper, darker electronic territory while still holding onto their anthemic, urgent indie grit. The band have teamed up with producer Pete Hutchings (Foals, Royal Blood, Gang of Youths), whose instinct for balancing scale with intensity has helped shape a sound that feels fresh, expansive and raw. Daytime TV have previously supported the likes of You Me At Six, The Amazons and Twin Atlantic, earning a strong reputation for compelling, high-impact live shows. Having found their identity, Daytime TV step into 2026 with clarity and confidence, ready to be defined by this new music.

title: Kizzi
description: Kizzi is a DJ who s carving her own path in the music world, blending UKG, club funk, and house with the vibrant influences of her South Asian Spice. She s made waves with her sets at Radio 1 s Big Weekend, Glastonbury Festival, and her own sell-out event, Kizzi Friends, which has packed out venues an impressive eight times.

title: Oscar Blue
description: Oscar Blue is an Irish singer-songwriter and storyteller who finds meaning in the mundane - the ordinary moments and small flashes of human connection that often say the most. A social care worker by day, his songwriting is shaped by the people and stories he encounters: resilience, loss, gratitude, loneliness and the quiet strength it takes to keep going. He turns that lived experience into cinematic folk storytelling, soaring rock soundscapes and songs that feel both deeply personal and built for the room. Completely independent and self-funded, Oscar has built a devoted international audience from the ground up, with over 20 million streams, sold-out headline shows across the UK and Ireland, sync placements across feature and short film, and live appearances alongside Kodaline, Kingfishr, Sons of the East, Seafret and more. His debut album Birds in Winter , released April 2026, captures the world between a rural refuge and an East London bedsit - a record shaped by gratitude, loss, love, solitude and the search for light in ordinary life. At SXSW, Oscar Blue arrives at the start of a much bigger story; an emerging Irish artist with the songs, the voice and the live force to make people stop and listen.

title: PCK Dance: Vessel
description: Exploring human instincts, self-perception, and connection, the award-winning PCK Dance presents VESSEL a powerful encounter of dance and live music newly created at Abbey Road Studios by composer Sean Pett. Founded by James Pett and Travis Clausen-Knight former artists of Company Wayne McGregor and Michael Clark Company PCK Dance has rapidly become one of the UK s most internationally recognised independent dance companies. Their visually arresting productions have toured across Europe, Asia, and the Americas, earning Critics Circle recognition at the National Dance Awards and praise from Graham Watts OBE, Chair of the Awards, who described the company as the closest that independent contemporary dance has to celebrity and the next big thing . VESSEL itself has been celebrated as mesmerising and electrifying .

title: Show Me the Future
description: A panel discussion between some top Korean and Korean origin rappers about the new digitised direction of Korea s rap scene

title: The Josh Barry Experience
description: Josh Barry is a dynamic force in the music industry, now embarking on his highly anticipated solo project, The Josh Barry Experience. Drawing inspiration from the soulful sounds of the 1970s, his music blends vintage soul with contemporary innovation, creating a boundary-pushing sound that is uniquely his own. After winning the Glastonbury Emerging Talent Competition in 2017, Josh released his powerful debut EP, Art Soul, in 2021 garnering over 3 million streams to date. Building on that success, his upcoming EP, Pride Purpose, invites listeners on an intimate journey through his life, continuing his mission to connect deeply through music. In 2023, Josh was recognised as part of the MOBO Unsung Class, This recognition further cements his growing presence in the industry. The Josh Barry Experience has had the honour of supporting renowned artists including Rag n Bone Man on his UK tour, Jazz virtuoso Cory Henry, The Wailers, Soul legend Lizz Wright, Jon Allen, House Gospel Choir, and more. With momentum building and critical acclaim growing, The Josh Barry Experience is well on its way to becoming a legacy act of this generation.

title: Maharani
description: Born in the Netherlands with Indian Tamil roots, Maharani is a multilingual singer-songwriter blending RnB, Trap-Soul, and Indian sonics. Her mission to create a new sound earned a major co-sign from legendary producer Timbaland who exclaimed, She changed the whole narrative of their sound, describing her Tamil fusion track Skyline as ground-breaking for her culture. She set a trend with her viral Tamixes series, reimagining and rewriting popular songs in Tamil. Her debut EP AnBae with producer ItsyaboiKay earned widespread play across the BBC and a feature in MTV News Bop Shop, where Virginia Lowman noted, they are charting their own lane in R B, and I am buckling in for the ride. Spotify also championed her on major playlists like RADAR Tamil. A compelling live performer, Maharani has performed internationally, recorded at BBC Maida Vale Studios, opened for stars like Arjun and F1rstman , and headlined her own sold-out London show via her Cinnamon Spice initiative, a platform for emerging South Asian talent. Maharani s artistry is deeply authentic. She forges a new genre not by just fusing sounds, but by reframing RnB, Soul Hip-Hop from a lens closer to home - creating a sound that is passionately her own and paving the way for a new generation.

title: Reuben James
description: Releasing his music on his own record label is something that James has sustained and championed the entirety of his career. It allows him to control every aspect of his music and legacy and he oversees a back catalogue that has now amassed in excess of 50 million plays across all platforms. If further proof of James infectious magnetism across the industry were needed, one need look no further than the long list of artists lining up to collaborate with him - from arena-filling superstars such as Stormzy and Sam Smith to the likes of Herbie Hancock, Lang Lang, Marcus Mumford and Disclosure amongst many others. It s these collaborations, blended with influences from the likes of Frank Ocean, D Angelo, Erykah Badu and Robert Glasper, that have led to James developing a unique sound and style of his own whilst emerging as one of UK music s most interesting voices.

title: Savage House
description: Director: Peter Glanz Cast: Claire Foy, Richard E. Grant, Bel Powley, Jack Farthing Richard E. Grant and Claire Foy lead this satirical, sickening and sharp takedown of the upper classes. England, 18th Century. The Savages are at their lowest: they despise each other, their house is falling apart, and they are disliked in polite society sleeping with their servants, and selling off jewellery to keep their gambling and drinking going. Oh, and Chauncey Savage s foot might be rotting off. This is until they are set to be visited by higher ranking nobleman. The honour gives them a renewed energy to get new outfits, polish their silver, and hire new help to make sure the House of Savage is looking as impressive as possible. Anchored by two outrageously hilarious performances by British legends Claire Foy and Richard E. Grant as you ve never seen them before, the sights and smells of Savage House will stick to your skin long after you ve exited the cinema. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Abdullah Maharvi
description: Abdullah Maharvi is a Punjabi singer-songwriter, now based in London, United Kingdom. As the fastest growing independent name in the South Asian music space his music combines contemporary influences from artists such as Mk.Gee, Frank Ocean Travis Scott. Whilst his approach to lyrics and melody is reminiscent of classic Indian and Pakistani traditional folk music of which he grew up studying. Maharvi s collaboration with South Asian producer-artist Umair on the album Rockstar Without A Guitar brought him significant acclaim; their song Come Through featuring Talha Anjum has amassed over 50 million streams since its release in April 2024 - 5x as much as any other song on the album. Abdullah Maharvi then released his first single of 2025 entitled CHAN MAHIYA and was received with praise amongst industry and fans with dozens of viral moments and UGC. He has since been co-writing for a number of side projects and preparing to roll out his debut EP in the fall of 2026.

title: Infinity Song - Wednesday
description: If you are a Platinum Pass Holder eligible for advance booking, then you can reserve your spot to see Infinity Song by RSVPing to the Mahogany showcase on the official SXSW London app. Please note that reservation spaces are limited and that entry is only guaranteed if you arrive before the showcase starts at 7:00pm. Admission for late arrivals will be subject to venue capacity. Infinity Song is a Soft Rock band based in New York City, comprised of 4 siblings: Abraham, Angel, Israel, and Momo Boyd. With a blend of tight vocal harmonies, dreamy lyricism, and sublime guitar riffs, the band creates a transcendent experience for the audience on every stage and in their recorded music. Homeschooled academically and musically, along with their 5 other brothers and sisters, by parents who founded the Boys Girls Choirs of Detroit, the siblings have been performing in front of audiences since Pre-K. Raised on classical, gospel, and jazz, they draw inspiration from artists like Pat Methany, Marvin Gaye, and The Winans Family. Infinity Song s journey began in 2006 when the Boyd patriarch, John Boyd, moved the family from Detroit to New York. The group performed all over the city, from the Metropolitan Museum of Art to Times Square, with Central Park s Bethesda Fountain becoming their primary stage for the next 12 years. Over time, they turned casual park visitors into loyal fans, eventually being introduced to JAY-Z. In 2016, the band was signed to Roc Nation by JAY-Z, who encouraged them to stay true to their unique sound. In 2020, they made a giant splash with their debut album Mad Love, which, combined with several viral videos, amassed millions of views and earned the attention of Hollywood s biggest names. Infinity Song s recent success marks a bold chapter in their musical evolution, which they ve described as their Metamorphosis. Their expanded sound, blending elements of soft rock, pop, and soulful melodies, has captured the attention of a global audience. The band s breakout moment came with the viral success of Hater s Anthem, a track that has been endorsed by Pop star Doja Cat and shared by millions of people worldwide. Leading many listeners to compare the band to legendary 70s groups such as Fifth Dimension, The Mamas and The Papas, and ABBA. But their journey didn t stop there. Slow Burn and Sinking Boat, two additional singles from their Metamorphosis era, also became viral sensations, fueling their rise to new heights and captivating even more fans. In 2024, the band embarked on a successful world tour, playing sold-out shows in Los Angeles, Toronto, London, Manchester, Brussels, Berlin, Paris, and more. They also graced the stages of major festivals such as Bonnaroo, Lollapalooza, and Pitchfork Paris - further solidifying their place in the global music scene. The band s 2024 achievement, a LIVE album, captures the magic of their dynamic performances and continues to fuel excitement for their upcoming tours singles. In 2025, Infinity Song set out on their highly anticipated World Tour II, expanding their global reach and elevating their reputation as one of the most genre-blending live acts of the decade. The tour included standout performances at major international festivals such as the Latitude Festival and Helsinki Festival, as well as shows across Tokyo and Osaka, Japan and other countries.. Their North American leg brought them to several sold-out shows with audiences across Canada, along with major U.S. cities including New York, Los Angeles, Chicago, Houston, and many others. The tour marked a defining moment in the band s evolution, revealing the depth of their global fanbase and their unmatched ability to command stages of every size. Energized by this new chapter, Infinity Song is poised to make an even bigger mark in the year to come.

title: KeepVibesNear
description: KeepVibesNear (KVN) is an East London-based alternative R B artist known for Noir R B a moody, intimate sound featuring stripped-back vocals and high vulnerability, often exploring troubled love . Born in Whitechapel with Nigerian heritage, he creates emotive, introspective music described as a leftfield blend of jazz, electronic, and soulful R B.

title: Korda Korder
description: I can t stop playing this - Steve Lamacq - BBC Radio 6 Stunning - HUW STEPHENS, BBC RADIO 6 Authentic - DIY Magical - JOHN KENNEDY, RADIO X East Sussex dreampop collective. Building on a wave of momentum that has seen them championed by the UK s most influential tastemakers. With a signature sound defined by shimmering guitar lines, expansive synths, and vocals that feel both intimate and ethereal, Korda Korder successfully bridge the gap between the nostalgic haze of Beach House and the melodic precision of Alvvays .

title: Kyra
description: British singer-songwriter Kyra, has quickly established herself as a key player in the UK music scene. Her unorthodox and gripping releases from debut EP project Xanadu , caught the attention of BBC Radio 1 s Nick Grimshaw, featuring in his track of the week series, Jamz Supernova s BBC1Xtra Top 5 EPs and Jess Izsatt for BBC Introducing. Showcasing her charismatic and distinct soulful vocal, Kyra released a selection of jazz infused singles; after the love , blossom spring (playlisted on Jazz FM) and excellent , a self care song created in ode to her beloved sister circle. 2023 became an exciting year of continued success for Kyra, with the release of her second EP as things grow , with tracks featured on various editorial streaming playlists such as New Music Friday and Riffs Runs on Spotify, as well as Brown Sugar and New in R B on Apple Music. The EP was also championed by BBC Radio London s Robert Elms, with a live radio performance of grown , the lead single from the latest project. Kyra s music has been amplified with an array of live shows across the UK, including a spellbinding Jazz Re:freshed showcase and performances at iconic London venues such as Jazz Cafe, Lafayette, Koko Camden, Soho House and Southbank. A Wilderness festival performance lending vocals to UK Jazz favourites Blue Lab Beats, led to Kyra and the duo collaborating on new music together, further cementing Kyra s presence within the burgeoning London Jazz scene. Kyra s undeniable vocal range leaves no question of her live abilities. Garnering new fans with each performance, it s clear Kyra is on the cusp of becoming an exciting breakout artist. With more music on the horizon delving deeper into the vulnerabilities of being a woman and motherhood, Kyra aims to use her voice to connect with lovers of jazz and soul across the globe. 2024 proved to be an exciting year for Kyra, with two sold out headline shows under her belt, a debut international showcase appearance at the renowned SXSW Music Festival in Austin, Texas and a feature on Blue Lab Beats latest album, Blue Eclipse. For the majority of 2025 Kyra s time has been spent in the studio, working with an array of new collaborators on exciting new music.
