The first new single Strong Ones , released last October alongside a sold out headline gig at the Royal Albert Hall s Elgar Room marks Kyra s first music release since 2023. Back to back singles have continued consistently into 2026. Kyra s further exploration of self has led to the delivery of poignant topics, surrounding the challenges of womanhood. Through each release, Kyra invites listeners into an ever-deepening world of vulnerability, strength and self-discovery. As she continues to evolve both sonically and personally, her artistry resonates far beyond the music marking her as a defining voice of a new generation of UK soul.

title: New Dutch Rave
description: Amsterdam s nightlife creatives take over to share the work happening after dark in one of Europe s most distinctive cultural scenes. With a.o. Hoax LeBeau is one of the Netherlands most electrifying drag performers and DJs and MMIV, the founding force behind queer collectives Supernature and Jezabel platforms where his 15+ year DJ career expands into multidisciplinary territory alongside fellow artists. NEW DUTCH RAVE is part of NL House where Dutch ambition meets a global audience on June 3 at SXSW London. For the full overview of sessions and experiences, visit NL House on Luma . (Non-badge holders can apply for entry by signing up via the Luma website)

title: Precious Pepala
description: Born and bred in Sheffield and with family roots in Zambia, Precious Pepala is quickly becoming one of the industries most exciting breakthrough artists. Her music is a blend of alternative pop/rock with hints of soul/gospel drawn from her years growing up in church as a pastors daughter. Addressing important topics such as mental health and religious trauma, Precious uses her lyrics and music to push big conversations. Being a 21 year old, black, female artist who stands at just 5 feet tall she is, in her own words, not the stereotypical face of power or change . However, thanks to her vocal power, songwriting talent and ever-growing social media presence, she is quickly becoming a voice for her generation and will undoubtedly continue to push the boundaries.

title: Shorts: Music Videos
description: Global sonic cultures with fresh sounds and electric visuals driving the pulse. At the crossroads of trendsetting sounds and bold images, MUSIC VIDEO SHOWCASE unfolds as a layered sensory experience rooted in the instinctive impulses that push beyond technique, structure and form. Moving through unconventional choreography of bodies, narrative and experimentation, the selection offers a vivid glimpse into global sonic cultures, each a visceral response to its own environment and imagination. Immerse yourself in a swirl of rhythm and spark - you may leave with dance moves already in motion. (79) Total Running Time: 50 min

title: Performance by Zoe Rahman, Sohini Alam and Himanish Goswami
description: Zoe Rahman, Sohini Alam and Himanish Goswami - Performance Zoe Rahman is a celebrated contemporary jazz pianist and composer whose expressive playing has earned her major UK jazz awards and international acclaim. Described in The Observer as a remarkable pianist by any standard , Rahman s style is deeply rooted in jazz yet it reflects her classical background, British/Bengali heritage and her very broad musical taste. A MOBO Award winner, Mercury Prize nominee and Ivor Novello Award winner, Zoe has established herself as a unique presence in the British music industry and has performed around the world. Sohini Alam s musical repertoire is rooted in folk, modern, and traditional Bengali music. A third-generation singer from a family of renowned Bangladeshi vocalists, her work is deeply informed by traditional forms while continually evolving through contemporary expression. She is particularly known for Nazrul songs and Rabindra sangeet. She is the lead vocalist of critically acclaimed bands Khiyo, Lokkhi Terra, In Place of War s GRRRL, and Musical Director of Komola Collective Himanish Goswami Himanish Goswami is a sought after tabla player in the UK. He has collaborated with well-known bands such as Kula Shaker and Lokkhi Terra and has featured on BBC One and BBC Radio Asian Network. He has performed at prestigious venues such as The Barbican, Ronnie Scott s Jazz Club and Uttam Manch (Kolkata). He regularly performs around the UK, Europe, India and the USA. Himanish is currently the lead tabla teacher at Newham Music (east London) and Noborag Music, a music organisation whose aim is to promote Indian music and culture in the UK. Musicians: Zoe Rahman (piano), Sohini Alam (vocals), Himanish Goswami (tabla).

title: 260 Asia Showcase
description: Up and coming crew 260 Asia showcase featuring: Jeremy Quest Make a Movie Hans

title: Kizzi
description: Kizzi is a DJ who s carving her own path in the music world, blending UKG, club funk, and house with the vibrant influences of her South Asian Spice. She s made waves with her sets at Radio 1 s Big Weekend, Glastonbury Festival, and her own sell-out event, Kizzi Friends, which has packed out venues an impressive eight times.

title: Opal Mag
description: Fostering influences from acts including Mazzy Star, The Sundays, The Breeders, and The Smashing Pumpkins, Opal Mag blends hazy, atmospheric soundscapes with confessional, unfiltered lyricism. Writing for the dreamers and the bedroom dwellers, her songs feel like secrets whispered late at night; raw, intimate, and achingly real.

title: La Carn
description: Director: Joan Porcel Cast: Lluis Garau A queer performance artist gets dangerously close to a stranger in an online chat room. Spanish dancer Lluis Garau adapts his own one-man-show of the same name into an electric and erotic nightmare of digital missed connections. Playing a version of himself, Garau is a young dancer with a penchant for trolling Chatroulette until someone interesting, or hot, or both, pops up. The film s tone switches to something darker when he connects with a masked man who may be behind some targeted murders of gay men in the area. Dancing between the intimacy of dark rooms and the perceived intimacy of theaters, Garau s central performance is so raw and all-absorbing, it becomes the film s heartbeat as the tension escalates to a fiery finale. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Bayaan
description: Director: Bikas Ranjan Mishra Cast: Huma Qureshi A detective story set in the remote town of Rajasthan, where a revered leader is accused of sexual assault by an anonymous letter. Against her superiors orders, young officer Roohi (Huma Qureshi) takes it upon herself to get to the bottom of a contentious sexual assault scandal involving some of the most powerful men in her community, unknowingly stumbling into a large web of corruption, mass complacency, and institutional enabling. Firmly rooted in the police procedural, Bayaan recalls the tenacity of Det. Oliva Benson from Law Order: SVU, all while carving out a distinctively contemporary and regionally specific take on the familiar genre. Bayaan is a strong vehicle for Qureshi, who delivers a commanding performance as a woman fighting against both a system designed to protect abusers and the people who help keep it in place. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Winter of the Crow
description: Director: Kasia Adamik Cast: Lesley Manville, Tom Burke, Zofia Wichlacz Lesley Manville leads a tense Cold War thriller based on a story by Nobel Prize-winning author Olga Tokarzuk. Tightly wound British academic Joan, played by the always excellent Lesley Manville, travels to Cold War-era Warsaw to deliver a paper on the treatment of psychiatric illness. Once in Poland, the professor is surprisingly caught in between a student uprising and escalating assassinations as authorities call in martial law. Manville is phenomenal as a spiky, on-edge professor who s pulled into something much bigger than herself and, as Joan s stern armour unravels, she (and we) are coolly reminded of the narrowness of her experience after being dropped into a brutal world where theory isn t going to cut it. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: The Whisper
description: Director: Gustavo Hern ndez Cast: Ana Clara Guanco, Marcelo Michinaux, Luciano C ceres Two siblings flee their violent father and come across something even more horrifying in the latest by horror master Gustavo Hern ndez Ib ez Vampire films are a dime a dozen. They are creatures that have been reinterpreted many times over in cinema, but when a fresh take appears, it stands out like a lighting bolt. The Whisper (El Susurro) does just that: horror master Gustavo Hern ndez Ib ez (The Silent House) anchors a universal story of family, belonging and intergenerational curses in a fresh new vision of vampirism. Fleeing from their violent father, siblings Lucía (Ana Clara Guanco) and Adrián (Marcelo Michinaux) take refuge in a remote mansion, only to find that their neighbours are intending to kidnap them to produce a snuff film. As Luc a fights to protect her brother, she must face their family s dark family curse. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Baul Mixtape: Various British Bangladeshi artists
CQ Wrestling
description: CQ Wrestling are a British grunge band led by Charlie Woods and Jake Mac, delivering a fierce yet melodic sound. Their lyrics confront social and political unrest while offering moments of euphoric escape, and their chaotic, high-energy live shows have built a devoted following through three sold-out UK tours, Glastonbury 2025, and appearances at major European festivals. Following critical acclaim for their debut album Plus Ultra (EMI, 2023), supported by BBC Radio 1, 6 Music and Radio X, the band are now fully independent. Their latest single Smoke Screen earned a second cover of Spotify s Rock List, driving placement across major playlists and rapid audience growth, with increasing traction in US markets. Touring plans are underway throughout the year.

title: Father
description: Director: Tereza Nvotov Cast: Milan Ondr k, Dominika Mor vkov , Dominika Zajcz, Martina Sľ kov How can society punish a man who s lost everything? It s a big day for Michal (Milan Ondr k) as the new CEO of his company is coming to visit. It also happens to be one of the hottest days of the year. Michal heads to the office after dropping off his daughter at daycare. All of the above are in fact the gaps in memory and what unravels throughout Father is the dizzying depiction of one man s life-changing fatal mistake. As Slovakia s official Oscar submission of 2026, director Tereza Nvotov builds a world of overwhelming chaos with an idyllic family at the centre of it. Inspired by real life tragic events, Milan Ondr k delivers a career best performance as a man left with both impossible grief and an incomprehensible guilt. (ISRA AL KASSI)

title: Jasper Tygner
description: Jasper Tygner is a North London born producer and live electronic artist whose sound blurs the lines between club culture and cinematic ambience. Rooted in UK electronica, ambient and garage influences, his music combines emotional depth with rhythmic precision. Emerging as one of the most distinctive new voices in alternative electronic music, Tygner has built a reputation for immersive live sets that merge synths, keys and visuals into a fluid, evolving experience. Blue marks his debut album a defining release that captures the introspective energy and widescreen emotion of his sound. Shaped by early raving experiences whilst growing up in London s electronic underground scene, Jasper Tygner cites legendary institution Plastic People, along with a deep reverence for jungle, dubstep and garage, as key formative influences. During the lockdown of 2020, Tygner started a series of Instagram bedroom rave live videos, featuring him performing on an array of drum machines and analog synthesisers. His on-the-fly performance series gave viewers a first-hand look at the songwriting and production processes behind many of his songs, while offering previews of their live renditions. These quickly garnered the attention of dance music heavyweights including Bicep, KiNK, Disclosure and DJ Seinfeld. In 2023, Tygner went on to release collaborations with DJ Boring, Pretty Girl and Art School Girlfriend. He s seen further support on BBC Radio from the likes of Danny Howard, Sarah Story, Pete Tong, Sian Eleri, Jaguar, The Blessed Madonna and Jack Saunders, while also providing guest mixes for Rinse FM, Foundation FM and Balamii. Tygner s impressive live repertoire utilises an arsenal of keys, piano and analog synthesisers, which has been showcased at notable festival performances including Coachella, Lightning in a Bottle, Glastonbury and Field Day, alongside headline shows in London, New York and LA.

title: Jerome Thomas
description: A true heart and soul Hackney artist, Jerome Thomas is an undeniable power house of talent and dedication. Known for his extensive vocal prowess and ability to convey soul as contemporary, He describes his genre as FOE which stands for freedom of expression/fusion of everything. He introduced himself in 2016 with Conversations - 5 tracks of stoned maladies and individualism that propelled him to the forefront of his own lane. He followed up with 2019 s Moodswings Vol.1 in which with razor precision he described deep sorrow, love, separation and elation. He returned in 2021 with That Secret Sauce a collaboration with London s Talos. The result was sonically more conscious, yet lyrically and melodically a more sensual set of stories. Jerome then made waves again with his 2023 Single Damn , securing a string of spotify playlists, such as, R B Weekly, New Music Friday, Chilled R B, plus many more. Damn also made it to 27 on the amazon R B charts. It s safe to say Jerome s climb is on a sure path of ascension. He also secured his first sync placement on HBO show Unreasonable Doubt with Bad Boujee taken off his latest project Submerge . Regarding live performance, for the entire journey Jerome has conducted and performed with some of London s premier Jazz musicians, including Fr1th and David Mrakpor. To date, he has five sold out headline shows under his belt, two of which were the Jazz Cafe. He has also sold out 2 shows for Alcazar Rome, (2019 2022) Amsterdam s Melkweg and most recently Berlin Poland. This challenging music is the result of a myriad of loyal producers such as Talos, Pitch92, Warren Xcellence, Mathsxjoy, Jarreau Vandal, Blue Lab Beats, Joe Armon Jones, Maxwell Owin and Bluestaeb. Jerome through these impressive works has garnered attention from Afropunk, COLORS, Clash, BBC 1Xtra, BBC Radio 1, BBC Radio 6 Music, Highsnobiety, Complex, Vogue Italia, MTV i-D.

title: Jords
description: Jords, is a Croydon-based artist who has earned his stripes by being an effortless talent. As a prolific songwriter and producer, Jords has consistently demonstrated his musical prowess and has been on a meteoric rise since the release of his motion picture-assisted project Almost an Adult in 2020. Notably, he holds the distinction of releasing the first UK rap album with legendary label Motown Records, releasing the album Dirt in the Diamond with notable features such as Masego, Wretch 32 and Kranium, showcasing his unique talent and appeal. As well as this, Jords released a 27-minute short film of the same name, which was selected for the TriBeCa Film festival, and nominated for 11 awards, including 3 UK music video awards.

title: Sonna Rele
description: Sonna Rele is a British singer- songwriter whose voice transcends boundaries and resonates with heartfelt emotion. With a unique blend of soul, pop, and R B influences, Sonna has carved out a space that is entirely her own. She rose to global recognition when she was personally chosen by Disney to perform the official theme song, Strong , for the live-action remake of Cinderella (2015). Sonna has earned industry respect and a devoted fanbase, collaborating with major artists like Ne-Yo, Lauryn Hill, Eric Benet, Dr Dre and many more.

title: The Kellows
description: The Kellows are an indie rock band from Sheffield. Your future headliners of tomorrow - RADIO 1 Such a great track - John Kennedy, RADIO X Already airplay from BBC Radio 1, 6, Radio X BBC Intro

title: Rachel Chinouriri - Wednesday
description: If you are a Platinum Pass Holder eligible for advance booking, then you can reserve your spot to see Rachel Chinouriri by RSVPing to the Mahogany showcase on the official SXSW London app. Please note that reservation spaces are limited and that entry is only guaranteed if you arrive before the showcase starts at 7:00pm. Admission for late arrivals will be subject to venue capacity. Rachel Chinouriri is one of Britain s brightest breakout stars. With the release of her 2024 debut album What A Devastating Turn Of Events she cemented her reputation as one of the UK s most accomplished young songwriters, crafting one of the most unique indie pop records in recent years. What A Devastating Turn Of Events landed in the Top 20 of the UK Official Charts and led to Rachel securing two BRIT Award nominations (Artist Of The Year and Best New Artist), following previous Ivor Novello, Rolling Stone UK and BBC Sound Of nominations. 2025 has seen Rachel continuing to ascend with a prominent support slot on Sabrina Carpenter s UK and Europe arena tour, selling out and upgrading her debut US headline tour, and playing her first headline show in Brazil. She drew adoring crowds at Glastonbury and All Things Go festivals, made her debut US TV appearance on The Today Show and features on the line up for iHeart Radio s Jingle Ball this winter. Her new EP Little House marked an expansion of her artistic vision and features the singles Can We Talk About Isaac? and 23:42 . Elsewhere she featured this year on Romeo as part of PinkPantheress Fancy Some More mixtape, and on Rizzle Kicks summer single Follow Excitement! . With her fiercely loyal fanbase the Darlings behind her, Chinouriri has clocked up over 425 million global streams to date. She has been praised by the likes of British Vogue ( Chinouriri is leading the indie pop renaissance ), The Observer ( brimming with fresh ideas ), NME ( consistently charming ), GQ ( Chinouriri is set to dominate ), BBC Radio 1, Later With Jools Holland, been supported by Spotify s RADAR programme and more. Elsewhere, she s received recognition and support from the likes of Adele, Lewis Capaldi, Sam Fender and Florence Pugh, who starred in the video for Rachel s single Never Need Me .

title: The Remedy
description: Director: Alex Kahuam Cast: Timothy Granaderos, London Thor, Doug Jones, Jenny O Hara. A son s desperate attempt at keeping his mother alive leads him to making sacrifices in more ways than one in this stylish gothic horror. Jason (Timothy Granaderos) is desperate to keep his mother alive by any means, so when a not-dodgy-at-all scientist (played by genre icon Doug Jones) sells him an unconventional remedy promising to heal and extend the lifespan of anyone who takes it Jason is in. While the remedy works, it also gives Maria an insatiable appetite for live meat and Jason must provide. Meanwhile, his sister Rachel senses evil and turns to spells to expel it from their home. The siblings, while both desperate to keep their mother alive, clash over their approach, discovering what is truly haunting their home and their family. The second collaboration between director Alex Kahuam and writer Daniel Kuhlman, The Remedy is an unflinching and increasingly unhinged journey between reality, nightmare and family trauma. (ISRA AL KASSI)

title: Kizzi
description: Kizzi is a DJ who s carving her own path in the music world, blending UKG, club funk, and house with the vibrant influences of her South Asian Spice. She s made waves with her sets at Radio 1 s Big Weekend, Glastonbury Festival, and her own sell-out event, Kizzi Friends, which has packed out venues an impressive eight times.

title: AmyElle
description: Scottish DJ and producer AmyElle has rapidly built a global following through her creative, technically skilled DJ sets and distinctive productions. Hotly tipped by BBC Radio 1, Mixmag, DJ Mag and Spotify where she became the first DJ and first female artist to feature in its DJ Mixes series she broke through with her single Feel The Heat , which amassed millions of streams and earned a BBC Radio 1 nomination for Best Dance Record of 2021 . AmyElle has performed at leading venues and festivals including Tomorrowland, Fabric, Printworks, Warehouse Project, Exit Festival and Amnesia, sharing line-ups with artists such as Fatboy Slim, Faithless, Hot Since 82 and Joris Voorn. With releases on labels including Armada, Anjunabeats, Selador, and Colorize, she continues to establish herself as a rising force in electronic music.

title: Circa Waves - Wednesday
description: If you are a Platinum Pass Holder eligible for advance booking, then you can reserve your spot to see Circa Waves by RSVPing to the End of the trail showcase on the official SXSW London app. Please note that reservation spaces are limited and that entry is only guaranteed if you arrive before the showcase starts at 6:40pm. Admission for late arrivals will be subject to venue capacity. Circa Waves was formed in Liverpool in May 2013 by Kieran Shudall. After writing a batch of songs and putting some demos online, he recruited second guitarist Joe Falconer, bassist Sam Rourke, and drummer Sian Plummer (replaced by Colin Jones in 2015). The band first gained a buzz and earned favourable comparisons to the Strokes, Arctic Monkeys, and the Vaccines before they hit the Top Ten with their debut, 2015 s Young Chasers on Virgin/EMI. Buoyed by singles like T-Shirt Weather and Stuck in My Teeth , the album reached the Top Ten of the U.K. albums chart and paved the way for a series of well-attended live shows, including a sold-out date at London s O2 . Without shying away from their influences, the band matured, and albums like 2017 s Different Creatures , 2019 s What s It Like Over There? showcase their knack for crafting their own distinctively infectious, post-punk anthems. In 2020, they reached number four on the U.K. albums chart with Sad Happy and returned in 2023 with Never Going Under . The bands album released in 2025 Death Love Pt.1 is the first instalment of a snapshot in time - a reflection on a moment of true terror, and the joy of coming through the other side. It s a brave and remarkable next step for a band in the finest form of their career.

title: Future Utopia - Wednesday
description: Future Utopia, the innovative musical project led by Grammy-winning songwriter, producer Fraser T. Smith, takes listeners on hypnotic journeys through disco-infused synths, vocals, and feel-good basslines. Known for high-energy live shows with a rave-like atmosphere, the project blends infectious grooves with immersive soundscapes, pushing creative boundaries at every turn. Future Utopia released debut album 12 Questions, in October 2020, featuring an incredible roll call of some of Britain s most vital and brilliant artists, including Stormzy, Dave, Kano, Ghetts, Bastille, Es Devlin, Simon Armitage and Idris Elba. The album discusses issues including faith, freedom, race, gender, wealth, equality and ecology - all born of Fraser s own anxieties about the world today. In 2021, Fraser won the Ivor Novello Award for Best Contemporary Song for Future Utopia s Children Of The Internet, his collaboration with Dave, taken from 12 Questions. The win is the latest plaudit for a creative partnership between Smith and Dave that has seen the pair win two previous Ivor Novello Awards, first in 2018 with Best Contemporary Song for Dave s Question Time, before they went on to win the prestigious prize again in 2020 for Dave s track Black (from the Mercury Prize winning album Psychodrama), which they also performed together at The BRIT Awards in 2020. In 2023, Future Utopia returned with the We Were We Still Are EP, which featured the mesmerising spoken word artist Kae Tempest, the ethereal, haunting vocal of Biig Piig and LA based artist TOMI. The focus track This Time featured Fraser s lead vocals and paved the way for his sophomore album Django s High, which was released in July 2024. Future Utopia, made up on stage of Fraser (lead singer, guitar, synths), Anthony Sweetstix Lewis (drums), Molly J (backing vocals) have become known for their electric live shows, including a headline UK tour in October 2023, a tour of France, Belgium and the Netherlands in February 2025 and festival appearances at All Points East, Boardmasters, Lowlands and Pukkelpop to name a few. 2025 saw Future Utopia release two singles: Don t Try This At Home followed by The Pleasure Trap in collaboration with Lava La Rue. Big summer festival slots included headline sets at both Lost Village and Latitude. 2026 promises to be a strong year of growth for the artist, with a sold-out London Show in January followed by new music in March (Fraser T. Smith is a multi-award winning, internationally acclaimed artist, producer, songwriter and musician - one of the most in demand creators in contemporary music today. He has co-written, mixed and produced 7 Number One singles in the UK, 2 Billboard Number One singles in the US, and contributed to 20 Number One albums).

title: Sophie Faith
description: Faith s soulful vocals flawlessly resonate with the jazzy production, while her deeply personal and experiential lyrics reveal themselves to awe-struck listeners - Lyrical Lemonade Solidified as a reputable star of British soul, she can already name a remarkable list of collaborators, from the incomparable Stormzy to jazz maestro Reuben James and media support ranging from Jamz Supernova and Jamie Cullum to Complex and Earmilk. Emerging in 2017, Faith quickly amassed a loyal following. Breakout single Say So, featured on ITV2 s Love Island, was followed by ruminative Bad Blood and the up-tempo Salt. Yet by the time of the Covid pandemic, Faith was uncertain about pursuing music. It took the blockbuster single Heart Is On My Sleeve, co-written with Stormzy, to re-inspire her. In her own words, I needed something soothing and Stormzy really threw me a lifeline. With plaudits from Victoria Jane and DJ ACE, the track capped off a stunning five-track EP, 2022 s The Right Side of Wrong. Exec-produced by 5 x GRAMMY-nominated Linden Jay, and infused with gospel harmonies alongside James virtuosic piano stylings, Faith s EP is a tour de force of modern soul. Building on her packed-out appearance at the Jazz Cafe in 2021, Faith embarked on a sold-out headline tour across Manchester, Brighton, and London. Her 2024 Hold My Drink tour coincided with the EP release Hold My Drink, Watch This, adding international dates in Amsterdam, Berlin, and Warsaw. The EP s singles were well-received at her sold-out Lafayette show. A final track featuring Kwaku Asante awaits, and with her debut studio album on the horizon, Faith is set for more success. Simultaneously, her songwriting has attracted interest from industry figures like Swedish House Mafia, Gorgon City, and Erik the Architect. In 2024, she will release new work with Defected Records. Previous collaborations with DJ Zinc, Girls of The Internet, and Alix Perez have established Faith in the dance music scene, especially with performances singing for Girls of the Internet, notably at Glastonbury.

title: TaalHaus
description: TaalHaus brings together four dynamic artists from the UK South Asian music scene for a unique showcase. Blending Indian classical, folk, sufi, ghazal and contemporary sounds, the ensemble features internationally renowned multi-instrumentalist Sahib G, versatile percussionist and producer Shri Gadhvi, vocalist and harmonium player Nirmal Joshi, and powerhouse singer Kesh K. Together, TaalHaus delivers an immersive live experience rooted in tradition while pushing modern musical boundaries through rhythm, melody and improvisation. FULL ARTISTS BIOS : SAHIB G Sahib-G is a prominent figure in the British Indian music scene, renowned as a versatile musician, excelling in genres such as Bhangra, Bollywood, Ghazals, Bhajans, and Hindi-Pop. His musical journey began in 1982 as the youngest member of the acclaimed band Heera. Over the years, his artistry evolved through inspiration drawn from Carlos Santana, leading him to establish his own recording studio in Southall. He later released his debut solo album Manuscript and went on to produce music for internationally celebrated artists including Kumar Sanu, Jasbir Jassi, and many others. Highly regarded as a composer, mandolinist, guitarist, and multi-instrumentalist, Sahib-G has toured extensively across the globe alongside legendary performers such as Jagjit Singh, Diljit Dosanjh, Rahat Fateh Ali Khan, Bappi Lahiri, Jaspinder Narula, Shaan, and many more. His versatility and musicianship recently led to him being invited as a session musician to perform Rabab on the latest Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse project. His work also extends into theatre and musical development, with notable projects including Rifco s Britain s Got Bhangra, the RSC s Much Ado About Nothing, Kuljit Bhamra s Chutney in the Street, and music development work for the hit stage musical Bend It Like Beckham. Currently, Sahib-G serves as the Musical Director and initiator of the upcoming Ghazal Lounge UK project, continuing his commitment to celebrating and evolving South Asian music on an international platform. SHRI GADHVI A natural understanding of structures, beats, rhythm and melodies is what sets the basis of a good musician. Performed worldwide and produced various genres and for many artists. Shri Gadhvi has mastered all these aspects and has now set the par for many musicians/producers with the unique and diverse style that he brings to the music scene. Shri s background and family history has encouraged him to take steps into his ancestral customs leading him down the musical path. Born in North London, Shri s admiration for musical rhythms sprung at an early age of 2 naturally, when his inquisition led him to play the TABLA. He was learning from The late Shri Pandit Sharda Sharda at the age of 5. His skill for this instrument has not gone without merit and has been constantly acknowledged and admired for his expertise. At the age of 7 he also picked up another instrument called the DHOL and began to do performances around the UK. From the age of 16, Shri has been travelling the world with concerts and performances in France, Spain, Portugal, Switzerland, Sweden, Norway, India, Dubai, Thailand, Bali + many more. Shri has also performed at many prestigious and well known venues such as The Royal Albert Hall, Wembley Arena, Berklee College of Music, the Commonwealth Games, BBC broadcasting house, The Troxy and many more. Along with performing in some profound places around the world, he has also accompanied some great artists such as Anup Jalota, Ashit Hema Desai, Pritam Chakrabarty, Harshdeep Kaur, Shankar Mahadevan, Kavita Krishnamurty, Talat Aziz, Richa Sharma, Mahalaxmi Iyer, Anuradha Paudwal, Sadhana Sargam, Sonu Nigam, Radhika Chopra, Jazim Sharma and majority of the Indian Idol show winners. Shri has made a name for himself in the world as a remarkable and gifted producer/percussionist and has been passing down his knowledge to music students since 2013 when he launched London School of Tabla. It is without a doubt we can expect unique and vibrant compilations from this upcoming talent. NIRMAL JOSHI Nirmal Joshi started his training at the age of 6 with Shri Chandravadan Engineer. Having received classical training for several years, he learned tabla from Puish Kumarji of Benaras Gharana. He has also received formal training from Pt Ramesh Jhule of Kirana Gharana. Nirmal is also a qualified teacher, with a PGCE from the renowned UCL institute of education. Nirmal has been performing since the age of 10. He has performed at many prestigious venues such as the Dorchester Hotel, the Royal Albert Hall, the Nehru Centre, and has made several TV appearances. He has also performed internationally on a number of occasions. Nirmal is highly skilled in singing and performing many different genres of music including ghazal, sufi, bhajan, folk, jazz and classical. With his guidance and teachings, you will excel in the world of Sangeet. KESH K A world class vocalist from Bedford UK. Worked with the legendary Sachin Ahuja for Kesh s second ever release and has worked with so many great artists and musicians and has also started KBH Academy teaching Indian classical instruments and vocal.

title: amaka.
description: amaka. is a Nigerian-born Alt-R B artist based in Toronto s east end. She is known for her soul-bearing lyrics and ethereal, emotionally immersive live performances. Her work has been recognized by Bandcamp as Best Soul Projects 2021 and supported by CBC Music, NOW Magazine, SOCAN, Lyrical Lemonade, BBC Radio 1Xtra and more. amaka. has graced stages across Canada, the U.S. and Amsterdam, building a reputation as a powerful live artist. 2025 saw amaka. s live performances go viral on YouTube, introducing her to a growing international audience. With a genre-exploring sound rooted in soul and a commanding stage presence, amaka. delivers intimate, resonant performances that are remembered long after the set ends.

title: Cordelia
description: Cordelia O Driscoll is a storyteller. Her lifelong fascination with the lore of classic songwriters has grounded her in the folk tradition of meditating on life s small moments. On her debut Caramel EP and its standout single Little Life , she muses on fulfilment, gratitude and being happy with what you have. With her dual background in theatre and music, O Driscoll s love of performance taught her to weave magic on stage. Having cut her teeth gigging around the country since she was 15 years old, her tales are sung with wisdom and world class, ethereal vocals. Living in London since 2020, O Driscoll worked in the Pavilion Cafe in Victoria Parkthrough two years of pandemic lockdowns. Despite the 5am starts in deep winter and 12-hour shifts, she fell in love with the simplicity of the job and her uninterrupted life outside of work. I thought, maybe I ll just do this, maybe I don t need more. Bottling that realisation, she wrote Little Life . Since the song s release in October 2023, millions of people all over the world have connected with its messages of simplicity and escape; the perfect introduction to the work of a generational talent. 2025 has seen Cordelia put out her debut long-player everything hurts (i love you) - a beautiful collection of stirring folk-tinged indie, with hints of Joni Mitchell meshing with O Driscoll s effortlessly witty observational lyricism. The album varies from musing on the ebb and flow of friendship, to drunken arguments, and notes on growing older, constantly showcasing O Driscoll s ability to write songs like journal entries. Capturing these moments in time, sometimes they re incredibly fleeting, she explains. Then you perform them and you re put back in that headspace, going back in time. It s like life flashing before your eyes.

title: Kizzi
description: Kizzi is a DJ who s carving her own path in the music world, blending UKG, club funk, and house with the vibrant influences of her South Asian Spice. She s made waves with her sets at Radio 1 s Big Weekend, Glastonbury Festival, and her own sell-out event, Kizzi Friends, which has packed out venues an impressive eight times.

title: Laurence Guy
description: Laurence Guy is no stranger to throwing a party. When he was younger, he and his friendswould take over village halls or pubs in his hometown of Guildford. That urge contnues to thisday, where he regularly puts on free daytme raves at his Stoke Newington local, The WhiteHart. It was an idea partly to help pubs in difcult post-Covid tmes, but it s spiralled into a wayfor him to do something for his fans. Last year he hit up a uni house party for a surprise show the police turned up, the speakers fell over, and it ended up being one of the funnest gigshe s done (despite regularly playing at some of the UK s most iconic venues and festvalsincluding Printworks, Glastonbury, Southbank Centre and Fabric). Music s power is in creatng community, and this is the essence of Laurence Guy s work. TheLondon-based DJ and producer s moto is music to make you feel feelings : whether that scomfort, nostalgia, bitersweetness or melancholy, he s triggering emotons in the listener. Hissound has an unmistakable warmth while being squarely aimed at the dancefoor: samples ftinto sprawling keys, and dusty drums thud under lush chords. Then there s the upbeateuphoria, which Guy s productons are increasingly embracing as he enters a new phase of hiscaree

title: Zumba + Lift Morning Workout
hall: Dray Walk Gallery - Zumba House, Class

title: Female Angel Investors Power Breakfast with BackedVC
description: Start your day surrounded by incredible women who angel invest. This special SXSW 2026 edition brings together a curated group of thoughtful, ambitious women investors for high-quality, meaningful conversation. What to expect? This is a no panels, no fluff event just a table full of smart, ambitious women trading real insights about what works (and what doesn t) in angel investing. You ll be matched with fellow women angels at a similar stage of experience, with an intention to make every conversation relevant, practical, and high-value. Expect honest knowledge-sharing, meaningful new connections, and the kind of peer support that fuels better investing. Hosted alongside the energy of SXSW, this edition brings together a uniquely dynamic mix of perspectives where innovation, creativity, and capital intersect. The conversations, learning, and connections created around the table are hard to put into words you really have to experience it. We operate under Chatham House Rules so it s a safe space for openness. Please note: all meet-ups are first come, first served once capacity is reached, entry will close. Arrive early, as spaces are limited.

title: Zumba Morning Workout
hall: Dray Walk Gallery - Zumba House, Class

title: Waymo Studio + Cafe
description: Open Monday-Thursday 09.00am-5.30pm. Experience the future of mobility at Waymo Studio + Cafe in Protein Studios. Grab a cuppa and see firsthand how our fully autonomous, sustainable and accessible driver will integrate into London transportation network.

title: Zumba Taster
hall: Dray Walk Gallery - Zumba House, Class

title: Designing Trust: How Legacy Brands Win in an Age of AI, Anxiety and Algorithmic Culture
description: As technology outpaces society s ability to adapt, trust has become one of the world s most fragile and valuable currencies. From AI-driven finance to TikTok-shaped culture, brands must now be transparent, emotionally intelligent and culturally relevant. Wolff Olins CEO Sairah Ashman will be joined by a Suresh Balaji, CMO at Lloyds Bank, to unpack how design can restore confidence amid declining institutional trust, discussing behavioural design, the impact of AI and new interfaces on expectations, how legacy brands can reinvent without losing themselves, and how design can humanise financial services. The session offers leaders a blueprint for staying credible and empathetic.

title: Human First: Designing AI That Empowers, Not Replaces
description: In the race to scale AI, it s easy to lose sight of who we re building for. This panel centers on a simple idea: consumers are human. We ll explore a human-centered design philosophy for AI one that prioritizes empowerment over automation, and relationships over transactions. Rather than optimizing for enterprise workflows alone, what does it look like to create AI that genuinely understands, supports, and collaborates with people?In the race to scale AI, it s easy to lose sight of who we re building for. This panel centers on a simple idea: consumers are human. We ll explore a human-centered design philosophy for AI one that prioritizes empowerment over automation, and relationships over transactions. Rather than optimizing for enterprise workflows alone, what does it look like to create AI that genuinely understands, supports, and collaborates with people?

title: The Media Empire Playbook: Scaling Your Content, Team & IP
description: Creators are rapidly evolving into mini studios, business owners, and even media empires. In this session, creator and media empire leader Simon Squibb, creatorpreneur Maddie Borge, and the strategic force behind BrandonB, Josh Saffer, will reveal their secrets to making this transition successfully. You will learn how to build optimized workflows from content pipelines to team structures and how to forge a powerful brand identity that connects with audiences across all platforms. The session also covers how to use music strategically, navigate copyright, and protect and scale IP into valuable, long-term assets.

title: From Pixels to Places: The Race to Model Reality
description: AI, AR, and 3D tech are transforming pixels into immersive, interactive spaces. The challenge is blending technical precision with emotional impact to create experiences that feel real and meaningful. Designers race to not just replicate reality, but reimagine it.

title: Opening Remarks
description: Opening remarks

title: Opening Remarks
description: Opening remarks

title: Opening remarks
description: Opening remarks

title: The Real Rise of the Simulator
description: Simulators are everywhere. They re certainly not new, but they ve not been this popular in years. Whether you want to sell trading cards, harvest crops, wash a car or be a goat - there s a sim for you. So what is it about these low-octane, occasionally surreal and supposedly mundane games that garners such success? What does it say about the modern gamer when escapism moves from the fantastical to the routine?

title: Abbey Road: Studio Four
description: Abbey Road: Studio Four, in association with Meta, is happening at 93 Feet East, Brick Lane, on 3 4 June. Studio Four is not a programme, a conference, or a showcase. It s a live creative environment - a place where the conversations, collisions and breakthroughs reshaping music, film, technology and art are happening in front of you. We re living through a moment of genuine rupture and renaissance in how music is made, heard, scored, performed, owned and felt. Studio Four puts you inside that moment. Not as an observer, but as someone who was there, and who helped shape it. As you move through the space, tracks are broken down and rebuilt live with audience input. Performances are captured and re-experienced in immersive spatial sound. Conversations that won t exist anywhere else bring together the people who defined the past with those actively reshaping the future. New creative tools are tested in public for the first time, by the people whose creative practices will determine how they re used. There is no boundary between process and performance, artist and audience. Everything is immediate, participatory and alive. You have to be there. Not to watch what s next. To step inside it while it s still being figured out.

title: Alex Faust
description: Alex Faust is a commercially focused operator in advertising and media technology, specialising in growth strategies across the US and EMEA. He has a track record of scaling revenue, building diverse teams, and initiating partnerships. He is an angel investor in consumer and tech startups. Currently Innovation and Partnerships Director at Bauer Media, he previously held commercial leadership roles at Spotify, Microsoft and adtech startups. He advises founders on business development, fundraising, and GTM. Join the two behind the operation at After Party Studios to discuss how you keep the crown.

title: Virtual Hospitals and Health-Centred Urban Design for All
description: In this session, we will explore the art of the possible and reimagine how healthcare should look by 2050. Join a panel of pioneers and experts across health, industry, investment and urban development as we discuss and design our city s future health landscape. Consider how virtual hospitals could transform access to care, how new homes, workplaces and public spaces can be shaped to support healthier living and why co-creation with local communities is central to this vision. Together, lets make London a place where people can thrive and lead healthy lives as we build an inclusive and future ready capital.

title: What if We Tried Talking to Young Men Instead of Just Talking About Them?
description: The male loneliness epidemic has been a key topic of discussion and concern for the past few years. And for good reason. Nearly 1 in 4 young men (15-34) report frequent loneliness, and men are 3.6 times more likely to die by suicide than women. As YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, Snap and more play active and everyday roles in the lives of young men, some of the content on these social platforms is undoubtedly contributing to the increasing loneliness, anxiety and depression among that same demographic. So Movember, the leading charity in men s health, set out to speak directly to these young men rather than just speaking about them. How? By meeting them where they are. In this session, Becca Hutson Editor-in-chief of The News Movement will sit down with Tom Ellis from Movember and content creator Olly Bowman to discuss how they re working together to offer young men an online space that is judgment-free while discussing the real and relatable struggles they face every day.

title: Unlocking Adaptive Spaces with Total Experience Design
description: Cultural infrastructure, sports stadiums and commercial developments need to be more adaptable than ever before to connect to the broadest range of audiences and maximise the return of investment. But a new approach is needed to develop transformative experiences for them. This talk will highlight the incredible opportunity for these sectors and share a new model to better develop the experiences that will drive them forward.

Currently Innovation and Partnerships Director at Bauer Media, he previously held commercial leadership roles at Spotify, Microsoft and adtech startups. He advises founders on business development, fundraising, and GTM. title: How Esports Are Unlocking the Next Level of Live Entertainment
description: Esports has moved from basements to billion-dollar arenas redefining what live entertainment means. This session brings together leading voices from venue operations, game development, professional play, and journalism to explore how live Esports has evolved into immersive fan experiences that rival major music tours and traditional sporting events, and how virtual worlds are now being brought to life through cutting-edge production and technology inside of iconic venues around the globe.

title: Your Next Colleague Might Be an Avatar
description: Victor Riparbelli, Founder CEO - Synthesia, will uncover why the future of video is interactive. He ll examine how AI avatars are moving beyond simple narration to become sophisticated personal tutors, capable of real-time roleplaying and workplace mentorship. Using Synthesia s pioneering platform, Victor will demonstrate how AI is transforming video through unique avatar customisation and envision building a future in which AI seamlessly enhances the human-centred workplace.

title: The Future of Advertising Is Already on Your Screen
description: Last year, SILVERSIDE AI helped Coca-Cola reimagine its iconic Holidays Are Coming campaign as a fully AI-generated film a cultural flashpoint that sparked both fascination and debate across the advertising industry. Now, Johnny Rohrbach, co-founder of independent AI innovation lab SILVERSIDE, joins Hannah Bowler, The Subthread, for a candid conversation about what that moment revealed: AI isn t a future trend anymore, it s already reshaping how brands create, produce, and tell stories. From global campaigns for Coca-Cola and LVMH to entirely new creative workflows, this session explores how AI is transforming the economics and craft of advertising while raising bigger questions around originality, authorship, and human taste. As content becomes infinite and production barriers disappear, what will separate meaningful creative work from everything else?

title: Beyond the Prompt: What Fashion Brands Are Learning About AI and Creative Direction
description: More than two years into the widespread adoption of generative AI, fashion brands are moving beyond experimentation. As AI begins to shape campaigns, visuals and even creative direction, the real question is no longer what s possible, but what should remain human. This conversation brings together brand leaders and cultural thinkers to explore where judgment, taste and authorship still matter most and how fashion brands are navigating this shift in practice.

title: Designing for Musicians, not Machines: How AI Becomes a True Creative Partner
description: AI music tools have unlocked extraordinary possibilities for creators, but their real power is only realized when people can understand, trust, and enjoy using them. This presentation features Jardson Almeida, co-founder of Moises, who will explore how musicians are embracing these tools and how companies can design products that meet users where they are. Attendees will learn practical strategies for helping people get more value from AI tools, understand how transparency and thoughtful UX supported by design psychology build trust, and see how the industry is working to make powerful technology genuinely accessible for career musicians and excited beginners alike

title: Fashion in Gaming: Building the Gaming IP Distribution Layer for Fashion
description: Fashion is locked out of gaming. Every garment a brand creates gets rebuilt three to five times to reach a single game, weeks of work, thousands of dollars, and the cultural moment is gone before it lands. A $180B market that fashion can barely reach, and where brand IP gets lost the moment it leaves the studio. Alvalan is changing that. We ve built the infrastructure that turns a sketch into a game-ready wearable in minutes, the same standardising role Spotify plays for music, but for fashion in gaming. And we re doing it in a way that protects and authenticates brand IP automatically as it moves from design to deployment.In this session, watch it happen live. Founder and CEO Louise Laing will demo the platform on stage, turning a fashion sketch into a deployable Roblox asset in real time. She ll share the technology behind it, AI-driven caging, rigging and weight painting and how Alvalan is enabling brands to scale into gaming with their IP protected, automated, and earning at every step.It s colourful, it s fast, and it s a category being built in the UK in real time.We want to make fashion in gaming open to everyone and to give brands the tools to own it.Come and see how.

title: Scaling Your Socials
description: Growing on social media today requires more than consistency it requires strategy. Chris Donnelly shares the systems, content frameworks, and audience insights that turn posts into scalable growth. Learn how to build authority, expand reach, and turn social platforms into real business leverage.

title: Sync 101: How to Get Your Music into Stuff
description: Sync isn t new. Nick Drake soundtracked a VW advert in the late 90s. John Lewis reinvented the Christmas advert by pairing high-budget emotional storytelling with slowed-down musical covers since 2009 and and Linkin Park will always be synonymous with the Bayhem of the late 2000s. But it s arguably never been more lucrative for artists, whether starting out or long-established. Since Sophie Ellis Bextor s meteoric run up the charts in 2024 via Saltburn, the process and potential of sync has reached a new generation of artists hoping to make it big in the industry anyway they can. Want to know how to get your music synced? This is the session for you.

title: Zumba Taster
hall: Dray Walk Gallery - Zumba House, Class

title: Paradise Presents: Why Reinvention is the Most Powerful Creative Tool
description: Reinvention isn t a comeback strategy. It s a creative advantage. In this conversation, John Newman shares the reality of evolving in public, from global chart success to launching his own label and reworking his most iconic record for a new generation. What does it actually take to rebuild momentum when the world thinks it alreadyFrom music to branding, this session explores why the most relevant creators and brands don t defend their past, they redesign it. A candid conversation about risk, control, storytelling and why reinvention might be the most powerful tool in modern creativity.

title: Deconstructing IP: The Cake Theory of Music, Film & Sports — From Cultural Ideas to Tradeable Global Assets.
Last Night a DJ Saved My Night
description: A night out in the Big Smoke is an event in itself. Whether you re local or shuttling down for the weekend - London remains a staple city for a good time - but it needs some help. Drawn from all corners of the capital s nightlife scene, our panelists take on the naysayers, investigate the barriers facing modern venues and call for a fairer, funkier night out for all parties.

title: Workshop: How to Build a Hit Immersive Show in Nine Months Ft. 'COME ALIVE!' The Greatest Showman Circus Spectacular
description: Nine months. One empty site. A global entertainment brand. A new immersive circus spectacular. How do you build a major immersive live show from scratch, create a world audiences want to step into, and open it in just nine months? With over 400,000 tickets sold to date, with an average of 95% capacity this session goes behind the scenes of COME ALIVE! The Greatest Showman Circus Spectacular at Earls Court. Moderated by landlords, The Earls Court Development Company, it brings together the show s creative teams across costume, set design, choreography and the producers behind the smash-hit show.

title: Alex Faust
description: Alex Faust is a commercially focused operator in advertising and media technology, specialising in growth strategies across the US and EMEA. title: Artists x Earth: Protect the Planet with Music
description: Music moves people faster than policy. Leading artists are standing up to protect the planet by partnering with new nature protection solutions, turning creativity into real-world impact. Bringing together pioneers from the music industry and nature protection organisations, we share case studies including EarthPercent s Sounds Right campaign, where nature is credited as an artist and receives royalties, Uon.Earth inviting us to protect a m2 of nature for 1.20, and Global Citizen s live events that deliver social impact. We discuss how artists, audiences, and nature protectors come together to collaborate and inspire urgent action for Earth.

Today, she is Director of Strategic Initiatives at the International Rescue Committee, deploying AI in complex humanitarian environments. She can speak to mentees on AI, digital transformation and strategy. She holds an MFA from Columbia University and an MSW from NYU, with postgraduate training from Cornell-Weill. She lives in Connecticut and works from New York and London. title: Contagion Revisited: A Pandemic of Mistrust
description: In 2011, hit thriller Contagion felt a world away. Then, the COVID-19 pandemic transformed imagined prophecies into universal realities. Hear from the architects behind Contagion as they reflect on the project s inception, predictions and real-world parallels fifteen years following the film s release. As we now face the long-term implications of pandemic fatigue, this conversation offers a vital look at how creative storytelling may be the tool that gets us back on track.

title: Spain in Transmission: New Digital Work
description: Spain is a country with a rich tradition in the visual arts, known best for classical masters such as Goya and Velazquez and institutions such as The Prado. However, Spanish culture has evolved to focus on innovation, and this is reflected by a wave of contemporary artists who are well aware of tradition but who are making work using the latest digital tools including AI. The participants will discuss the impact of digital tools on their practice. Their work will be on view in the Truman Brewery for the duration of SXSW London 2026.

title: How the Next Generation of European Start-Ups Can Crack the US
description: European tech is having a moment: public and private tech companies are now worth nearly $4 trillion and a new generation of breakout companies is proving that global ambition can be built from anywhere in Europe. Even though European founders now have global ambitions from day one, to scale globally is still a significant challenge. Timing, location, and your first hires can make or break the move. In this conversation, Reshma Sohoni and Matt Robinson draw on their hard-won experience building, backing and scaling European companies in the US to help founders think through when to go, where to land, and how to scale without losing what makes European companies unique.

title: Building a Better Internet for Creativity
description: Join Patreon Chief Operating Officer Paige Fitzgerald in conversation to explore how creators are navigating a new era of the internet, where AI is reshaping what gets made, who gets paid, and what creative ownership even means. Over $2B already flows annually from fans to the Patreon creators they care about, signaling a shift away from the attention economy toward a more intentional one.

title: Memes Are Money: Rewiring the Fandom Economy with Gen Alpha
description: What do Dogecoin, Italian Brainrot, and Skibidi Toilet have in common? They re all billion-dollar brands that started out as memes. The media industry is still wrestling with the implications - but Gen Alpha are way ahead of them. The new attention economy is being forged by community-owned brands that propagate through memes, social video, and UGC gaming. Hew Moran (8m+ YouTuber aka the internet s sharpest meme mind ) and Nick Walters (Strategist at Gen Alpha marketing specialist SuperAwesome) dive deep into a swamp of s**tposts, sneaker-clad sharks, and skibidi slop, emerging with insights on the future of digital media.

title: How Black Founders are redefining the Creative Economy
description: The Digital Catapult Black Founders Programme in partnership with Sony Music and most recently Channel 4, has run for 3 years and has supported over 30 black founded companies within the creative technology sector with 9 companies going on to gain funding worth over 1.8m. This session will highlight the founders from within the programme to deep dive into their businesses, where they are post programme, what it takes to successfully raise funding, the real challenges as a black founder within the creative space, as well as hearing from Channel 4 and Sony Music into how founders from diverse backgrounds are really challenging the status quo, the importance of supporting programmes like this and the longer term impacts that come about.

title: How a supportive community can help save lives
description: Hear from Simon Gunning, CEO of the suicide prevention charity, Campaign Against Living Miserably (CALM) CALM was the most awarded brand in 2023, which included taking home the Cannes Lions Grand Prix for their incredible campaign: The Last Photo. Hear from their CEO, who will be talking about the importance of creating a culture in which we are all willing and able to prevent suicide for ourselves and each other.

title: Jodie Bryant
description: Jodie Bryant is a presenter and radio host. She is the host of the Future Pop show on Radio 1 and has hosted the AIM Independent Music Awards, as well as presented for the BBC Children in Need livestream. She is an ambassador for Youth Music, recognising her work championing new artists and grassroots music. Jodie also hosts her own live music night, Discover Live, supporting emerging artists, some of whom have gone on to break through following performances on the platform. She also presents her digital series Fangirl Files, where she interviews artists, celebrities and personalities as a super-fan. Guests have included pop icon Cher Lloyd, December10 and Mimi Webb.

title: Max Cooper - Spatial Sound Installation presented by L-Acoustics
description: Max Cooper is an electronic composer, multidisciplinary artist, music label founder and former scientist who has carved out a unique space in music and visual art. He holds a PhD in computational biology while also being the first contemporary electronic musician to perform at the ancient Acropolis theatre in Athens and one of the first wave of artists to produce in Dolby Atmos. Cooper has spent over 15 years exploring the intersections between the arts and sciences through music, collaborations, and his label Mesh, alongside installations, performances, immersive experiences, online media, music videos and live events. Anchored throughout is his emotive approach, connecting how ideas and forms feel in a way that is accessible to all. I ve always had a strong emotive bond with aesthetics, and found at an early age that the purified forms of electronic music carried a lot of weight for me, as did the reductive natural aesthetics of the sciences. That felt connection to both opened up a world I ve been lost in ever since. Pivoting from computational biology to electronic music might not seem like the most natural career transition, but for Cooper the commonalities are clear: In both fields, you re in a sense free. Free from the limits of living systems, free from the limits of corporeal sound. The only constraints are in your mind. Max Cooper has collaborated with Zaha Hadid Architects, Dolby, L-Acoustics, 4DSOUND, Barbican Centre, W1 Curates, The Babraham Institute, and has exhibited work at institutions including the Centre Pompidou Metz, ZKM Karlsruhe, Museum Wave Seoul and the Science Museum London.

title: Offline by FORA
description: The latest event from Fora, launching during SXSW London. Designed to get people up from their desks and mingling with other industry pros. Offline s first event will feature live music from Soho Radio DJs Ross Allen (12:00 14:00), Kate Hutchinson (14:00 16:00), Karim Bitar (16:00 18:00) and Noble Heath (18:00 21:00), offer a selection of great cuisines from three food stalls and drinks aplenty from a pop-up bar. Cheers! As well, you can also get your Michelin fill from Legado, your specialty coffee fix from Flying Horse and tuck into authentic Thai dishes at Singburi.

title: Responsible AI and the Future of Sustainability
description: When much of the conversation remains stuck between hype and ethics, this is a chance to focus on the business reality: how do companies scale AI in ways that benefit people, planet, and long-term resilience? This roundtable brings together leaders from sustainability, procurement, operations, and business strategy. The conversation will examine how businesses can scale these technologies in ways that create value rather than new fragilities.

title: New London: A Design-Led Vision for Solving Britain’s Housing Crisis
description: The UK s housing crisis is often framed as a planning problem - but what if the real issue is scale - and confidence? In this SXSW London fireside, Gensler s Ian Mulcahey and Andy Silverster, London Editor at The Times, will discuss why incremental development and New Towns may no longer be enough to meet the demands of population growth, infrastructure pressure and long-term economic resilience. At the centre of the conversation is a broader argument: the UK has become increasingly cautious about ambitious city building at precisely the moment it needs greater confidence in shaping its future. While other global cities and nations continue to think boldly about land creation, infrastructure and long-term urban expansion, the UK often remains constrained by short-termism and political hesitation. The session explores a more radical proposition: what if the UK started thinking about creating entirely new land and new urban territories? From reclaimed coastal landscapes in places like the Netherlands, Singapore and Hong Kong to the future potential of offshore infrastructure, the conversation examines how countries are physically expanding to support housing, industry, renewable energy and food production - and asks whether the UK has the political and cultural appetite to think at that scale again.Topics explored include: Whether the UK is running out of viable development land The limitations of continued green belt and industrial land reuse Why the country needs renewed confidence in large-scale city building How renewable energy infrastructure is already reshaping territorial boundaries The role of major infrastructure in unlocking long-term growth Whether building more land could become a legitimate future strategy for housing, jobs and national resilience Framed as a provocative conversation about cities, democracy and national ambition, the session challenges audiences to reconsider the physical and political limits of urban growth in the 21st century.

title: London in Video Games
description: Prepare for a high-octane blend of digital archaeology and chaos as NakeyJakey bounces onto the SXSW London stage atop his signature yoga ball, teasing a mysterious new twist that remains strictly under wraps. In this erratic yet strangely poignant visual presentation, the Snakeboy himself dives deep into the uncanny valley of virtual London, dissecting the medium s obsession with translating the Big Smoke into playable pixels. Expect a fever dream of rapid-fire edits, nostalgic deep-dives into questionable NPC accents, and a heartfelt interrogation of the architectural liberties taken by developers across the decades. It s an eclectic exploration of the UK s capital that promises to be equal parts insightful critique and pure, unadulterated yoga-ball-fueled mayhem.

title: So you’ve Cracked the Algorithm: Here’s how you Don’t Break it!
description: When something finally clicks and the algorithm starts working in your favour, the real challenge begins. This talk explores how to stay consistent, keep your content fresh, and grow sustainably without overthinking your success into failure.

title: The Main Event
description: The business of sport is growing. Sport is fast becoming a global, diversified, and fully-fledged asset class, fuelled by investment, innovation, and the increasingly sophisticated expectations of fans. At the same time, the platforms shaping its future are changing the game, moving well beyond traditional broadcasting. Live sport is now one part of the puzzle, embedded within broader entertainment ecosystems, blending streaming with social, interactive, and always-on fan experiences. From global fight nights to second-screen strategies, this session explores how the future of sport is being built for a fully global and more connected audience and what that shift means for platforms and partners responding to a new set of expectations.

title: WTF is Neuroaesthetics?
description: Join us to learn how the newest branch of neuroscience is changing how we feel, think and connect. You ve heard of neuroscience. You ve heard of aesthetics. But put them together and suddenly we re asking some of the most exciting, and urgent, questions in design, culture and public health. How does art change our brains? How does music help us synchronise? Why do some spaces make us feel energised and others leave us cold? And what happens when we actually start measuring how our brains and bodies respond? Neuroaesthetics is the science of how aesthetic experience shapes the mind and body, and it s revolutionising how we think about visual design and marketing, arts spaces, cultural institutions and the built environment. Galleries, concert halls, immersive experiences and civic spaces aren t just for leisure or entertainment. They re sites of genuine healing, connection and transformation - and we now have the technologies to show it. In a world of growing quantification, most technology hands us a verdict. Maybe your smartwatch tells you you re stressed, you didn t recover, you slept poorly. Kinda s work offers a counterpoint to traditional data communication approaches. Join us to explore how neuroaesthetics is being applied across sectors, starting with marketing and the built environment, and moving to arts and culture. While neuroaesthetics is informing design across all fields, the newest paradigms for emotional data visualisation are currently being pioneered in cultural design. We ll share how these art-science approaches are changing the way we see human responses, arguing that making space for multiple ways of knowing, curiosity and interpretation is crucial to the new frontier of cultural and civic design, as a model of care.

title: Built Local, Going Global: Investing Beyond the Usual Map
description: At a moment when more investors are rethinking where innovation happens, this panel will examine what it means to build locally and what the firm Union Heritage Ventures sees on The Horizon. This discussion will unpack the lessons that come from backing overlooked ecosystems before they become obvious: how pattern recognition is formed, how conviction is built ahead of consensus, and how a disciplined investment approach can uncover differentiated opportunities beyond the coasts. For anyone thinking about the future of venture, emerging markets of innovation, and where the next generation of high-impact companies may come from, this conversation offers a timely look at what investors see when they know how to look beyond the usual map.

title: Murdoch Mysteries: Always On - Building TV Fandom that Outlasts the Screen
description: As distribution becomes harder to pin down and hits die young, TV IP is increasingly treated as an always-on framework rather than a season-to-season event. This panel explores how one long-running series continues to draw audiences through a rewatch podcast, social storytelling, IRL experiences, stunt casting that turns surprise appearances into lasting returns, curriculum tie-ins, and Roblox-style worlds that turn passive viewers into active participants. When audiences come from different places, platforms, and often years apart, what keeps them watching the show? We ll hear from some of TV s leading world builders (and Jason Mewes, one very unexpected superfan) on how they approach production, distribution, and fandom to keep the series relevant and growing nearly 350 episodes after the original pitch.

title: The Agent Has Entered the Store: How Agentic AI is Reshaping Commerce
description: As commerce races toward a new frontier, agentic AI is shifting the landscape from traditional digital assistance to fully autonomous AI systems capable of planning, deciding, and executing customer journeys end-to-end. This evolution is rewriting how industries, including financial services, technology, consumer goods, and travel, engage with their customers. Join senior leaders from across sectors for an insightful discussion moderated by Muriel Schtickzelle, Head of Quotient and Customer, Innovation and Growth Europe at Oliver Wyman. The panel will explore three key agentic AI economies shaping the customer of tomorrow.

title: Workshop: Stop Renting Gen Z’s Attention: From Creator Reach to Maker Trust
description: Brands know they need creators. Most just use them too late. The prevailing model is broken: build the campaign, then bring in creators to push it out. By that time, the idea is fixed and often already out of step with the communities it s meant to reach. Because Gen Z doesn t respond to reach. They respond to relevance. Not to messaging, but to meaning. Meaning made with communities. Communities who hold trust. This is the shift: from creators as channels to makers as collaborators. In a world where trust beats scale, brands need a curated, embedded network of Gen Z creators who don t just amplify ideas but shape them from the start. People who live the culture, understand the nuance, and build with communities, not for them. This is even more crucial in an age where AI can accelerate output but can t replace cultural instinct. The cost of getting culture wrong isn t just underperformance, it s irrelevance, diminished growth and the loss of trust. This session marks the launch of Edelman s Gen Z MakersBench, a global resource of highly vetted makers who are the ultimate collaboration source. Backed by the first trusted creator certification of its kind, this delivers unprecedented reassurance for brands that the recommended creators are thoroughly safe partners and makers. Because the future isn t about renting attention at scale. It s about earning trust through the people who shape it.

title: Accessibility & the City: Making London Work for Everyone
description: London is resilient, diverse and full of potential. But how easy is it to get around? Can we do better - if so what does better look like?

title: Bosh! A Creator Takeaway With John Fisher
description: Join us for an unfiltered fireside chat with TikTok sensation John Fisher, the creator behind the viral BoshTok! accounts. We ll dive into his journey from everyday creator to internet icon, unpacking the lessons he s learned along the way. Hear how he s built successful brand partnerships while staying true to his unique style, and discover the secrets to creating content that truly resonates.

title: Where Next for Mobile Gaming?
description: Mobile gaming is a powerhouse creative industry, generating major economic value, cultural influence, and millions of daily moments of play right in the palm of your hand. At the top of its game, where can the industry go next?

title: The Next Frontier: Audience Building in the Platform Era – with Ant & Dec
description: Ant Dec are UK television royalty. They have created some of the most iconic moments in British television - but part of their enduring success has been always looking to what s next. Over three decades, they ve achieved a level of popularity across age groups that is unique in the entertainment space simultaneously engaging millions of fans across Instagram, Tik Tok and other platforms, alongside their primetime ITV shows. In 2026, they re building further. From launching a hit podcast Hanging Out With Ant Dec earlier this year, to their own digital entertainment network Beltabox, to piloting social formats through their production company Mitre Studios they are actively redefining what it means to be an entertainment brand in 2026.

title: Aditi Rajagopal
description: Aditi is Head of Immersive Experience Design at Atlantic Productions, designing new ways to tell stories with cutting edge immersive technology across VR, AR, web and IRL. She previously worked in creative and innovation teams at Meta across India, Southeast Asia and the UK. She is an advocate for diversity in the creative industry, spending her free time guest lecturing, mentoring early career creatives and love bombing new hobbies. title: Christopher Morrison
description: Christopher Morrison is a writer, director and educator whose work spans over 200 projects across immersive theatre, VR, video games, film and live performance. He has collaborated with AAA game studios, Cirque du Soleil and P G. His VR project The Werewolf Experience played at Cannes Film Festival in 2023. He is an XR mentor for SXSW and has spoken at the UK House of Lords. As an Adjunct Professor at California College of the Arts, he teaches immersive storytelling. His next VR project, Drunk Zombie Duelists, received a Dutch government grant. Cirque du Soleil has called him a huge asset to the creative process. www.realityplus.org Information for mentees: - In order to book a session with one of our mentors, please download the SXSW London app. - Please arrive 5 minutes early to check in for your mentor session. title: Deirdre Parsons
description: Deirdre Parsons has led DLF UK since its establishment in 2012, managing meditation and healthcare projects across diverse settings, from businesses to schools, universities, prisons, veterans, addiction recovery, homelessness charities, and the NHS. With 30 years experience as a TM teacher, she has made significant contributions to numerous research studies on Transcendental Meditation. title: Francesca R Zerenghi
description: An Entertainment Publicist and Founder of FRAN, an independent curated publicity consultancy representing Directors of Photography, Directors and companies across the screen industry, Francesca Zerenghi champions artistry with UK roots and a global reach, with a focus on authenticity and long-term career development, an inclusive mindset, and a creative approach to building talent visibility, reputation and professional reach. Her expertise spans Talent Publicity, Networking Impact, Panels Masterclasses, and mentoring. Francesca is the former Founder and Editor-in-Chief of Cinegirl, now evolved into CineVoices part of British Cinematographer, a new community for everyone working across the creative craft industries in film, TV and broadcast. She has moderated panels and industry conversations at Camerimage, Eurocine and the BFI, as well as at film schools and universities, and regularly delivers masterclasses on industry access, career strategy and inclusion. Francesca also contributes to best practice across higher education and professional development within the screen industries.

title: Gaelle Moran (Screenwriting & Direction)
description: Immersive, Film and TV Writer, Director. title: Lucy Brown (Filmmaking + Inclusive Storytelling)
description: Lucy Brown is an award-winning filmmaker and Professor of Film and Television at the University of Westminster. She has produced projects for BBC, Channel 4, Nickelodeon and Disney, focusing on collaborative creativity, inclusive storytelling and talent development. Speak with her about developing film and TV projects, navigating screen careers, building industry networks and turning creative ideas into screen projects.
