title: Michelle Toh (Multimedia Journalism)
description: Michelle Toh is an award-winning international journalist, formerly with CNN and FORTUNE. She is founder and host of Behind the Business, a Webby Award-winning video podcast named an Instant Hit Show on Spotify, featuring leaders from Airbnb, Rivian and Canva. Her reporting has appeared in TIME, USA Today and the South China Morning Post, and she contributes to The New York Times and Los Angeles Business Journal. She can advise on creator-led storytelling, podcast and video strategy, audience growth, and building a new media brand. Information for mentees: - In order to book a session with one of our mentors, please download the SXSW London app. - Please arrive 5 minutes early to check in for your mentor session. Late arrivals (anyone not present at the start of their booked session) may lose their slot to a walk-up attendee. - If a session is fully booked, you re welcome to try as a walk-up in case a booked attendee doesn t attend. - We would recommend preparing a brief introduction about yourself, along with any specific questions or areas you d like guidance on from your mentor.

title: Miranda Bailey
description: Miranda Bailey is a filmmaker and founder of Cold Iron Pictures and The Film Arcade. Cold Iron s BY DESIGN premiered at Sundance 2025. She was nominated for an Independent Spirit Award for producing JAZZY (Tribeca 2024). Her producing credits include THE SQUID AND THE WHALE, SUPER, SWISS ARMY MAN and THE DIARY OF A TEENAGE GIRL. The Film Arcade specialises in unconventional projects, with titles including DON T THINK TWICE and GOD S COUNTRY. www.mirandabailey.com Information for mentees: - In order to book a session with one of our mentors, please download the SXSW London app. - Please arrive 5 minutes early to check in for your mentor session. title: SOMOH
description: SOMOH is the solo moniker of Sophia Mohan. Combining elements of bedroom-pop and 90 s indie rock, SOMOH s deeply personal and inviting nostalgic songcraft explores themes of love, hardship, friendships, and family whilst channelling the likes of beabadoobee, Soccer Mommy and Alex G.

title: Zumba Taster
hall: Dray Walk Gallery - Zumba House, Class

title: Aditi Rajagopal
description: Aditi is Head of Immersive Experience Design at Atlantic Productions, designing new ways to tell stories with cutting edge immersive technology across VR, AR, web and IRL. She previously worked in creative and innovation teams at Meta across India, Southeast Asia and the UK. She is an advocate for diversity in the creative industry, spending her free time guest lecturing, mentoring early career creatives and love bombing new hobbies. title: Christopher Morrison
description: Christopher Morrison is a writer, director and educator whose work spans over 200 projects across immersive theatre, VR, video games, film and live performance. He has collaborated with AAA game studios, Cirque du Soleil and P G. His VR project The Werewolf Experience played at Cannes Film Festival in 2023. He is an XR mentor for SXSW and has spoken at the UK House of Lords. As an Adjunct Professor at California College of the Arts, he teaches immersive storytelling. His next VR project, Drunk Zombie Duelists, received a Dutch government grant. Cirque du Soleil has called him a huge asset to the creative process. www.realityplus.org Information for mentees: - In order to book a session with one of our mentors, please download the SXSW London app. - Please arrive 5 minutes early to check in for your mentor session. title: Deirdre Parsons
description: Deirdre Parsons has led DLF UK since its establishment in 2012, managing meditation and healthcare projects across diverse settings, from businesses to schools, universities, prisons, veterans, addiction recovery, homelessness charities, and the NHS. With 30 years experience as a TM teacher, she has made significant contributions to numerous research studies on Transcendental Meditation. title: Francesca R Zerenghi
description: An Entertainment Publicist and Founder of FRAN, an independent curated publicity consultancy representing Directors of Photography, Directors and companies across the screen industry, Francesca Zerenghi champions artistry with UK roots and a global reach, with a focus on authenticity and long-term career development, an inclusive mindset, and a creative approach to building talent visibility, reputation and professional reach. Her expertise spans Talent Publicity, Networking Impact, Panels Masterclasses, and mentoring. Francesca is the former Founder and Editor-in-Chief of Cinegirl, now evolved into CineVoices part of British Cinematographer, a new community for everyone working across the creative craft industries in film, TV and broadcast. She has moderated panels and industry conversations at Camerimage, Eurocine and the BFI, as well as at film schools and universities, and regularly delivers masterclasses on industry access, career strategy and inclusion. Francesca also contributes to best practice across higher education and professional development within the screen industries.

title: Screen Roundtable:  How to Find and Develop New Talent
description: There is no healthy film industry without the nurturing and support of new talent. In this roundtable, professionals managing leading talent development programs in the UK share their advice on how to set up and deliver meaningful initiatives, finding and platforming talent, and the key opportunities in the sector today.

title: Venture Spotlight Final
description: Five startups. One prize. 100,000. The best emerging university technology in Europe has fought through two semi-finals to get here. Now the five finalists take the stage for one last three-minute pitch, followed by a grilling from an esteemed judging panel including Chris Barnes, Omran Al Kuwari and Zeynep Koruturk. Presented by Aydan Al-Saad, this is the session where Europe s most promising deep tech, life science, biotech, climate and creative technology founders find out if their breakthrough can capture the backing it deserves. The science is extraordinary. The stakes are real. Who takes home the 100,000?

title: The GTA Effect
description: GTA is shaping up to be the biggest cultural drop of the decade. With a trailer that shattered records and a fandom that s already remixing, roleplaying, and dissecting every pixel, Rockstar s next launch, GTA 6, will influence everything from fashion and music to memes and marketing. This panel unpacks what GTA means for culture, creators, brands, and the entertainment industry and how to navigate the internet when everyone s in Vice City.

title: Music Groupchat: Publishing & Songwriting Futures
description: Publishing and songwriting executives delve into the inner and outer workings of the craft and business, from creative collaboration to royalty flows and sync opportunities. How a co-write changes a career, and how sync placement brings a song to the world. Join industry leaders and hitmakers for uplifting conversations on protecting the craft, celebrating the songwriter s voice, and building a healthier, more transparent future for music creators.

title: Mascara and Match Day
description: For three decades I ve led global beauty brands and sat in football stands, and I ve lost count of how many times I ve been told I don t *look* the part,whether as a C-suite marketer or as a true fan. Explore how grit and grace qualities forged in the terraces and in boardrooms can upend outdated notions of who belongs where. Why 15% of Premier League TV viewers are women, and how brands and individuals can challenge stereotypes, welcome new audiences, because whether it s match day or market day, the real game is about showing up, authentically, relentlessly, and unapologetically.

title: The Anti-Trend Economy: How to Engineer Brand Loyalty for the Discerning Consumer
description: Micro-trends are dead. Thanks to explainer videos on social media, and a rejection of disposable aesthetics, the 2026 consumer has evolved into something more discerning. They no longer shop on vibes alone; they check the care label, they calculate price per wear, and they know that overconsumption doesn t make them happier in the long run. But many people still care about style. In this new era of critical thinking, how can brands build loyalty? This session brings together the architects of fashion s New Standard to discuss how brands can thrive in a climate of radical transparency. Editor of Capsule Holly Beddingfield will lead the discussion on how to build strategic durability and cultural relevance in 2026, alongside author and quality expert Andrea Cheong and Amazon Fashion UK s Fiona Collins.

title: Opening Remarks: Reinvention for the Agentic Era
Aditi Rajagopal

title: Building Products That Stick
description: Great products aren t just built they re remembered. In a world of endless apps and constant updates, the products that endure are the ones that seamlessly add to one s life, becoming a daily haunt on our phones and laptops. So, how do you build products that become part of every day?

title: CELAVI
description: C E L V I - Remember that name BBC Radio 1 C E L V I are a fierce, genre-defying duo from Bangor, North Wales - Gwion and Sarah - armed with an explosive take on nu-metal that blends the raw power of metal, goth, emo, industrial, electro, and rock into an anthemic, boundary-pushing sound. They re rewriting the rules and bringing a fierce new energy to the scene, with their unapologetic and empowering anthems. Wales first bilingual nu-metal band are already making waves with support from Metal Hammer, BBC Radio 1, BBC 6 Music, BBC Introducing in Wales, BBC Radio Wales, BBC Radio Cymru and BBC Radio Cymru 2 (National Welsh language Radio Stations). They ve also been featured on Amazon Music s Breakthrough Rock and Best New Bands Editorial Playlists. C E L V I made history by reaching Number 1 on Rhys Mwyn s Siart Amgen 2024 (Alternative Chart 2024) on BBC Radio Cymru, the prestigious national Welsh language radio station. Their explosive Welsh-language nu-metal anthem, COFIA R ENW (REMEMBER THE NAME), is the first-ever track by a Welsh-language nu-metal band to claim the top spot. They ve also been selected as a PPL Momentum Wales Artist and been awarded with a PPL Momentum/Sbardun Accelerator Fund grant by PRS Foundation, PPL and Arts Council of Wales to work with Grammy-nominated producer Romesh Dodangoda (Bring Me The Horizon, Nova Twins, Holding Absence) on their nu-EP. Dubbed The loudest thing to come from North Wales , C E L V I are working with Loki Films (known for his work with Sleep Token, LORNA SHORE and Limp Bizkit) to bring their sonic mayhem bangers to life in music videos that deliver a full-throttle slap to the face! C E L V I have had an exciting 2026 so far, having already been named one of the winners of the Sound of Miwsig 2026 Wales - selected through an industry-wide and public poll. The duo are continuing to build momentum across Wales and England, having headlined Fuel Rock Club on St David s Day and supported alternative metal artist and producer n0trixx. They have also performed at FOCUS Wales, an international showcase festival that spotlights emerging talent and industry-ready artists. Looking ahead, they will open for Nottingham s acclaimed As December Falls at Chepstow Castle in August 2026 - a milestone show marking the next chapter of their rise.

title: One World Orchestra
description: One World Orchestra is a UK-based intercultural music collective bringing together artists from across the world to create bold, cinematic and emotionally charged performances that blend global traditions with contemporary sounds. The ensemble features musicians and vocalists representing multiple cultures, many with lived experience of migration and displacement, combining Arabic melodies, Latin rhythms, electronic textures, British and European folk traditions and powerful vocal storytelling. For SXSW London at 93 Feet East, presented in partnership with Abbey Road Studios, OWO brings its unique global sound into the heart of the courtyard with a specially created acoustic performance full of rhythm, colour and surprise. Expect powerful voices, rich instrumental textures and an uplifting live experience that celebrates connection, culture and the energy of music without borders.

title: Choreographing the Future
description: What happens when we use technology to make life more livable, rather than just more efficient? By weaving nature and technology together, art duo DRIFT creates physical experiences that recalibrate how we sense, feel, and connect - not only with each other, but with the planet itself. This session explores how architecture and multisensory design can engage the nervous system, inspire belonging and connection. Discover what natural processes can teach us about resilience, and how technology can partner with nature - transforming our environments from fast and functional to grounding, meaningful, and alive.

He is an XR mentor for SXSW and has spoken at the UK House of Lords. As an Adjunct Professor at California College of the Arts, he teaches immersive storytelling. Francesca is the former Founder and Editor-in-Chief of Cinegirl, now evolved into CineVoices part of British Cinematographer, a new community for everyone working across the creative craft industries in film, TV and broadcast. She has moderated panels and industry conversations at Camerimage, Eurocine and the BFI, as well as at film schools and universities, and regularly delivers masterclasses on industry access, career strategy and inclusion. title: Is Swimmability the Next Frontier in Urban Resilience?
description: Swimmable Cities launched in July 2024 to help cities make rivers, lakes, and waterfronts safe and swimmable. What began as a grassroots initiative has expanded to 193 organizations in 100 cities across 34 countries. Co-founder Chris Romer-Lee, creator of the Thames Baths floating pool, will discuss how swimmability is reshaping urban life, similar to walkability. Following the Paris Olympics, cities are reimagining waterways as vibrant public spaces. By 2030, Swimmable Cities aims to assist 300 cities in achieving clean, accessible waterways, providing a platform for tracking progress and sharing insights. This panel includes key spokespeople from the Greater London Authority and Royal Docks Management Authority to discuss the future of urban swimming in London by 2050.

title: Sparklmami
description: Sparklmami s debut record In This Body invites us into a lush world of reverie, nostalgia, and possibility . Across nine songs, we re immersed in a gorgeous pastiche of cascading vocal runs and breathy sighs, golden saxophone improvisations, and twinkling drum beats. It s a sound that pulls from myriad places: the glimmering, verdant jazz made in 1970s Brazil, the open-hearted Mexican boleros she grew up listening to at home, the whimsy of Indian singer Runa Laila, and of course the improvisations and ideas of her bandmates [Eddie Burns (Executive Producer, Drums), William Corduroy (Executive Producer, Bass), Alec Trickett (Percussion), Josh Jessen (Keys, Synths) and Kenneth Leftridge Jr. (Saxophone)] . But it s Sparklmami s imagination that really drives the record, especially when she revisits her early life equipped with the wisdom she s acquired in the years since. Singing in both Spanish and English, Sparklmami dives into the murky and wondrous world of her childhood memories. Whether she s recounting a surreal dream of a man hugging the house to the point of breaking it on It Was 5 AM or bearing witness to a loved one s pain on Fajas, she conveys a sense of emotional turbulence and surreal childlike perception while also singing with the poise, understanding, and nuance of a grown woman. In This Body is a record rooted in connection: in deep love for your community, your musical collaborators, and yourself. But it s also one of undeniable loss. When I made this album, I was figuring out so much on my own while still grieving my past, she says. People with unresolved pain and grief can t help but pass it on. To love is to grieve, to grieve is to love.

title: The Athlete Brand Economy
description: In our session we ll showcase how x+y s proprietary data is uncovering the gap between athlete visibility (VIE score) and how well they are monetising the opportunity (EQV score). Our recent report showcases the athletes that are maximising the opportunity, and who s leaving value on the table. Emile Heskey will provide his perspective on the shifting narrative of the athlete brand and provide real-world examples of how he s helping his sons Jaden and Reigan to understand their value as they build their own professional careers.

title: From Attention to Presence: When Brands Start Behaving
description: For years, brands competed for visibility likes, reach, and fleeting moments in feeds. As AI becomes the interface, what matters is no longer being seen, but being present: clear, consistent, and trustworthy across interactions. Brands are no longer experienced through messages or campaigns, but through how they respond, adapt, and act in real time. The opportunity is to move beyond rented attention and design behavior that endures.

title: Hollywood VFX, Indie Budgets: How Creators Keep Control with AI
description: How can independent creators use AI for VFX responsibly, without sacrificing creative control and vision? This panel moves beyond the hype to deliver practical insights, spotlighting how emerging tech can empower creators to blend AI with traditional craft, streamline production workflows, and achieve cinematic quality on modest budgets. Hear from industry leaders and working creators about actionable solutions for thriving in the AI-driven creator ecosystem where professional-grade VFX is now accessible to all.

title: Skin as Data: What Your Face is Revealing About Burnout, Beauty Lies and Digital Damage
description: Your skin isn t lying- but everything you ve been told about it is. Dermatologist Dr Emma Craythorne, award-winning beauty journalist Claire Coleman, influencer and entrepreneur Ashley Stobart and Chief Strategy Officer Cat Wiles, dismantle the beauty-industrial complex currently profiting from filter dysmorphia and diagnostic anxiety. This session reframes skin as biofeedback: a real-time map of stress, sleep, gut health, and what it means to be human in 2026. Not aspirational nonsense - evidence. Not self-care - self-knowledge. A cultural reckoning with an industry being forced to grow up, and a blueprint for building honest, science-literate skin culture in the age of AI-perfected faces.

title: Workshop: Refugee Storytelling in a Fracturing Humanitarian World
description: Join UNHCR s Matthew Saltmarsh and Omer Elnaiem for an unfiltered conversation on what happens when the global humanitarian safety net frays. They will dive into the harsh reality of how dwindling aid is affecting refugees and how the UN Refugee Agency is trying, in this new landscape, to tell refugees stories.

title: The Fantastical Isn't a Fantasy
description: Hollywood special effects and screen graphics have had a significant impact on near-term innovations across UX, digital product and experience design as interfaces and experiences - set in futuristic worlds - are rapidly being realised in the real world. Territory Studio is at the forefront of these innovations creating speculative futures and often bridging them to market whether that s studio design experiences for Hollywood fictional, near-future automotive, and present-day immersive projects. David will share how these stories get told, how the future is imagined and the increasingly blurry lined between science fiction and the everyday.

title: Have a Guinness with a Creator: How the Brand is Alive and Kicking
description: Guinness cultural presence is anchored by its Premier League and Six Nations partnerships moments where fandom, ritual and national conversation collide. The brand is now building an ecosystem of creators, live experiences and passionate communities. to bring these sports to life. Nick. Emma and creator John Nellis, to share how they are expanding on their traditional remit with brands, and platforms to feed their fandoms. Learn the new metrics of brand building with Creators, and why all marketers need to look at how the shift in channel engagement can t be ignored for growth.

She is also co-founder of IAMJT, a studio for artist development, music consulting and strategic partnerships. At IAMJT, she works with Amsterdam Dance Event on their Pro conference program and with Coachella s Innovation unit on artist partnerships and community design across AI and gaming. She has worked with Microsoft, Mixcloud, Wondersauce and Future Works. title: Rabbit Cult
description: Rabbit Cult is a bunnymetal duo from Finland. Their electronic pop metal entwines the matriarchal roots of Finnish folklore, and the striking tone of the traditional Finnish harp-like instrument kantele, turning their music into a message only rabbits can deliver. Conducted by multi-instrumentalist Marianne (drums, percussions, guitar, bass, synth, vocals) Salla-Marja (vocals, kanteles) this group of two writes and produces their music together.

title: Rake's Bar Happy Hour by Andaz Hotel
description: Rake s Bar is part of the Andaz London Liverpool Street - a five-star lifestyle hotel where the energy of East London meets the heritage of the City. Set beside Liverpool Street Station and moments from Shoreditch, the hotel brings together bold design, local culture, spacious rooms and suites, distinctive restaurants and bars, and characterful event spaces in one of London s most connected neighborhoods. From creative stays to social dining and standout gatherings, Andaz London invites you to experience the city with curiosity, individuality and a strong sense of place Andaz London Liverpool Street is showing up at SXSW London as a cultural home base for visitors, creators, founders and curious minds exploring the festival. Located at the gateway between the City and East London, the hotel places guests close to Shoreditch and the surrounding creative districts, with restaurants, bars and event spaces designed for connection before, during and after the day s programming. As SXSW London brings together music, screen, tech, culture and ideas from 1 6 June 2026, Andaz London offers a stylish, locally rooted place to stay, meet, dine and keep the conversation going.

title: Ree Sewell (Artist Development + Audience Growth)
description: With over a decade of experience across event management, presenting, radio, tour management and A R, Ree has worked with independent and major labels including Universal, BMG and Believe. She is now Senior Artist and Label Relations Manager at SoundCloud, focusing on artist development, audience growth and fostering music communities. title: Rob McGee (Artist Discovery + A&R Strategy)
description: Rob McGee is a booking agent and newly appointed Head of New Music at One Fiinix Live. title: Tina Edwards
description: Edwards is out there extensively researching scenes, genres and artists, cutting through the convenient listening of your latest Made For You playlist and helping us remember what true discovery should be Dazed and Confused Edwards is deeply plugged into what s next for global record culture - Cue BURN, vinyl alliance Essential jazz into dance selections every time - Gilles Peterson An internationally-acclaimed DJ, Tina Edwards encourages dancers to ignore the booth and stay present on the dance floor. Tina began DJing shortly before the pandemic; since, her demand behind the decks has taken her and her red box of 45s around the world, performing in Australia, Japan, Singapore, LA, Chicago and across Europe. She s played Glastonbury, Montreux Jazz, North Sea and headline sets for the likes of BAFTAs Official After Party (2025, 2026), film premieres, Netflix and Ray Ban. Tina s sets are wildly eclectic and full of wild cards but her grounding comes from the language of jazz; the Londoner is recognised as one of the few DJs of her generation to uphold jazz dance floor culture. She does this with her Internationally-renowned night Love Is Everywhere, with some of the most established jazz dancers from across Europe and Asia making a point of performing. Wherever she plays, her sets avoid predictable arcs; Latin jazz, cumbia, off-beat disco, Afro-diasporic grooves, psychedelia and obscure 45s from her travels all contribute to her playful, intoxicating sets. Elsewhere, Tina s reputation for sharing and breaking new artists is upheld on her Internationally revered radio show Bandcamp Selects, Bandcamp fan club Jazz-ish Jazz Club, and through live events too, namely with LGBTQIA+ community-oriented platform Queer Jazz and resonate; the latter of which aims to democratise information in the music industry through events, discussions and editorial. Tina Edwards is one of Worldwide FM s longest serving residents (2017-2022), and she hosted and produced Next Big Thing for British Airways for seven years, reaching millions of listeners. In 2022 she presented the TV documentary JAZZ UK: Spitting Fire (BBC One, BBC Four). She s also regularly heard on BBC Radio 3 (Jazz Fix, Round Midnight), and has written for Downbeat, The Guardian, Monocle, WeJazz and many more well-respected platforms. She was Editor of limited edition Listen Zine for KEF Speakers.

title: Two Decades of Mating in Captivity: Exploring the State of Modern Love & Desire with Esther Perel
description: Can we want what we already have? We once asked whether desire could survive children, marriage, and the growing demands of modern love. Today, we ask if it can survive our technologies and the increasingly disembodied ways we live.

title: Zumba Taster
hall: Dray Walk Gallery - Zumba House, Class

title: Headlines & Harmonies
description: What happens when two very different public voices come together for an unfiltered conversation? Join broadcaster Piers Morgan and internationally acclaimed soprano Katherine Jenkins for a lively discussion on fame, media, culture, public opinion, and life in the spotlight full of sharp perspectives, surprising moments, and candid conversation.

title: To Collaborate is Not to Siphon
description: A new generation of creatives is redefining the rules of engagement, building ecosystems rather than just audiences and collaborating across disciplines without losing ownership of their voice. As brands, galleries, and institutions seek relevance, the conversation must shift from access to alignment. This panel brings together leading voices actively shaping that future to set the tone for meaningful creative partnerships in the next era.

title: Beyond Digital. Why Real Life Experience wins
description: As AI and algorithms dominate reach, brands face a new challenge: attention without connection. This panel examines why physical experiences are becoming essential again, not as a replacement for digital, but as its emotional anchor. From global market perspectives to the rise of experiential retail led by lifestyle and tech brands, we ll explore how IRL creates trust, memory and meaning in an overstimulated world.As AI and algorithms dominate reach, brands face a new challenge: attention without connection. This panel examines why physical experiences are becoming essential again, not as a replacement for digital, but as its emotional anchor. From global market perspectives to the rise of experiential retail led by lifestyle and tech brands, we ll explore how IRL creates trust, memory and meaning in an overstimulated world.

title: Creativity as an Engine of Growth: should culture be defined as critical infrastructure for cities?
description: Creativity is a powerful catalyst for urban growth. This session explores how culture, design and the Creative Industries shape places that attract people, ideas and investment. The panel examines what makes a district magnetic, how creative ecosystems generate economic and social value, and why London s Growth Plan places such weight on the experiential economy. Drawing on lessons from Earls Court, the discussion considers how to nurture conditions in which creativity thrives and how these approaches can inform future development strategy across the capital and beyond.

title: Irkalla - Gilgamesh's Dream
description: Director: Mohamed Jabarah Al-Daradji Cast: Youssef Husham Al-Thahabi, Hussein Raad Zuwayr, Samar Kazem Jawad, Ameer Jabarah A heartbreaking tale with glimpses of hope for the Baghdad children left behind after the war. In the labyrinthine streets of Baghdad, orphaned and homeless children roam aimlessly with nothing to rely on but each other. In a world of scarcity, some children like Moody (Hussein Raad Zuwayr) are lured by the empty promises of the armed militia, while others like nine-year-old dreamer Chum-Chum (Youssef Husham Al-Thahabi) find a rare sliver of hope in dreaming. Drawn to the Tigris River and the ancestral myth of Gilgamesh, Chum-Chum sets out on a journey to bring his parents back to life in this bittersweet epic from Mohamed Jabarah Al-Daradji, a heartbreaking but hopeful tale on the power of myth and memory. (ISRA AL KASSI)

title: The Datafication of Sport: How Analytics Created the Global Fan
description: Sport is no longer just watched it s measured, tracked, predicted, and optimized in real time. From fantasy leagues and betting markets to player metrics, AI-driven performance analysis, and second-screen experiences, data has transformed how fans engage with sport globally. This session explores how analytics turned fandom into obsession, why modern audiences crave deeper statistical storytelling, and how leagues, clubs, broadcasters, and tech platforms are using data to globalize sports culture at unprecedented scale. As sport becomes increasingly digital, personalized, and predictive, what happens to emotion, tribalism, and the human side of the game?

title: Andreea Magdalina (Community Building + Ethical Business)

She is also co-founder of IAMJT, a studio for artist development, music consulting and strategic partnerships. At IAMJT, she works with Amsterdam Dance Event on their Pro conference program and with Coachella s Innovation unit on artist partnerships and community design across AI and gaming. She has worked with Microsoft, Mixcloud, Wondersauce and Future Works. title: RANJ x Clifr
description: RANJ x Clifr is an R B duo that creates genre-fluid music, sprinkling space pop, house, hip-hop and jazz influences into their songs, all blended in with a distinctive R B flavour of their own. Their journey started as part of a Carnatic fusion band, Kelvikkuri which experimented with jazz music. The duo released their debut EP 593 Vol. 1 in 2021, which flipped the script, introducing a new age sound that alternated between R B and rap. They went from strength to strength, following that up with a full length album Antihero and another EP Play Me! in 2023. 2025 saw a slew of new singles, beginning with the sexy Pharrell-inspired I M JUST A WOMAN FORREAL - all leading up to RANJ s massive 27-track mixtape, 27 Club, which dropped on February 27th, 2026.

title: Screen Roundtable: How to Rethink Film Distribution
description: As big studios continue the trend of conglomeration, shrinking an already struggling market, alternative methods of distribution have become ever-important. Industry professionals who have ensured their films reached wide audiences with guerilla-style campaigns and smaller budgets share their advice on how to get films seen while keeping the filmmaker in the driving seat.

description: Shain Shapiro is the Founder and Chairman of music and creative economy consultancy Sound Diplomacy, and the Founder and Executive Director of the nonprofit Center for Music Ecosystems. title: Design the Experience, Score the Emotion: The Impact of Building Multi-Sensory Brand Worlds
description: Today s best brands aren t just recognizable, they re inhabitable. Welcome to the age of world building: where branding behaves more like storytelling, and design becomes the scaffolding of a universe people actually want to live in. This session explores how to craft immersive, multi-sensory brand experiences that connect through sound, touch, motion and more. From sonic branding to spatial design, learn how to build richer brand worlds that resonate on every sensory level. Because your audience doesn t just see your brand they should be immersed in it.

title: Amoeba
description: The line between free-living and parasitic existence as a teenage girl is carefully walked in Siyou Tan s debut feature film which explores the pitfalls of conformity and the consequences of going astray. 16-year-old Choo Xin Yu (Ranice Tay) is the new student at an elite all girls school. Instead of doing her best to fit in and comply, Choo Xin Yu quickly forms a girl gang with three others. The authoritarian backdrop of their competitive school becomes a symbol and reflection of their day to day existence in Singaporean society, with the school uniform being the only thing the young women comply with. Choo Xin Yu turns the camera on the world, the way she wants to see it. With glimpses of what the gang choose to document, through their eyes and perspective, Tan creates a compelling tapestry of loyalty, righteousness and sisterhood, making the paths and broken bonds harder to stomach. Whether within the schools walls, inside of a teenage girl s bedroom or on the streets of Singapore, Amoeba explores coming-of age under authoritarian rule, capturing the changing face of Singaporean identity and the desire to remain young while longing for real life to begin. (ISRA AL KASSI)

title: Talking Taste
description: Talking Taste explores how the conversation around food is evolving from restaurants and hospitality to media, culture and influence. As audiences change and attention spans shrink, how do great food stories still cut through? Does just good food launch a restaurant nowadays? Lets tuck in.

title: Andreea Magdalina (Community Building + Ethical Business)
description: Andreea Magdalina is founder of shesaid.so , a global community of music artists, creatives and executives focused on diverse voices and ethical business practices. She is also co-founder of IAMJT, a studio for artist development, music consulting and strategic partnerships. At IAMJT, she works with Amsterdam Dance Event on their Pro conference program and with Coachella s Innovation unit on artist partnerships and community design across AI and gaming. She has worked with Microsoft, Mixcloud, Wondersauce and Future Works. Information for mentees: - Please book a session using the RSVP function via the app. - Please arrive 5 minutes early to check in for your mentor session. Late arrivals (anyone not present at the start of their booked session) may lose their slot to a walk-up attendee. - If a session is fully booked, you re welcome to try as a walk-up in case a booked attendee doesn t attend. - We would recommend preparing a brief introduction about yourself, along with any specific questions or areas you d like guidance on from your mentor.

title: Andrew Adegbemi (Career Navigation + Media Mentorship)
description: Andrew is keen to support mentees in developing careers within media and marketing, helping them define career goals, overcome challenges, and identify effective routes, networks and contacts. Andrew is a Senior Advertising Sales Manager with over 16 years of experience at organisations including Google, ITV, Sky and currently SoundCloud. Andrew has partnered with brands to support strategic media deliverables and build impactful advertising solutions and also mentors young people through MEFA. title: Ben Cooper (Audience Storytelling + Creative Results)
description: Join Ben Cooper to explore how powerful storytelling grows audiences and turns creative ideas into real impact. Drawing on his experience as Controller of BBC Radio 1, 1Xtra and Asian Network, and Chief Content Officer at Bauer Media Audio, he shares insights from working across artists, audiences and advertisers. Discuss creating memorable narratives across audio, visual and social platforms, and learn practical approaches to making ambitious ideas resonate and deliver meaningful results. title: Carl Young (Label Partnerships + Digital Campaigns)
description: Carl Young is Vevo s Head of Music Talent, EU, overseeing label and distribution partnerships. Since 2015, he has developed and led Vevo s original content programming and talent procurement for digital campaigns, curating franchises including DSCVR, DSCVR Artists to Watch, Footnotes, Extended Play and Live from Vevo Studios. He plays a key role in defining Vevo s audience and industry reputation, focusing on editorial and content strategy worldwide. title: Cliff Fluet (AI + Music Technology)
description: Cliff Fluet is a lawyer, advisor and sector leader at the intersection of creativity, technology and the third sector. A Partner at Lewis Silkin and founder of Eleven Advisory, he has advised AI and music tech companies including Jukedeck, AI Music and Audoo. He chairs Help Musicians and Music Minds Matter, and is inaugural Chair of Music Technology UK. He lectures at Berklee College of Music and NYU, has spoken at SXSW and Reeperbahn, and won Mentor of the Year at the UK Social Mobility Awards 2025. title: Delenn Vaughan (Creative Direction + Worldbuilding)
description: Delenn is a multidisciplinary creative rooted in the cultures of Ghana, the West Indies and South London, specialising in music-focused creative direction, production and worldbuilding. She has led campaigns for artists including Teni the Entertainer, Ayra Starr and BlaqBonez, transforming bold, culturally rich ideas into impactful musical narratives. Her work bridges genres and communities, crafting soulful, cinematic experiences driven by the power of music. title: From Influence to Authority: The Rise of Expert Creators
description: Trust has shifted to people with proof. As platforms behave like search engines, creators who teach, analyse, and show receipts are winning attention and conversion. For brands, that means partnering for outcomes, briefing content for discoverability, building an expert bench.

title: Gemma Gilday (Commercial Partnerships + Global Campaigns)
description: Gemma is Senior Director of Global Amazon Music at BMG, leading global strategy worldwide. She previously led Global Streaming Optimisation at BMG for Black Sabbath, Fatboy Slim and The Kinks, and spent eight years at Universal Music Group on campaigns for The Beatles, Lady Gaga, Elton John and Billie Eilish. A mother of three, she advocates for equality, parental rights and sustainable careers in music. Ask her about DSP strategy, metadata, working with DSPs, and being a parent or woman in music. title: Music Groupchat: Media's Role in Spotlighting Underground Scenes
description: The media s hand in surfacing underground sound before the algorithms catch up. From championing local heroes to amplifying fresh scenes, telling stories that matter and giving talent its first real spotlight. Join music journalists, editors, and curators for an inspiring look at how thoughtful coverage launches careers, connects communities, and celebrates culture before the world hears it. No gatekeeping. No chasing clicks. Just the genuine thrill of discovery and the power of sharing something new.

Speak with her about careers in arts marketing, campaigns on limited budgets, audience growth and marketer skills. title: Made in EU
description: Director: Stephan Komandarev Cast: Gergana Pletnyova, Todor Kotsev, Gerasim Georgiev A tense social drama exposing the inequality of labour in Bulgaria through a worker s bout with COVID Single mother Iva (Gergana Pletnyova) feels unwell, but can t get sick leave from her precarious clothing factory job. When she is eventually declared the first COVID case in her small Bulgarian town, a dangerous game of accusation unravels, with the labourer shunned not only from her community but also from her home. Pletnyova gives a stunning lead performance in Stephan Komandarev s piercing social examination, a drama with the fast pulse of a thriller that closely observes a crumbling European dream from the trampoline of the pandemic. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: COOL IS FUEL - Why Culture Wins in the Age of AI
description: In a world where anyone can generate images, music, film, copy, or code in seconds, technology is no longer the differentiator. Culture is. Taste is. Human perspective is. Manon Dave - Head of Future World Design at BBC R D, creative technologist, songwriter, and product leader behind the generative AI systems embedded in millions of Mercedes-AMG vehicles, explores why the future belongs not to the companies with the biggest models, but to the people with the strongest point of view. Drawing on two decades across music, storytelling, broadcast media, and AI, Manon argues that culture has always been built at the edges: by niche communities, underrepresented voices, and creators shaping what the mainstream adopts next. Manon makes the case that the more powerful AI becomes, the more priceless the human experience wielding it.

title: The Sport Investment Spectrum
description: Join leaders and athletes as they discuss sports rise as players across the investment world. From athletes going from pitch to boardroom and clubs beginning to consider themselves as incubators.

title: Eaves Wilder
description: Eaves Wilder began songwriting around the age of eight harnessing an early obsession with 60 s Motown records, The Beatles and the left-field pop of Lily Allen. Her inspirations are endless but it was the riot grrl movement that first blew the doors open for her. Her debut self -releases at the age of 16 via the Orchard, featuring dreamy shoe gaze guitars and Wilder s signature sweet vocals with intelligent and deceptively cutting lyrics, led to her signing at the age of 18 with Secretly Canadian with whom she released her first first official tracks and an EP Hookey in 2023 / 24. Her debut album campaign (due for release Spring 26) kicks off this autumn with the first single titled Everybody Talks released by Secretly Canadian on September 23rd

It s Nice That is a UK-based creative platform and media brand championing global creative talent through editorial, events and community, connecting audiences with the ideas, people and projects shaping today s creative industries. title: Klaverson
description: Klaverson creates cinematic downtempo music that blends soul, ambient electronica and textured live instrumentation into deeply immersive soundscapes. With over 50 million streams, the producer has earned support from BBC Radio 1, BBC Radio 6 Music, Spotify and BBC Introducing, while also performing DJ sets alongside artists including Thundercat and Gorillaz. Earlier this year, Klaverson debuted his live show at Spectaculare in Prague, marking a new chapter in the evolution of his atmospheric and emotionally driven live experience.

title: Lo Rays
description: Glasgow-based modern pop-metal band Lo Rays blends catchy hooks, glitchy synths and metal riffs. Known for their post-apocalyptic visuals and DIY green screen videos, the band has built a strong online following including an Instagram reel that drew over 1.4 million views. They have recently joined the Denmark-based rock and metal label Prime Collective. As 2025 closes, Lo Rays is fresh from their first European show at Pro Weekend Fest in Spain and a London performance supporting Mimi Barks at Alyx Holcombe s MANTRA gig night. Before the year ends, they ll play Venom Fest in Newcastle and the Scottish Alternative Music Awards in Glasgow. Earlier in 2025, they were named One to Watch by BBC Introducing Rock on Radio 1 and nominated for Act of the Year by BBC Introducing Scotland. Their tracks have received regular airplay from Alyx Holcombe, Nels Hylton, and BBC Radio Scotland. Known for their high-energy, theatrical performances featuring dancers, vintage VHS-screen visuals, and post-apocalyptic storylines Lo Rays have supported acts such as I See Stars, VUKOVI, and North Atlas. They ve also played The Alternative Escape, as part of The Great Escape, along with Scottish festivals Bute Fest and Red Crust Festival. Finnish songwriter Laura Takala formed Lo Rays in 2018 after moving to Glasgow to follow her dream to make music in the footsteps of her heroes Biffy Clyro. She met Scott Bathgate (guitar), Ryan Jones (bass) and Murray Bartle (drums) at a local gig.

title: Naomi Naylor (Arts Marketing + Audience Growth)
description: Naomi Naylor is Head of Marketing at Southbank Centre, leading campaigns for over 1,000 annual events across music, dance, theatre, comedy, literature and visual arts. With 20+ years in the cultural sector, she has built impactful brands, delivered audience-focused campaigns and led high-performing teams. Speak with her about careers in arts marketing, campaigns on limited budgets, audience growth and marketer skills. title: The Legal Lounge
hall: T1  - SXSW London Expo and Platinum Lounge

title: Workshop: BBC Studios: Navigating the Creator, Partnership and Brand Partnerships Economy
description: BBC Studios will host a curated roundtable session bringing together industry leaders for a series of focused discussions on the evolving media landscape. Designed as an intimate, interactive format, the session will explore three key areas shaping the future of content and distribution: Creators : for creators, aspiring creators and those looking to work with creators, hosted by Helen O Donnell, Interim VP Content Creators, BBC Studios together with Content Creator and Presenter Tyrell Charles. Partnerships : how to work effectively with third parties to develop, scale and monetise content and IP, hosted by Anais Gonzales Espinosa, Director Digital and Platform Partnerships, BBC Studios Brand Partnerships : exploring our commercial relationships with brands, and how the commercial teams within BBC Studios have created engaging, commercially impactful storytelling for brands, hosted by Laura Jenkins, Director Brand Partnerships, BBC Studios.

title: Zumba + Lift Workout
hall: Dray Walk Gallery - Zumba House, Class

title: Designing the First Multi-Species City
description: London was the first city to build a subway for humans; what happens when it becomes the first city designed for all inhabitants - foxes, pigeons, and humans alike?

title: Pritt
description: South London s Pritt continues to make her mark being Eelam Tamil, which has influenced her style immensely, allowing herself to be as expressive as possible to take a pivotal stance in her art. Denmark born artist uses her femininity to empower stereotypes of women and take an Eastern meets Western stand on her music; an un-orthodox approach on honest relatable lyrics couples with Carnatic melisma. Pritt has worked with the likes of BBC 1Xtra presenter Sian Anderson Mayor of London on NEXXT STEP: Women in Music on a project called BEHIND EVERY GREAT CITY She has also worked with grime rapper Chip and his label, Cash Motto in the past, after winning his artist search in 2019, been on the front cover of Projext Liquid magazine as of January 2021, Pritt was announced as one of 8 Future Sounds artists for BBC Asian Network is the first Tamil woman to ever do so. Pritt now hosts her own radio show on BBC Asian Network where she curates playlists to put Tamil South Indian artists on the map. Being the trailblazer she is, Pritt has toured around Canada, USA, Australia, Switzerland, and of course London. Pritt has featured on music and fashion publications such as GQ India, GUAP, Pop SUGAR, Clash Magazine, Trench Magazine and EAR MILK worked with brands the likes of Nike, Adidas, Apple, Mix Cloud, Pirate studios, PUMA, Levi s, Kurt Geiger, Cartier, and many more! She has also performed at The Great Escape and Reading Leeds Festival in 2023, in 2024 Pritt supported Ustad Noor Bakhsh at Southbank Centre, and later that year supported Sid Sriram at the O2 Arena - a huge year for her debut with her band. In 2025, Pritt performed at SXSW both in Austin and London, a huge stage for her internationally and in her home city. In January 2024, Pritt was featured on Hello! Magazine s Rising Stars of 2024 which was sold all over the UK in major supermarkets and newsstands. She was made one of Amazon Music s Breakthrough Artists in March - Pritt is a force to be reckoned with. Pritt s 1st Tamil cover Unakkul Naane has hit over 100 million streams, putting her on the map internationally. She dropped her single Kaalam in May 2023 which is sitting on 1.5 million streams and counting. In 2025, Pritt released Kaanal Neere which received a lot of love especially with the lyrics and the serene evergreen visuals to match it. With so much anticipation around her, 2026 is all about collaboration enjoying the process of creating a body of work that encompasses everything Pritt.

title: Worldbuilding for the Senses: When Art Meets Experience Meets Brand
description: What happens when storytelling escapes the screen? Explore how worldbuilding is evolving into a multi-sensory discipline where art and brand meet to create experiences people don t just watch, but live.

title: Dyslexia Doesn’t Define Us
description: This conversation explores why it is time to rethink how society understands dyslexia. Blending lived experience with clinical expertise, the session will examine how stigma, language, and missed identification can shape a child s confidence, mental health, and future. Kate Griggs will discuss the global movement to reframe dyslexia through the work of Made By Dyslexia, while Maggie Aderin-Pocock will reflect on her experience growing up as an unidentified dyslexic and how recognizing her strengths shaped her career. Harold Koplewicz, MD, President and Medical Director of Child Mind Institute, will provide clinical context on dyslexia, why it is often misunderstood, and what effective support for children and families can look like.

title: Andreea Magdalina (Community Building + Ethical Business)
description: Andreea Magdalina is founder of shesaid.so , a global community of music artists, creatives and executives focused on diverse voices and ethical business practices. She is also co-founder of IAMJT, a studio for artist development, music consulting and strategic partnerships. At IAMJT, she works with Amsterdam Dance Event on their Pro conference program and with Coachella s Innovation unit on artist partnerships and community design across AI and gaming. She has worked with Microsoft, Mixcloud, Wondersauce and Future Works. title: Nature Needs a Publicist: How Design, Content, Experience and Culture can Elevate Nature's Story
description: Most events remain one-night spectacles. This session presents events as ecosystems, living networks of people, resources, and stories delivering net-positive impact on culture, community, and planet. In the realm of business with conscience and culture-shaping ideas, INCA Productions, Sunshine and THE NAT unpack purpose-led creativity rewiring capital, care, and culture flows, offering distinctive, material insight for rethinking events. We ll be taking you behind the scenes of THE NAT Gala. Sharing how we brought Hollywood, Wall Street, global philanthropists and leading conservationists together, to turn a single event into a massive positive catalyst for environmental restoration.

title: Designing for Trust
description: As AI powered products move from novelty to foundational pieces, designers face that all important question can I trust this? . How do you design and program a product when trust is as key a need as convenience and reliability?

title: How the American Left Learned to Speak the Internet
description: Fresh off Zohran Mamdani s historic mayoral victory in New York City, this conversation explores how one of the most closely watched political campaigns in the United States defied conventional wisdom and what it signals about the future of progressive politics globally. Bringing together broadcaster Hasan Piker and strategist Zara Rahim, the session examines both sides of that transformation: the rise of alternative political media ecosystems and the campaigns learning how to speak within them. As one of the most influential political commentators online, Piker has built a massive global audience by translating politics through livestreaming, internet culture, humor, and direct audience engagement outside the confines of traditional television or party aligned media. Rahim, meanwhile, helped architect a campaign that understood how to operate inside this new media reality rather than fight against it. Together, the conversation will look back at how the Mamdani campaign navigated questions around democratic socialism, economic populism, Islamophobia, Palestine, and generational frustration while building resonance across communities many believed could not coexist politically. It will also explore the broader transformation taking place inside American politics, where younger audiences increasingly distrust institutions and form political identity through creators, streamers, podcasts, and alternative media spaces.

title: Connection Continuum: Preventing Suicide and Combating Loneliness
description: Suicide is one of the leading causes of death among young people globally. Yet for most, crisis does not arrive without warning it builds through months or years of quiet disconnection, unmet need, and missed opportunity for support. This session brings together leaders across community, clinical, and digital approaches to explore what a more connected system of support looks like in practice from grassroots efforts like Empty Chairs, to national services like Shout 85258, to community-based programs such as Chelsea in the Community, alongside digital tools like the Child Mind Institute s Mirror app. Together, these perspectives point toward a more connected system of support one that reaches young people earlier, meets them where they are, and links them to care in ways that are timely, trusted, and scalable.

title: Ananto
description: Ananto is a multifaceted saxophonist, singer, and rapper whose sound lives at the intersection of heritage and modern expression. Drawing from his Indian roots and deep influences in jazz and R B, Ananto creates music that feels both timeless and forward-thinking. Rooted in improvisation and groove, his saxophone work carries the emotional depth of jazz while his vocals and rap bring a contemporary edge shaped by soul, rhythm, and storytelling. Ananto seamlessly blends melodic phrasing with rhythmic flow, crafting performances that move fluidly between genres without losing authenticity. Inspired by the richness of Indian musical identity and the expressive freedom of jazz and R B, Ananto s artistry is a celebration of cultural fusion. Whether on stage or in the studio, he uses music as a bridge connecting tradition and innovation, discipline and spontaneity, sound and story. With a distinct voice and an ever-evolving sound, Ananto represents a new generation of artists unafraid to honor their roots while redefining the boundaries of modern music.

title: Booktok to Screen: Making Hits out of Views
description: As online reading communities reshape the publishing landscape, BookTok has become a powerful engine for discovery and demand. This panel explores where we can find the next big series sensation and how viral literary trends translates (or not) to the screen, digging into what makes a story adaptable, and how platforms identify, develop, and scale IP with built-in audiences primed for global success.

title: Break Fifty
description: Break Fifty is a South UK Nu-Core band pushing genre boundaries. They have earned a reputation and garnered praised from publications such as Metal Hammer , Kerrang! , Knotfest and Distorted Sound . In 2023, Break Fifty unveiled a new line up promising to bring even more energy and passion. Break Fifty have performed at the likes of RADAR Festival and Burn It Down Festival; and supported artists such as Graphic Nature. Experience Break Fifty at SXSW.

title: Cultural Fluency: Mastering Relevance in a Dynamic World
description: Culture has always moved faster than business, yet a rare few brands manage to grow with it rather than be left behind. What separates cultural allies from cultural opportunists? This session will identify the universal principles and decisions that shape true cultural credibility. Rather than simply retelling brand history, the panel will focus on how authenticity is earned, how trust is maintained, and how brands evolve without losing their soul. This is not nostalgia. It s a practical exploration of relevance and what modern brands can learn from the timeless pursuit of identity and belonging.

title: Leela Rosa
description: Rotterdam-based artist and producer Leela Rosa crafts an alt-pop universe in the grey areas of human connection. Her sound is a deliberate collision: clean, folk-adjacent vocals woven through tactile electronic textures, anchored by introspective songwriting. It is music that inhabits liminal space between facade and self, desire and distance, memory and reinvention. Embedded in Rotterdam s electronic culture through her day job at Clone Records, Leela Rosa bridges underground energy with intimate indie storytelling. Her aesthetic carries subtle traces of 90s and early 00s nostalgia, layered with a sleek, contemporary electronic sensibility reminiscent of the CPH+ scene. Following her debut EP Keep Applying Pressure! , her upcoming project Blueprints for a Moving Target expands on themes of identity in motion and the restless space between who you were and who you are becoming. Live, she merges these worlds with her band and immersive visuals into an experience that feels both explosive and vulnerable the kind of set where you dance while crying and cry while dancing. FFO: Imogen Heap, Saya Gray, yeule, FKA Twigs, Erika de Casier, Bon Iver

title: From Policy to Practice: Building National AI Capability
description: As governments move from AI ambition to delivery, the question is how to build the foundations that let people, businesses and public services use the technology well. George Osborne, Head of OpenAI for Countries and former UK Chancellor, joins SXSW London to explore what national AI implementation looks like in practice: trusted infrastructure, smart regulation, workforce readiness, public adoption, and localised tools that can improve education, healthcare and public services. From the UK to the wider world, this session looks at how countries can turn AI policy into capability, competitiveness and broad public benefit.

title: Happy Hour with Universal and the UK & European Guild of Music Supervisors
description: Join SXSW London and our Screen partners at daily Happy Hour drinks. Meet filmmakers and industry professionals from all over the world. This is your unmissable stop before your evening screening. This happy hour is hosted by The UK European Guild of Music Supervisors, bringing together music supervisors, composers, rights holders and those passionate about music for film, TV, games and media. The Guild is a professional community championing the craft of music supervision through advocacy, networking and industry development across the UK and Europe. Universal Music Publishing Group is a leading global music publishing company home to the greatest artists, songwriters and song catalogues in the world. With an unparalleled roster and iconic catalogue of five million songs, UMPG has the industry s premiere global creative and sync team. We connect the best music to film, TV, advertising, stage, video games and digital content - bringing to life the moments where music and visuals meet.

title: Saatchi & Saatchi New Creators’ Showcase
description: Now in its 36th year, the Saatchi Saatchi New Creators Showcase presents a curated line-up of emerging creator talent from around the world. Featuring non-commercial work across filmmaking, animation, advertising and beyond, the showcase highlights new voices in motion and offers a platform for creators to be seen and discovered by the industry. Under the 2026 theme What defines a creator? , Saatchi Saatchi s Chief Creative Officer, Franki Goodwin, explores how creative boundaries have dissolved. Today s creators are no longer crossing over, but blending makership, movies, music and marketing in new ways - reshaping talent, media formats and IP, and redefining collaboration itself. This year also introduces a dedicated live music showcase, featuring emerging artists interested in working across commercial and creative projects. Alongside the performances, there will be a structured networking session aimed at connecting new talent with decision makers from the agency, production and music industries.

title: Screen Meetup with Queer Filmmakers Network
description: The Queer Filmmakers Network aims to connect, support and champion LGBTQIA+ Filmmakers. Organising networking events, workshops and showcases The Queer Filmmakers Network are building an active peer group and offering support to all members through their platform. Whether you are just starting out or are an established filmmaker, come through to celebrate the queer stories of this year s SXSW London Screen festival and beyond.

title: One World Orchestra
description: One World Orchestra is a UK-based intercultural music collective bringing together artists from across the world to create bold, cinematic and emotionally charged performances that blend global traditions with contemporary sounds. The ensemble features musicians and vocalists representing multiple cultures, many with lived experience of migration and displacement, combining Arabic melodies, Latin rhythms, electronic textures, British and European folk traditions and powerful vocal storytelling. For SXSW London at 93 Feet East, presented in partnership with Abbey Road Studios, OWO brings its unique global sound into the heart of the courtyard with a specially created acoustic performance full of rhythm, colour and surprise. Expect powerful voices, rich instrumental textures and an uplifting live experience that celebrates connection, culture and the energy of music without borders.

title: Mustafa Atik (Festival & Platinum Pass Only)
description: Please note, this is only open to Festival Platinum pass holders, not music wristbands. Mustafa Atik is a self-taught house music DJ and producer, born in Cyprus in 1985. He is predominantly recognized for his sound across deep, afro, and melodic house. Over the years, Mustafa has built a strong presence in the local scene, performing at some of the most respected and sought-after venues in Cyprus, where he has developed a reputation for delivering refined, groove-driven sets with emotional depth. His style blends organic rhythms with melodic textures, creating an immersive atmosphere on the dancefloor. Alongside his DJ career, Mustafa has released his own productions, reaching listeners worldwide and gaining recognition across international platforms. His track Show Me has surpassed 1 million views on YouTube, marking a significant milestone in his growing global presence. His work reflects a deep passion for house music, drawing inspiration from global influences while maintaining a distinct and authentic sound. Continuously evolving as an artist, Mustafa aims to connect people through music, bringing a unique energy to every performance.

title: PCK Dance: Vessel
description: Exploring human instincts, self-perception, and connection, the award-winning PCK Dance presents VESSEL a powerful encounter of dance and live music newly created at Abbey Road Studios by composer Sean Pett. Founded by James Pett and Travis Clausen-Knight former artists of Company Wayne McGregor and Michael Clark Company PCK Dance has rapidly become one of the UK s most internationally recognised independent dance companies. Their visually arresting productions have toured across Europe, Asia, and the Americas, earning Critics Circle recognition at the National Dance Awards and praise from Graham Watts OBE, Chair of the Awards, who described the company as the closest that independent contemporary dance has to celebrity and the next big thing . VESSEL itself has been celebrated as mesmerising and electrifying .

title: Shorts: Protest Will Not Be Tolerated
description: Where control is constant, rebellion slips through, and frustration and truth push back against systems of control. Across (and below) streets, borders, and households, the films in PROTEST WILL NOT BE TOLERATED trace acts of quiet defiance under pressure; from whispered rumours and bureaucratic barriers, to forbidden voices and everyday risks, each story reveals how control is enforced, and how it s subtly resisted. Moments of play, hunger, curiosity and connection become gestures of rebellion, whether against state power, social expectation, or inherited silence. In worlds where protest is not permitted, resistance takes other forms: intimate, absurd, fragile, and persistent. Here, protest is not as expected, instead everything hums with an uncontainable resistance. Total Running Time: 96 min Content Warnings: Flashing images, strong language, sexism

title: The Invite
description: Director: Olivia Wilde Cast: Olivia Wilde, Seth Rogen, Penelope Cruz, Edward Norton Olivia Wilde directs and stars in a poignant, hilarious, and emotionally honest portrayal of human relationships led by a stellar cast Annoying neighbours are a dime a dozen. Annoying neighbours who pop round to share a flan and invite you into their weekly orgy schedule not so much. The Invite is a portrait of two married couples as they meet and clash over a casual get-together. J For a film set entirely in one apartment, Wilde s direction is vivid and energetic, each scene building on and subverting the next, unpicking the layers of the relationships between the key characters. With all four cast members Rogen, Cruz, Norton, and Wilde herself on top form, The Invite is a type of filmmaking that is sorely lacking on our screens: a film about adult feelings for adults. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Funky Freaky Freaks
description: Director: Han Chang-lok Cast: Baek Ji-hye, Shin Jung-hang, Joo Min-hyeong A trio of teens gets into all sorts of mischief in this cool, fast-paced slice of South Korean youth Han Chang-lok s stylish feature debut sees a tight friendship between three teens begin to unravel following the arrival of a handsome new student at their high school. Edited with the frenzied touch of early 2000s television and featuring a soundtrack that matches that early MTV aesthetics, Funky Freaky Freaks paints a vivid portrayal of South Korean youth while drawing timely reflections on the anxieties of being young and online, the gnarly knots of Gen Z communication, and the ever-dangerous beauty standards of a generation raised on the perfectly polished imagery of pop idols. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Intelligence Rising
description: Director: Elena Andreicheva Cast: Marc Warner, General Patrick Sanders, Patricia Geli, Skype Jaan Tallinn, Lucy Lim, General Stanley McChrystal, Pippa Malmgren, Yuval Noah Harari Leading global authorities join AI expert Marc Warner for a war-game exploring how artificial intelligence might reshape power. Much has been said about artificial intelligence s effect on fields such as the creative arts, but we are only now beginning to experience the geopolitical consequences. Intelligence Rising chronicles a bold experiment: some of the world s most prominent thinkers in politics, military strategy, philosophy, and technology gather to play an intricate wargame designed by Marc Warner, a founder and leader who s been at the frontier of AI progress for 15 years. What ensues is a stark warning, but also a much-needed reminder of the power of human autonomy and empathy. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: BIMM Birmingham Choir
description: BIMM Birmingham Gospel Choir are a fresh, energetic group bringing a contemporary sound to traditional Gospel. The group comprises of current students and alumni of BIMM University Birmingham, hailing from various backgrounds to share the joy, unity, and hope of Gospel music.

title: Ellie Prohan (Delegate Pass Only)
description: Please note, this is only open to delegate pass holders, not music wristbands. Ellie Prohan is a UK-based DJ, radio presenter and music personality known for hosting shows on KISS, KISSTORY and Hits Radio, alongside live DJ performances across festivals, club nights and touring events. Of Iranian heritage, she s built a following through energetic multi-genre DJ sets, music broadcasting and entertainment content, becoming a recognisable face in UK dance and radio culture. She s also involved in music advocacy and ambassador work, including with Rio Ferdinand Foundation and War Child.

title: PRETTY DIRTY The Life and Times of Marilyn Minter
description: Director: Jennifer Ash Rudick, Amanda M Benchley Cast: Marilyn Minter, Jane Fonda, Jeff Koons, Lizzo, and Padma Lakshmi A provocative and visually arresting portrait of the artist Marilyn Minter. Google Marilyn Minter, and what comes up are images of women s body parts. Mouths, eyes, feet, and tongues, covered in glitter, pearls, and dirt. Encouraged by Diane Arbus in her early years, Minter has been a working artist since the 1980s, attracting controversy for her work exploring the aesthetics of beauty, desire, and the female body. Her work has inspired the high femme aesthetic of pop culture, and she has created portraits of Jane Fonda, Pamela Anderson, Lizzo, and more. Drawing on archival material, studio footage, and (very) candid interviews, Pretty/Dirty strips away the glitter to reveal a portrait of an artist who, nearing 80, has lived a life refusing to compromise. The question remains: is the art world ready to embrace her? (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Screen Keynote: Sharon Horgan
description: BAFTA-winning Sharon Horgan has long been known to audiences due to her tremendous onscreen work on series such as Catastrophe and Bad Sisters, but the multi-hyphenate is also the creative force behind many other hits, such as Motherland, Amandaland, and Netflix s recent hit Vladimir. Horgan joins SXSW London to talk about wearing many hats as an actress, writer, producer, director, and the founder of leading production company Merman, which she has built into a global production hub.

title: Shorts: Bloodlines and Faultlines
description: Family as refuge, anchor and fault line - fragile bonds that comfort and confine us, quietly shaping us across generations. Families here (or anywhere) are anything but simple. Across places and generations, these films trace bonds that comfort, confine, protect and wound, sometimes all at once. Childhood summers sit beside inherited violence, grief reshapes responsibility, and devotion is tested by expectation and deception. Mothers and daughters navigate systems that deny them agency; siblings hold each other when no one else will; grown children return to places that still define and reshape them. In each story, family is both anchor and fault line; messy, fragile, and yet (im)possible to outgrow. Total Running Time : 86 min

title: Akina Eman
description: Akina Eman is an emerging Jamaican recording artist whose sound sits at the intersection of R B, Reggae, Neo-Soul, and contemporary Pop. Since officially launching her professional music career in April 2019, during her first year of university, Akina has steadily built a catalogue defined by emotional clarity, vocal precision, and a strong sense of storytelling. For Akina, music is a spiritual medium, a way to connect to self, to others, and to something greater. That philosophy is evident in every release. She was recently signed by UK based label-Outdeh Records and her growing discography highlights a clear upward trajectory. Most recently, she released Long Way (February 11, 2026), with an upcoming release of another single Gravity - on April 2, 2026. In 2025 she released For You (May 9, 2025), and her second EP called Slumber Tapes on October 29, 2024. Earlier releases such as Proud (2024), Chance (2023), and her debut Grand Rising EP (2021) with her single Sultry (2019) showcased her consistency and commitment to refining her sound. Akina s performance r sum continues to expand with notable appearances that demonstrate her ability to translate her recordings into compelling live experiences. Recent performances include Deh Yah at Skyline Levels (December 2025), the Harry J Live Couch Series Live Show at the iconic Harry J Recording Studio (February 2025), and High Tide at Jamnesia (December 2024). Each stage has further positioned her as an artist capable of delivering both vulnerability and command in equal measure. A defining moment in her career came in 2025 with her hauntingly beautiful rendition of Bob Marley s Stir It Up for the Harry J Live Couch Series, released in honour of Marley s birthday. The performance achieved viral traction across digital platforms and earned international recognition, including a feature on BBC signaling Akina Eman as a voice with global appeal and cultural relevance. With dream collaborations that include James Fauntleroy, Frank Ocean, and Doechii, Akina Eman represents a new generation of Caribbean artists grounded in soul, expansive in vision, and ready for the world stage. She offers a rare combination: a culturally relevant voice, and an artist whose trajectory is not only promising, but already in motion.

title: Get Jiro (Episodes 1 and 2)
description: Creators: Brian Gatewood and Alessandro Tanaka Cast: Brian Tee This edgy series, based on Anthony Bourdain s graphic novel, asks the question: how far would you go for good food? Adapted from beloved chef and host Anthony Bourdain s graphic novel written alongside Joel Rose, Get Jiro is set in a near future in Los Angeles, where the ruling class has taken a curious new shape: chefs. As good food becomes scarcer and scarcer, people will go to shocking (and often blood-drippingly violent) lengths for a seat at the city s best restaurants. Mysterious sushi chef Jiro (Brian Tee) arrives in this volatile climate with a thirst for power and the recipe for the perfect rice. They do say revenge is a dish best served cold (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: IYAMAH
description: Growing up on the sounds of African drumming and reggae music in the seaside town of Brighton (UK), IYAMAH began to use the piano as a way of diary-like expression developing her songwriting at age twelve, inspired by the likes of Alicia Keys, Jill Scott and India Arie. IYAMAH moved to North London aged nineteen to study at the Institute of Contemporary Music Performance, winning a scholarship for the best vocals award. She went onto to release her debut EP Truth EP.1 which instantly gained attraction around the world, leading her to be handpicked to tour with the likes of Mahalia and Masego. She has since then performed at multiple festivals including Cross The Tracks, Lost Village, and R L, had huge support from BBC introducing and recorded live sessions for Metropolis Songs, Red-bull and Majestic Casual, and sold-out multiple headline shows in London including The Jazz Caf , O MEARA and Lafayette. She has accumulated over 35 million streams and received continuous support from BBC 1Xtra, Elton John and The Joe Budden Podcast. IYAMAH is also known for writing top lines on DnB tracks, collaborating with the likes of My Nu Leng, DJ Zinc, Polo Bryson and DJ Marky, and has toured the summer festivals including Glastonbury, Boomtown and SW4. Receiving huge amounts of support from radio including Clara Amfo s Hottest Record on BBC Radio 1. Following her own path as a solo artist, IYAMAH is focused on developing a distinct soulful R B sound which she describes as Rootsy Soul ; a combination of all of her favourite genres. She continues to stay connected to her musical upbringings, her story telling, and a lyrical focus on identity, self-worth, and female empowerment.

title: Lucky Iris
description: Composed of songwriter-producer duo Maeve and Jasper, Lucky Iris make crunchy pop music with melodic vocal hooks and hyperpop infused beats. The pair are fresh off the release of their debut mixtape, fallinlovewiththedj out 23rd January via EMI North record label partner Launchpad+. Since announcing their new mixtape in late September last year, Lucky Iris have received ongoing praise from respected tastemakers across music and culture, including Rolling Stone UK, Clash, DIY, Wonderland, NME, The Forty Five, BBC Radio 1 Dance, and BBC 6 Music. Ending the year featuring on billboards around London as one of Spotify UK s class of 2025. But there are no signs of the momentum slowing down. Since the release in the final weeks of January, Lucky Iris have been made a Spotify Radar First Listen Artist, featured on Huw Stephens Round Table on Radio 6 Music, been in the mix for New Music Fix on 6, and most recently have been announced as one of the NME 100 for 2026.

title: Muco
description: Muco is a singer, songwriter and composer of British-Burundian heritage whose music is shaped by a love of language and storytelling. He specialises in reimagining medieval folk songs and poetry in Middle and Old English, alongside original songs with lyrics inspired by myth and folklore. His latest projects, Summer Crossing and Book of Voices , blend influences from his dual heritage, pairing Middle-English lyrics with the music of the Inanga (a zither from Burundi/Rwanda). Muco crafts performances which honour historical and folk traditions while resonating with modern audiences, offering an evocative and transportive blend of past and present.

title: Mulimba
description: Returning after a hiatus to reset and make music, MULIMBA released his You, Forever EP in March 2026, finalising his comeback. The British-Zambian s genre-blending career constantly delivers Hip Hop-inspired gospel beats with Daft Punk-style hooks, providing one of the most aggressively original soundscapes for his indie vocals to shine on. A true alternative indie artist with his hands firmly wrapped around a sound that he s spent his whole life curating. After being relentless in the studio over the last few years with the likes of FKA Twigs producer Manni Dee, MULIMBA has more songs ready to release following the EP, as well as music with the Gorillaz, Kneecap, Louis VI collaborator Jelani Blackman. Everything up to this point, he has been researching and developing for these very tracks. To understand MULIMBA, consider the artists Ash has opened for: Friendly Fires, Tom Walker, and Elderbrook. Along with playing festivals in Europe, he sold out solo headline shows at the Old Blue Last, Camden Assembly and recently at Paper Dress Vintage in October 2025, which all offer a small glimpse into his potential and ambition. With gaining radio support on BBC 6 Music BBC Introducing London for the whole EP as well as performing at some bespoke festivals last year, 2026 will have a busy summer of festivals and support tour dates ahead, including The Great Escape and SXSW London. His next headline show will be at The Grace in Islington, London, on Wednesday, 16th September.

title: Pillsbury Law SXSW London Reception
