description: Sign Up Here https://notifications.pillsburylaw.com/45/6468/landing-pages/rsvp-blank.asp Pillsbury is proud to host an evening of hors d oeuvre, drinks and conversation with business leaders, investors and innovators across the creative economy.

title: Ramnik
description: For as long as she s known Ramnik has lived and breathed music, contributing holistically across a range of ventures channelling her passion into broadcasting, interviewing, DJing, curation, and lyricism. Each evolution is grounded in community, culture, and emotional connection. As a presenter, she has delivered over 130 radio broadcasts across the past decade, spanning university, community, internet, and national radio. She is committed to supporting emerging, underrepresented, and underrated talent and sounds. Ramnik s hosted multiple festival stages, including The Great Escape Platform B stage, where she introduced and interviewed the likes of Rubii and Bawo. Over the past year she s been DJing and presenting on the BBC Asian Network, including a month-long residency showcasing the many facets of her sound. From this, she has gone on to sit in for radio veterans Bobby Friction, Manara, and Dipps Bhamrah, as well as taking part in their All Star DJ Takeover and AN Certified Live event. Stepping out of the radio booth and into the club, Ramnik s supported UKG heavyweights Silva Bumpa, Yung Singh, and Prozak, spinning at bucket-list venues including Village Underground, The Cause, EartH, and Patterns Brighton. Her ideal setting is anywhere among friends, which made the early Jawani4eva nights at The Standard, an intimate British Vogue featured Diwali party, and after-hours events at the esteemed Ambassadors Clubhouse and Tamil Prince some of her most memorable gigs. Supported by the incredible creative community fostered by Jawani, her own vocal project is now in the works, set for release this summer in collaboration with fellow SXSW 2026 artist Bal T and Arkout. With nearly 10 years of radio experience behind her, Ramnik knows what it takes to produce a standout track. With self-expression, community, female frequencies, and her Panjabi heritage at the forefront, Ramnik builds on a decade of foundations and steps confidently into what s next.

title: James Thompson
description: James Thompson is a UK-based vocalist and recording artist from Derby, known for blending the emotional depth of gospel with the tone and storytelling of contemporary R B. Raised in a musical household, his voice was shaped early on by a foundation of live performance and rich musical influences that continue to define his sound today. A highly sought-after backing vocalist, James has contributed to major productions including The Lion King (2019), Rocketman , and the Did Somebody Say Just Eat? campaign. His voice has featured across leading television platforms such as The X Factor , Britain s Got Talent , and the MOBO Awards. In both studio and live settings, he has worked with a wide range of artists including Becky Hill, Dave, Snoop Dogg, Katy Perry, Gregory Porter, Cory Henry, Westlife, Karen Clark-Sheard, Donald Lawrence, Sinach, Fred Hammond, Donnie McClurkin, and Bebe Winans. As a solo artist, James brings a distinct perspective rooted in honesty, vulnerability, and faith, expressed through a sound that bridges gospel and R B for a global audience. With his debut project on the horizon, he is establishing himself as a compelling new voice with both musical credibility and crossover appeal.

title: Oscar Jerome
description: Oscar Jerome is a London songwriter, guitarist, vocalist and producer whose music moves fluidly between jazz, soul, folk, funk and alternative rock. A former member of Kokoroko, Jerome recently released his third solo album, The Fork an introspective, richly layered record pairing intricate guitar work with thoughtful, emotionally detailed songwriting. Drawing influence from artists as varied as Prince, Jimi Hendrix, John Martyn, Ali Farka Tour and Q-Tip, Jerome has become one of the defining voices within London s genre-blurring contemporary music scene.

title: Takara
description: Takara is an Australian independent artist redefining success without a label. Blending neo-soul, R B, afrobeat, and world music influences, her music is a lush mix of sensuality and self-expression. Known online as @takarathekoala , she s built a loyal global following of over 500,000 fans and generated more than 5 00 million views across platforms, entirely independent. Takara has redefined success on her own terms, crafting deeply human music that resonates louder than any major label machine.

title: Zac Jone$
description: Zac Jone$ , is a Kingston-based reggae artist, entrepreneur, and visionary shaping the next wave of Jamaican music and culture. Known for his effortless fusion of reggae, rap, and dancehall, Zac creates music with universal appeal rooted in love, spirituality, and upliftment. That mission that also drives his independent label and creative company, $TONY . He first gained attention with his mixtape Ridin Thru Kingston and has since evolved into one of the island s most exciting rising voices. His latest single, Treasure Beach, serves as both a tribute to his family roots in St. Elizabeth and the title track of his highly anticipated debut album Treasure Beach (2026). Zac has collaborated with a range of notable artists including Shenseea, Agent Sasco, Jesse Royal, Runkus, Tessellated, and Felly, with standout releases like Miss Jamaica (feat. Agent Sasco), Lonely , and The Weed Song (with Jesse Royal) highlighting his versatility and growing influence. Beyond music, Zac has become a trusted partner for global brands, serving as the face of Hennessy Pure White s 25th Anniversary campaign, with billboards across the Caribbean and Central America featuring his image and music. With a clear vision, expanding fanbase, and a sound that honors its Jamaican roots while connecting globally, Zac Jone$ is poised to become the island s next cultural icon.

title: Mike Aremu
description: Born and raised in Nigeria, West Africa, Mike Aremu began his musical journey at just eight years old, playing a variety of instruments in church. At 18, he chose to major on the saxophone a decision that would shape his destiny as one of the most respected and influential saxophonists of his generation, renowned for bringing a heavenly sound to earthly stages. Mike s unique ability to blend the rich heritage of traditional African rhythms with contemporary gospel expressions has made him a global minister of music. His gift has opened doors to minister alongside iconic figures such as Kirk Franklin, Donnie McClurkin, Fred Hammond, Mary Mary, and CeCe Winans, as well as legendary jazz artists like Kenny G, Hugh Masekela, Najee, and India Arie. He is the visionary behind Sax Appeal, Nigeria s largest jazz concert, and has graced some of the world s biggest Gospel platforms including nine performances at The Experience Lagos , and appearances at the London Festival of Life, FOL Wales, FOL Scotland, and the Greenbelt Festival. In the UK, Mike has sold out landmark venues like the Jazz Caf , Pizza Express Jazz Club , and the O2 Academy Islington , becoming one of the few African instrumentalists to achieve this feat. His new Christmas concert series, Sax in the Manger , launched to overwhelming success with a sold-out 2023, 2024 and 2025 editions. More than a musician, Mike lives out his faith through service. He actively ministers in care homes, bringing music, comfort, and the message of Christ to the elderly. In celebration of his 50th birthday, he launched the 50 Care Home Concerts Challenge, performing across 50 care homes to spread joy and the Gospel to seniors in his community. Mike s impactful ministry has been recognized with numerous awards, including the TOMA Award for Best Instrumental Album, multiple AGMA Awards for Instrumental Excellence, Crystal Awards, MEGA Awards, and AMEN Awards. He is also a two-time nominee at the prestigious KORA Awards and was honored as a JCI TOYP awardee for Best Self-Developed Personality. Mike Aremu continues to shape the sound of Gospel music worldwide one note, one soul, and one spirit-filled performance at a time.

title: AVS
description: Avs is a DJ hailing from West London, exploring the soulful side of the underground music scene. Her sets move through soul, deep rhythms, bass and unexpected cuts - not being bound by genre but by feeling. Rooted in London s community-led music culture, she is passionate about bringing people together through sound, drawing inspiration from both music and visual arts. You can expect sounds spanning from Broken Beat, Baile, House, UK Garage, UK Funky, Funky House, Soul and Dubstep. You might ve caught her at Fabric, Lost Village Festival, Night Tales, Village Underground, Rinse FM, Cafe 1001, Voices and more. Alongside her DJ career, she is also a freelance graphic designer, focusing on branding community-led initiatives, trusted by museums, community projects and charities. Storytelling sits at the heart of her approach, shaping how she connects sound, energy and visual influence.

title: Al Cologne
description: London based artist Al Cologne s sound is charged with an electric, queer tenderness, gently fizzing with emotion that has condensed over years of diaristic, kitchen-sink songwriting. Yearning, intimacy, sexual discovery are bottled in songs shaped by mood rather than fixed arrangement. Their debut EP Milk Teeth released March 2026 via Joy of Life International, drifts in the orbit of Angel Olsen, Sharon Van Etten, Big Thief, and Faye Webster.

title: Bluck
description: Part of London s new wave of drum bass talent, Bluck is making her mark in the capital and beyond. She has played standout venues including Steelyard and Corsica Studios, as well as The Warehouse and O2 Academy Leeds, sharing lineups with Chase Status, Andy C, Hybrid Minds and Shy FX. Her appearances extend to shows and brands such as Louder, Shogun Sessions and DJ Mag. Rooted in rolling jungle, her sets fuse old-school weight with a modern edge, a style that guides her own productions. In 2026, she was selected as Hospital Records Women in D B mentee. Beyond the studio, Bluck has built a growing online presence through content exploring the neuroscience and psychology of raving. With momentum across live shows, mentorship, and digital platforms, she is firmly establishing herself as part of the next wave of UK drum bass.

title: Celeste Ojatula
description: Celeste Ojatula is an alternative singer-songwriter and producer from Lagos, Nigeria. Her music is a fusion of genres and sounds; influenced by her love for life and stories. She believes music should transport you to a different place and time. Her 2024 debut EP Alo ( tales ) introduced listeners to her world, pairing romantic, moon‑lit folk melodies with sharp storytelling on songs like Ol ronb and Alu Join Join. Press praised the project for balancing nostalgia with bold futurism and positioning Celeste as the enigmatic force shaping Nigeria s contemporary folk soundscape. Her 2025 sold-out headline show in Lagos Alo Live (from a Familiar Place) cemented her reputation for immersive, multi‑sensory performances that many have praised as magical . Celeste and her team have successfully performed at major events like AFRIMA, Ake Book and Arts Festival, Lagos Fashion Week and more. She was also an ambassador for Mentally Aware Nigeria and Accountability Lab. Now, Celeste is gearing up for Our Time in the Sun a radiant, Afro-pop tinged EP arriving this May that expands her soulful palette into brighter textures. With her growing global audience, Celeste Ojatula is fast becoming one of the most interesting new voices in African music.

title: Screen Keynote: CLAIRE FOY
description: Emmy, Golden Globe and SAG Award-winner Claire Foy is one of the most renowned actresses of her generation, gaining worldwide acclaim thanks to her role as Queen Elizabeth II on Netflix s hit series The Crown. Foy is revered as a nuanced performer, who has traversed genre from historical drama to romantic fantasy and tragedy in films such as Academy-award winner Women Talking, All of Us Strangers and the recently acclaimed H is for Hawk and The Magic Faraway Tree. In the riotous satire Savage House, one of this year s Headliners at SXSW London, Foy gets to show yet another one of her facets as an 18th-century lady who will do just about anything to ensure a better life for her family. Join us as Foy discusses her deliciously subversive latest role, as well as other key performances in her singular onscreen career.

title: Zitah
description: Zitah is an R B/Soul singer-songwriter from South London, blending influences from Hip Hop, Afro sounds and Pop. Alongside releasing music, she is a powerful live performer, having graced major stages including Glastonbury, Strawberries Cream Festival and the Isle of Wight Festival. She has appeared on BBC s Little Mix: The Search and at the BRITs 2020, and has performed alongside artists such as Ed Sheeran, Stormzy, RAYE, Fireboy DML, Santan Dave, BNXN and Sault. In 2022, Zitah also is a Drum n Bass top liner, her collaboration Tell You What I Did with Pola Bryson incredibly surpassed 11 million streams. 2026 marks a breakthrough moment for Zitah as she prepares to release her debut EP Villain Era , led by the single I m Trouble , I Shot Cupid and others to follow.

title: PS Hitsquad
description: Peckham-born rapper PS Hitsquad is one of the most compelling voices to emerge from UK rap s past decade. First gaining recognition as a founding member of Zone 2 alongside Kwengface, he helped define South London drill with striking lyrical dexterity and raw street storytelling at just 16. As a rising solo star, standout releases like Guten Tag (20M+ streams have cementing his dazzling wordplay and commanding delivery. Now entering a new chapter with the House Anxiety label, PS is displaying an artistic evolution that both pushes boundaries and plays to his strengths, emerging at the forefront of the musical landscape that he brands as post-drill . Recent single Popeye , featuring Cristale, captures this shift an energetic, bar-for-bar exchange that blends playful references with incisive lyricism. Across recent releases including Chasing My Grave , 123 and Turtle , he shows clear artistic progression, expanding his sound while retaining his core intensity. With growing support from platforms such as Clash, Wonderland, GRM Daily and Mixtape Madness, and a debut album on the horizon, PS Hitsquad is not just part of UK rap s next wave, he s helping define it.

title: Meduulla
description: Meduulla is a 26-year-old Zimbabwean-born rapper and DJ from North Manchester whose music defies labels. Combining modern flows and witty lyricism with jazz-infused hip-hop beats, her sound both reflects the present day and carries a nostalgic edge. Meduulla first broke onto the scene as a finalist on BBC s The Rap Game UK , quickly establishing herself as a force to be reckoned with.

title: Samory I
description: Samory I is a roots reggae singer from Kingston, Jamaica, whose ethereal voice evokes deep emotion and delivers powerful roots reality messages. Rising from Kencot with a passion nurtured in the church, he broke onto the scene with his acclaimed debut album Black Gold (2017), produced by Rory Stonelove Gilligan, featuring the breakout hit Rasta Nuh Gangsta (10M+ YouTube views, 6M+ Spotify streams). Since then, Samory I has collaborated with reggae heavyweights including Protoje and Jesse Royal, released standout singles such as Life Is Amazing and Love and Mercy , and delivered his 2023 album Strength, named one of Rolling Stone s 100 Best Albums of the Year. A commanding live performer across Europe s top reggae festivals, Samory I embodies a new era of roots reggae grounded, conscious, and limitless.

title: The Boy With the Light-Blue Eyes
description: Director: Thanasis Neofotistos Cast: Giorgos Karydis, Pablo Soto, Syrmo Keke, Sofia Filippidou A cursed blue-eyed boy seeks love in a remote mountain ruled by superstition Petros (Giorgos Karydis) spent his whole life trying to understand why his strict grandmother and overly protective mother made him wear a cumbersome face mask to hide his eyes from the rest of the world. When an elderly man dies in their remote mountain village soon after locking eyes with the teen, Petros finally understands why: his light-blue eyes carry a curse. Shunned by the community and afraid for his life, Petros escapes with the help of his mother in Thanasis Neofotistos s poetic, beautifully shot queer allegory on the dangers of ostracism. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: The Whisper
description: Director: Gustavo Hern ndez Cast: Ana Clara Guanco, Marcelo Michinaux, Luciano C ceres Two siblings flee their violent father and come across something even more horrifying in the latest by horror master Gustavo Hern ndez Ib ez Vampire films are a dime a dozen. They are creatures that have been reinterpreted many times over in cinema, but when a fresh take appears, it stands out like a lighting bolt. The Whisper (El Susurro) does just that: horror master Gustavo Hern ndez Ib ez (The Silent House) anchors a universal story of family, belonging and intergenerational curses in a fresh new vision of vampirism. Fleeing from their violent father, siblings Lucía (Ana Clara Guanco) and Adrián (Marcelo Michinaux) take refuge in a remote mansion, only to find that their neighbours are intending to kidnap them to produce a snuff film. As Luc a fights to protect her brother, she must face their family s dark family curse. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Faithchild
description: Faith Child is a MOBO and multi-award-winning rapper from South London, widely recognised as one of the UK s leading Contemporary Christian artists. For over a decade, he has been at the forefront of Christian urban music, blending faith with hard-hitting beats and delivering bold, culture-shaping anthems. A dynamic performer known for his energy, authenticity, and powerful message, Faith has electrified stages in over 20 countries, captivating audiences from intimate gatherings to crowds of over 50,000, while redefining what independent artists can achieve.

title: The Night
description: Director: Paul Urkijo Alijo Cast: Yune Nogueiras, Elena Irureta, Ane Gabarain, I ake Irastorza The stories of women and witches intersect in this gorgeous folk horror set in the 17th Basque Country. In 17th Spain, everyone expected the Inquisition. Set in the harsh world of the Catholic witch-hunts, the young bride Kattalin (Yune Nogueiras) escapes from her violent new husband with a little help from a poisonous mushroom. Shrouded by the night and the woods, she stumbles upon three old women, gossiping and sharing horror stories. Unfolding in a series of horror vignettes, Gaua ( the night in the Basque language), sees writer-director Paul Urkijo Alijo weave a tale of corruption, sexual liberation, and classic storytelling into a folk feminist parable that culminates in a feverish finale. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: The Other Side of the Sun
description: Director: Tawfik Sabouni Cast: Abdelkafi Alhaj, Mahmoud Alqadah, Mohammad Hamki Five men process the trauma of captivity by reconstructing their time in a Syrian prison Tawfik Sabouni s remarkable film takes its name from the infamous Syrian prison of Saidnaya, where he was kept captive during the Assad regime. Following the fall of the authoritarian ruler, the filmmaker returned to the crumbling grounds of Saidnaya alongside four other former prisoners to cathartically dissect the horror of their captivity through poetic, detailed reconstructions. In the reopening of wounds, the men powerfully reclaim their voices. As cinema tries to grapple with the horrifying effects of war and conflict as it unfolds in real time, The Other Side of the Sun proves a singular insight into the intricacies of trauma and the restorative power of art. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Barrio Triste
description: Director: Stillz Cast: Brahian Acevedo, Juan Pablo Baena, Samuel Andr s Celis Visionary music director Stillz makes his debut in this violent, raw and hunting 90s-set found footage. Sitting somewhere between documentary, fiction and nightmare, Barrio Triste is the unclassifiable film debut of visionary music director Stillz (who s made videos for Bad Bunny, Rosal a, and Omar Apollo). Set in the outskirts of Medell n, Colombia, in the 90s, the premise is deceptively simple: a camera falls into the hands of a group of lawless young men, who use it to document the everyday violence, criminality, and lovelessness they have become used to. Violent, raw, and sad, it s a film of bold creative swings and extreme emotionality. Stillz is clearly a singular, original voice, and it shows in every frame of Barrio Triste. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Chisara Agor
description: CHISARA (Agor) is an artist without boundaries, writing and producing music to live within their inter-disciplinary worlds of performance and art. They have built a reputation for passionate and theatrical live performances as they continue to share their genre bending afro, indie-folk and jazz tinged alt-pop. Chisara s emphasis on rhythms, unmistakable vocal performance and poetic storytelling speak to an ancestral memory while interrogating the chaotic present. Their most recent single And I Fell To The Earth showcases Chisara s step into their burgeoning Afro-alt-pop sound as a precursor to an EP and future album. Chisara is not going anywhere quietly and has graced iconic stages across London as well as opening for the worlds biggest artists like Corinne Bailey Rae, BlackStreet, Anais Mitchel and Tom Odell.

title: Farooque Bhai Project
description: Farooque Bhai Project is the musical brainchild of NYC-based Bangladeshi Canadian artist Issa Farooque, widely recognized as a pioneer of modern Bangla Alt-Pop. Blending witty, nostalgic, and often socially conscious lyrics with catchy, funk-pop tunes, he has gained popularity in the Bengali indie music scene with hits like Golapi , 1990 (Naach Bangali Naach) , Duita Dekhi and Shokaleo . The vibes are real. Dushtami Sharakhon.

title: Gabriella Bongo & Ayah Marar
description: Electronic music artist from South London, primarily making drum and bass music. Signed to Hospital Records in 2025, has played shows across Europe and has had multiple mixes air on BBC Radio 1 and KISS FM.

title: Jabulile Majola
description: Jabulile Majola is an Afro-folk singer-songwriter whose music grows out of a life shaped by faith, identity and storytelling. Born Jabulani Majola in Pietermaritzburg and adopted into a pastor s family in rural Greytown, KwaZulu Natal at the age of two, he grew up alongside 26 siblings. His foundations in the church led him to discover a passion for singing early on, finding his first musical grounding in hymns and community song. Raised bi-lingually in English and isiZulu, his fascination with language evolved into poetry and rap, with songs recorded in rural studios in Greytown. Following high school, he moved to Cape Town and began exploring folk music, immersing himself in the works of Bon Iver, Paul Simon, Joseph Shabalala and Leonard Cohen. Inspired by their storytelling, he directed his own talent with the pen towards a deeply personal project named after his mother, Jabulile Majola, the only piece of her identity he ever knew. Through his writing, Jabulile explores his complex identity with tremendous vulnerability, carried by a voice that holds remarkable emotional depth. Speaking about his debut E P Isitifiketi, he explains, I write music telling stories of how it was growing up, and the different things that shaped the way in which I think and imagine. His growing impact saw him named Apple Music s Up Next artist in South Africa, where he shared that he is proud of this platform for championing African storyteling, voices that are full of life and deserve to be heard by the world. His work has gained endorsements from artists including Samthing Soweto, Sun-El Musician, Bongeziwe Mabandla, Jeremy Loops and L psley, while critics have described his music as a whisper floating above an acoustic guitar; waves lapping at the shore beneath it. Through his performances and recordings, Jabulle Majola continues to establish himself as one of South Africa s most compelling emerging storytellers.

title: Jungli
description: Jungli is a DJ and music producer from London, now based in Toronto. From the age of 15, he began spinning at local house parties and banqueting halls in his area. Today, he plays on top sound systems and in clubs across Toronto, New York, and London, showcasing a distinctive blend of UK bass, South Asian, and Afro-influenced sounds. At a Jungli set, you can expect to hear his own original and unreleased productions, alongside a wide range of genre-blending tracks that might even leave your Shazam struggling.

title: Tiwa Savage - Thursday
description: If you are a Platinum Pass Holder eligible for advance booking, then you can reserve your spot to see Tiwa Savage by RSVPing to the DJ Ace s Everything RnB showcase on the official SXSW London app. Please note that reservation spaces are limited and that entry is only guaranteed if you arrive before the showcase starts at 7:00pm. Admission for late arrivals will be subject to venue capacity. In her mind, Tiwa Savage still has so much more to accomplish. In fact, she feels her most excited yet, having just moved back to England. There s so much of my life here, it makes the most sense to be here, at this point of my career, she rationalises. After more than two decades as a musician inclusive of songwriting and backing vocals too the decorated 45 year old is a seasoned pro, having experienced many firsts as one of Afrobeats sole women across the late 2000s. It s hard being the first one, I often feel like a scapegoat. Accepting of the duality that comes with being a pioneer, a forebear, and fundamentally a door opener for women s representation across the modern-afrobeats realm, she d often bear the brunt of sexist remarks, being pitted against many of her peers of the same sex, and now facing rigidity in being allowed to stand alongside the contemporary class of women in the space. A lot of the time the conversation is about passing the torch, but I m far from ready to retire, we can all stand as one, together, she solutionaises. Known for her enigmatic offering to the canon, seminal songs like Kele Kele Love, ushered her into a frothing space, inclusive of Burna Boy, Davido, Sarkodie, Seyi Shay and May7even, giving identity and reference to afrobeats. Across her career, she s graced her origin genre, but also branched outward on her own terms. Her last EP Water Garri inclusive of hybrid, afropiano cuts like the Ayra Starr assisted Gara . Even further back, on her third album Celia, Glory claws at funk and dance, almost winking at Tiwa s future. In 2025, Tiwa Savage is unapologetically her, showing no signs of compromising once more. I ve done what people, labels, and others have wanted for so long. This album is for me. Aptly titled THIS ONE IS PERSONAL, the singer-songwriter is at her most tender, her vulnerabilities seeping to the surface. I ve been able to give myself the heartbreak album from an afrobeats artist that I could never find, she shares. Using Mary J. Blige as an analogy, she shares that she s always wanted an album in the afrobeats and Nigerian pop realms to be really vulnerable with, reflecting her love-related scorn authentically. We all need that scream in the mirror moment. Over the span of two years, across recording sessions Nashville, London, and San Francisco, Tiwa inspected her introspections, traversed the matters of the heart, mind and soul and ultimately, archived her womanhood for herself and the world to see transparently. Part of THIS ONE IS PERSONAL s methodology involves tributing past works that made both listeners and Savage herself feel. I love music of today, but there s so much power in what came before and what it taught me, Tiwa explains. For album opener I m done quickly instills this methodology, spotlighting ballad culture of the 80s and 90s. Backed by solely a sobering piano arrangement, Tiwa s experienced, razor-sharp runs build, her hurt immediately easy to gauge for listeners. I ve got to learn how to love me first, you don t know the value of my love, she croons. I m done was hard to record for the songstress herself, the San Francisco recording session briefly paused for Tiwa to cry in real time. I took some moments in the bathroom, I still cry hearing the song now, she admits. Sensing her emotion, Mystro, the albums co-executive producer, ensured she reached every note needed for the conviction that I m done holds, clearing the studio to provide space for her emotions. We got there, but it took a lot of me to do so. Like a vixen, Tiwa Savage gets under to get over in what she calls a rebound -tinged Angel Dust . Uniting the likes of bhangra, R B, jazz, reggae, and afrobeats, the songstress lures her lover for the night. I ve never felt so dangerous, dangerous, dangerous, scandalous, she retorts. Carnivorous in its arrival Angel Dust is as engulfing as honey, courting the listener and her temporal love interest for the night. A similar universe is ignited across the album, with the likes of For The Night more pronounced in its aims. What doesn t kill you will make you stronger, what doesn t heal you won t keep you sober, Tiwa sings across her similar afropiano and afrobeats led pastures. We ve all been there, she laughs. It s about being honest. Tiwa Savage continues to enthrall listeners, her early ear, carved across her adolescence in London, taking the lead in places. I will always be a 90s R B lover, she teases. It s the era I came of age to. Abundantly canvassing this across her third album, she samples the seminal So Into You by Tamia on You 4 Me . It s such a good song. Across the reworked afrobeats and R B meld, Tiwa Savage shines, her evocative vocals yearning for fresh love. Crafted by Mystro once more, You 4 Me is an urgent example of a seamless producer, artist relationship, reminiscent of D-Mile and Victoria Mon t. Marked not only by Tiwa Savage s relocation back to the country, but in her career to date, Tiwa Savage s relationship with England specifically London is one that inspires her creative pursuits. Across THIS ONE IS PERSONAL, Tiwa Savage ushers her creative pursuits forward once more, the appearance of Skepta helping her pave things forward. Skepta adds a tempered, softer edge to On The Low . Contemplating going public, the pair weave between public and private, the percussive soundscape that provide afrobeats foundations pacing the duo s way. It s about trading the private for being able to tell your friends and loved ones about those flowers, that date night, that movie, Tiwa says. Tiwa Savage s greatest reference vocally is Brandy. Having collaborated with the vocal bible previously ( Somebody s Son ), songs such as Holding It Down across this album, tribute this influence further. Filled with runs that salute Brandy Norwood, Tiwa Savage doubles down on a might of her own here, the diversity of her voice and vocal production laid bare for listeners consumption. New Jack Swing, 90s classic mid-tempos, that s the era of music here. Of course Brandy is a reference, but so are bands like Jagged Edge, she says. Further afield, Tiwa faces herself firmly in the mirror, both in the face of love, and in fear of it. Across Scared of Love and Pray No More she articulates her inner thoughts surrounding the surrender to her prospective partner head on. Rap-singing on the former, she begins her fall into the emotion. Accept my invitation and let me be your inspiration, cause it s now or never if we want a forever, we can t be scared of love, she pleads, almost floating across the guitar-heavy soundscape. Bleeding into Scared of Love is Pray No More Tiwa s plea now a demand. We re going all the way, and I m just tryna catch up, she sings. Here, Tiwa Savage s maturation comes to the fore, her experience, informing the beauty in her articulation around love. Tiwa concludes THIS ONE IS PERSONAL with an old friend. Starting her songwriting career alongside the now prolific James Fauntleroy, both remain a constant in one another s life. At this milestone, Savage courted Fauntleroy initially to aid in Change s production and songwriting. When I heard his voice on the reference track, I couldn t remove him, replicating his voice was near impossible, Tiwa reveals. A hauntingly magnetic brand of ghoul lies in Fauntleroy s riffs as he helps alongside a meld of the oregon, electric guitar, and piano in crafting a manifesto of change, committing to oneself for a higher power. This is changing for God, for a bigger purpose, she continues. Ending here feels poignant and right. When the dust settles, when the lessons are learned and experienced, one can move forward more informed and urgent than ever in the ever evolving quest for more. Tiwa Savage, is at her most actualised at the other side of recording this album. Her accolades consisting of EMA and Headie wins, pioneering status, and longevity pale in comparison to her sense of awareness, the illumination of her personal life, and ultimately her overcoming. I needed to do this for me, I had something to prove to myself, she summarises. With over two million monthly listeners at her feet, the world awaits the return of afrobeats and Nigerian pop dynasty.

title: Kibo
description: Kibo is a rap experimentalist, world builder, and unstoppable lyrical force hailing from Harrow, London. Operating just outside the capital s traditional musical hubs, he has carved a distinct path as one of the underground s most inventive voices. His music, equal parts chaos, craft and cartoon logic, is a vibrant splicing of global club culture, Rap lore, and genre-unnameable sound design. In Kibo s world, nothing is off limits. From Jimmy Floyd Hasselbaink to Justin Timberlake, Jet Set Radio to Jujutsu Kaisen, all are welcomed into his one-of-a-kind sonic mythology. His debut 2022 mixtape FBFR: Fuego Baby Furtado Returnz (Sorry 4 Da Longness!) and follow-up single Victory Lap showcased a maximalist narrative arc brought to life through rapid-fire delivery, absurdist humour, and a uniquely London approach to self-mythologising. Kibo has described his artistic ethos as Kwengletarianism , a philosophy of radical self-expression rooted in repurposing the everyday into the fantastical, bridging a gap between the myth and the mundane. To help further contextualize this ever-evolving world, Kibo also launched Ragamyff back in January a longform, fly-on-the-wall documentary exploring the roots of Kwengletarianism and his uniquely conceptual approach to music, art and life. Shot and directed by Marco Grey (Little Simz, Ezra Collective, Wretch 32), the film captures Kibo at his most reflective and unfiltered, interspersed with off-the-dome freestyle footage and studio b-roll. Already tipped as one of 10 musicians to watch in 2026 by Dazed and a UK Artist To Watch In 2026 by Complex, look out for more from Kibo soon.

title: Zion Marley
description: Zion Marley is a rising artist carrying forward the legacy of two powerful musical bloodlines son of Ms. Lauryn Hill and grandson of reggae icon Bob Marley. While rooted in a rich cultural and musical heritage, Zion is carving his own path with a message grounded in love, unity, and purpose. He made his official solo debut in 2024 with the reggae single Best Of Me, which has already garnered over 400,000 streams across platforms, signaling strong early reception and growing interest in his artistry. The track blends heartfelt lyricism with uplifting energy, offering a clear introduction to his creative identity and vision. Zion also joined The Fugees on their highly anticipated reunion tour, performing across major cities and building a strong live presence. Since then, he has continued to perform worldwide, connecting with audiences through both his music and his message. Beyond music, Zion lives the values he promotes. He remains active in community outreach, including hands-on involvement in food drives supporting underserved communities reflecting his commitment to service and compassion. Continuing to build momentum, Zion has followed up with a growing run of singles that showcase his evolving sound blending roots reggae with contemporary influences while deepening his message of love, consciousness, and authenticity.

title: House Gospel Choir
description: House music + a Gospel Choir = well, you get it right?! House Gospel Choir is where spirituality meets the dancefloor. For over 10 years, their trademark combination of soaring gospel vocals and house music has been a sanctuary of healing and good vibes for audiences globally. Founded by Natalie Maddix, House Gospel Choir has diligently and faithfully built and developed a beautiful community of vocalists, producers, DJs and creatives from across London and the UK, an embodiment of Natalie s own cultural, spiritual and musical beliefs. HGC s mantra We Are One is testimony of their multi-cultural and diverse approach to their artistry. Natalie explains We can t all speak at the same time and have our voices heard, but we can sing together as one voice and be understood . With a BBC Radio 1 Essential Mix under their belt, music collaborations with legends including Todd Terry, DJ Spen, Adelphi Music Factory, Purple Disco Machine, Rudimental, and Oliver Heldens, to name just a few, HGC have carved out their place as one of the most exciting live acts on the circuit. From Glastonbury to Strictly Come Dancing, Ibiza to The Brit Awards, Parklife to Steven Bartlett s Diary Of A CEO Live, celebrity weddings covered by Vogue or live streams with Kylie Minogue, HGC s trademark high-energy performances create a collective euphoria - part underground rave, part Sunday service, always an unforgettable memory! The late great Frankie Knuckles once said house music is church for people that have fallen from grace. Anyone who has been caught up in the rapture of a true house classic can testify to its power to unify and uplift and HGC take this responsibility seriously! Tagline: House Gospel Choir blends some of the best vocalists in the UK with huge house hits! Whether at Glastonbury or in the club, this is where spirituality meets the dancefloor! One Liner: House music + a Gospel Choir = well you get it right?! Previous Press: Mixmag live session: https://youtu.be/rBt4z8Y3GYU?si=lPsN7AssoA7bRlg9 BBC 1Xtra Live Lounge: https://youtu.be/X3rdvTBXrPE?si=5kHrXsKnbrpSST1r Grammy Awards interview: https://www.grammy.com/news/house-gospel-choir-rechoired-interview DJ Mag review: https://djmag.com/reviews/house-gospel-choir-love-message

title: Amaria BB - Thursday
description: If you are a Platinum Pass Holder eligible for advance booking, then you can reserve your spot to see Amaria BB by RSVPing to the Becca D s Deadly showcase on the official SXSW London app. Please note that reservation spaces are limited and that entry is only guaranteed if you arrive before the showcase starts at 7:00pm. Admission for late arrivals will be subject to venue capacity. Amaria BB is a talented singer and songwriter from London, widely known for her soulful vocals and captivating performances. With a unique blend of R B and Dancehall, her music resonates with listeners on a deep emotional level. Her powerful lyrics and mesmerizing melodies have earned her a dedicated fan base and critical acclaim in the music industry. Her music is a direct reflection of her personal experiences and innermost thoughts, making her songs remarkably relatable and heartfelt. Her soulful voice effortlessly conveys a range of emotions from vulnerability to strength leaving a lasting impact on all who listen. With her infectious energy, raw talent, and unwavering authenticity, Amaria BB creates a powerful connection with her audience. As she continues to evolve as an artist, she remains committed to creating music that moves and uplifts the soul while inspiring audiences around the world.

title: Bal-T
description: Hailing from West London, Bal-T grew up surrounded by music, influenced early on by his grandfather s records and his cousins passion for sound. That musical upbringing laid the groundwork for his signature style a high energy blend of club-ready genres like Jungle, Garage and DnB all layered with the rhythmic influence of his South Asian roots. Over the past decade, Bal-T has carved out a reputation for genre-bending sets that ignite everything from intimate underground spaces to major festival stages. He s lit up venues such as KOKO and Fabric, brought his sound to weddings and warehouse raves alike, and earned a loyal following along the way. His dynamic sets have drawn support from scene shaping names including Yung Singh and DJ Habibeats, with backing from BBC Asian Network and widespread love across community and club circuits. As a resident at Jawani 4eva, Bal-T has shared stages with icons such as Panjabi MC while championing a lane that blends diasporic identity with forward-thinking UK club culture. His crossover into the wider dance space has seen him perform alongside artists like Saint Ludo, Tommy Villiers, and Star.One. Bal-T s reach is increasingly global. With shows across North America, India, East Asia, and Australia including a standout set on Toronto s Freq Room platform he continues to broaden his international footprint. 2025 marks a major milestone, with performances on the main stage at Breaking Borders, as well as the Red Bull Stage at Boomtown, underscoring his rise as a boundary-pushing force within the next generation of global club DJs. Blending heritage with innovation, Bal-T is building bridges between cultures, dancefloors, and genres and shaping the sound of where club music is headed next.

title: Hoax & PVC
description: If there s one artist that needs to be on your radar right now, it s Hoax. Since sending shockwaves through the circuit with his massive anthem Crowd Control, the Devon-based young gun is now officially signed to Hospital Records to embark upon the next chapter of his journey to the top. Raised in a music-filled household, Hoax quickly found a passion for all forms of bass-heavy music and was soon drawn to the UK s underground dance scene. Combining his knowledge of the piano, music theory and a love for heavy-hitting club music, it was only a matter of time before he started creating his own tracks and perfecting the art of DJing. Now making a name for himself with his signature rumbling basslines and immaculately crisp productions, his creative output and energetic DJ sets have led to a string of heavyweight releases and exciting bookings alongside some of the most established names in the industry. With his recent releases including Care 4 U and an offical rework of Breach s Jack via Hospital Records racking up support from the likes of Sub Focus, Camo Krooked, DJ Marky, Charlie Tee, Break and Hybrid Minds to name just a few, Hoax is no stranger to the airwaves either, with his music frequenting spins across the likes of BBC Radio 1, Kiss FM and Capital Dance. So put your tin foil hats down because this is the real deal - Hoax is only just getting warmed up!

title: Jords
description: Jords, is a Croydon-based artist who has earned his stripes by being an effortless talent. As a prolific songwriter and producer, Jords has consistently demonstrated his musical prowess and has been on a meteoric rise since the release of his motion picture-assisted project Almost an Adult in 2020. Notably, he holds the distinction of releasing the first UK rap album with legendary label Motown Records, releasing the album Dirt in the Diamond with notable features such as Masego, Wretch 32 and Kranium, showcasing his unique talent and appeal. As well as this, Jords released a 27-minute short film of the same name, which was selected for the TriBeCa Film festival, and nominated for 11 awards, including 3 UK music video awards.

title: Msaki
description: Asanda Lusaseni; known professionally as Msaki, is a South African composer, singer, and songwriter. Although she is known as a musician, Msaki trained in fine art, graphic design, film photography and curation at Rhodes University, Leeds University, Nelson Mandela Metropolitan University and East London Technikon. She is a South African Music Award (SAMA) winning musician, who contributed to Black Coffee s Grammy Award winning album as writer, composer and artist. Her practice now entails intentionally synthesizing her musical and visual expression through work that merges installation and performance. This expression is seen through the launch of her album: Of Love in Protest, at Nirox Sculpture Park 2022, Johannesburg, and presentation of Ndiyozilanda at the National Arts Festival 2023. She is the 2022 Standard Bank Young Artist of the Year for Music and a part of this year s South African Pavilion at the Venice Biennale. Msaki is a brand in motion. Her work is mainly embodied through music, songwriting, music production and various iterations within visual arts. Some of these expressions are now finding more time to blossom. Parallel to building her name as an artist, Msaki s heart was always about building structures that would hold her desire to serve. It was clear from the start that the philosophy was BUILD as you GROW, LIFT as you RISE. There is a clear transition and shift in energy and focus that is being communicated to anyone that wishes to be part of her future.

title: ODUMODUBLVCK Thursday
description: If you are a Platinum Pass Holder eligible for advance booking, then you can reserve your spot to see Odumodublvck by RSVPing to the Clash showcase on the official SXSW London app. Please note that reservation spaces are limited and that entry is only guaranteed if you arrive before the showcase starts at 7:00pm. Admission for late arrivals will be subject to venue capacity. Born in Lagos and raised in Abuja, Nigeria, ODUMODUBLVCK has been one of the most exciting voices out of the country in the last decade! He operates under a self-defined genre he calls Okporoko Rhythms: a form of Hip-Hop that takes influences from Grime, Fela Kuti s Afrobeat, and Progressive R B. This musical core informs his deep, thoughtful lyricism which takes centre-stage on every track and stage he graces. Pairing his larger-than-life personality with his arresting flow, delivered with a trademark menacing growl and undeniable on stage showmanship, has led critics and fans alike to draw comparisons with the legendary Ruff Ryder, the late, great DMX and afrobeat legend BURNABOY. He garnered even more praise, with Co signs raining in from the likes of Davido, Wizkid, Skepta, Stormzy as the leader of the next generation.

title: Finn Askew
description: With guitar in hand and a clear creative vision, Finn Askew has emerged as one of the UK s most exciting young artists. Raised in a small Somerset market town, Finn was surrounded by creativity from an early age. His father is an artist, his sisters performed in musical theatre, and it was his mother who first encouraged him to pick up the guitar. He started playing at just seven years old, taking lessons before eventually teaching himself and developing his own style. It wasn t until auditioning for a school play that he discovered his voice, and with it, a new way to express himself. Drawing inspiration from artists as varied as Lil Peep, The Beach Boys, Nirvana, Justin Bieber, Frank Ocean and Juice WRLD, Finn began crafting the genre-blurring sound that defines his music today: emotionally honest, melodic, and instinctive. His breakthrough came during the pandemic with the release of Roses. The track quickly gained global traction after Taeyong of NCT 127 played it on a livestream and sang along, sparking a surge of attention. Soon after, Jungkook shared the song on social media, helping propel it past 65 million streams and introducing Finn to an international audience. Since then, Finn has continued to build momentum. He was named a VEVO DSCVR Artist to Watch, performed at major UK festivals including Reading and Leeds Festivals and The Great Escape, and joined both Odeal and Anees on tour.

title: Jaguar
description: Jaguar is the host of the Radio 1 Introducing Dance show, as well as a DJ, producer and host. Jaguar is hugely passionate about supporting grassroots music and championing diversity in the industry. In 2024 she released a gender report with The Jaguar Foundation as a way to help drive change forward. She has also been awarded an Honorary Masters by The University of Coventry in recognition of her outstanding contribution to the promotion and support of women in dance music. She runs her own label, club night and podcast, UTOPIA, which is all about having fun with no filters and celebrating LGBTQ+ communities. She s played major line-ups ranging from California s Lightning in a Bottle Festival and Warehouse Project to her residency at Pikes Ibiza. Last year Jaguar released her EP on her label UTOPIA, with debut single WILD receiving support from artists such as Peggy Gou. This year she returned to host IMS alongside Pete Tong, with exciting new releases on the way and a summer residency at London s queer hotspot, Dalston Superstore.

title: Makoto & Ayah Marar
description: Makoto Shimizu, known simply as Makoto, is one of the most respected and enduring figures in drum bass. Originally from Tokyo and now based in London, his sound blends soulful musicality with the energy of UK bass culture. Inspired by artists such as Ryuichi Sakamoto, he began producing after discovering drum bass through Gilles Peterson s radio shows. His breakthrough came in 1999 when LTJ Bukem signed him to Good Looking Records, launching a career defined by his signature fusion of fast breaks and soul. He has since released extensively, including three albums on Hospital Records, alongside releases on UKF, DnB Allstars, Liquicity and his own imprint Human Elements. His collaboration with MC Conrad on Golden Girl remains a genre classic. Continuing to evolve, Makoto s music blends soul, disco and funk influences into modern drum bass, while he maintains a global touring schedule across Europe, the Americas and Asia.

title: Whiney B2B Hugh Hardie & PVC
description: Will Hine aka Whiney, one of Hospital Records youngest ever signings, has been lighting up dance floors and festivals with his trademark underground sounds for over seven years. Introduced to classical instruments at 7 and drum bass at 14, Will signed his first track No More to Hospital Records at the age of 18. His debut album Talisman received nominations in three categories at The Drum Bass Arena Awards - Best Newcomer, Best Album and Best Track for the anthem Flashlight (ft. Inja) which held the Beatport top spot for over 2 weeks. No longer viewed as a newcomer, his second album Waystone cemented his name as one of the scene s most well-respected artists followed by the three-part Bubblers series. His compositions have also been included by the likes of BT Sport, Soccer AM, Need For Speed Heat, Watch Dogs, Forza Horizon 3 4, GRIP and many more. In recent years, Will has become renowned for carving out his own grime and drum bass infusion with the renowned Headlines (ft. Subten). This caught the ears of UK icon P Money and the duo have since released their Streets, Love Other Stuff LP via Hospital Records, boasting hit singles including Buss The Red , Sorry I m Not Sorry and Dead In The Eye (ft. Example). Hugh Hardie first came to prominence at the start of 2014 when his track Tearing Me Apart was used to celebrate Hospital Records landmark birthday on their We Are 18 compilation. The Cambridge-based virtuoso was exclusively signed to the roster a year later with the release of his EP City Soul , which set the precedent for his bright and uplifting liquid productions. The year 2017 saw Hugh truly put his stamp across the drum bass world with his highly regarded full-length debut LP Colourspace . Hugh s second studio album Shadows Silhouettes was released in 2019 to further critical acclaim. In 2020 Hugh released his 7 Tunes In 7 Days project, which was written during his period of lockdown isolation in April and wowed the drum bass community. The producer set himself a challenge to write a new track from scratch every day for a week, and the outcome was nothing short of spectacular. Over the years, Hugh has built a loyal following and earned himself top DJ spots across Europe s biggest events including Glastonbury, Sun Bass and plenty of global Hospitality events.

title: Zero T & PVC
description: 20+ years, four albums, countless EPs and endless 12 s on every drum bass label you could care to mention; Zero T (fka Zero Tolerance) has long been a stalwart of the scene. Debuting on Reinforced Records at the turn of the century, Zero T continues to be a prolific contributor to the ever-growing landscape of jungle music. His career highlights include curating the FabricLive.52 album, collaborating with Noam Chomsky and remixing Goldie, Dillinja and Lemon D amongst many others. 2022 saw the release of his latest LP Kilburn - a concept album made with blind jazz pianist Andre Louis aka Onj. His other projects include Searchlight: a live jazz soul project made with long-time production partner Beta (released on Goldie s new imprint Fallen Tree/1hundred), and a solo alias Bruk Rogers for broken beat productions, signed to Tru Thoughts.

title: Counting the BPM of UK rap
description: Whilst no one can truly pinpoint the best year for rap, 2016 was no doubt a prized vintage. With everyone rushing to their rose-tints for a look back at the year of the Mannequin Challenge, Konnichiwa and *that* XXL cypher - let s bring things into the present and take stock of the UK scene as it stands today.

title: XR Programme
description: Step beyond the Story. Our XR programme surrounds you with bold, immersive experiences across video installations, virtual reality, and mixed reality, where stories move with you. Feel your way through dreamlike landscapes, challenge yourself with new perspectives, and dive (literally) into playful, thought-provoking work from creators who push storytelling into new dimensions, inviting you to inhabit new worlds. Step beyond the Story. Our XR programme surrounds you with bold, immersive experiences across video installations, virtual reality, and mixed reality, where stories move with you. Feel your way through dreamlike landscapes, challenge yourself with new perspectives, and dive (literally) into playful, thought-provoking work from creators who push storytelling into new dimensions, inviting you to inhabit new worlds.

title: Beyond the Audition: Casting Directors
description: Casting directors are secret architects of culture, shaping careers and defining what audiences see on screen. In the year of the first ever Best Casting Oscar, casting directors Sophie Holland ( The Witcher, Wednesday) and BAFTA-winning Lucy Pardee (Die My Love, Aftersun, Wuthering Heights) unpack the creative, strategic, and increasingly influential role casting plays across film and TV, from discovering new talent to building ensembles that drive global hits.

title: Betting Big on Brazil: How Brazilian Musical Identity Became a Global Currency
description: TBCThere are reasons to bet big on Brazil in 2026. It is a top-ten global economy and a top-ten global music market, with a population of 213 million people and a land mass slightly smaller than Europe. Music is in the Brazilian blood, even its airport is named after Jobim! Yet this global powerhouse has gone local: the top 100 artists on YouTube are all local and perform in Portuguese. This panel reveals how that strong domestic base has become a global competitive advantage, with Brazilians exporting their music and culture to Portugal, Bolivia, and Argentina. Now, the Brazilian experience touches down in Shoreditch for SXSW LDN to let the sunshine in: a panel of Brazilian experts will unpack the four pillars that propelled Brazilian music and culture, turning their local identity into global currency.

title: Big Stories, No Borders: The New Economics of Global Co Productions
description: As financing models evolve and audiences globalise, international collaboration is no longer optional. This session explores how to structure partnerships, navigate creative compromise, and build projects that travel, scale, and resonate across markets.

title: Artist-First, AI Powered
description: Hear from Splice CEO Kakul Srivastava as she shares her insights on artist-led, AI-augmented creative tech, the state of the music industry and where art is headed next.

title: Filming the Unheard: Voice, Power, and the Politics of Representation in Cinema
description: Earlier this year, Tunisian director Kaouther Ben Hania made history as the first Arab woman to earn three Oscar nominations. Her most recent nod came through devastating docudrama The Voice of Hind Rajab , which uses real-life voice recordings of the titular 6-year-old girl killed during the war in Gaza. In this panel, Ben Hania is joined by The Voice of Hind Rajab producer Nadim Cheikroutha to discuss the increasing importance of platforming unheard voices in cinema and the political power of representation.

title: Screen Meet-up with Disguise
description: This meet-up is hosted by Disguise, the industry-leading platform at the heart of a new era of visual experiences. We provide a powerful, integrated system of software, hardware, and services designed to help creators deliver next-generation real-time spectacles. From Hollywood film sets and global TV broadcasts to A-list live events and immersive location-based experiences, we partner with the world s most ambitious studios and brands to push the boundaries of what s possible. The revolution in storytelling is happening now, and the barrier to entry has never been lower. We d love to show you how these tools are being used by studios of all sizes to redefine the creator economy. Whether you re a seasoned cinematographer or a creative lead at a boutique agency, join us to explore how to scale your next project with the latest XR and Virtual Production tools and workflows. Please note: all meet-ups are first come, first served once capacity is reached, entry will close. Arrive early, as spaces are limited.

title: The Back Catalogue Gold Rush
description: TBC

title: From Sanctuary to Stage: How Game Soundtracks Immerse Audiences Onscreen and Onstage
description: Join four of the most influential composers in games to explore how game music has evolved into one of the most powerful storytelling tools in modern entertainment, blending cinematic composition with interactive design to create deeply immersive experiences. And, ahead of this year s Game Music Festival , why these soundtracks - once confined to screens - are now commanding concert stages traditionally reserved for classical or film music and succeed in bringing new audiences to cultural venues.

title: Responsibilities of Creative Leadership
description: Director of the BFI Filmmaking Fund, Mia Bays and The Young Vic s Artistic Director and filmmaker Nadia Fall explore what it means to lead with intent in today s creative industries. From shaping institutional culture to opening doors, a position influence carries weight beyond the job title. This conversation unpacks responsibility, accountability, and the real impact of decision-making on talent, audiences, and the ecosystems that sustain film, theatre, and television.

title: Embers
description: Director: Aoqian Sun Cast: Song Jia, Wen Qi, Zu Feng, Zhang Zhanyi, Ma Ling A crematorium worker in China mixes up two sets of ashes with deadly consequences Huang Yan (Song Jia) manages the furnace at a crematorium. On one fateful afternoon, she mixes two sets of ashes at work, with the families of the dead receiving wrongful remains. The woman believes the bad luck coming from the mix-up is causing the deadly illness ravaging her body and, on a desperate attempt to save her life, sets out to undo her mistake with the unexpected help of a young girl caught in the embroil. Aoqian Sun expertly traverses the lines between social drama and thriller to prod at questions of death and family within contemporary Chinese society. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Remake
description: Director: Ross McElwee Cast: Adrian McElwee, Charleen Swansea Renowned documentarian Ross McElwee poignantly grieves the death of his son by examining a life captured on screen. For decades, veteran American documentarian Ross McElwee captured his son s growing up on screen, from his wife s pregnancy in 1993 s Time Indefinite to the delicate nature of their maturing relationship in 2011 s Photographic Memory. In Remake, the director processes Adrian s devastating death by looking back at extensive archival footage shot by two generations of McElwee men, presented alongside a stalled attempt to fictionalize his 1986 classic doc, Sherman s March. The result is both a touching elegy and a piercing dissection of memory, image, and the blurry boundaries of personal documentary filmmaking. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Does Mental Health Science Funding Need a New Paradigm in the Age of AI?
DONT MAKE THIS WEIRD
description: DONT MAKE THIS WEIRD is an artist from Wales, making emotionally charged pop for cathartic release. His sound fuses cinematic electronics, hyper-pop sparkle, and raw, confessional songwriting inspired by his experience of queer love lust and heartbreak. He recently released his debut self-titled album Co-produced with Gethin Pearson (Charli XCX, Orla Gartland) and Oscar Moos (Nxdia, Col3trane). The project earned support across Spotify editorial playlists ( New Music Friday, GLOW, Fresh Finds, Goosebumps ), and praise from pop tastemaker Sam Murphy, who called it the best album by a male artist this year. Further industry support has come from BBC Radio including being made Obsession of the Week on The Jo Whiley Show. Press includes i-D , NOWNESS, The Line of Best Fit , Attitude and DIY , with CLASH calling him a startling new talent. Live performances include Toronto Pride, NXNE, School Night North America, FOCUS Wales, SWN Festival, and Liverpool Sound City and recent headline show at The Divine. He directs all of his own visuals, with work featured by NOWNESS, screened at Vienna Short Film Festival, and nominated for a Berlin Music Video Award.

title: Deejay AD
description: Deejay AD is a veteran Birmingham DJ who has been spinning since 1998. A regular crate digger at Punch Records, he went on to play on Kiss 100, Radio 1Xtra, Rinse FM and more. With international gigs in Ibiza, LA, Russia, Prague and beyond, he is also co-founder of Rebearth Records (distributed by Sony/The Orchard) alongside Bassboy.

title: Jess Jade
description: Jess Jade is an emerging R B artist from Western Sydney whose career began at 14 on The X‑Factor Australia as part of the girl group Trill. Over the past eleven years, she has developed a distinct sound through collaboration, recording, and consistent live performance. Across three EP releases, her debut EP Bet (produced by Sergio Selim) has surpassed 100,000 streams, establishing her as a rising voice in Australian R B. A dynamic and compelling performer, Jess brings her original music to life through arrangements with bands, musicians, and DJs. She has opened for international R B artist Janine, supported T‑Pain in Melbourne, and performed alongside neo‑soul artist Anieszka at Sydney s Caterpillar Club. Beyond music, Jess featured in the Ellesse x JD Sports campaign with rapper Dibz, showcased across JD stores in Sydney, further highlighting her creative versatility. With each new project and performance, Jess continues to cement herself as a compelling force in Australia s contemporary R B scene.

title: tmdistant
description: tmdistant is an alternative artist based in Basildon, Essex, UK, who spent his early years between Hertfordshire and North West London. Raised in a Christian household, his initial musical experiences came from singing in his church choir, which ignited his passion for music. Influenced by bands such as Deftones, Linkin Park, and Superheaven, as well as contemporary artists like Aries and EKKSTACY, his music features an eclectic mix of alternative, hyperpop, grunge, and rock elements. His work often explores introspective and emotional themes, resonating deeply with listeners

title: Screen Keynote: Tom Quinn
description: Tom Quinn is the CEO and founder of leading American film powerhouse Neon. A groundbreaking creative company, Neon has gathered a whopping 57 Oscar nominations in nine years in the business, with 11 wins. Neon has also secured an unprecedented run of Palme d Or winners at the prestigious Cannes Film Festival, with six wins in a row. In his highly anticipated talk at SXSW London, Quinn will discuss turning Neon into a leader in the production space, a box office phenomenon, and the famed marketing campaigns that had audiences lining up in theaters across the world for films like Osgood Perkins Longlegs.

title: Drazy
description: Drazy is an up-and-coming artist from Birmingham who s been turning heads since the age of 15. With a natural ear for music, he first broke into the industry behind the scenes producing and engineering for some of the biggest names in the game, including Calvin Harris and D-Block Europe. After years of earning his stripes and building a solid reputation in the studio, Drazy took a step back from the background to focus on his own artistry. The result? A sound that s melodic, genre-blending, and undeniably his own. His distinct vibe and rising momentum eventually caught the attention of Stefflon Don, leading to him signing with her label, 54London. Now stepping fully into the spotlight, Drazy is proving that he s not just behind the hits he is one.

title: Anieszka
description: Anieszka is an Australian/Mauritian artist based in Sydney, Australia. Fusing R B, Soul and her Mauritian roots, she provides introspection with her thought-provoking writing and dulcet vocals. With over 10 million streams and recognition as a 2025 Acclaim All-Star, Anieszka s infectious yet deeply emotive music has been championed by triple j, Bandcamp Daily, BBC Radio, Vogue France, Capital XTRA, RINSE FM, COLORS, and RUSSH Magazine. With numerous performances across Sydney, Melbourne, and London, and showcases at BIGSOUND (2023) and SXSW Sydney (2024 2025), her live presence is as soothing as it is magnetic - drawing listeners into a world that feels both intimate and expansive. Dubbed the R B Angel, her music feels like a love letter to our inner selves. There s no doubting Anieszka s ability to fill every crevice with soul and remind us what it is to be human. She does not conform to standards, nor current trends, she s here to break down barriers with hypnotic, soulful music in its purest form.

title: Trappy SelfSuccess
description: Birmingham-born and raised, Trappy has been a commanding presence in the UK grime and rap scene for 15 years. Starting his musical journey at just 12 years old, he was inspired by his family s deep connection to music his cousins were artists, his father a sound engineer with a love for rap, and his nan immersed in gospel. Growing up during the rise of bassline and early grime, Trappy saw the movement unfold and knew he had to be part of it. Trappy is a seasoned artist whose style is heavily influenced by legends like Ghetts, Safone, and C4. In the crowded grime scene, where every voice competes for dominance, he carved out his space with raw aggression, sharp projection, and undeniable energy. His journey through platforms like Lord of the Mic, the RedBull Cypher where he clinched victory and Prize Fighter solidified his reputation as a battle-tested, high-caliber artist.

title: High Regard
description: Emerging from the vibrant music scene of Hertfordshire, alt rock trio High Regard serve a throwback sensation, enwrapped within guttural guitar riffs, and intermingled with an innovative and modern rage.

title: Dyvr
description: Dyvr is a UK-based trans electronic artist creating sweaty, euphoric club music rooted in queer and trans experience. Blending raw vulnerability with unapologetic bangers, her sound channels dancefloor release into emotional and political catharsis. Her live shows are immersive, loud, and fiercely communal.

title: Mayhem NODB
description: Mayhem NODB is among the most unique Grime artists with his bassy, powerful delivery. His energy is personified since his debut at 14 years old. From then, the Birmingham powerhouse has taken multiple strides which now sees an extensive catalogue which boasts notable collaborations with Jaykae, Mike Skinner, P Money, President T and many more. With the level of ability Mayhem possesses he has collected a great share of accolades along the way. These range from placements in the Grime Shutdown playlist with his release P110 Hoods Hottest (On Toppa Dat), to opening up for Skepta at a sold out Alexandra Palace show and inclusion in the Channel 4 documentary Pirate Mentality . You would be hard pressed to find an artist whose live presence is both felt and respected as much as Mayhem NODB is by both fans and peers.

title: The Value Shift
description: There has never been a better time to be an artist. There has never been a more important time to be a manager.

title: RTKAL
description: Hailing from Birmingham, RTKal brings a fresh blend of UK rap and Dancehall, mixing sharp bars with rhythm driven, melodic flows. His sound carries that bounce, pulling from Caribbean influence while staying grounded in real UK storytelling. Switching easily between laid back vibes and direct delivery, RTKal knows how to make music that hits in both the streets and live settings. It s a sound built on energy, feeling and authenticity, repping a new wave coming out of Birmingham.

title: RISSA
description: Inspired by the worlds of R B and Gospel music, Polynesian/Australian artist RISSA is creating a sonic world of her own that uniquely connects to her audience. Since releasing her debut EP Made To Feel in October 2023, RISSA has amassed over 25+million streams and sold 200+ EP vinyl copies, quickly gaining traction and new audiences across Australia, America, UK, Canada and more. Following the release of her second EP WHERE LOVE ONCE WAS and successfully wrapping her headline Australia tour in early 2026, RISSA is claiming her place as a leading artist coming out of Australia.

title: Toys Meets Tech: The New Technologies of Toy Story 5
description: Join us for an engaging deep dive into the next generation of animation with Thomas Jordan, the VFX supervisor of Toy Story 5. Discover the innovative technologies that brought your favourite toys to life in this latest instalment. From groundbreaking animation techniques to advanced lighting systems and cutting-edge rigs, Thomas will share insights on how his team leveraged these tools to streamline workflows, tackle complex challenges, and ultimately enhance the storytelling.

title: Deejay AD
description: Deejay AD is a veteran Birmingham DJ who has been spinning since 1998. A regular crate digger at Punch Records, he went on to play on Kiss 100, Radio 1Xtra, Rinse FM and more. With international gigs in Ibiza, LA, Russia, Prague and beyond, he is also co-founder of Rebearth Records (distributed by Sony/The Orchard) alongside Bassboy.

title: Roya
description: Director: Mahnaz Mohammadi Cast: Melisa S zen, Maryam Palizban, Hamidreza Djavdan, Mohammad Ali Hosseinalipour, Bacho Meburishvili An imprisoned teacher in Iran struggles with the weight of a forced confession. The film begins in darkness, as Iranian teacher Roya (Melisa S zen) wakes up locked in a three-by-three room. Formally daring, Mohammadi used sound and first-person point-of-view to fully immerse us in the experience of her tormented protagonist: darkness, snatches of conversation, dreams and confusion all blend. Unsure of her crime and with her family cruelly being used as blackmail, Roya is forced to choose between doing a sham confession or returning to prison. Directed by documentarian and activist Mahnaz Mohammadi, Roya is not an easy watch but it s a necessary one. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: BEX
description: Who is BEX? If punk had a baby with 90s grunge, it would be named BEX. Rebellious attitude, DIY fashion, and dreamy melodies collide with the raw power of Garbage and The Cranberries . With riot grrrl ethics, distorted bass tones, and layered harmonies, BEX commands attention sonically while captivating visually with a doll-like, disarming stage presence. BEX is a powerhouse: a dream, a nightmare, and a force not to be reckoned with. Introducing new instruments, evolving soundscapes, and theatrical live performance, she pushes into 2026 with unpredictability at her core. Grunge has reawakened in her, and conformity is firmly out the window. A multidisciplinary artist at the intersection of music, fashion, and community, BEX resists categorisation. She is the full spectrum - quietly reflective when needed, and unapologetically loud when it matters most. A 90s icon born in the wrong era: fierce, fearless, and entirely herself. Fresh from supporting the powerhouse Nova Twins, BEX s SXSW set launches a defining summer. FFO: Bring Me The Horizon | Nova Twins | Wargasm | Enter Shikari | Bad Omens | Poppy | Garbage | The Cranberries | The Cure | Bikini Kill | Ramones | The Clash | Green Day | Blink-182 | Paramore | Avril Lavigne | System of a Down Career Highlights Press Awards NME 100 Essential Emerging Artists 2025 Punk s newest daughter mixing explosive nu-rock with gritty attitude Kerrang! Sound of 2025 A big breakthrough feels assured Full Pelt Music New Artist of the Year 2024 Key Festival Appearances Takedown | Collision | Manchester Punk Festival | Download Festival | Boomtown | Rebellion | Pride Rock | The Great Escape | 2000trees | Burn It Down | IFF Secret Showcase | Riot Girl Day Tenerife Team: Grace Taylor: Hootenanny, It s Never Simple - Management Steve Zapp Danielle Reginiano: ITB - Live Agent

title: AMERICAN ZOO
description: Director: Tim Travers Hawkins Truth is way, way stranger than fiction in this twisty documentary about a lost zoo and an American family playing God with nature. Emmy-nominated documentarian Tim Travers Hawkins was hiking when he stumbled upon the ruins of a zoo in the middle of the woods. A set of lost home movies became the starting point of American Zoo, a documentary that blasts open the doors of the old zoo to uncover three generations of family secrets that will take you from the sheltered forests of rural U.S. to the height of German fascism, investigating the viciousness of humans who experimented with genetic modifications in animals, believing to be able to bend the living to their will. A feat of precision, Hawkins s latest is best enjoyed without much prior knowledge of this twisty, almost unbelievable story. But trust us, you are in for one hell of a ride. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: On the Road
description: This sensual Venice winner trails a young sex worker who falls in love with an older truck driver while on the run. If Beat writer Jack Kerouac met the erotic spirit of Alfonso Cuar n s classic Y Tu Mam Tambi n, the result would most likely resemble David Pablos s searing erotic drama, produced by famed Mexican actor Diego Luna. Set in the little-explored queer scene of long-distance truck driving, On the Road follows rebellious hustler Veneno (Victor Prieto Simental), who makes a meager living by trading sexual favors at a roadside diner. His life goes from stationary to transit once he meets trucker Mu eco (Osvaldo Sanchez Valenzuela), with the two rapidly embroiled in a dangerous chase as Veneno s past threatens to catch up with them. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Dyan Tai
description: Self-described Gaysian Empress of Sydney, Dyan Tai (they/them) is a music producer and performance artist who makes electronic hyperpop music fused with global music soundscapes, Beijing opera filled with Y2K harajuku pop-punk nostalgia. Named as one of the outstanding acts to watch at BigSound (NME) and praised by Flume for their sound design (Triple J), Dyan makes sophisticated palette pop (Rolling Stone) with a personality that shines up the room (SXSW Sydney, AuReview). From humble beginnings in Malaysia to finding a niche on Sydney cabaret stages, the trilingual Chinese-Australian artist s captivating live shows have been described as a queer, euphoric dream that you don t want to wake up from (5 star review, The Age). Following the release of their viral song, Let s Get Bubble Tea! , they went on their first international UK/EU tour in Nov 2025 with a coveted spot at Anti Social Camp London (Pink Pantheress, Glass Animals), and will return as a showcase artist at SXSW London this year.

title: TRXXY
description: Trxxy (pronounced Tricky ) is a rapper from Manchester blending raw UK street life with the rhythm and feel of his Jamaican roots. Inspired by family and foundation including his brother Manchester Hypes and the Burstgang legacy Trxxy brings real moments to the mic, channeling dancehall, rap, and reflection with clarity and presence. His music is lived-in. Motivating. Unfiltered. Known for his visuals and sharp delivery, tracks like Truely (Mhmm) and Breeze connect listeners to something deeper than just sound they connect to story.

title: PA777IENCE
description: PA777IENCE is a RnB and neo-soul singer, producer, and multi-instrumentalist born and raised on Dharug Land (Western Sydney, Australia) No stranger to flooring audiences with their powerful voice, PA777IENCE is a musical rebirth a name that combines a karmic lesson of patience with the angel number for God, Heaven and The Divine. Inviting us into their private world of vulnerabilities, loves, losses and wins, PA777IENCE s music is about deeply felt lessons and dreams for the future. Inspired by greats like Erykah Badu, Jill Scott and Jazmine Sullivan, PA777IENCE puts their own distinct twist on neo-soul to create idiosyncratic beats and melodies, both familiar and new. Their rich, luscious and open-hearted sound arrives fully formed on Mastery, their 2024 six-track debut EP featuring collaborations with Mo Ju, GLO, Setwun and Saskia playlisted on both apple music and spotify. Equal parts vulnerable and powerful on-stage, PA777IENCE has brought the energy at Vivid Sydney, Sydney Festival, World Pride 2023, SXSW Sydney and more festivals in so called Australia and also wowed audiences across the East Coast with their debut headline tour in March 2025. PA777IENCE has also supported iconic Australian artists Okenyo, Ngaiire and Mo Ju, performed with Thelma Plum and KUČKA on their triple j Like A Versions, and taken part in prestigious writing camps with APRA AMCOS and One Off Traks. Recently signed to tastemaker agency Niche Productions, PA777IENCE is singing their hopes into reality.

title: Reinventing Literary Icons
description: From John Le Carr to Arthur Conan Doyle and Agatha Christie, iconic stories don t just endure: they must evolve. This session brings together producers and literary estate guardians to unpack how classic IP is reimagined for modern audiences without losing any of their potency, appeal or impact.
