How series, stage and screen adaptation expand worlds, update perspectives, and building fresh relevance while protecting the voice, legacy, and cultural impact that made these stories timeless.

title: Brixx
description: Brixx is an artist from Birmingham with roots spanning both the United States and Jamaica. Born in the United States, she moved to the UK as a young girl. With a mother originally from Birmingham and a father from Jamaica, her music reflects a rich blend of both cultures and influences. Emerging rapper Brixx first unveiled her debut single, Man , introducing listeners to her distinctive sound and compelling story. The Birmingham-based artist has a unique background that has intricately shaped the music she creates today. After years of grafting and tirelessly perfecting her craft, she is now beginning to reap the rewards of her hard work and dedication. Following the buzz generated by her gritty Blackbox Freestyle, which sparked conversations across the scene, Brixx elevated her momentum with her On Da Radar Freestyle released a month later. Since then, the freestyle has amassed over one hundred thousand views, further establishing her as one of Birmingham s rising voices. Brixx s music is rooted in authenticity, often exploring real-life issues and deeply personal experiences. Raised by Caribbean parents, she spoke Patois before she spoke English, and that influence naturally carries into both her music and everyday conversations. She frequently uses Patois to emphasise her delivery, adding another layer of identity and authenticity to her sound.

title: Big Zeeks
description: Big Zeeks The DJ From Harlesden Now Evolved into An artist THE DEFINITION OF UK DANCEHALL , Named crowned THE FRESH PRINCE OF HARLESDEN By Diggy Ustle By Uk s Reggae Icon Gappy Ranks. With an uplifting distinctive sound. From the heart of Harlesden, north west London, Big Zeeks is an independent dancehall lyricist, who consistently sets the bar high and has firmly cemented his place as a vital figure within the new generation of UK dancehall, with hits such as #Happy featuring Stylo G and D Double E, Yo , Ya Unnastand and It s A Lot Of Money . Big Zeeks has built up a cult following, easily bridging the gap between UK Rap and Jamaican Dancehall and building a sound reputation working with some of the most critically acclaimed artists such as Dexta Daps, Chronic Law, Spice, Shenseea, Kranium, Jahvilani, Rygin King, Govana and WSTRN. Zeeks has performed at his own sell out headline shows, toured the world as a supporting act globally and performed at various international festivals including Glastonbury, City Splash and SXSW. Zeeks is immersed in his local Harlesden community and has become known for paying it forward, engaging with schools and shelters, giving out hundreds of free meals to vulnerable families, earning him The Fresh Prince of Harlesden nickname. Reflecting on past triumphs, Big Zeeks accomplishments speak volumes, with over 3 million streams and over 2 million YouTube views, he continues to forge connections with fans across the globe.

title: Dream State
description: Welsh Rock band Dream State burst onto the scene in 2014, quickly carving a space for themselves at the forefront of modern rock. Combining elements from electronic and heavier genres, they have uniquely crafted their own sound. The group currently consists of guitarist and founding member Aled Evans, bassist Jake Bowen and vocalist Jessie Powell. Latest single Words Unsaid captures the bands evolving identity, a familiar taste of the past, but a more and more exciting statement of where the band are heading next. dreamstateofficial.com

title: Future Shade
description: FUTURE SHADE is an electronic duo formed by Jannah Beth and Andrew Meyer, working between Australia and Aotearoa. Their music lives in the space between euphoria and nostalgia, emotional, soulful electronic songs shaped by long conversations, shared histories, and an instinctive trust in process. The duo met while creating Jannah Beth s solo project Orbits Echoes, discovering an immediate creative alignment rooted in emotional honesty, patience, and curiosity. Their process often begin with dialogue, allowing songs to emerge through freestyles, repetition, and subtle evolution rather than forced outcomes. FUTURE SHADE explores themes of love, transience, cosmic connection, and shared humanity. Their sound blends organic and electronic textures, balancing melancholy and release, music designed for sunset festival stages as much as intimate listening. The name FUTURE SHADE is inspired by Future Shade , the album by The Herd a philosophy of planting seeds for futures beyond ourselves. It reflects a long-term commitment to lineage, care, and intentional creation: honouring the shade inherited from those before, while creating spaces of rest, connection, and meaning that may never fully be experienced by the creators themselves, for those who come after.

title: Boy Boy
description: Boy Boy s music is characterized by his unique flow, hard-hitting lyrics, and eclectic mix of dancehall, reggae, and hip-hop influences. His powerful delivery and ability to switch seamlessly between different styles have earned him comparisons to some of the greatest dancehall artists of all time. Despite his youth, Boy Boy has quickly made a name for himself in the industry, gaining a devoted following of fans around the world.

title: Thandi Phoenix
description: South African/ Australian artist Thandi Phoenix first broke through with a run of releases that established her as one of Australia s standout voices, including featuring on the longest running #1 by a female artist on the ARIA Club Charts. Her soulful vocals and songwriting have led to collaborations with global dance acts including Rudimental, Sigma and Sleepy Tom, while her music earned full rotation across Australian commercial and independent radio. Handpicked for her captivating live performances and magnetic energy, Thandi has supported artists including Miguel, Tinashe and Jhen Aiko, alongside appearances at major festivals and renowned live venues across Australia. Now based in London, Thandi is entering a new era, blending R B, amapiano, afrobeats and dance driven production into a sound built on hypnotic rhythms, alluring vocals and intimate songwriting that lingers in the body long after the music stops. At SXSW London, she ll be performing unreleased material that marks the beginning of this next chapter, instinctive, genre-blurred and equally at home in the club, on a festival stage or through an intimate vocal performance.

title: Deejay AD
description: Deejay AD is a veteran Birmingham DJ who has been spinning since 1998. A regular crate digger at Punch Records, he went on to play on Kiss 100, Radio 1Xtra, Rinse FM and more. With international gigs in Ibiza, LA, Russia, Prague and beyond, he is also co-founder of Rebearth Records (distributed by Sony/The Orchard) alongside Bassboy.

title: Devaura
description: Born in Aotearoa, and based in Dharug/Western Sydney, DEVAURA is a rising star in the emerging Australian music scene, known for her ethereal vocals and innovative soundscapes. She burst onto the scene in 2023, captivating audiences with her powerful performances and charismatic lyricism. A versatile artist with a background in both singing and rapping, DEVAURA has quickly gained recognition for her unique style and authentic storytelling. Her recent accolades include debuting at #9 on the ARIA Album Charts and delivering standout performances for, winning the SXSW Sydney Justin Cosby Prize Fbi Radio Best Live Act in 2025 . Overcoming significant challenges as a queer, first-generation immigrant and formerly houseless, DEVAURA s resilience shines through in her music. Her songs explore themes of love, loss, and self-discovery, inviting listeners to connect with her personal journey. DEVAURA s live performances are nothing short of mesmerising, with her seamless transitions between beats and soulful vocals, combined with her magnetic stage presence.

title: Screen Meet-up with The British Blacklist
description: This meet-up is hosted by The British Blacklist, bringing together Black film and TV professionals, creatives and those passionate about championing Black British talent across screen industries. The British Blacklist is a UK-based platform celebrating and connecting Black creatives through editorial, events and industry advocacy, creating visibility, opportunity and community across film, television and the wider creative sector. Please note: all meet-ups are first come, first served once capacity is reached, entry will close. Arrive early, as spaces are limited.

title: Amoeba
description: The line between free-living and parasitic existence as a teenage girl is carefully walked in Siyou Tan s debut feature film which explores the pitfalls of conformity and the consequences of going astray. 16-year-old Choo Xin Yu (Ranice Tay) is the new student at an elite all girls school. Instead of doing her best to fit in and comply, Choo Xin Yu quickly forms a girl gang with three others. The authoritarian backdrop of their competitive school becomes a symbol and reflection of their day to day existence in Singaporean society, with the school uniform being the only thing the young women comply with. Choo Xin Yu turns the camera on the world, the way she wants to see it. With glimpses of what the gang choose to document, through their eyes and perspective, Tan creates a compelling tapestry of loyalty, righteousness and sisterhood, making the paths and broken bonds harder to stomach. Whether within the schools walls, inside of a teenage girl s bedroom or on the streets of Singapore, Amoeba explores coming-of age under authoritarian rule, capturing the changing face of Singaporean identity and the desire to remain young while longing for real life to begin. (ISRA AL KASSI)

title: SICKO
description: Director: Aitore Zholdaskali Cast: Ayan Utepbergen, Dilnaz Kurmangali A young couple makes a dangerous fortune by faking cancer in this biting Kazakh thriller Set in the sprawling Kazakh metropolis of Almaty, this blood-pumping thriller follows Azamat (Ayan Utepbergen) and Tansholpan (Dilnaz Kurmangali), a young couple who are recently married and terribly broke. When violent debt collectors knock on their door, the two hatch up a plan with dangerous consequences: to fake Tansholpan s terminal cancer to rack up donations. When the scam goes viral, the two find themselves embroiled in a dangerous web of lies that gives this fast-paced, stylish social satire about the throes of capitalism and the underbelly of social media a razor-sharp edge. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Screen Keynote: Russell T Davies
description: BAFTA-winning screenwriter and producer Russell T Davies has created several award-winning, culture-shifting series, including the groundbreaking Queer as Folk and Bob and Rose. He went on to successfully revive the classic British science fiction series Doctor Who, returning to the BBC to oversee the series in 2021, where he also worked on the spin-off series The Way Between the Land and the Sea. With his new original drama Tip Toe coming soon to Channel 4, Davies joins SXSW London to discuss his pioneering, genre-traversing career, his legacy in television with both adapted and original work, and the exciting projects he has in store.

title: Shorts: Bodies
description: Exploring the body as language that is controlled, desired, ageing, and/yet always in flux. BODIES brings together films where the body becomes a form of language, that is read, performed and never fully free. Under surveillance, in space(s) of leisure and/or loneliness, and within communities shaped by time, bodies negotiate how they are seen and what they can express. As desire flickers through constraint, ageing becomes visibility rather than disappearance, and intimacy is both sought and withheld. Across these worlds, from the controlled to the playful, the body resists stillness, constantly shifting between exposure and protection, performance and truth. Total Running Time : 92 mins Content Warnings: Flashing images, sudden loud noises, strong language, cartoon sex, nudity, discussions of domestic abuse and suicide, anatomical gore, drug and alcohol use

title: Memory
description: Director: Vladlena Sandu Cast: Vladlena Sandu A haunting blend of documentary and dream, Vladlena Sandu s film recalls growing up during wartime. Born in Crimea and raised in war-torn Chechnya, director Vladlena Sandu looks back at her past through fragments: family secrets, rumours, and the stories no one was allowed to tell. Using reconstructions and evocative, poetic imagery that recalls a childhood growing up in the Soviet Union, Memory is as much a personal reckoning as it is an ode to all children who grow up under siege, trying to find a language for the violence, destruction and war around them. For her feature-length debut, Sandu collected memories from her friends, as well as her own, to create a film that is as confronting as it is healing to watch. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Shorts: Distant Voices
description: The soft murmur of distant voices. The hauntological and oblique, our personal lost utopias, memories we just can t shake. Seven films that chart our memories, realities, and objects of nostalgia: The last remaining Mimic in England, impersonating those we have lost; Bubba, a virtual browser-dog snuffed out with new programming; a student who finds himself waking in the strangest of places; a mythical Mayan figure that brings death and despair; a shadow puppeteer haunted by memories of the Khmer Rouge; a man walking through island streets, searching for ghosts; a simple game of trading cards, taking place in the space between then and now. DISTANT VOICES investigates the hauntological, those lost things we can never get back. Total Running Time: 94 min Content Warning: Loud sounds, death, distressing scenes

title: Win or Die: Inside the New Season of House of the Dragon
description: Showrunner and Co-Creator Ryan Condal joins Production Designer Jim Clay and cast members Steve Toussaint, Harry Collett, Abubakar Salim, Jefferson Hall and Abigail Thorn for an exclusive conversation on the upcoming and much anticipated third season of the HBO Original series House of the Dragon ahead of the launch on HBO Max on June 22 in the UK and Ireland. As the Targaryen civil war deepens, the panel will explore the show s expanding scale, shifting alliances and character arcs, alongside the craft behind bringing the battle of the Gullet to life. Expect insider insight and an exclusive peek at the upcoming season. No dragons onstage, though.

title: Becoming
description: A beautiful coming of age story where the act of rebellion is a search for stability amidst family chaos. Teenage swimmer Mila takes care of the family home and her kid sister while her mother pursues affairs and opportunities. When Mila is recruited to the open water swimming team led by the stabilising and alluring Vlad, she is finally able to shine. However, in the aftermath of a teammate s accident and her mother s plans to move with a new paramour, Mila is faced with a new kind of fight: can rebelling also mean resisting adultification? (ISRA AL KASSI)

title: Honey I'm Home
description: Amsterdam-based shoegaze outfit Honey I m Home have suspected they were cursed ever since their rehearsal space burned down the day before their very first show, but recent developments suggest that the curse may finally have lifted. Early 2026 they re named as the #1 emerging act at Europe s leading showcase festival ( ESNS ): Honey I m Home is the coolest new band from the Netherlands. The packed room gets their faces melted off, but occasionally gets a breather in the form of a beautiful verse. The unexpected breaks, original melodies and smart tempo changes give the set its own, authentic sound. Drawing from the raw energy of 90s alternative rock acts like the Smashing Pumpkins My Bloody Valentine and blending it with a fresh, modern sound, they ve created their own unique take on the shoegaze, indie, and emo genres. On stage, the band s full power is unleashed: captivating live shows that balance between fragile intimacy and explosive outbursts. The band toured with Rocket , Hotline TNT Foxing in the EU UK, and played key EU showcase festivals including ESNS , Reeperbahn , SXSW in the US Left of the Dial . Recent singles got featured on Clash , Line of Best Fit BBC6 .

title: Mark Taiwo
description: Mark Taiwo is an emerging R B, Soul, and Afro-Bashment artist from Essex whose music effortlessly blends sultry melodies and emotional depth. With his silky baritone vocals, Mark captivates listeners with a wide vocal range, perfectly showcasing intricate riffs and controlled runs that bring his songs to life. Drawing inspiration from the likes of Usher, Jazmine Sullivan, Summer Walker, and Daniel Caesar, Mark s sound is rich, intimate, and full of passion. Mark s music revolves around the universal themes of love and loss, exploring the complexities of relationships and the emotions that accompany them. His ability to convey vulnerability through his lyrics and performance allows listeners to connect deeply with his songs. Whether it s the heartache of unrequited love or the bittersweet memories of a past relationship, Mark s storytelling cuts straight to the core, making every track feel personal and profound. As he continues to carve out his place in the music industry, Mark Taiwo is quickly becoming an artist to watch. With each release, his talent and unique sound only grow stronger, cementing him as a promising voice in contemporary R B.

title: VOYA
description: VOYA are a Cardiff-based duo, Linford Hydes and Eddie Al-Shakarchi, blending the emotive songwriting and rich production of early 80s British synth-pop with a dark, modern electronic edge. Their sound merges stylish new-wave energy with cinematic depth, creating performances that are both powerful and immersive. Linford , trained in musical theatre at the Royal Welsh College of Music and Drama, brings a commanding stage presence and a classically trained yet soulful vocal delivery that defines VOYA s live identity. Eddie , an award-winning producer and multi-instrumentalist (notably contributing to Boy Azooga s acclaimed 1, 2 Kung Fu! album), merges his PhD-level expertise in audio processing with creative artistry to build evocative soundscapes that perfectly frame Linford s vocals. Together, VOYA deliver a unique musical and visual experience, fusing emotion, precision, and performance flair. Their live lineup also features the operatic vocals of Jessy Allen , adding further depth and power to their shows. VOYA have self-produced their debut 12-track album, releasing their first single Too Shy for Love in December 2024 alongside a self-directed video. VOYA were hand-selected by Walt Disco to support them at Clwb Ifor Bach in Cardiff. Their premiere at The Great Escape 2023 featured two showcases, followed by debut performances at the Green Man Festival (Settlers Stage, 2023), and a headline slot at the International Queer Film Festival: IRIS Prize . They also sold out Chapter Arts Centre in Cardiff in 2023. VOYA continue to receive strong support from BBC Radio Wales , where they are a regular Welsh A-List artist, and can now be heard on Huw Stephens BBC 6 Music show . In recognition of their rising impact, VOYA received the Triskell Award at the Welsh Music Prize 2023, celebrating them as one of the best emerging acts of 2024 . Most recently, they showcased internationally at Pro Weekend Festival in Castell n, Spain (October 2025), with support from FOCUS Wales .

title: Rivals: Mid Season Finale
description: Watch the series finale with a special cast and crew panel Set in the cutthroat world of the Cotswolds, ex-Olympian Rupert Campbell-Black (Alex Hassell) is battling media mogul Lord Tony Baddingham (David Tennant). The rest is very much not safe for work. Based on the late great Dame Jilly Cooper s book series, this special screening of Rivals second series finale will be followed by a cast and crew panel to discuss, in deliciously spoilerific detail, all the entanglements, backstabbings and shoulder padded outfits of this latest season. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: V.I.C
description: V.I.C is a Nigerian-born, UK-raised artist carving out a bold lane in alternative rap with sharp lyricism and genre-bending sound. He first broke through globally with A Teen on the FIFA 22 soundtrack and has continued building momentum with standout releases like Wasteman and Diamonds. His growing impact has crossed into film, with a recent placement on the soundtrack for GOAT, expanding his reach beyond music. With his debut album Industry Games set for release this year, V.I.C is entering a defining new chapter of his career. Now, he brings that energy to the stage as he announces his first tour, delivering a high-impact live show that s rapidly turning heads across the UK.

title: The Cure Meets The Man Who Laughs
description: Director: Josh Frank This is a treat for music and film fans alike: SXSW London is delighted to present the World Premiere of The Cure Meets The Man Who Laughs in collaboration with Silents Synced. The film is a re-scored edition of the 1928 silent classic by the great German Expressionist filmmaker Paul Leni, following an 18th-century disfigured clown who becomes the star of a travelling carnival, scored with music from legendary band The Cure. Developed by Austin-based indie cinema owner Josh Frank, the format brings a fresh alternative event experience to theatres worldwide. The first series played in the US and Canada, and featured Radiohead, R.E.M., and Pearl Jam. The Man Who Laughs Meets the Cure marks a global expansion in collaboration with Universal Music Group.

title: Cactus for Breakfast
description: Cactus For Breakfast is a Berlin-based surf-inspired psychedelic garage rock band formed in 2023. Bringing together members from Germany, the US, and Indonesia, the band channels an eclectic, intercultural energy into a sound that blends sun-soaked riffs, raw textures, and a playful, unmistakable cactus vibe. Known for their lighthearted approach and dynamic live shows, they actively involve the audience, turning performances into shared experiences where crowds rarely leave without singing along. Their music balances gritty textures with catchy melodies, never taking itself too seriously while maintaining a strong musical identity. Following the release of their first two EPs, Horsemilk Hangover (2024) and Sugarcoat Bad Memories (2025), the band has built momentum across Germany and the UK, performing at festivals and club shows alike. With a growing presence on the European live circuit, Cactus For Breakfast continue to expand their sound and audience, with new material lined up and their debut album scheduled for release in 2027.

title: Half Happy
description: Shooting up through Cardiff s flourishing music scene is Half Happy. The four-piece bounce around the walls of dream-pop, indie-rock and post punk but don t stay in one place for too long. These 90 s babies mould their influences with fresh force, creating engaging melody-stuffed storytelling. Honest observations melt into day-dream lyrics that stick close to tight indie drums. The bass lines strut while the guitar riffs walk the line of heavy and heavenly. Over 10 years of best-friendship between the band is palpable from their live performance. It s a running joke that isn t getting old yet, the band is way more than Half Happy.

title: 00ab
description: 00ab is an Upper Clapton based artist of Yoruba descent crafting vibrant, genre-fluid music that moves between Afrohouse, Amapiano, alternative rap and jazz-inflected sounds. Rooted in a rich and varied life experience, his work blends introspection, playful lyricism, and club-ready energy through a self-defined approach that is both playful and thoughtful. Emerging in 2020, 00ab has developed a distinctive sound shaped by experimentation and global influence, evolving from lo-fi and boom bap foundations into a boundaryless sonic world. Named by Clash Magazine as one of the artists set to define 2024, and highlighted by New Wave Magazine for his ability to glide through the barriers of genre, his work continues to resist categorisation, with tracks like Menace showcasing his balance of vulnerability and self-aware lyricism. His 9-track EP ab, we wanna party! (2025) captures the high-energy, nightlife-driven spirit of his sound, while his 2026 releases without you (2 track), matcha bros (with Taifunds), and fool me twice (2 track) continue to expand his range across mood and form. Positioned by Deeds Magazine as part of a new generation shaping alternative African-influenced music Beyond music, his creative journey spans fashion and performance, including walking for Balenciaga. A natural performer, he creates immersive, high-energy live experiences centred on movement, connection, and shared emotion driven by one core intention: to make you feel something.

title: PRETTY DIRTY The Life and Times of Marilyn Minter
description: Director: Jennifer Ash Rudick, Amanda M Benchley Cast: Marilyn Minter, Jane Fonda, Jeff Koons, Lizzo, and Padma Lakshmi A provocative and visually arresting portrait of the artist Marilyn Minter. Google Marilyn Minter, and what comes up are images of women s body parts. Mouths, eyes, feet, and tongues, covered in glitter, pearls, and dirt. Encouraged by Diane Arbus in her early years, Minter has been a working artist since the 1980s, attracting controversy for her work exploring the aesthetics of beauty, desire, and the female body. Her work has inspired the high femme aesthetic of pop culture, and she has created portraits of Jane Fonda, Pamela Anderson, Lizzo, and more. Drawing on archival material, studio footage, and (very) candid interviews, Pretty/Dirty strips away the glitter to reveal a portrait of an artist who, nearing 80, has lived a life refusing to compromise. The question remains: is the art world ready to embrace her? (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: It Would Be Night in Caracas
description: Directors: Mariana Rond n, Marit Ug s Cast: Natalia Reyes, dgar Ram rez, Mois s Angola, Sheila Monterola A woman must reconstruct her identity and sense of self in this gripping thriller set at the height of Venezuela s political crisis Set against the chaos of Venezuela s political crisis in 2017, this tense, propulsive thriller executive produced by dgar Ram rez follows Adelaida (Natalia Reyes) as she navigates a Caracas where survival demands impossible choices. Returning from her mother s funeral, she finds her home is seized by a violent female militia. Ousted from her own house and surrounded by a city of escalating tensions and collapsing institutions, Adelaida discovers that her neighbour has just passed away and left her Spanish passport behind. Set during the few tension-filled days that follow, It Would Be Night in Caracas deftly navigates the morality of survival, as Adelaida navigates a corrupt system, a lawless environment and her own morals to ensure her survival. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Marc By Sofia
description: Director: Sofia Coppola Cast: Marc Jacobs Acclaimed director Sofia Coppola s first documentary captures her long-time friend and collaborator, designer Marc Jacobs In her first foray into documentary-making, Oscar-winning auteur Sofia Coppola turns to her long-time friend and iconic fashion designer Marc Jacobs. Their collaborations have spanned decades, with Coppola starring in his campaigns, collaborating with him during his time at Louis Vuitton, and directing his perfume ads after he launched his own label. Taking a fly-on-the-wall approach, Coppola gives us an intimate glimpse into the everyday process and creative drive of a singular voice in the fashion world. The doc takes us back to the heyday of the nineties, when his star was on the ascendant as fashion s most trend-averse designer, working his way from Perry Ellis to Louis Vuitton, up until the time he eventually launched his own label. Watching Marc by Sofia is an invitation to join the most glamorous of hangs. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Test
description: Director: Sam McConnell Cast: Brock Yurich, Matthew Morrison, Tammy Blanchard, Mike Edward, Paloma Garcia-Lee An amateur bodybuilder wrestles with his queerness while pursuing his dreams of becoming a champion Eddie (Brock Yurich) dreams of becoming a bodybuilding champion and escaping rural Ohio. His gruelling training routine makes it easier to hide unspoken desires from a doting mother and girlfriend, but the pressures of an upcoming championship and the arrival of a charming coach bring his pretense to a startling halt. In a world where conversations around societal expectations on the body both when it comes to performance and aesthetics are everywhere, Sam McConnell offers a sharp insight into the dichotomy between a polished exterior and crumbling interior, all while sensitively observing the joys and challenges of realising one s queerness in an environment hostile to difference. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: VNSA
description: VNSA pronounced ve-ne-ssa or v-n-s-a, she is the $XY BLK SUPERSTAR. Be it while she s creating in her tiny 4 walled bedroom, in a fully equipped recording studio or even live on stage in front of 10 to how ever many people, this 22 year old singer/songwriter and performer, will stop at nothing to achieve her seat at the table of Global Superstars, in spite of what she may or may not have.

title: Leviticus
description: Director: Adrian Chiarella Cast: Joe Bird, Stacy Clausen, Mia Wasikowska A queer horror story about two teenage boys confronted with a supernatural entity who takes the form of the one they desire the most. The budding romance between teens Naim (Joe Bird) and Ryan (Stacy Clausen) barely has time to flourish before they are discovered and subjected to a religious conversion ritual by their local pastor. The cure they are subjected to unleashes a terrifying ordeal. In a cruel twist of fate, the vile, shapeshifting entity takes the form of the person they desire the most: each other. Drawing on the intimacy of modern horror as It Follows, director Adrian Chiarella announces himself as a bold new voice in genre filmmaking, delivering a visceral, emotionally charged debut that s as devastating as it is terrifying. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Master Peace
description: Rising new-wave artist, Master Peace, known for his rap-like stage energy and genre-blurring sound, has been rapidly drawing support from tastemakers like Dazed, NME, BBC Radio 1, and JME. 2024 was a breakout year for Master Peace, marked by the release of his critically acclaimed debut album, How To Make A Master Peace , which has been widely regarded as a game-changer in the UK music landscape. Building on this momentum, his journey to date has been nothing short of remarkable. Peace won the Rising Star Award at the Ivor Novello Awards, an incredible recognition of his talent and impact, was nominated for Rolling Stone Magazine s The Play Next Award and The MTV Push UK IRE 2025 Award, and recently had the honor of playing with The Streets on The Kasabian Arena Tour and touring his debut album across the UK. Master Peace s distinctive blend of indie and rap continues to push boundaries, positioning him as one of the most exciting new voices in UK music.

title: Mellt
description: Mellt are a four-piece band originally from Aberystwyth, but now based in Cardiff. Their debut album Mae n Hawdd Pan Ti n Ifanc (It s Easy When You re Young) won the coveted Welsh Language Album of the Year in 2018, and was nominated for the Welsh Music Prize the same year. Their second album Dim Dwywaith was released in October 2023 on Clwb Music and received incredible reviews from Clash, NME and an 8/10 review from Uncut Magazine. It also reached the short list for the Welsh Music Prize and Welsh Language Album of the Year. With numerous live shows under their belt, this energetic four-piece manages to captivate an audience wherever they go, firmly making their mark in Wales vast music landscape. With chant-like vocals, shrieking guitar and punching drumbeats, Mellt are crowd anarchists. Citing influences from The Clash, The Band and The Replacements, with their catchy guitar riffs and perfect blend of slacker rock and post punk sounds. Mellt s clever and intricate lyrics take them to the next level. Mae Mellt yn bedwarawd o Aberystwyth yn wreiddiol, sydd bellach wedi u lleoli yng Nghaerdydd. Enillodd eu halbwm cyntaf Mae n Hawdd Pan Ti n Ifanc , Albwm Cymraeg y Flwyddyn yn 2018, a chyrhaeddodd yr albwm rhestr fel y Wobr Gerddoriaeth Gymreig yr un flwyddyn. Fe gafodd ei ail albwm Dim Dwywaith ei rhyddhau yn mis Hydref 2023 ar label Clwb Music gyda sawl adolygiad anhygoel gan Clash, NME ac adolygiad 8/10 gan gylchgrawn Uncut. Fe wnaeth yr albwm gyrraedd rhestr fer y Wobr Gerddoriaeth Gymraeg ac Albwm Gymraeg y Flwyddyn hefyd. Ar l chwarae nifer o sioeau byw, mae r pedwarawd deinameg yn llwyddo i hoelio sylw cynulleidfaoedd gan wneud eu marc yn glir ar dirlun eang cerddoriaeth Cymru. Gyda llais sydd bron yn llafarganu, git r sgrechlyd a drymiau beiddgar, mae Mellt yn anarchwyr y dorf, gan achosi twrw lle bynnag maen nhw. Mae Mellt wedi cael eu dylanwadu gan The Clash, The Band a The Replacements gyda u riffiau git r bachog, synau slacyr roc a post pync, mae geiriau Mellt yn goron ar y cyfan.

title: Orla Rae
description: Orla Rae is a Brighton-born, 24 year-old singer and songwriter carving out a distinct lane in UK R B and soul. Blending elements of R B, soul, pop and jazz, her sound is defined by rich harmonies, powerful vocals, and emotionally precise songwriting. Drawing inspiration from artists such as Stevie Wonder, Victoria Mon t, Brandy and D Angelo, Orla Rae delivers a style that feels both timeless and forward-thinking. She has been championed by BBC Radio 1Xtra, earning two Track of the Week placements, as well as support from BBC Introducing, Reprezent Radio, KISS Xtra and Rinse FM. Her debut EP has surpassed 300,000 streams, earning strong editorial playlist backing from Spotify, Apple music and more. Recently featured on BBC Radio 1Xtra s On Air Demo on CassKidd s show, she received high praise from CassKidd, DJ Target and the wider 1Xtra team, and is set to record a live session at Maida Vale later this year. Currently part of the ILUVLIVE x Stellar Songs artist development programme, Orla Rae has also toured Europe with Blue Lab Beats and supported the likes of Lynda Dawn and Sophie Faith. With a growing reputation for her captivating live performances, she continues to build momentum ahead of her second EP campaign, set for spring/summer 2026. With a standout live presence and a rapidly rising profile, Orla Rae is a compelling new voice in UK R B and is undeniably one to watch in 2026.

title: The Red Hangar
description: Director: Juan Pablo Sallato Cast: Aron Hern ndez, Boris Quercia, Catalina Stuardo An Air Force Captain must choose between duty and morals in this tight Chilean thriller set during the military coup This nail-bitingly tense Chilean thriller takes place on September 11th, 1973, as Captain Jorge Silva (Aron Hern ndez) receives a life-changing order. He is to transform his training academy into a prison camp and torture centre ahead of the rapidly unfolding military coup d etat. As the clock ticks louder and louder, the captain must choose between risking his life through disobedience or his soul by compliance. The result is a strikingly shot and paced character study as well as a rare insight into the sombre machinery of military political corruption in South America. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Christopher Ellis
description: The Singing Wizard , international Reggae artist Christopher Ellis is a talented accomplished singer/songwriter born in London. The youngest son of The Godfather of Reggae , the late Alton Ellis, Christopher s musical foundation is rooted in the early sounds of Jamaica, with his father leading as one of the pioneers of an original sound the Rock Steady era. Growing up in a musical family Christopher often spent time around reggae legends such as John Holt, Ken Boothe and Delroy Wilson, absorbing the craft of musical artistry and stage performance. It would come as no surprise that he would soon begin astounding audiences with his incredibly natural vocal ability from the early age of 11. Touring internationally with his father, Christopher quickly developed a unique sound and a profound level of professionalism that would continue to support him in his career in the future. Signing to The Marley s Ghetto Youth International label in 2009, founded by Stephen Damien Marley, Christopher later released his first single End of Time which became the catalyst for his evolving catalogue with his Better than Love EP (2013) Here We Are (2017) and monumental love ballad Flame against the Wind (2020). With the success of his earlier projects, Christopher propelled his career to unthinkable heights when he mastered his sound with singles Still Go A Dance and Rub A Dub . Both songs culturally shook the genre, with its impact dominating the sound system world! Christopher s flourishing career has led him to perform internationally at some of the most distinguished stage shows culturally from Reggae Jam to Rebel Salute and having toured with the likes of The Marley s, Snoop Dogg and Freddie McGregor to name a few, the notable singer has hit stages in countries such as Canada, Italy and New Zealand. With sell out shows in London s renowned Jazz Caf to successfully touring across Japan, consistent features on the airwaves in the UK and abroad, Christopher has a worldwide fanbase which continues to expand.

title: Alana Bubba
description: Introducing Alana Bubba. A North London singer-rapper-songwriter, with a sensual fusion of neo soul, hip hop and jazz. Her calming sounds take us on extensive journeys exploring topics of self love and real life experiences, as she translates her frustrations into bars.

title: Hana Lili
description: Hana Lili is on a mission to pursue her truth. For the Welsh artist, songwriting has always been away to make sense of things words can t quite describe. Her self-titled EP, Hana, marks her boldest step yet, an intimate collection that distils emotion into its rawest form. Blending confessional storytelling with the dynamic spirit of 90s and early 00s alt-rock, Hana crafts songs that balance vulnerability and power. Drawing inspiration from artists like Alanis Morissette, Radiohead and Gwen Stefani, she channels emotional honesty through soaring melodies and unfiltered energy. Each song feels like a conversation with herself. Written and co-produced alongside Mark Vallance (Tom Walker), Ben Beetham (Kid Kapichi, Hot Wax), Mike Horner (Karen Dio) and Chris Ostler (Black Honey), Hana s music merges introspection with rebellion, turning personal chaos into connection. Raised in Wales and a first-language Welsh speaker, Hana s sense of place runs deep. Her roots in the Welsh Eisteddfod tradition, a celebration of language, music, and storytelling instilled in her a lasting belief in the power of creative expression. With a sound that bridges intimacy and universality, Hana Lili stands as one of alt-pop s most distinct and fearless emerging voices.

title: Jedsoul
description: Hailing from West London, JEDSOUL is a multifaceted talent with accolades as an R B singer-songwriter and producer. Influenced by the likes of Bryson Tiller, PARTYNEXTDOOR Justin Bieber, he meticulously blends atmospheric production with sharp melodies and raw lyricism to create a sound that is both vulnerable and confident. His recent run of activity has sparked serious momentum earning co-signs from key figures in the scene, including kwn, Remi Burgz, and Knucks. In addition to writing and producing his own material, JEDSOUL has built a strong production and songwriting portfolio, with credits including JayO, Ceebo, Gwamz, and Tay Iwar.

title: Jemere Morgan
description: Jemere Morgan, heir to the renowned musical legacy of the Royal Family of Reggae MORGAN HERITAGE, emerges as a vibrant and dynamic force in the music industry. The son of Gramps Morgan, Jemere has traveled the globe, captivating audiences with his soulful performances and infectious energy. With experience gained from touring alongside his family and under his father s guidance, Jemere has honed his craft and embraced the responsibility of spreading messages of love and unity through his music.His musical journey began with the release of his debut record, First Kiss, in2011, followed by Sunshine Glow and the chart-topping single Neighborhood Girl. Jemere s talent shone brightly as he collaborated with industry stars like the late Jo Mersa Marley, J Boog, Stonebwoy and Kabaka Pyramid, earning recognition and a dedicated fan base worldwide. A standout moment in his career came with his feature on Morgan Heritage s Grammy-winning album, Strictly Roots, and his collaboration with Kabaka Pyramid on the Grammy Award-winning The Kalling. Now a Grammy Award-winning artist himself, Jemere continues to impress with his latest single, Higher We Go, produced by Damian Jr Gong Marley, showcasing his exceptional talent and unwavering commitment to his craft.With his infectious positivity and undeniable talent, Jemere Morgan embodies the essence of reggae music-a beacon of hope and inspiration for all. His journey is a testament to his unwavering dedication to making a difference through music and spreadinglove to all corners of the world.

title: shame - Friday
description: If you are a Platinum Pass Holder eligible for advance booking, then you can reserve your spot to see Shame by RSVPing to the Huck Magazine showcase on the official SXSW London app. Please note that reservation spaces are limited and that entry is only guaranteed if you arrive before the showcase starts at 7:00pm. Admission for late arrivals will be subject to venue capacity. Shame have announced their return with new album Cutthroat, out 5th September 2025 via Dead Oceans, and have shared the thrilling new video for the album s title track, directed by Ja Humby, featuring the band performing within a motorcycle wall of death. Watch the Cutthroat video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iEZfLcdJemo Cutthroat is shame at their blistering best; an unapologetic new album made with Grammy winning producer John Congleton at the helm. It s about the cowards, the cunts, the hypocrites, says vocalist Charlie Steen. Let s face it, there s a lot of them around right now. Still in their twenties and having proved themselves several times over via legendary live shows and three critically-acclaimed albums, the five childhood friends - Charlie Steen, guitarists Sean Coyle-Smith and Eddie Green, bassist Josh Finerty and drummer Charlie Forbes - went into Cutthroat ready to create a new Ground Zero. This is about who we are, says Steen. Our live shows aren t performance art - they re direct, confrontational and raw. That s always been the root of us. We live in crazy times. But it s not about Poor me. It s about Fuck you . Crucial to this incendiary new outlook was producer John Congleton (St. Vincent, Angel Olsen). From their initial meeting, Congleton s no-bullshit approach became a guiding force to streamline the band s ideas. Stamped throughout with Shame s trademark sense of humour, the album takes on the big issues of today and gleefully toys with them. With Trump in the Whitehouse and Shame holed up in Salvation Studios in Brighton, they cast a merciless eye on themes of conflict and corruption; hunger and desire; lust, envy and the omnipresent shadow of cowardice. Musically, too, the record plays with visceral new ideas. Making electronic music on tour for fun, Coyle-Smith had previously seen the loops he was crafting as a separate entity to the things he wrote for Shame. Then, he realised, maybe they didn t have to be. This time, anything could go if it sounded good and you got it right, he says. Cutthroat s first single and title track takes this idea and runs with it into, quite possibly, the best song Shame have ever laid to tape. It s a ball of barely-contained attitude packed into three minutes of indie dancefloor hedonism. It also masterfully introduces the lyrical outlook of the record: one where cocksure arrogance and deep insecurity are two sides of the same coin. I was reading a lot of Oscar Wilde plays where everything was about paradox, Steen explains. In Cutthroat , it s that whole idea from Lady Windermere s Fan, Life s far too important to be taken seriously . This cheeky self-awareness, too, is important. As much as Shame want to burst the bubbles of bluster and ego, encouraging us to look in the mirror and ask ourselves, He who casts the first stone , they also understand that, at its heart, life is often ridiculous. The result is an album that revels in the idiosyncrasies of life, raising an eyebrow and asking the ugly questions that so often get tactfully brushed over. But the one answer that Cutthroat gives with a resounding flourish is that, right now, Shame have never sounded better.

title: XR Programme
description: Step beyond the Story. Our XR programme surrounds you with bold, immersive experiences across video installations, virtual reality, and mixed reality, where stories move with you. Feel your way through dreamlike landscapes, challenge yourself with new perspectives, and dive (literally) into playful, thought-provoking work from creators who push storytelling into new dimensions, inviting you to inhabit new worlds. Step beyond the Story. Our XR programme surrounds you with bold, immersive experiences across video installations, virtual reality, and mixed reality, where stories move with you. Feel your way through dreamlike landscapes, challenge yourself with new perspectives, and dive (literally) into playful, thought-provoking work from creators who push storytelling into new dimensions, inviting you to inhabit new worlds.

title: Memory
description: Director: Vladlena Sandu Cast: Vladlena Sandu A haunting blend of documentary and dream, Vladlena Sandu s film recalls growing up during wartime. Born in Crimea and raised in war-torn Chechnya, director Vladlena Sandu looks back at her past through fragments: family secrets, rumours, and the stories no one was allowed to tell. Using reconstructions and evocative, poetic imagery that recalls a childhood growing up in the Soviet Union, Memory is as much a personal reckoning as it is an ode to all children who grow up under siege, trying to find a language for the violence, destruction and war around them. For her feature-length debut, Sandu collected memories from her friends, as well as her own, to create a film that is as confronting as it is healing to watch. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: The Boy With the Light-Blue Eyes
description: Director: Thanasis Neofotistos Cast: Giorgos Karydis, Pablo Soto, Syrmo Keke, Sofia Filippidou A cursed blue-eyed boy seeks love in a remote mountain ruled by superstition Petros (Giorgos Karydis) spent his whole life trying to understand why his strict grandmother and overly protective mother made him wear a cumbersome face mask to hide his eyes from the rest of the world. When an elderly man dies in their remote mountain village soon after locking eyes with the teen, Petros finally understands why: his light-blue eyes carry a curse. Shunned by the community and afraid for his life, Petros escapes with the help of his mother in Thanasis Neofotistos s poetic, beautifully shot queer allegory on the dangers of ostracism. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Whistle
description: Director: Christopher Nelius N ot everyone in the world can whistle. Not many people in the world can whistle, even fewer can whistle Mozart s Queen of the Night aria. The top whistlers in the world travel from all over the world to compete in the prestigious Masters of Musical Whistling competition in L.A. At the heart of this riot of a documentary is organiser extraordinaire and competition founder Carole Anne Kaufman. A passionate whistler, she runs a tight but gentle ship and offers a heartfelt behind the scenes look into neverending drama of event production. Part musical, part comedy, and with the stakes of a sports movie, Whistle sets the scene for a showdown: will nerves take over? Or is it as simple as put your lips together and blow ? (ISRA AL KASSI)

title: Bayaan
description: Director: Bikas Ranjan Mishra Cast: Huma Qureshi A detective story set in the remote town of Rajasthan, where a revered leader is accused of sexual assault by an anonymous letter. Against her superiors orders, young officer Roohi (Huma Qureshi) takes it upon herself to get to the bottom of a contentious sexual assault scandal involving some of the most powerful men in her community, unknowingly stumbling into a large web of corruption, mass complacency, and institutional enabling. Firmly rooted in the police procedural, Bayaan recalls the tenacity of Det. Oliva Benson from Law Order: SVU, all while carving out a distinctively contemporary and regionally specific take on the familiar genre. Bayaan is a strong vehicle for Qureshi, who delivers a commanding performance as a woman fighting against both a system designed to protect abusers and the people who help keep it in place. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: James: Getting Away With It
description: Director: Chris Atkins Cast: Jim Glennie, Tim Booth, David Baynton-Power, Saul Davies, Mark Hunter The first-ever authorised tell-all documentary about iconic British band James If you ever caught yourself screaming, Oh, sit down! Sit down! Sit down next to me! amongst friends and strangers at a rowdy pub as a barman calls final rounds, you know you have had a proper good British night out. This feeling is courtesy of iconic Manchester band James, whose hits have accompanied generations of Brits for almost half a century. In their first-ever authorised documentary, the band chronicles their rise and fall, and rise again, as well as their unique phases, including a brief stint in a cult in the 80s, taking direct advice from psychics, and participating in medical experiments to pay the bills. A treat for fans old and new. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: The Other Side of the Sun
description: Director: Tawfik Sabouni Cast: Abdelkafi Alhaj, Mahmoud Alqadah, Mohammad Hamki Five men process the trauma of captivity by reconstructing their time in a Syrian prison Tawfik Sabouni s remarkable film takes its name from the infamous Syrian prison of Saidnaya, where he was kept captive during the Assad regime. Following the fall of the authoritarian ruler, the filmmaker returned to the crumbling grounds of Saidnaya alongside four other former prisoners to cathartically dissect the horror of their captivity through poetic, detailed reconstructions. In the reopening of wounds, the men powerfully reclaim their voices. As cinema tries to grapple with the horrifying effects of war and conflict as it unfolds in real time, The Other Side of the Sun proves a singular insight into the intricacies of trauma and the restorative power of art. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Father
description: Director: Tereza Nvotov Cast: Milan Ondr k, Dominika Mor vkov , Dominika Zajcz, Martina Sľ kov How can society punish a man who s lost everything? It s a big day for Michal (Milan Ondr k) as the new CEO of his company is coming to visit. It also happens to be one of the hottest days of the year. Michal heads to the office after dropping off his daughter at daycare. All of the above are in fact the gaps in memory and what unravels throughout Father is the dizzying depiction of one man s life-changing fatal mistake. As Slovakia s official Oscar submission of 2026, director Tereza Nvotov builds a world of overwhelming chaos with an idyllic family at the centre of it. Inspired by real life tragic events, Milan Ondr k delivers a career best performance as a man left with both impossible grief and an incomprehensible guilt. (ISRA AL KASSI)

title: Feast or Famine
description: Directors: Adrian Choa, Michael Boccalini Cast: Marco Pierre White, Joshua Owen, Usman Haider, Amar Takhar East London s buzzy Angelina restaurant chases their first Michelin star in this doc narrated by legendary chef Marco Pierre White There is a certain air of mystery around the famed Michelin stars sparsely given to the world s best restaurants and chefs. Few people can explain the system as well as legendary chef Marco Pierre White, the first Brit to earn three of them and the first to famously give them all back. The fascinating Feast or Famine lifts the heavy curtain of secrecy that surrounds high-end dining, with White demystifying the Michelin myth while the highly-regarded team behind East London s buzzy Angelina restaurant chases their first ever coveted star. Watch it, and make your reservation. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: AMERICAN ZOO
description: Director: Tim Travers Hawkins Truth is way, way stranger than fiction in this twisty documentary about a lost zoo and an American family playing God with nature. Emmy-nominated documentarian Tim Travers Hawkins was hiking when he stumbled upon the ruins of a zoo in the middle of the woods. A set of lost home movies became the starting point of American Zoo, a documentary that blasts open the doors of the old zoo to uncover three generations of family secrets that will take you from the sheltered forests of rural U.S. to the height of German fascism, investigating the viciousness of humans who experimented with genetic modifications in animals, believing to be able to bend the living to their will. A feat of precision, Hawkins s latest is best enjoyed without much prior knowledge of this twisty, almost unbelievable story. But trust us, you are in for one hell of a ride. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Becoming
description: A beautiful coming of age story where the act of rebellion is a search for stability amidst family chaos. Teenage swimmer Mila takes care of the family home and her kid sister while her mother pursues affairs and opportunities. When Mila is recruited to the open water swimming team led by the stabilising and alluring Vlad, she is finally able to shine. However, in the aftermath of a teammate s accident and her mother s plans to move with a new paramour, Mila is faced with a new kind of fight: can rebelling also mean resisting adultification? (ISRA AL KASSI)

title: Shorts: Competition Programme 2
description: Watch the best of the best. A curated showcase of this year s Best Short Film nominees at SXSW London, spotlighting emerging voices from around the world. Experience a dynamic range of perspectives, styles, and genres. Each film is a contender and a glimpse into the future of filmmaking.

title: Hijra
description: Director: Shahad Ameen Cast: Khairiah Nathmy, Lamar Faden, Raghad Bokhari, Nawaf Al-Dhufairy A young girl and her grandmother have their pilgrimage to Mecca interrupted by a runaway in this multigenerational Saudi road movie A pilgrimage to Mecca is derailed when a devout grandmother (Khairiah Nathmy) loses one of her granddaughters and decides to embark on a road trip with remaining grandchild Janna (Lamar Faden) to find her before anyone else does. With the help of opportunistic driver Ahmed (Nawaf Al-Dhufairy), they make their way cross-country, letting the contradictions and unspoken tensions between them unfold. Director Shahad Ameen uses the landscape to draw parallels between the physical and emotional journeys of her characters (and, in a broader sense, the multiplicity of women in Saudi Arabia) so powerfully, you ll feel the wind on your skin long after leaving the cinema. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Remake
description: Director: Ross McElwee Cast: Adrian McElwee, Charleen Swansea Renowned documentarian Ross McElwee poignantly grieves the death of his son by examining a life captured on screen. For decades, veteran American documentarian Ross McElwee captured his son s growing up on screen, from his wife s pregnancy in 1993 s Time Indefinite to the delicate nature of their maturing relationship in 2011 s Photographic Memory. In Remake, the director processes Adrian s devastating death by looking back at extensive archival footage shot by two generations of McElwee men, presented alongside a stalled attempt to fictionalize his 1986 classic doc, Sherman s March. The result is both a touching elegy and a piercing dissection of memory, image, and the blurry boundaries of personal documentary filmmaking. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Winter of the Crow
description: Director: Kasia Adamik Cast: Lesley Manville, Tom Burke, Zofia Wichlacz Lesley Manville leads a tense Cold War thriller based on a story by Nobel Prize-winning author Olga Tokarzuk. Tightly wound British academic Joan, played by the always excellent Lesley Manville, travels to Cold War-era Warsaw to deliver a paper on the treatment of psychiatric illness. Once in Poland, the professor is surprisingly caught in between a student uprising and escalating assassinations as authorities call in martial law. Manville is phenomenal as a spiky, on-edge professor who s pulled into something much bigger than herself and, as Joan s stern armour unravels, she (and we) are coolly reminded of the narrowness of her experience after being dropped into a brutal world where theory isn t going to cut it. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Made in EU
description: Director: Stephan Komandarev Cast: Gergana Pletnyova, Todor Kotsev, Gerasim Georgiev A tense social drama exposing the inequality of labour in Bulgaria through a worker s bout with COVID Single mother Iva (Gergana Pletnyova) feels unwell, but can t get sick leave from her precarious clothing factory job. When she is eventually declared the first COVID case in her small Bulgarian town, a dangerous game of accusation unravels, with the labourer shunned not only from her community but also from her home. Pletnyova gives a stunning lead performance in Stephan Komandarev s piercing social examination, a drama with the fast pulse of a thriller that closely observes a crumbling European dream from the trampoline of the pandemic. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: The Red Hangar
description: Director: Juan Pablo Sallato Cast: Aron Hern ndez, Boris Quercia, Catalina Stuardo An Air Force Captain must choose between duty and morals in this tight Chilean thriller set during the military coup This nail-bitingly tense Chilean thriller takes place on September 11th, 1973, as Captain Jorge Silva (Aron Hern ndez) receives a life-changing order. He is to transform his training academy into a prison camp and torture centre ahead of the rapidly unfolding military coup d etat. As the clock ticks louder and louder, the captain must choose between risking his life through disobedience or his soul by compliance. The result is a strikingly shot and paced character study as well as a rare insight into the sombre machinery of military political corruption in South America. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Irkalla - Gilgamesh's Dream
description: Director: Mohamed Jabarah Al-Daradji Cast: Youssef Husham Al-Thahabi, Hussein Raad Zuwayr, Samar Kazem Jawad, Ameer Jabarah A heartbreaking tale with glimpses of hope for the Baghdad children left behind after the war. In the labyrinthine streets of Baghdad, orphaned and homeless children roam aimlessly with nothing to rely on but each other. In a world of scarcity, some children like Moody (Hussein Raad Zuwayr) are lured by the empty promises of the armed militia, while others like nine-year-old dreamer Chum-Chum (Youssef Husham Al-Thahabi) find a rare sliver of hope in dreaming. Drawn to the Tigris River and the ancestral myth of Gilgamesh, Chum-Chum sets out on a journey to bring his parents back to life in this bittersweet epic from Mohamed Jabarah Al-Daradji, a heartbreaking but hopeful tale on the power of myth and memory. (ISRA AL KASSI)

title: It Would Be Night in Caracas
description: Directors: Mariana Rond n, Marit Ug s Cast: Natalia Reyes, dgar Ram rez, Mois s Angola, Sheila Monterola A woman must reconstruct her identity and sense of self in this gripping thriller set at the height of Venezuela s political crisis Set against the chaos of Venezuela s political crisis in 2017, this tense, propulsive thriller executive produced by dgar Ram rez follows Adelaida (Natalia Reyes) as she navigates a Caracas where survival demands impossible choices. Returning from her mother s funeral, she finds her home is seized by a violent female militia. Ousted from her own house and surrounded by a city of escalating tensions and collapsing institutions, Adelaida discovers that her neighbour has just passed away and left her Spanish passport behind. Set during the few tension-filled days that follow, It Would Be Night in Caracas deftly navigates the morality of survival, as Adelaida navigates a corrupt system, a lawless environment and her own morals to ensure her survival. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Roya
description: Director: Mahnaz Mohammadi Cast: Melisa S zen, Maryam Palizban, Hamidreza Djavdan, Mohammad Ali Hosseinalipour, Bacho Meburishvili An imprisoned teacher in Iran struggles with the weight of a forced confession. The film begins in darkness, as Iranian teacher Roya (Melisa S zen) wakes up locked in a three-by-three room. Formally daring, Mohammadi used sound and first-person point-of-view to fully immerse us in the experience of her tormented protagonist: darkness, snatches of conversation, dreams and confusion all blend. Unsure of her crime and with her family cruelly being used as blackmail, Roya is forced to choose between doing a sham confession or returning to prison. Directed by documentarian and activist Mahnaz Mohammadi, Roya is not an easy watch but it s a necessary one. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Teenage Sex and Death at Camp Miasma
description: Director: Jane Schoenbrun Cast: Gillian Anderson, Hannah Einbinder Hannah Einbinder and Gillian Anderson star in a psychosexual horror two-hander directed by Jane Schoenbrun As a writer-director, Jane Schoenbrun has already built a distinctive body of work, with their horror films We re All Going to the World s Fair and I Saw the TV Glow exploring online life, identity, memory and nostalgia. Their latest film is a two-hander between Hannah Einbinder (Hacks) and Gillian Anderson (The X-Files, Sex Education, Scoop), with the former playing an indie filmmaker set to reboot the cult slasher flick Camp Miasma with the film s original final girl, played by Anderson. Both actresses completely game to inhabit the distinctive horror-infused worlds that Schoenbrun is building. Teenage Sex and Death at Camp Miasma is an intellectual sleazefest, an ode to slashers and a takedown of modern industry politics. But at its core is the brewing connection between two women, bound by movies and desire. Every scene between Einbinder and Anderson is so charged, you ll be afraid the screen might catch fire. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: The Night
description: Director: Paul Urkijo Alijo Cast: Yune Nogueiras, Elena Irureta, Ane Gabarain, I ake Irastorza The stories of women and witches intersect in this gorgeous folk horror set in the 17th Basque Country. In 17th Spain, everyone expected the Inquisition. Set in the harsh world of the Catholic witch-hunts, the young bride Kattalin (Yune Nogueiras) escapes from her violent new husband with a little help from a poisonous mushroom. Shrouded by the night and the woods, she stumbles upon three old women, gossiping and sharing horror stories. Unfolding in a series of horror vignettes, Gaua ( the night in the Basque language), sees writer-director Paul Urkijo Alijo weave a tale of corruption, sexual liberation, and classic storytelling into a folk feminist parable that culminates in a feverish finale. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Teenage Sex and Death at Camp Miasma
description: Director: Jane Schoenbrun Cast: Gillian Anderson, Hannah Einbinder Hannah Einbinder and Gillian Anderson star in a psychosexual horror two-hander directed by Jane Schoenbrun As a writer-director, Jane Schoenbrun has already built a distinctive body of work, with their horror films We re All Going to the World s Fair and I Saw the TV Glow exploring online life, identity, memory and nostalgia. Their latest film is a two-hander between Hannah Einbinder (Hacks) and Gillian Anderson (The X-Files, Sex Education, Scoop), with the former playing an indie filmmaker set to reboot the cult slasher flick Camp Miasma with the film s original final girl, played by Anderson. Both actresses completely game to inhabit the distinctive horror-infused worlds that Schoenbrun is building. Teenage Sex and Death at Camp Miasma is an intellectual sleazefest, an ode to slashers and a takedown of modern industry politics. But at its core is the brewing connection between two women, bound by movies and desire. Every scene between Einbinder and Anderson is so charged, you ll be afraid the screen might catch fire. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)

title: Teenage Sex and Death at Camp Miasma
description: Director: Jane Schoenbrun Cast: Gillian Anderson, Hannah Einbinder Hannah Einbinder and Gillian Anderson star in a psychosexual horror two-hander directed by Jane Schoenbrun As a writer-director, Jane Schoenbrun has already built a distinctive body of work, with their horror films We re All Going to the World s Fair and I Saw the TV Glow exploring online life, identity, memory and nostalgia. title: On the Road
description: This sensual Venice winner trails a young sex worker who falls in love with an older truck driver while on the run. If Beat writer Jack Kerouac met the erotic spirit of Alfonso Cuar n s classic Y Tu Mam Tambi n, the result would most likely resemble David Pablos s searing erotic drama, produced by famed Mexican actor Diego Luna. Set in the little-explored queer scene of long-distance truck driving, On the Road follows rebellious hustler Veneno (Victor Prieto Simental), who makes a meager living by trading sexual favors at a roadside diner. His life goes from stationary to transit once he meets trucker Mu eco (Osvaldo Sanchez Valenzuela), with the two rapidly embroiled in a dangerous chase as Veneno s past threatens to catch up with them. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: The Remedy
description: Director: Alex Kahuam Cast: Timothy Granaderos, London Thor, Doug Jones, Jenny O Hara. A son s desperate attempt at keeping his mother alive leads him to making sacrifices in more ways than one in this stylish gothic horror. Jason (Timothy Granaderos) is desperate to keep his mother alive by any means, so when a not-dodgy-at-all scientist (played by genre icon Doug Jones) sells him an unconventional remedy promising to heal and extend the lifespan of anyone who takes it Jason is in. While the remedy works, it also gives Maria an insatiable appetite for live meat and Jason must provide. Meanwhile, his sister Rachel senses evil and turns to spells to expel it from their home. The siblings, while both desperate to keep their mother alive, clash over their approach, discovering what is truly haunting their home and their family. The second collaboration between director Alex Kahuam and writer Daniel Kuhlman, The Remedy is an unflinching and increasingly unhinged journey between reality, nightmare and family trauma. (ISRA AL KASSI)

title: SICKO
description: Director: Aitore Zholdaskali Cast: Ayan Utepbergen, Dilnaz Kurmangali A young couple makes a dangerous fortune by faking cancer in this biting Kazakh thriller Set in the sprawling Kazakh metropolis of Almaty, this blood-pumping thriller follows Azamat (Ayan Utepbergen) and Tansholpan (Dilnaz Kurmangali), a young couple who are recently married and terribly broke. When violent debt collectors knock on their door, the two hatch up a plan with dangerous consequences: to fake Tansholpan s terminal cancer to rack up donations. When the scam goes viral, the two find themselves embroiled in a dangerous web of lies that gives this fast-paced, stylish social satire about the throes of capitalism and the underbelly of social media a razor-sharp edge. (RAFA SALES ROSS)

title: Leviticus
description: Director: Adrian Chiarella Cast: Joe Bird, Stacy Clausen, Mia Wasikowska A queer horror story about two teenage boys confronted with a supernatural entity who takes the form of the one they desire the most. The budding romance between teens Naim (Joe Bird) and Ryan (Stacy Clausen) barely has time to flourish before they are discovered and subjected to a religious conversion ritual by their local pastor. The cure they are subjected to unleashes a terrifying ordeal. In a cruel twist of fate, the vile, shapeshifting entity takes the form of the person they desire the most: each other. Drawing on the intimacy of modern horror as It Follows, director Adrian Chiarella announces himself as a bold new voice in genre filmmaking, delivering a visceral, emotionally charged debut that s as devastating as it is terrifying. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)
